verses.269: He grants wisdom to whom He wills, and whoever is granted wisdom has truly been given much good — yet none reflect on this except people of understanding. Wisdom is described here as a gift so significant it eclipses ordinary wealth.
verses.270: Whatever you spend, or whatever vow you make, Allah surely knows it — and for the wrongdoers there is no helper. Even private, unspoken commitments are treated as fully known and fully binding.
verses.271: If you disclose your charitable giving, it is good, but if you conceal it and give it to the poor privately, that is better for you, and it will remove some of your misdeeds — Allah is fully aware of everything you do. Public and private giving are both approved, though quiet generosity is given the higher place.
verses.272: Guiding people is not something placed upon you, for Allah guides whom He wills — whatever good you spend benefits your own soul, and you spend only seeking Allah's pleasure, so whatever good you give will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged. Responsibility for outcomes is lifted here, leaving only the responsibility to give.
verses.273: Charity is especially for the poor who are held back by their circumstances in the cause of Allah, unable to move about the land; the unaware might think them well off because of their restraint from asking, but you would recognize them by their appearance, since they do not beg people persistently — whatever good you spend, Allah is fully aware of it.
verses.274: Those who spend their wealth night and day, secretly and openly, will have their reward with their Lord, with no fear upon them, nor will they grieve. A short verse of pure reassurance closes out a long meditation on giving.
verses.275: Those who consume interest cannot stand except like one driven to madness by the touch of Satan, because they claim that trade is just like interest, when Allah has permitted trade and forbidden interest; whoever receives a warning from their Lord and stops may keep what has already passed, and their matter is with Allah — but whoever returns to it, those are companions of the Fire, remaining there forever.
verses.276: Allah deprives interest of blessing and gives increase for charitable deeds — Allah does not love anyone who is persistently ungrateful and sinful. The two paths, exploitation and generosity, are shown ending in opposite outcomes even in purely material terms.
verses.277: Those who believe, do righteous deeds, establish prayer, and give charity will have their reward with their Lord, with no fear upon them, nor will they grieve — the same reassurance offered earlier to the secretly generous is now extended to the whole life of faith.
verses.278: Believers are told to be mindful of Allah and give up what remains of interest, if they are truly believers. A previous allowance is closed here entirely, framed as the real test of sincerity.
verses.279: If you do not give it up, then be warned of a war from Allah and His Messenger — but if you repent, you may keep your principal, neither wronging others nor being wronged yourselves. Even the sternest warning in the passage ends by naming the fair way out.
verses.280: If a debtor is in hardship, let there be a delay until times of ease; but if you were to give up the debt entirely as charity, that would be better for you, if only you knew. The strict right to be repaid is followed immediately by an invitation to set it aside altogether.
verses.281: Be mindful of a Day when you will all be returned to Allah, and every soul will be paid in full for what it has earned, with no one wronged. A brief, solemn reminder is placed just before the passage's most detailed instruction.
verses.282: When you take on a debt for a specified term, write it down, and let a scribe write it fairly between you — no scribe should refuse to write as Allah has taught him; let the debtor dictate, being mindful of Allah and not diminishing anything of what is owed. Call two witnesses from among your men, or one man and two women, so that if one forgets, the other can remind her; witnesses should not refuse when called upon, and do not disdain writing down whether the debt is small or large — this is more just with Allah, more upright for testimony, and more likely to prevent doubt, except in the case of a present transaction conducted between you on the spot, in which case there is no blame if you do not write it. Take witnesses when you sell to one another, and let no scribe or witness be harmed, for that would be sinful conduct — fear Allah, and Allah teaches you, for Allah has full knowledge of everything.
verses.283: If you are on a journey and cannot find a scribe, a security deposit may be taken in hand — but if one of you trusts another, let the one entrusted fulfill his trust and be mindful of Allah, his Lord. Do not conceal testimony, for whoever does has a sinful heart, and Allah knows fully what you do. What began as detailed legal procedure ends with a simple appeal to conscience for whenever the procedure cannot be followed.
verses.284: To Allah belongs everything in the heavens and everything on the earth; whether you disclose what is within yourselves or conceal it, Allah will call you to account for it, forgiving whom He wills and punishing whom He wills, for Allah has power over all things.
verses.285: The Messenger believes in what has been revealed to him from his Lord, and so do the believers — each one believes in Allah, His angels, His Books, and His messengers, saying they make no distinction between any of His messengers; and they say they hear and obey, asking for His forgiveness, for to Him is the final return.
verses.286: Allah does not burden any soul beyond what it can bear; each soul earns what it does and bears what it earns. The surah closes with a prayer taught to every believer who recites it — not to be held to account for mistakes or forgetfulness, not to be given a burden too heavy to carry as those before were burdened, to be forgiven, shown mercy, and granted help against a disbelieving people — the longest surah in the Book ending, fittingly, in a plea rather than a proclamation.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Meem —
verses.2: Allah — there is no god but Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence.
verses.3: He has sent down the Book to you in truth, confirming what came before it, and He sent down the Torah and the Gospel earlier as guidance for people, and He sent down the criterion between right and wrong.
verses.4: Those who then reject Allah's revelations will face a severe punishment — Allah is Exalted in Might, Owner of Retribution. The surah opens by insisting this Book stands in continuity with earlier revelation, not apart from it.
verses.5: Truly nothing on earth or in the heavens is hidden from Allah.
verses.6: It is He who shapes you in the wombs as He wills — there is no god but Him, the Exalted in Might, the Wise. Identity, before anyone chooses anything, is already shown here to be a gift.
verses.7: He has sent down the Book; in it are verses that are precise and foundational, and others that are open to interpretation. Those with deviation in their hearts chase the ambiguous ones, seeking discord and their own interpretation, but none truly knows their meaning except Allah — those firm in knowledge say, 'We believe in it; all of it is from our Lord.' Only people of real understanding take this to heart.
verses.8: 'Our Lord, do not let our hearts drift after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from Yourself — You are the Bestower.' A prayer against one's own future wavering is placed right after the warning about ambiguity.
verses.9: 'Our Lord, You will surely gather all people for a Day about which there is no doubt — Allah does not fail His promise.' Certainty about the outcome grounds the same believers who just admitted uncertainty about their own hearts.
verses.10: For those who disbelieve, neither their wealth nor their children will help them against Allah at all — they will be fuel for the Fire. Resources that feel like security are stripped of that meaning entirely.
verses.11: Their case is like that of Pharaoh's people and those before them who denied Our signs — Allah seized them for their sins, and Allah is severe in punishment. A pattern already proven in history is offered as the clearest warning available.
verses.12: Say to those who disbelieve: you will be overcome and gathered to Hell — a wretched resting place. The outcome is spoken as already decided, not merely threatened.
verses.13: There was a sign for you in the two armies that met at Badr — one fighting in Allah's cause, the other disbelieving, seeing the believers as twice their number to the eye; Allah supports with His help whom He wills. A battlefield becomes proof rather than mere history.
verses.14: The love of desired things is made appealing to people — women, children, heaps of gold and silver, fine horses, cattle, and fertile land — these are the enjoyment of worldly life, but the best return is with Allah. Nothing on the list is condemned; it is simply outranked.
verses.15: Say: shall I tell you of something better than all that — gardens beneath which rivers flow, to remain in forever, purified spouses, and Allah's approval. What was just named as attractive is answered here with something even more so.
verses.16: Those are the ones who say, 'Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us our sins and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.' A short prayer models exactly how such approval is asked for.
verses.17: The patient, the truthful, the obedient, those who spend in charity, and those who seek forgiveness before dawn — a compact portrait of a whole life of devotion, folded into a single verse.
verses.18: Allah bears witness that there is no god but Him, and so do the angels and people of knowledge, upholding justice — there is no god but Him, the Exalted in Might, the Wise. Testimony to the oneness of God is placed here alongside the testimony of those who simply live by justice.
verses.19: The true religion in Allah's sight is submission to Him; those given the Scripture before differed only after knowledge had reached them, out of mutual envy — whoever rejects Allah's revelations will find Allah swift in account. Division, the verse insists, came from rivalry rather than confusion.
verses.20: If they argue with you, say, 'I have submitted myself entirely to Allah, and so have those who follow me.' Say to the People of the Scripture and the unlearned, 'Have you submitted yourselves?' If they do, they are rightly guided; if they turn away, your duty is only to convey the message — Allah sees His servants.
verses.21: Those who reject Allah's revelations, kill prophets without right, and kill those who call for justice — give them news of a painful punishment. The verse names the gravest possible offenses in the same breath.
verses.22: Those are the ones whose deeds will come to nothing in this world and the next, and they will have no helpers. Effort without the right foundation is shown here to simply evaporate.
verses.23: Have you not seen those given a portion of the Scripture, invited to Allah's Book to judge between them, only for a group among them to turn away in aversion? Even direct access to guidance does not guarantee anyone will accept its verdict.
verses.24: That is because they claim the Fire will not touch them except for a few days — they have been deceived in their own religion by what they invented. A comforting story people tell themselves is exposed here as pure invention.
verses.25: How will it be when We gather them for a Day about which there is no doubt, and every soul is paid in full for what it earned, with no injustice done? The rhetorical question leaves the answer entirely unavoidable.
verses.26: Say: 'O Allah, Owner of Sovereignty, You give sovereignty to whom You will and take it from whom You will; You honor whom You will and humble whom You will; all good is in Your hand; You are capable of all things.' Power, in every direction it moves, is traced back to a single source.
verses.27: You cause the night to enter the day and the day to enter the night; You bring the living out of the dead and the dead out of the living; You give provision to whom You will without limit. The same sovereignty is now shown in the ordinary daily rhythms anyone can observe.
verses.28: Believers should not take disbelievers as allies in preference to other believers — whoever does has nothing to do with Allah, except as a precaution taken for genuine safety; Allah warns you to be mindful of Himself, and to Him is the final return.
verses.29: Say: whether you conceal what is in your hearts or reveal it, Allah knows it — He knows all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth, and He is capable of all things. Privacy of thought is shown here to be no privacy at all before Him.
verses.30: On the Day every soul will find laid out before it whatever good and whatever evil it has done, it will wish there were a great distance between itself and its evil — Allah warns you to be mindful of Himself, yet He is full of kindness toward His servants. Even the sternest warning closes on tenderness.
verses.31: Say: 'If you truly love Allah, then follow me; Allah will love you and forgive you your sins — Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.' Love of God, the verse suggests, is proven by imitation rather than by feeling alone.
verses.32: Say: 'Obey Allah and the Messenger'; if they turn away, then Allah does not love the disbelievers. A short, unambiguous line closes the chapter's opening meditation on faith and its proofs.
verses.33: Allah chose Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of Imran above all the worlds — a lineage of chosen households is named just before the story of one of those households begins.
verses.34: Descendants, one from another — Allah is Hearing, Knowing. A single line quietly bridges generations before the narrative zooms in on one family.
verses.35: The wife of Imran said, 'My Lord, I dedicate what is in my womb entirely to You, so accept this from me — You are the Hearing, the Knowing.' A vow is made before the child's identity is even known.
verses.36: When she delivered, she said, 'My Lord, I have delivered a female' — though Allah already knew what she had delivered, and the male she had hoped for was not like the female she was given — 'I have named her Mary, and I seek Your protection for her and her offspring from Satan, the outcast.'
verses.37: Her Lord accepted her graciously, caused her to grow in a good manner, and placed her in the care of Zechariah; whenever he entered her sanctuary he found provision with her, and he asked, 'Mary, where does this come from?' She answered, 'From Allah — He provides for whom He wills without account.'
verses.38: There Zechariah called upon his Lord: 'My Lord, grant me from Yourself righteous offspring — You are the Hearer of supplication.' Witnessing the provision given to Mary stirs in him a request of his own.
verses.39: The angels called to him while he stood praying in the sanctuary: 'Allah gives you good news of John, confirming a word from Allah, honorable, restrained, and a prophet from among the righteous.'
verses.40: He said, 'My Lord, how can I have a boy when old age has come upon me and my wife is barren?' — 'Thus Allah does what He wills,' came the reply. Doubt is met not with rebuke but with a simple restatement of divine capability.
verses.41: He said, 'My Lord, give me a sign' — 'Your sign is that you will not speak to people for three days except by gesture; remember your Lord often, and glorify Him in the evening and the morning.'
verses.42: The angels said, 'Mary, Allah has chosen you, purified you, and chosen you above the women of all the worlds.' The story shifts fully onto Mary herself, addressed directly by name.
verses.43: 'Mary, be devoutly obedient to your Lord, prostrate, and bow with those who bow.' Even someone singled out this way is instructed toward ordinary, disciplined worship.
verses.44: This is news of the unseen that We reveal to you — you were not present when they cast lots to decide who would be responsible for Mary's care. The Prophet is reminded he could not have known this story except by revelation.
verses.45: The angels said, 'Mary, Allah gives you good news of a word from Him, whose name is the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, honored in this world and the next, and among those brought near to Allah.'
verses.46: 'He will speak to people in the cradle and in maturity, and will be among the righteous.' A miracle is promised before the child is even born.
verses.47: She said, 'My Lord, how can I have a child when no man has touched me?' — 'Thus Allah creates what He wills; when He decrees a matter, He simply says to it, Be, and it is.'
verses.48: He will teach him writing, wisdom, the Torah, and the Gospel — the child's future is described before his birth, as fully formed as any prophecy.
verses.49: As a messenger to the Children of Israel, he would say, 'I have come to you with a sign from your Lord: I design for you from clay the shape of a bird, then breathe into it, and it becomes a real bird by Allah's permission; I heal the blind and the leper, and give life to the dead by Allah's permission; and I inform you of what you eat and what you store in your houses — surely in that is a sign for you, if you are believers.'
verses.50: 'And I come confirming the Torah that came before me, and to make lawful for you some of what was forbidden to you; I have brought you a sign from your Lord, so fear Allah and obey me.'
verses.51: 'Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him — that is a straight path.' Jesus's own message is stated here in the plainest possible terms.
verses.52: When Jesus sensed disbelief from them, he asked, 'Who will be my helpers for Allah?' The disciples answered, 'We are Allah's helpers; we believe in Allah, and bear witness that we are submitted to Him.'
verses.53: 'Our Lord, we believe in what You have sent down and we have followed the Messenger, so record us among those who bear witness.' A whole community responds to a single question at once.
verses.54: And they schemed, and Allah schemed — and Allah is the best of schemers. Human plotting against the truth is answered by a plan far greater than theirs.
verses.55: Allah said, 'Jesus, I will take you and raise you to Myself, and purify you of those who disbelieved, and place those who follow you above those who disbelieved until the Day of Resurrection — then to Me is your return, and I will judge between you concerning that in which you differed.'
verses.56: As for those who disbelieved, He will punish them severely in this world and the next, and they will have no helpers. A firm boundary is drawn between the outcomes of belief and rejection.
verses.57: But those who believed and did righteous deeds will be given their reward in full — Allah does not love wrongdoers.
verses.58: This is what We recite to you of the verses and the wise reminder — the whole account of Mary and Jesus is framed here as revelation given for reflection, not merely as history.
verses.59: The example of Jesus in Allah's sight is like that of Adam — He created him from dust, then said to him, Be, and he was. A miraculous birth is placed in the same category as the very first human creation.
verses.60: The truth is from your Lord, so do not be among the doubters — a simple, direct instruction follows a story built almost entirely out of wonder.
verses.61: To whoever argues with you about this after knowledge has come to you, say: 'Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves, then let us pray earnestly and invoke Allah's curse upon whoever is lying.' Sincerity is put to the ultimate test here, rather than argument continuing indefinitely.
verses.62: This is certainly the true account — there is no god except Allah, and indeed, Allah is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.63: And if they turn away, then Allah is fully aware of those who cause corruption.
verses.64: Say: 'O People of the Scripture, come to a word common between us and you — that we worship none but Allah, associate nothing with Him, and none of us take others as lords besides Allah.' If they turn away, say, 'Bear witness that we are Muslims.' Common ground is proposed here before any disagreement is even addressed.
verses.65: O People of the Scripture, why do you argue about Abraham, when the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until after him? A simple chronological fact undercuts a whole line of dispute.
verses.66: Here you are, arguing about matters you have some knowledge of — why then do you argue about what you have no knowledge of at all? Allah knows, and you do not.
verses.67: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but was one inclined entirely toward truth, submitted to Allah, and he was not among those who associate partners with Him.
verses.68: The people closest to Abraham are those who followed him in his time, this Prophet, and those who believe — Allah is the protector of the believers.
verses.69: A faction among the People of the Scripture wishes to lead you astray, but they mislead only themselves without realizing it.
verses.70: O People of the Scripture, why do you reject Allah's revelations while you yourselves witness their truth?
verses.71: O People of the Scripture, why do you mix truth with falsehood and hide the truth while you know it?
verses.72: A group among them said, 'Believe in what was revealed to the believers at the start of the day, and reject it by its end, so that perhaps they will turn back.' A deliberate strategy of confusion is exposed here rather than left implicit.
verses.73: 'And do not believe except those who follow your own religion' — say, 'True guidance is the guidance of Allah,' lest anyone should be given what you were given, or argue against you before your Lord; say, 'All bounty is in Allah's hand; He gives it to whom He wills.'
verses.74: He specifies His mercy for whom He wills, and Allah is the Owner of great bounty. Generosity, the verse insists, cannot be gatekept by any single group.
verses.75: Among the People of the Scripture is one who, if entrusted with a great amount, will return it, and among them is one who, if entrusted with a single coin, will not return it unless you keep demanding it — because they say, 'There is no blame on us regarding the unlearned,' and they speak a lie against Allah while knowing it.
verses.76: But whoever fulfills his commitment and is mindful of Allah — indeed, Allah loves those who are righteous. Individual honesty is shown to matter regardless of a group's reputation.
verses.77: Those who exchange Allah's covenant and their own oaths for a small price will have no share in the Hereafter — Allah will not speak to them, nor look at them, nor purify them on the Day of Resurrection, and for them is a painful punishment.
verses.78: A faction among them twists their tongues in reciting the Scripture so you would think it is from the Scripture, though it is not from the Scripture; they say it is from Allah, though it is not from Allah — they speak a lie against Allah while they know it.
verses.79: It is not conceivable that a person to whom Allah gives the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood would then say to people, 'Be servants to me rather than Allah' — rather, he would say, 'Be pious scholars of the Lord, because of your teaching the Scripture and your study of it.'
verses.80: Nor would he order you to take angels and prophets as lords — would he order you to disbelief after you had submitted? Devotion to a prophet is never allowed to eclipse devotion to Allah.
verses.81: Allah took a covenant from the prophets: 'Whatever I give you of the Scripture and wisdom, then a messenger comes confirming what is with you, you are to believe in him and support him' — He asked, 'Do you affirm this and accept My binding covenant?' They said, 'We affirm it.' He said, 'Then bear witness, and I am with you among the witnesses.'
verses.82: Whoever turns away after that — those are the defiantly disobedient. A covenant this old and this explicit leaves little room to claim ignorance.
verses.83: So do they seek other than the religion of Allah, while everything in the heavens and earth has submitted to Him, willingly or unwillingly, and to Him they will all be returned? The question quietly points out that submission to Allah is already the natural state of creation.
verses.84: Say: 'We believe in Allah and in what was revealed to us, and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and their descendants, and what was given to Moses, Jesus, and the prophets from their Lord; we make no distinction between any of them, and we are submitted to Him.'
verses.85: Whoever seeks a religion other than submission to Allah, it will never be accepted from him, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers.
verses.86: How will Allah guide a people who disbelieved after having believed, and after witnessing that the Messenger is true, and after clear proofs had come to them? Allah does not guide wrongdoing people.
verses.87: For such people the penalty is that upon them is the curse of Allah, the angels, and all of humankind together.
verses.88: Abiding eternally therein — the punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be given any respite.
verses.89: Except those who repent after that and set themselves right — indeed, Allah is Forgiving, Merciful. Even this severe warning ends with a door left open.
verses.90: Those who disbelieve after their belief and then increase in disbelief — their repentance will never be accepted, and it is they who are truly astray. A distinction is drawn here between a genuine change of heart and a hardening pattern of rejection.
verses.91: Those who disbelieve and die while disbelievers — not even an earth full of gold would be accepted from any of them if offered as ransom; for them is a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.
verses.92: You will never attain true righteousness until you spend from what you love — and whatever you spend, Allah knows it fully. Generosity is measured here by what it costs the giver, not by its size alone.
verses.93: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel had forbidden for himself before the Torah was revealed; say, 'Bring the Torah and recite it, if you are being truthful.' A specific claim is answered by inviting direct verification.
verses.94: Whoever invents a lie about Allah after that — such people are the true wrongdoers.
verses.95: Say: 'Allah has spoken the truth, so follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth, for he was not among the polytheists.'
verses.96: The first House established for humanity was the one at Makkah, blessed and a guidance for all the worlds.
verses.97: In it are clear signs, among them the standing place of Abraham; whoever enters it is safe; pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by whoever is able to make the journey — and whoever disbelieves, Allah is free of need of all the worlds.
verses.98: Say: 'O People of the Scripture, why do you reject Allah's revelations while Allah is a witness over everything you do?'
verses.99: Say: 'O People of the Scripture, why do you turn believers away from Allah's path, seeking to make it appear crooked, while you yourselves are witnesses to its truth? Allah is not unaware of what you do.'
verses.100: O believers, if you obey a faction among those given the Scripture, they will turn you back into disbelief after your belief. A warning against outside influence follows directly after describing that influence's tactics.
verses.101: How could you disbelieve while Allah's verses are being recited to you and His Messenger is among you? Whoever holds firmly to Allah is guided to a straight path.
verses.102: O believers, fear Allah as He should be feared, and do not die except as people submitted to Him.
verses.103: Hold firmly to the rope of Allah together, and do not become divided; remember Allah's favor upon you, when you were enemies and He joined your hearts, so that by His favor you became brothers, and you were on the edge of a pit of Fire, and He saved you from it — this is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so that you may be guided.
verses.104: Let there be among you a group inviting to good, ordering what is right, and forbidding what is wrong — those are the ones who will succeed.
verses.105: Do not be like those who became divided and differed after clear proofs had come to them — for them is a great punishment. Division, once again, is treated as one of the most serious failures a community can fall into.
verses.106: On a Day some faces will turn white and some faces will turn black; to those with blackened faces it will be said, 'Did you disbelieve after your belief? Then taste the punishment for what you used to reject.'
verses.107: As for those whose faces turn white, they will be in Allah's mercy, abiding therein forever.
verses.108: These are the verses of Allah, recited to you in truth — Allah does not intend injustice for any of the worlds.
verses.109: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and to Allah all matters are returned.
verses.110: You are the best nation ever produced for humanity — you order what is right, forbid what is wrong, and believe in Allah; had the People of the Scripture believed, it would have been better for them, though some among them are believers, while most are defiantly disobedient.
verses.111: They will not harm you except for a slight annoyance, and if they fight you, they will turn their backs in flight, and then they will not be helped.
verses.112: Humiliation has been cast upon them wherever they are found, except when protected by a covenant with Allah or with people; they have drawn upon themselves anger from Allah and have been struck with poverty, because they used to reject Allah's revelations and kill prophets without right, for their disobedience and transgression.
verses.113: They are not all the same — among the People of the Scripture is a community standing upright, reciting the verses of Allah during the hours of the night and prostrating in prayer. A sweeping condemnation is immediately qualified with a genuine exception.
verses.114: They believe in Allah and the Last Day, order what is right and forbid what is wrong, and hasten to good deeds — those are among the righteous.
verses.115: Whatever good they do will never be denied its reward, and Allah is fully aware of the righteous.
verses.116: As for those who disbelieve, neither their wealth nor their children will avail them at all against Allah — those are the companions of the Fire, abiding therein forever.
verses.117: The example of what they spend in this worldly life is like a frost-bearing wind that struck the harvest of a people who wronged themselves and destroyed it — Allah did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.
verses.118: O believers, do not take as intimate companions those outside your own community, for they will not spare you any corruption and wish you nothing but harm; hatred has already appeared from their mouths, and what their hearts conceal is far worse — the signs have been made clear to you, if only you would reason.
verses.119: Here you are, loving them while they do not love you, though you believe in the Scripture in its entirety; when they meet you they say, 'We believe,' but when they are alone they bite their fingertips at you in rage — say, 'Die in your rage,' for Allah knows well what is in every heart.
verses.120: If something good touches you, it distresses them, and if something bad befalls you, they rejoice at it — but if you remain patient and mindful of Allah, their plotting will not harm you at all, for Allah encompasses everything they do.
verses.121: Recall when you left your family early to position the believers for battle at their posts — Allah is Hearing, Knowing. The narrative shifts here from advice about outside influence into the memory of a specific, difficult day.
verses.122: When two factions among you were about to lose heart, though Allah was their protector — and upon Allah let the believers place their trust. Even wavering resolve is met with reassurance rather than rebuke.
verses.123: Allah had already given you victory at Badr while you were weak in number — so be mindful of Allah, that you might be grateful.
verses.124: Recall when you said to the believers, 'Is it not enough that your Lord should reinforce you with three thousand angels sent down?'
verses.125: Yes — if you remain patient and mindful of Allah, and the enemy comes upon you suddenly, your Lord will reinforce you with five thousand angels, clearly marked.
verses.126: Allah made this only as good news for you and so that your hearts would be at rest through it — victory comes only from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise. The angelic reinforcement is described here as reassurance more than as the actual mechanism of victory.
verses.127: That He might cut off a section of the disbelievers or subdue them, so that they would turn back in disappointment.
verses.128: It is not up to you whether Allah turns to them in forgiveness or punishes them, for they are truly wrongdoers. The outcome, even for the Prophet himself, is left entirely in Allah's hands.
verses.129: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth; He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills — Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.130: O believers, do not consume interest, doubled and multiplied — be mindful of Allah, that you may succeed. The passage returns abruptly from battle back to everyday financial conduct.
verses.131: And guard yourselves against the Fire, which has been prepared for the disbelievers.
verses.132: Obey Allah and the Messenger, that you may receive mercy.
verses.133: Hasten toward forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as wide as the heavens and the earth, prepared for the righteous.
verses.134: Those who spend in ease and in hardship, who restrain their anger, and who pardon other people — Allah loves those who do good. Generosity, self-control, and forgiveness are named together as a single quality rather than three separate virtues.
verses.135: And those who, when they commit an immoral act or wrong themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins — for who forgives sins except Allah — and who do not persist knowingly in what they have done.
verses.136: For them the reward is forgiveness from their Lord and gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever — an excellent reward for those who labor toward it.
verses.137: Similar situations have already passed before you, so travel through the land and observe how those who denied the truth ended up.
verses.138: This is a clear statement for all people, and a guidance and instruction for the righteous.
verses.139: Do not weaken and do not grieve, for you will be superior if you are truly believers. Comfort is offered here in the immediate aftermath of visible hardship.
verses.140: If a wound has touched you, a similar wound has already touched other people too; We alternate these days of victory and loss among people, so that Allah may make evident who truly believes and honor some among you as martyrs — Allah does not love wrongdoers.
verses.141: And so that Allah may purify the believers and destroy the disbelievers. Loss, painful as it is, is described here as serving a purpose rather than being random.
verses.142: Did you think you would enter Paradise while Allah had not yet made evident who among you strives and who among you is patient?
verses.143: You were certainly longing for death before you encountered it, and now you have seen it with your own eyes. A wish spoken before hardship is measured here against the reality once it actually arrived.
verses.144: Muhammad is only a messenger; other messengers have passed before him — so if he were to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels? Whoever turns back will not harm Allah at all, and Allah will reward those who are grateful.
verses.145: It is not possible for a soul to die except by Allah's permission, at a predetermined time; whoever desires the reward of this world We will give him some of it, and whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter We will give him some of it — and We will surely reward the grateful.
verses.146: How many a prophet has fought alongside him companions in great number, who were not weakened by what befell them in Allah's cause, nor did they weaken or surrender — Allah loves the steadfast.
verses.147: Their only words were, 'Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our excesses, make our feet firm, and give us victory over the disbelieving people.'
verses.148: So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the good reward of the Hereafter — Allah loves those who do good.
verses.149: O believers, if you obey those who disbelieve, they will turn you back on your heels, and you will end up as losers.
verses.150: Rather, Allah is your protector, and He is the best of helpers. A clear alternative is offered right after the warning against following the wrong allies.
verses.151: We will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve, because they associated with Allah something for which He sent down no authority — their refuge is the Fire, a wretched dwelling for the wrongdoers.
verses.152: Allah had certainly fulfilled His promise to you when you were defeating them by His permission, until you lost courage and disputed among yourselves about the command and disobeyed after He had shown you what you loved; among you were some who desired this world and some who desired the Hereafter — then He diverted you from them to test you, though He has already forgiven you, for Allah is full of bounty toward the believers.
verses.153: Recall when you were fleeing without turning back for anyone, while the Messenger was calling out to you from behind — so He gave you distress upon distress, so that you would not grieve over what escaped you or what befell you, and Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.154: Then after distress He sent down security, a drowsiness overtaking a group of you, while another group was anxious about themselves, thinking wrongly about Allah — thoughts of ignorance — saying, 'Do we have any say in this matter at all?' Say, 'The matter belongs entirely to Allah.'
verses.155: Those of you who turned away on the day the two armies met — it was Satan alone who caused them to slip because of something they had done; but Allah has already forgiven them, for Allah is Forgiving, Forbearing.
verses.156: O believers, do not be like those who disbelieved and said of their brothers who traveled or went out to fight, 'If they had stayed with us, they would not have died or been killed' — Allah made that only a cause of regret in their hearts, for it is Allah who gives life and causes death, and Allah sees everything you do.
verses.157: If you are killed in Allah's cause or you die, forgiveness from Allah and mercy are far better than whatever they could ever accumulate.
verses.158: And whether you die or are killed, it is certainly to Allah that you will be gathered.
verses.159: It was by mercy from Allah that you were gentle with them — had you been harsh and hard-hearted, they would have scattered away from you; so pardon them, ask forgiveness for them, and consult them in the matter; and once you have made a decision, put your trust in Allah, for Allah loves those who rely upon Him.
verses.160: If Allah helps you, none can overcome you; but if He abandons you, who then could help you after Him? Upon Allah let the believers place their trust.
verses.161: It would not befit a prophet to hide something unlawfully — whoever hides something will bring what he hid on the Day of Resurrection; then every soul will be paid in full for what it earned, and none will be wronged.
verses.162: Is one who pursues Allah's pleasure like one who has drawn Allah's anger upon himself, whose refuge is Hell? What a wretched destination.
verses.163: They are all at varying degrees in Allah's sight, and Allah is fully aware of everything they do.
verses.164: Allah has certainly conferred a great favor upon the believers by sending among them a messenger from among themselves, reciting to them His verses, purifying them, and teaching them the Scripture and wisdom, though before that they were clearly astray.
verses.165: Why, when a calamity struck you — though you had struck them with twice as much before — did you say, 'How did this happen?' Say, 'It is from yourselves' — Allah is capable of all things.
verses.166: What befell you on the day the two armies met was by Allah's permission, and so that He might make evident who among you were true believers.
verses.167: And so that He might make evident those who practiced hypocrisy — told to come fight in Allah's cause or at least defend, they said, 'If we knew how to fight, we would have followed you'; they were nearer to disbelief that day than to belief, saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts, and Allah knows well what they concealed.
verses.168: Those who said of their brothers, while sitting back themselves, 'If only they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed' — say to them, 'Then prevent death from yourselves, if you are being truthful.'
verses.169: Do not think of those who have been killed in Allah's cause as dead — rather, they are alive with their Lord, receiving provision.
verses.170: Rejoicing in what Allah has given them of His bounty, and delighting in the good news for those left behind who have not yet joined them, that there will be no fear upon them nor will they grieve.
verses.171: They receive good tidings of favor from Allah and a great bounty, and that Allah does not allow the reward of believers to be lost.
verses.172: Those who responded to Allah and His Messenger after injury had befallen them — for those among them who did good and remained mindful of Allah is a great reward.
verses.173: Those to whom people warned, 'The enemy has gathered against you, so fear them,' only increased in faith and said, 'Sufficient for us is Allah, and He is the best Disposer of affairs.' Warnings meant to frighten only strengthened their conviction instead.
verses.174: So they returned with favor and bounty from Allah, having suffered no harm, and they pursued Allah's pleasure — Allah is the Owner of great bounty.
verses.175: That is only Satan frightening you through his allies, so do not fear them, but fear Me, if you are truly believers. Fear itself is redirected here, from a human enemy toward a proper reverence for Allah alone.
verses.176: Do not let those who hasten toward disbelief grieve you — they will not harm Allah at all; Allah intends to give them no share in the Hereafter, and for them is a great punishment.
verses.177: Those who purchase disbelief in exchange for faith will never harm Allah at all — for them is a painful punishment.
verses.178: Let not those who disbelieve suppose that Our extending their time is good for them — We only extend it so that they may increase in sin, and for them is a humiliating punishment. What can look like leniency is revealed here as a form of consequence in itself.
verses.179: Allah would not leave the believers in the state you are in until He separates the corrupt from the good, nor would He inform you of the unseen — but Allah chooses of His messengers whom He wills, so believe in Allah and His messengers; if you believe and are mindful of Him, you will have a great reward.
verses.180: Let not those who withhold what Allah has given them of His bounty think it is good for them — rather, it is bad for them, for what they withheld will be wrapped around their necks on the Day of Resurrection; to Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.181: Allah has certainly heard the statement of those who said, 'Allah is poor and we are rich' — He will record what they said, along with their killing of prophets without right, and will say, 'Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.'
verses.182: That is for what your own hands have sent forth — and Allah is never unjust to His servants.
verses.183: Those who said, 'Allah has instructed us not to believe in any messenger until he brings us a sacrifice consumed by fire' — say, 'Messengers came to you before me with clear proofs and with what you describe, so why did you kill them, if you were truthful?'
verses.184: If they deny you, then messengers before you were also denied, though they had brought clear proofs, scriptures, and the enlightening Book — you are placed here in a long line of rejected messengers, not an isolated one.
verses.185: Every soul will taste death, and you will only be given your full compensation on the Day of Resurrection; whoever is drawn away from the Fire and admitted into Paradise has truly succeeded, for the life of this world is nothing but the enjoyment of delusion.
verses.186: You will surely be tested in your possessions and in yourselves, and you will surely hear much abuse from those given the Scripture before you and from the polytheists; but if you are patient and mindful of Allah, that is a matter worthy of real resolve.
verses.187: Allah took a covenant from those given the Scripture to make it clear to people and not conceal it — but they discarded it behind their backs and exchanged it for a small price; how wretched is what they purchased with it.
verses.188: Do not think that those who rejoice in what they have done and love to be praised for what they did not even do are safe from punishment — for them is a painful punishment.
verses.189: To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is capable of all things.
verses.190: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, are signs for people of true understanding.
verses.191: Those who remember Allah while standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and who reflect deeply on the creation of the heavens and the earth, say, 'Our Lord, You did not create this without purpose — exalted are You, so protect us from the punishment of the Fire.'
verses.192: 'Our Lord, whoever You admit to the Fire, You have truly disgraced him, and the wrongdoers will have no helpers.'
verses.193: 'Our Lord, we heard a caller calling to faith, saying, Believe in your Lord, and we believed — Our Lord, forgive us our sins, remove from us our misdeeds, and cause us to die among the righteous.'
verses.194: 'Our Lord, grant us what You have promised us through Your messengers, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection — You never fail to keep Your promise.' Reflection on creation flows directly into an extended, urgent prayer.
verses.195: Their Lord answered them: 'I will never let the deeds of any doer among you be lost, male or female — you are of one another; those who emigrated, were expelled from their homes, were harmed in My cause, fought, and were killed — I will surely remove their misdeeds from them and admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow, a reward from Allah, and with Allah is the best of rewards.'
verses.196: Do not be deceived by the free movement throughout the land of those who disbelieve.
verses.197: It is only a brief enjoyment; then their refuge will be Hell — a truly wretched resting place. Apparent freedom is reframed here as something painfully temporary.
verses.198: But those who fear their Lord will have gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever, as a welcome from Allah — and what is with Allah is best for the righteous.
verses.199: Among the People of the Scripture are some who believe in Allah and in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed to them, humbly submissive to Allah, not exchanging Allah's revelations for a small price — for them is their reward with their Lord.
verses.200: O believers, be patient, outdo one another in patience, remain firm at your posts, and be mindful of Allah, so that you may succeed. The surah closes with a call to endurance, echoing the hardship it has walked through from its opening verses to its very last.

verses.1: O mankind, be mindful of your Lord, who created you from a single soul, created its mate from it, and spread from the two of them countless men and women; be mindful of Allah, in whose name you make requests of one another, and honor the ties of kinship — Allah is ever watchful over you. The surah opens by grounding every relationship that follows in a single shared origin.
verses.2: Give orphans their property, do not exchange the bad for the good, and do not consume their wealth into your own — that is truly a great sin. A community's honesty is measured here by how it treats those with no power to check the accounts.
verses.3: If you fear you cannot deal justly with orphan girls, then marry other women who are lawful to you — two, three, or four; but if you fear you cannot be just, then only one, or those your right hand possesses — that is more likely to prevent injustice.
verses.4: Give women their bridal gifts as a genuine obligation; if they willingly give up any part of it, then enjoy it freely and wholesomely.
verses.5: Do not hand over your wealth, which Allah has made a means of support, to those not yet capable of sound judgment — but provide for them and clothe them from it, and speak to them in kind words.
verses.6: Test orphans until they reach marriageable age; if you find sound judgment in them, hand over their property; do not consume it wastefully or hastily out of fear that they will grow up and claim it; whoever is wealthy should abstain entirely, and whoever is poor may take only what is fair; and when you hand over their property, call witnesses — Allah is sufficient as a Reckoner.
verses.7: Men shall have a share of what parents and close relatives leave, and women shall have a share of what parents and close relatives leave, whether it is little or much — a legally binding share. A right long denied is stated here without qualification.
verses.8: If relatives, orphans, or the needy are present at the time of division, give them something from it and speak to them kindly.
verses.9: Let those who would fear for their own weak offspring if they left them behind be mindful of Allah in how they treat other people's orphans, and let them speak words that are just and appropriate.
verses.10: Those who consume the property of orphans unjustly are really consuming nothing but fire into their own bellies, and they will burn in a blazing Fire.
verses.11: Allah instructs you concerning your children: the male shall have a share equal to that of two females; if there are more than two females, they get two-thirds of the estate, and if only one, she gets half; each parent gets a sixth if the deceased left children, but if there are no children and the parents are the heirs, the mother gets a third; if there are siblings, the mother gets a sixth, after any bequest or debt is settled — you do not know which of your parents or children is nearer to you in benefit; this is an obligation from Allah, and Allah is Knowing, Wise. A precise and detailed formula sits at the heart of a chapter otherwise devoted to compassion for the vulnerable.
verses.12: You get half of what your wives leave if they have no child, but if they have a child, you get a quarter, after any bequest or debt; they get a quarter of what you leave if you have no child, but if you have a child, they get an eighth; if a man or woman leaves neither parent nor child but has a sibling, each sibling gets a sixth, and if more, they share a third, after any bequest or debt that does no harm — an instruction from Allah, and Allah is Knowing, Forbearing.
verses.13: These are the limits set by Allah; whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger will be admitted into gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever — that is the great attainment.
verses.14: But whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger and transgresses His limits will be admitted into a Fire, abiding therein, and he will have a humiliating punishment. Detailed inheritance law is framed here as something people either respect or violate at real cost.
verses.15: For those among your women who commit immorality, call four witnesses from among yourselves; if they testify, confine the women to their houses until death takes them or Allah ordains another way for them.
verses.16: If two among you commit it, punish them both; but if they repent and reform, leave them alone — Allah is ever Accepting of repentance, Merciful.
verses.17: Allah's acceptance of repentance is only for those who do wrong in ignorance and then repent soon after — Allah turns to them, for Allah is Knowing, Wise. A window for genuine turning back is drawn carefully here.
verses.18: There is no acceptance of repentance for those who keep doing evil right up until death approaches and only then say, 'Now I repent,' nor for those who die as disbelievers — for them We have prepared a painful punishment.
verses.19: O believers, it is not lawful for you to inherit women against their will, nor to pressure them so you can take back part of what you gave them, unless they commit a clear act of immorality; live with them honorably, and if you dislike them, it may be that you dislike something in which Allah has placed great good.
verses.20: If you wish to replace one wife with another and you have given the first a great amount, do not take any of it back — would you take it in injustice and obvious sin?
verses.21: How could you take it back, when you have been intimate with one another and she has taken from you a solemn covenant? Marriage is described here in the language of a binding trust, not a transaction that can simply be reversed.
verses.22: Do not marry women whom your fathers married, except what has already occurred in the past — that was an immoral, hateful, and evil practice.
verses.23: Forbidden to you are your mothers, daughters, sisters, paternal and maternal aunts, brothers' daughters, sisters' daughters, foster mothers who nursed you, foster sisters, wives' mothers, and stepdaughters under your care if you have consummated the marriage with their mothers — if not, there is no blame; also forbidden are the wives of your own sons, and marrying two sisters simultaneously, except what has already occurred — Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.24: Also forbidden are already-married women, except those bound to you by lawful ownership — this is Allah's decree upon you; beyond that, it is lawful for you to seek wives with your wealth in honest marriage, not fornication; give them their due compensation as an obligation, though there is no blame in whatever you mutually agree to beyond that — Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.25: Whoever among you cannot afford to marry free believing women may marry from among your believing maids — Allah knows best about your faith, and you all come from one another; marry them with the permission of their guardians and give them their due compensation fairly, in chastity, not in fornication or secret liaisons; if they are married and commit immorality, their punishment is half that of free women — this option is for those among you who fear falling into sin, though patience is better for you — Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.26: Allah wants to make things clear to you, guide you to the practices of those who came before you, and accept your repentance — Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.27: Allah wants to accept your repentance, but those who follow their own desires want you to deviate completely from the right path.
verses.28: Allah wants to lighten your burdens, for humankind was created weak. A whole passage of demanding law is punctuated here with a reminder of divine mercy behind it.
verses.29: Do not consume one another's wealth unjustly, but only through mutual consent in trade — and do not kill one another, for Allah is ever Merciful to you.
verses.30: Whoever does that in aggression and injustice will be cast into a Fire — and that is easy for Allah.
verses.31: If you avoid the major sins you have been forbidden, We will remove your lesser misdeeds and admit you through a noble gate.
verses.32: Do not wish for what Allah has favored some of you over others with; men will have a share of what they have earned, and women will have a share of what they have earned — ask Allah of His bounty, for Allah is fully aware of all things.
verses.33: For everyone We have appointed heirs to what parents and close relatives leave; give those bound to you by oath their due share — Allah is Witness over all things.
verses.34: Men are the protectors and maintainers of women, because Allah has favored some over others in certain respects and because they spend from their wealth; righteous women are devoutly obedient, guarding in their husbands' absence what Allah has ordered them to guard; toward those from whom you fear arrogant defiance, first advise them, then part from their beds, then a light discipline — but if they obey you, do not seek a way against them; Allah is ever Exalted, Grand.
verses.35: If you fear a breach between a couple, appoint an arbitrator from his family and one from hers; if the two arbitrators genuinely desire reconciliation, Allah will cause harmony between them — Allah is Knowing, Fully Aware.
verses.36: Worship Allah and associate nothing with Him, and do good to parents, relatives, orphans, the needy, the near neighbor, the distant neighbor, the companion at your side, the traveler, and those bound to you by oath — Allah does not love the arrogant, the boastful.
verses.37: Those who are stingy, urge others toward stinginess, and hide what Allah has given them of His bounty — We have prepared a humiliating punishment for those who reject faith like this.
verses.38: And those who spend their wealth only to be seen by people, and do not believe in Allah or the Last Day — whoever takes Satan as a companion has taken the worst possible companion.
verses.39: What would it have cost them to believe in Allah and the Last Day and spend from what Allah provided them? Allah knows them fully. A quiet question underlines how little was actually being asked of them.
verses.40: Indeed, Allah does not wrong anyone even by the weight of an atom, and if there is a good deed, He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward.
verses.41: How will it be when We bring a witness from every nation, and bring you as a witness against these people? The Prophet's role as witness is stated here almost in passing before the far heavier scene that follows.
verses.42: On that Day those who disbelieved and disobeyed the Messenger will wish the earth could be leveled flat over them, and they will not be able to hide a single statement from Allah.
verses.43: Do not approach prayer while intoxicated, until you know what you are saying, nor in a state of major impurity, except when merely passing through, until you have bathed; but if you are ill, traveling, have relieved yourselves, or been intimate with your spouses and cannot find water, then seek clean earth and wipe your faces and hands with it — Allah is ever Pardoning, Forgiving.
verses.44: Have you not seen those given a portion of the Scripture, purchasing error and wishing you too would lose your way?
verses.45: Allah knows your enemies best — Allah suffices as an ally, and Allah suffices as a helper.
verses.46: Among the Jews are some who twist words out of their context, saying, 'We hear and we disobey,' or, 'Hear, but be not heard,' and twisting their tongues to defame the religion; had they instead said, 'We hear and we obey,' and, 'Grant us patience while we understand,' it would have been better for them — but Allah has cursed them for their disbelief, so they believe only a little.
verses.47: O you who were given the Scripture, believe in what We have sent down, confirming what is already with you, before We obliterate faces and turn them backward, or curse them as We cursed those who violated the Sabbath.
verses.48: Allah does not forgive associating anything with Him, but He forgives whatever is short of that for whomever He wills — whoever associates partners with Allah has invented a tremendous sin.
verses.49: Have you not seen those who claim purity for themselves? Rather, Allah purifies whom He wills, and no one will be wronged even by a thread.
verses.50: See how they invent a lie about Allah — that alone is sufficient as a clear sin.
verses.51: Have you not seen those given a portion of the Scripture believing in idols and false objects of worship, and saying of the disbelievers, 'These people are better guided than the believers'?
verses.52: Those are the ones Allah has cursed — and whoever Allah curses, you will never find a helper for him.
verses.53: Or do they have a share of the dominion? If so, they would not give people even a speck on a date seed.
verses.54: Or do they envy people for what Allah has given them of His bounty? We already gave the family of Abraham the Scripture and wisdom, and gave them a great kingdom.
verses.55: Some among them believed in it, and some turned away from it — Hell is sufficient as a blazing fire for them.
verses.56: Those who reject Our verses, We will burn in a Fire; every time their skin is thoroughly roasted, We will replace it with other skin, so they may keep tasting the punishment — Allah is ever Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.57: But those who believe and do righteous deeds We will admit into gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever, with purified spouses, and We will admit them into deep, refreshing shade.
verses.58: Allah commands you to return trusts to their rightful owners, and when you judge between people, to judge with justice — excellent indeed is what Allah instructs you to do; Allah is ever Hearing, Seeing.
verses.59: O believers, obey Allah, obey the Messenger, and those in authority among you; if you disagree over anything, refer it back to Allah and the Messenger, if you truly believe in Allah and the Last Day — that is best and produces the best outcome.
verses.60: Have you not seen those who claim belief in what has been revealed to you and before you, yet wish to refer disputes to something other than Allah's judgment, though they were commanded to reject it? Satan wants to lead them far, far astray.
verses.61: When it is said to them, 'Come to what Allah has revealed and to the Messenger,' you see the hypocrites turning away from you in aversion.
verses.62: How then, when disaster strikes them because of what their own hands have sent forth, do they come to you swearing, 'We intended nothing but good conduct and reconciliation'?
verses.63: Those are the ones Allah knows what is in their hearts — so turn away from them, admonish them, and speak to them words that will truly reach them.
verses.64: We sent no messenger except to be obeyed by Allah's permission; had they, when they wronged themselves, come to you and asked Allah's forgiveness, and had the Messenger asked forgiveness for them, they would have found Allah Accepting of repentance, Merciful.
verses.65: But no, by your Lord, they will not truly believe until they make you the judge of what is in dispute between them, then find within themselves no discomfort about what you have decided, and submit in full submission.
verses.66: Had We decreed for them, 'Kill yourselves,' or, 'Leave your homes,' very few of them would have done it — but had they done what they were advised, it would have been better for them and would have strengthened them.
verses.67: And We would have surely given them from Ourselves a great reward.
verses.68: And We would have guided them to a straight path. Obedience that seems demanding here is described only in terms of the reward that would have followed it.
verses.69: Whoever obeys Allah and the Messenger will be among those Allah has favored — the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous — what excellent company they are.
verses.70: That is a bounty from Allah, and sufficient is Allah as the One who knows.
verses.71: O believers, take your precautions, and advance in groups or all together, according to what wisdom requires.
verses.72: Among you is one who lags behind, and if disaster strikes you, says, 'Allah has favored me in that I was not present with them.'
verses.73: But if bounty from Allah comes to you, he says — as though there had never been any bond of friendship between you — 'If only I had been with them, I would have achieved a great success.' Hypocrisy is shown here reacting only to outcome, never to loyalty.
verses.74: So let those who would sell the life of this world for the Hereafter fight in Allah's cause; whoever fights in Allah's cause and is killed or achieves victory, We will give him a great reward.
verses.75: And what excuse do you have not to fight in Allah's cause, and for the oppressed men, women, and children who cry out, 'Our Lord, take us out of this town of oppressive people, and appoint for us from Yourself a protector, and appoint for us from Yourself a helper'?
verses.76: Believers fight in Allah's cause, while disbelievers fight in the cause of false objects of worship — so fight against the allies of Satan, for the plotting of Satan is truly weak.
verses.77: Have you not seen those who were told, 'Restrain your hands, establish prayer, and give charity,' but when fighting was ordained for them, a group among them feared people just as they should fear Allah, or even more, and said, 'Our Lord, why have You ordained fighting for us — if only You had delayed it a little longer'? Say, 'The enjoyment of this world is small, and the Hereafter is better for whoever is mindful of Allah, and you will not be wronged even by a thread.'
verses.78: Wherever you may be, death will overtake you, even if you are in high, fortified towers; if something good happens to them, they say, 'This is from Allah,' but if something bad happens, they say, 'This is from you' — say, 'All of it is from Allah'; what is wrong with these people that they can hardly understand a single statement?
verses.79: Whatever good comes to you is from Allah, and whatever bad befalls you is from yourself; We have sent you as a messenger to humankind, and sufficient is Allah as Witness.
verses.80: Whoever obeys the Messenger has truly obeyed Allah; and whoever turns away, We have not sent you as a guardian over them.
verses.81: They say obedience, but when they leave your presence, a group among them plots something other than what they said — Allah records everything they plot, so leave them and rely on Allah, for Allah is sufficient as a Disposer of affairs.
verses.82: Do they not reflect on the Quran? Had it been from anyone other than Allah, they would surely have found in it much contradiction.
verses.83: When news of security or fear reaches them, they spread it about; but had they referred it to the Messenger or to those in authority among them, those who investigate would have known the matter — were it not for the favor and mercy of Allah upon you, all but a few of you would have followed Satan.
verses.84: So fight in Allah's cause; you are not held responsible except for yourself, and encourage the believers — perhaps Allah will restrain the might of the disbelievers, for Allah is greater in might and more severe in punishment.
verses.85: Whoever intercedes for a good cause will have a share of its reward, and whoever intercedes for an evil cause will bear a share of its burden — Allah is ever the Keeper over all things.
verses.86: When you are greeted with a greeting, respond with one better than it, or at least return it in kind — Allah keeps count of everything.
verses.87: Allah — there is no god but Him — will surely gather you together on the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt; and who is more truthful in speech than Allah?
verses.88: What is the matter with you, that you have become two groups over the hypocrites, when Allah has cast them back for what they have earned? Do you wish to guide those Allah has left astray? Whoever Allah leaves astray, you will never find a way for him.
verses.89: They wish you would disbelieve just as they disbelieved, so you would all be alike — so do not take allies from among them until they emigrate in Allah's cause; if they turn away, seize them and kill them wherever you find them, and take neither ally nor helper from among them.
verses.90: Except for those who reach a people bound to you by treaty, or who come to you with hearts restrained from fighting either you or their own people — had Allah willed, He could have given them power over you, and they would have fought you; so if they withdraw, do not fight you, and offer you peace, Allah has left you no cause against them.
verses.91: You will find others who wish to be safe from you and safe from their own people, yet every time they are called back to conflict, they plunge back into it — if they do not withdraw from you, offer you peace, and restrain their hands, then seize them and kill them wherever you find them; over such people We have given you clear authority.
verses.92: It is not for a believer to kill another believer, except by mistake; whoever kills a believer by mistake must free a believing slave and pay compensation to the family, unless they forgo it as charity; if the deceased was from a people at enmity with you but was himself a believer, then simply free a believing slave; if from a people with whom you have a treaty, then compensation to his family along with freeing a believing slave; whoever cannot find the means should fast two consecutive months as repentance from Allah — Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.93: Whoever kills a believer intentionally, his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever; the wrath and curse of Allah are upon him, and a great punishment has been prepared for him.
verses.94: O believers, when you go forth in Allah's cause, investigate clearly, and do not say to one who greets you with peace, 'You are not a believer,' seeking the fleeting goods of this worldly life — with Allah are abundant spoils; you too were once like that, and Allah conferred His favor upon you, so investigate carefully, for Allah is fully aware of what you do.
verses.95: Not equal are the believers who stay behind without a valid excuse and those who strive in Allah's cause with their wealth and their lives; Allah has preferred those who strive with wealth and life over those who stay behind by a degree, though Allah has promised good to both — but He has preferred the strivers over those who stay behind with a great reward.
verses.96: Degrees of rank from Him, along with forgiveness and mercy — Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.97: As for those the angels take in death while they were wronging themselves, the angels ask, 'What was the matter with you?' They say, 'We were oppressed in the land.' The angels say, 'Was Allah's earth not spacious enough for you to emigrate therein?' — their refuge is Hell, a truly wretched destination.
verses.98: Except for the genuinely oppressed among men, women, and children who could not devise a plan nor find a way.
verses.99: For these, it may be that Allah will pardon them — Allah is ever Pardoning, Forgiving.
verses.100: Whoever emigrates in Allah's cause will find on earth many places of refuge and abundant resources; and whoever leaves his home, emigrating toward Allah and His Messenger, and then death overtakes him, his reward has already fallen due with Allah — Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.101: When you travel through the land, there is no blame upon you for shortening your prayers if you fear that the disbelievers might cause you harm — indeed, the disbelievers are ever a clear enemy to you.
verses.102: When you are among the believers and lead them in prayer during danger, let one group stand with you and hold their weapons; when they have prostrated, let them withdraw and let another group, who has not yet prayed, come and pray with you, taking their precaution and their weapons — the disbelievers wish you would neglect your weapons and belongings so they could attack you all at once; there is no blame if you lay down your weapons because of rain or illness, but always take your precaution — Allah has prepared a humiliating punishment for the disbelievers.
verses.103: When you have completed the prayer, remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on your sides; and when you are secure again, establish regular prayer — prayer has been decreed upon the believers at specified times.
verses.104: Do not weaken in pursuit of the enemy; if you are suffering, they too are suffering as you are, but you hope from Allah what they do not hope for — Allah is ever Knowing, Wise.
verses.105: We have sent down to you the Book in truth so you may judge between people by what Allah has shown you — so do not be an advocate for those who deceive.
verses.106: And seek forgiveness from Allah — Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.107: Do not argue on behalf of those who betray themselves — Allah does not love anyone who is treacherous and sinful.
verses.108: They may hide from people, but they cannot hide from Allah, for He is with them even when they plot at night words He does not accept — Allah ever encompasses everything they do.
verses.109: Here you are, arguing for them in the life of this world — but who will argue for them before Allah on the Day of Resurrection, or who will be their disposer of affairs then?
verses.110: Whoever does evil or wrongs himself and then seeks Allah's forgiveness will find Allah Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.111: Whoever earns sin only earns it against himself — Allah is ever Knowing, Wise.
verses.112: Whoever earns a fault or a sin, then blames it on an innocent person, has carried a slander and a manifest sin.
verses.113: Were it not for the favor of Allah and His mercy upon you, a group of them would have resolved to lead you astray — but they mislead only themselves and cannot harm you at all; Allah has revealed to you the Book and wisdom and taught you what you did not know before — the favor of Allah upon you has truly been great.
verses.114: There is no good in most private conversation, except from one who calls for charity, kindness, or reconciliation between people; whoever does that seeking Allah's pleasure, We will give him a great reward.
verses.115: Whoever opposes the Messenger after guidance has become clear to him, and follows a path other than that of the believers, We will let him continue what he has turned to, and We will drive him into Hell — a truly wretched destination.
verses.116: Allah does not forgive associating anything with Him, but He forgives whatever is short of that for whomever He wills — whoever associates partners with Allah has strayed far, far astray.
verses.117: Instead of Him, they call upon nothing but female deities, calling upon nothing but a rebellious Satan.
verses.118: Allah has cursed him, and he said, 'I will surely take from among Your servants a specific portion.'
verses.119: 'I will mislead them, arouse in them false desires, order them to slit the ears of cattle, and order them to distort the creation of Allah' — whoever takes Satan as an ally instead of Allah has truly suffered a clear loss.
verses.120: He makes them promises and arouses desires in them — but Satan promises them nothing except delusion.
verses.121: For such people, the refuge is Hell, and they will find no way to escape it.
verses.122: But those who believe and do righteous deeds We will admit into gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever — the promise of Allah is true, and who is more truthful in statement than Allah?
verses.123: It is not according to your own wishful thinking, nor the wishful thinking of the People of the Scripture — whoever does evil will be recompensed for it, and will find for himself no protector or helper besides Allah.
verses.124: Whoever does righteous deeds, male or female, while being a believer, will enter Paradise, and will not be wronged even by the smallest speck.
verses.125: Who is better in religion than one who submits himself entirely to Allah, does good, and follows the way of Abraham, inclining toward truth — Allah took Abraham as a close, intimate friend.
verses.126: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and Allah ever encompasses all things.
verses.127: They ask you a ruling concerning women; say, 'Allah gives you a ruling about them, along with what has already been recited to you in the Book concerning orphan girls to whom you do not give what is decreed for them, though you wish to marry them, and concerning oppressed children — that you maintain justice for orphans; and whatever good you do, Allah is fully aware of it.'
verses.128: If a woman fears cruelty or aversion from her husband, there is no blame on them if they reach a settlement between themselves — settlement is best, though the human soul is prone to selfishness; but if you do good and are mindful of Allah, then Allah is fully acquainted with what you do.
verses.129: You will never be able to treat your wives with perfect equality, no matter how much you desire it — but do not turn away from one completely, leaving her suspended; if you reconcile and are mindful of Allah, then Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.130: If they separate, Allah will provide abundance for each of them out of His vast bounty — Allah is ever All-Encompassing, Wise.
verses.131: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth; We have already instructed those given the Scripture before you, and you as well, to be mindful of Allah — but if you disbelieve, then to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, for Allah is ever Free of need, Praiseworthy.
verses.132: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and sufficient is Allah as a Disposer of affairs.
verses.133: If He wills, He could eliminate you, O mankind, and bring others in your place — Allah is fully capable of that.
verses.134: Whoever desires the reward of this world should know that with Allah is the reward of both this world and the Hereafter — Allah is ever Hearing, Seeing.
verses.135: O believers, stand firmly for justice as witnesses for Allah, even if it is against yourselves, your parents, or your relatives; whether the person is rich or poor, Allah is more worthy of both — so do not follow your own desire, lest you deviate from justice; and if you distort your testimony or refuse to give it, Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.136: O believers, believe in Allah, His Messenger, the Book He has revealed to His Messenger, and the Scripture He revealed before — whoever disbelieves in Allah, His angels, His Books, His messengers, and the Last Day has strayed far, far astray.
verses.137: Those who believe, then disbelieve, then believe again, then disbelieve, and then increase in disbelief — Allah is not going to forgive them, nor is He going to guide them to any path.
verses.138: Give tidings to the hypocrites that a painful punishment awaits them.
verses.139: Those who take disbelievers as allies instead of believers — do they seek honor with them? Honor, in its entirety, belongs to Allah alone.
