verses.140: It has already been revealed to you in the Book that when you hear Allah's verses being rejected and ridiculed, do not sit with those doing so until they engage in a different conversation, or you will be like them — Allah will gather all the hypocrites and disbelievers together in Hell.
verses.141: Those who wait to see what happens to you — if a victory comes from Allah, they say, 'Were we not with you?' But if the disbelievers gain a foothold, they say, 'Did we not have the advantage over you, and did we not protect you from the believers?' Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection, and He will never grant the disbelievers a way to overcome the believers.
verses.142: The hypocrites seek to deceive Allah, but it is He who deceives them; when they stand for prayer, they stand lazily, showing off to people, and remembering Allah only a little.
verses.143: Wavering between belief and disbelief, belonging neither to one side nor the other — and whoever Allah leaves astray, you will never find a path for him.
verses.144: O believers, do not take disbelievers as allies instead of believers — do you wish to give Allah clear proof against yourselves?
verses.145: Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire, and you will never find a helper for them.
verses.146: Except for those who repent, correct themselves, hold firmly to Allah, and are sincere in their religion for Allah — those will be counted among the believers, and Allah will give the believers a great reward.
verses.147: What would Allah do with your punishment if you are grateful and if you believe? Allah is ever Appreciative, Knowing.
verses.148: Allah does not like the public mention of evil except by one who has been wronged — Allah is ever Hearing, Knowing.
verses.149: Whether you disclose a good deed, conceal it, or pardon an evil act, Allah is ever Pardoning, Fully Capable.
verses.150: Those who disbelieve in Allah and His messengers and wish to draw a distinction between Allah and His messengers, saying, 'We believe in some and disbelieve in others,' wishing to take some way in between — those are truly the disbelievers.
verses.151: For the disbelievers, We have prepared a humiliating punishment.
verses.152: But those who believe in Allah and His messengers and make no distinction between any of them — those He will give their rewards, for Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.153: The People of the Scripture ask you to bring down for them a book from heaven — they asked Moses something even greater than that, saying, 'Show us Allah plainly,' so a thunderbolt struck them for their wrongdoing; then they took the calf as an object of worship even after clear proofs had come to them, yet We pardoned that, and We gave Moses clear authority.
verses.154: We raised the mount above them for their covenant, and We said to them, 'Enter the gate bowing humbly,' and We said, 'Do not transgress the Sabbath,' and We took from them a solemn covenant.
verses.155: So for their breaking of the covenant, their disbelief in Allah's signs, their killing of prophets without right, and their saying, 'Our hearts are wrapped in cover,' Allah sealed their hearts because of their disbelief, so they believe only a little.
verses.156: And for their disbelief, and their speaking against Mary a tremendous slander.
verses.157: And for their saying, 'We killed the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, the Messenger of Allah' — they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him, but it was made to appear so to them; those who differ about it are truly in doubt, having no knowledge of it except the following of assumption, and they certainly did not kill him with certainty.
verses.158: Rather, Allah raised him up to Himself — Allah is ever Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.159: There is not one of the People of the Scripture who will not believe in him before his death, and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them.
verses.160: For the wrongdoing done by the Jews, We forbade them good things that had been lawful for them, and because of their turning many away from Allah's path.
verses.161: And their taking of interest despite being forbidden from it, and their consuming of people's wealth unjustly — We have prepared for the disbelievers among them a painful punishment.
verses.162: But those firmly grounded in knowledge among them, and the believers, believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed before you, along with those who establish prayer and give charity and believe in Allah and the Last Day — those We will give a great reward.
verses.163: We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, the tribes, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and We gave David the Psalms.
verses.164: And We have told you about some messengers before, and some messengers We have not told you about — and Allah spoke directly to Moses.
verses.165: Messengers who brought good news and warning, so that people would have no argument against Allah after the coming of the messengers — Allah is ever Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.166: But Allah bears witness to what He has revealed to you — He has revealed it with His own knowledge — and the angels bear witness as well, though Allah is sufficient as a Witness.
verses.167: Those who disbelieve and turn people away from Allah's path have certainly strayed far, far astray.
verses.168: Those who disbelieve and commit wrong — Allah is never going to forgive them, nor is He going to guide them to any path.
verses.169: Except the path of Hell, abiding therein forever — and that is easy for Allah.
verses.170: O mankind, the Messenger has come to you with truth from your Lord, so believe — that is better for you; but if you disbelieve, then to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and Allah is ever Knowing, Wise.
verses.171: O People of the Scripture, do not commit excess in your religion, and do not say about Allah except the truth — the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, was only a messenger of Allah, His word directed to Mary, and a spirit from Him; so believe in Allah and His messengers, and do not say, 'Three' — desist, it is better for you; Allah is only one God, exalted far above having a son; to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and sufficient is Allah as a Disposer of affairs.
verses.172: The Messiah would never disdain being a servant of Allah, nor would the angels nearest to Him — and whoever disdains His worship and is arrogant, He will gather them all to Himself.
verses.173: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, He will give them their full compensation and increase them from His bounty; and as for those who disdained and were arrogant, He will punish them with a painful punishment, and they will not find for themselves any protector or helper besides Allah.
verses.174: O mankind, a clear proof has come to you from your Lord, and We have sent down to you a clear light.
verses.175: As for those who believe in Allah and hold firmly to Him, He will admit them into His mercy and bounty, and guide them to Himself on a straight path.
verses.176: They ask you for a ruling; say, 'Allah gives you a ruling concerning one who has no direct heirs — if a man dies leaving no child but has a sister, she gets half of what he left, and he inherits from her entirely if she dies without a child; if there are two sisters, they get two-thirds of what he left; if there are siblings, both brothers and sisters, then the male gets a share equal to that of two females' — Allah makes this clear to you so that you do not go astray, and Allah has full knowledge of all things. The surah closes, fittingly, on the same subject of inheritance and family right that opened it, having journeyed through faith, justice, and mercy along the way.

verses.1: O believers, fulfill your contracts — lawful to you are grazing livestock, except what is specifically recited to you, without permitting hunting while in the state of pilgrim sanctity; Allah decrees what He wills. The surah opens on the theme of binding commitments before it ever turns to law.
verses.2: Do not violate the sanctity of Allah's rites, the sacred month, the sacrificial animals, the garlanded animals, or those making their way to the Sacred House seeking bounty and pleasure from their Lord; once free of pilgrim sanctity, you may hunt; and do not let hatred of a people who once barred you from the Sacred Mosque drive you to aggression — cooperate with one another in righteousness and piety, not in sin and hostility, and be mindful of Allah, for He is severe in punishment.
verses.3: Forbidden to you are carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, anything dedicated to other than Allah, anything killed by strangling, beating, a fall, or goring, or partly eaten by a wild animal unless you slaughter it before it dies, and anything sacrificed on stone altars, along with divination by arrows — all defiantly disobedient acts; this day the disbelievers have despaired of overcoming your religion, so do not fear them, but fear Me; this day I have perfected your religion for you, completed My favor upon you, and approved Islam as your religion.
verses.4: They ask you what is lawful for them — say, lawful are all good things, along with what trained hunting animals catch for you; eat what they catch, mentioning Allah's name upon it — and be mindful of Allah, for He is swift in account.
verses.5: This day all good foods are made lawful to you; the food of the People of the Scripture is lawful for you, and your food is lawful for them; chaste believing women, and chaste women from the People of the Scripture before you, are lawful for you in marriage, once you give them their due compensation, in chastity, not in fornication or secret liaisons — whoever denies faith, his deeds become worthless, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers.
verses.6: O believers, when you rise for prayer, wash your faces and your hands to the elbows, wipe your heads, and wash your feet to the ankles; if you are in a state of major impurity, then purify yourselves; but if you are ill, traveling, have relieved yourselves, or been intimate with your spouses, and cannot find water, seek clean earth and wipe your faces and hands with it — Allah does not intend to make things difficult for you, but intends to purify you and complete His favor upon you, so that you may be grateful.
verses.7: Remember Allah's favor upon you, and His covenant which He bound you to when you said, 'We hear and we obey' — be mindful of Allah, for He knows well what is in every heart.
verses.8: O believers, stand firmly for Allah as witnesses in justice, and do not let hatred of a people prevent you from being just — be just, that is nearer to righteousness, and be mindful of Allah, for He is fully aware of what you do. Justice, the verse insists, has to survive even hostility.
verses.9: Allah has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward.
verses.10: As for those who disbelieve and deny Our verses, they are the companions of Hellfire.
verses.11: O believers, remember Allah's favor upon you when a people determined to raise their hands against you, and He restrained their hands from you — be mindful of Allah, and upon Allah let the believers rely.
verses.12: Allah took a covenant from the Children of Israel, and We appointed twelve leaders among them; Allah said, 'I am with you if you establish prayer, give charity, believe in My messengers and support them, and lend Allah a goodly loan — I will surely remove your misdeeds and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow; but whoever among you disbelieves after that has truly strayed from the right way.'
verses.13: For breaking their covenant We cursed them and hardened their hearts; they distort words from their proper meaning and have forgotten a portion of what they were reminded of — you will continue to discover deceit from all but a few of them, so pardon them and overlook, for Allah loves those who do good.
verses.14: And from those who say, 'We are Christians,' We also took a covenant, but they too forgot a portion of what they were reminded of — so We stirred among them enmity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection, and Allah will inform them of everything they used to do.
verses.15: O People of the Scripture, Our Messenger has come to you, making clear much of what you used to conceal from the Scripture and pardoning much — a light has come to you from Allah, and a clear Book.
verses.16: By it Allah guides whoever seeks His pleasure to the ways of peace, and brings them out of darkness into light by His permission, and guides them to a straight path.
verses.17: Those who say, 'Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary,' have certainly disbelieved — say, who could stop Allah at all if He willed to destroy the Messiah, his mother, and everyone on earth entirely? To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and everything between them, and He creates what He wills.
verses.18: The Jews and the Christians say, 'We are the children of Allah and His beloved ones' — say, then why does He punish you for your sins? Rather, you are human beings from among those He has created; He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills, and to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and everything between them, and to Him is the final destination.
verses.19: O People of the Scripture, Our Messenger has come to you making things clear after a long gap between messengers, so that you would not say, 'No bearer of good news or warner came to us' — a bearer of good news and a warner has now come to you, and Allah is capable of all things.
verses.20: Moses said to his people, 'Remember Allah's favor upon you — He appointed prophets among you, made you kings, and gave you what He had not given to any others among the worlds.'
verses.21: 'Enter the Holy Land which Allah has decreed for you, and do not turn back in flight, for you would end up as losers.'
verses.22: They said, 'Moses, there is a people of great strength in that land, and we will never enter it until they leave it; if they leave, then we will enter.'
verses.23: Two men who feared Allah and had been favored by Him said, 'Enter upon them through the gate — once you have entered it, you will surely be dominant; and place your trust in Allah, if you are truly believers.'
verses.24: They said, 'Moses, we will never enter it as long as they remain there — so go, you and your Lord, and fight; we are simply going to sit here.'
verses.25: He said, 'My Lord, I control no one except myself and my brother, so separate us from this defiantly disobedient people.'
verses.26: Allah said, 'It is forbidden to them for forty years — they will wander confused throughout the land, so do not grieve over this defiantly disobedient people.' A generation's stubborn refusal costs them the very land they had been promised.
verses.27: Recite to them, in truth, the story of Adam's two sons — when each offered a sacrifice, it was accepted from one and not the other; the second said, 'I will surely kill you,' but the first replied, 'Allah only accepts from those who are righteous.'
verses.28: 'Even if you stretch out your hand against me to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand against you to kill you — I fear Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.29: 'I would rather you bear my sin along with your own and become a companion of the Fire — that is the recompense of wrongdoers.'
verses.30: Yet his soul permitted him to kill his brother, and he killed him, becoming one of the losers. Restraint and jealousy are set here side by side, and the story lets the outcome speak for itself.
verses.31: Then Allah sent a raven, scratching at the earth, to show him how to conceal his brother's corpse — he said, 'How could I have been unable to be even like this raven, and conceal my brother's body?' And he became one of the regretful.
verses.32: Because of that, We decreed upon the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul, unless in retribution for another soul or for corruption in the land, it is as if he had killed all of humankind — and whoever saves a life, it is as if he had saved all of humankind; Our messengers came to them with clear proofs, yet even after that many of them continued to transgress in the land.
verses.33: The recompense for those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive to spread corruption in the land is that they be killed, crucified, have their hands and feet cut off from opposite sides, or be exiled from the land — such is their disgrace in this world, and in the Hereafter they will have a great punishment.
verses.34: Except for those who repent before you gain power over them — know that Allah is Forgiving, Merciful. Even the most severe of penalties has a door of mercy left open before it.
verses.35: O believers, be mindful of Allah, seek the means of closeness to Him, and strive in His cause, so that you may succeed.
verses.36: As for those who disbelieve, if they possessed everything on earth, and twice as much besides, to ransom themselves from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it would not be accepted from them, and they will have a painful punishment.
verses.37: They will wish to leave the Fire, but they will not be able to leave it, and theirs will be a lasting punishment.
verses.38: As for the thief, male or female, cut off their hands as a recompense for what they have earned, a deterrent punishment from Allah — Allah is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.39: But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and reforms himself, Allah will surely turn to him in forgiveness — Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.40: Do you not know that to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth? He punishes whom He wills and forgives whom He wills, and Allah is capable of all things.
verses.41: Let not those who hasten into disbelief grieve you — among them are those who say with their mouths, 'We believe,' though their hearts do not believe; and among the Jews are avid listeners for falsehood, listeners on behalf of others who have not even come to you, distorting words from their proper meanings; if they are given a certain ruling they accept it, and if not, they beware — whoever Allah intends to test, you will have no power at all against Allah on his behalf; those are the ones Allah does not intend to purify their hearts, and for them in this world is disgrace, and in the Hereafter a great punishment.
verses.42: They are avid listeners for falsehood and consumers of what is forbidden; so if they come to you for judgment, judge between them or turn away from them — if you turn away, they cannot harm you at all, and if you judge, judge between them with justice, for Allah loves those who act justly.
verses.43: But how could they come to you for judgment while they have the Torah, in which is Allah's own judgment, and then turn away after that? They are not believers at all.
verses.44: We sent down the Torah, in which was guidance and light — the prophets who submitted to Allah judged by it for the Jews, as did the rabbis and scholars, according to what they were entrusted to preserve of Allah's Book, and they were witnesses to it; so do not fear people, but fear Me, and do not sell My verses for a small price — whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed, those are the disbelievers.
verses.45: We decreed for them: a life for a life, an eye for an eye, a nose for a nose, an ear for an ear, a tooth for a tooth, and wounds equal for equal; but whoever forgoes it as charity, that is an expiation for him — and whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed, those are the wrongdoers.
verses.46: We sent Jesus, son of Mary, following in the footsteps of the earlier prophets, confirming the Torah that came before him, and We gave him the Gospel, in which was guidance and light, confirming the Torah before it, and as guidance and instruction for the righteous.
verses.47: Let the people of the Gospel judge by what Allah has revealed in it — whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed, those are the defiantly disobedient.
verses.48: We sent down to you the Book in truth, confirming the Scripture that came before it and standing as a criterion over it — so judge between them by what Allah has revealed, and do not follow their desires away from the truth that has come to you; for each of you We have made a law and a way of life, and had Allah willed, He could have made you one single community, but He tests you through what He has given you — so race one another toward good deeds, for to Allah you will all return, and He will inform you of what you used to differ about.
verses.49: So judge between them by what Allah has revealed, and do not follow their desires, and beware lest they tempt you away from part of what Allah has revealed to you; and if they turn away, know that Allah intends only to afflict them for some of their sins, and indeed many people are truly defiantly disobedient.
verses.50: Do they seek the judgment of ignorance instead? And who is better than Allah in judgment, for a people of firm certainty?
verses.51: O believers, do not take the Jews and the Christians as close allies — they are allies of one another; whoever among you takes them as allies is one of them, for Allah does not guide a wrongdoing people. The instruction is aimed at guarding communal loyalty during a period of active political tension, not at declaring individual friendship impossible.
verses.52: You will see those with sickness in their hearts hastening toward them, saying, 'We fear that a misfortune may befall us' — but it may be that Allah will bring about victory or a decision from Himself, so that they will become regretful over what they secretly harbored within themselves.
verses.53: And the believers will say, 'Are these the ones who swore their most solemn oaths that they were with you?' Their deeds will have become worthless, and they will end up as losers.
verses.54: O believers, whoever among you turns back from his religion, Allah will bring in his place a people whom He loves and who love Him, humble toward the believers, powerful and firm against the disbelievers, striving in the cause of Allah, unafraid of the blame of any critic — this is Allah's bounty, which He gives to whom He wills, and Allah is All-Encompassing, Knowing.
verses.55: Your true ally is only Allah, His Messenger, and the believers who establish prayer and give charity while bowing in humility.
verses.56: Whoever takes Allah, His Messenger, and the believers as allies — indeed, the party of Allah is the one that will overcome.
verses.57: O believers, do not take as allies those who treat your religion as ridicule and amusement, whether from among the People of the Scripture before you or the disbelievers — and be mindful of Allah, if you are truly believers.
verses.58: When you call to prayer, they take it as ridicule and amusement — that is because they are a people who do not use reason.
verses.59: Say: 'O People of the Scripture, do you resent us for anything other than that we believe in Allah, in what has been revealed to us, and in what was revealed before, and that most of you are defiantly disobedient?'
verses.60: Say: 'Shall I inform you of something worse than that as a recompense with Allah? Those Allah has cursed and become angry with, some of whom He turned into monkeys and pigs, and those who worshipped false deities — such people are in a far worse position, further astray from the right way.'
verses.61: When they come to you they say, 'We believe,' though they entered with disbelief and left with it, and Allah knows best what they were concealing.
verses.62: You will see many of them hastening into sin, aggression, and the consuming of what is forbidden — how evil is what they used to do.
verses.63: Why do the rabbis and the scholars not forbid them from sinful speech and from consuming what is forbidden? How evil indeed is what they have been doing.
verses.64: The Jews say, 'The hand of Allah is chained' — it is their own hands that are chained, and they are cursed for what they have said; rather, His hands are extended, and He spends however He wills; what has been revealed to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in transgression and disbelief, and We have cast among them enmity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection — every time they kindle the fire of war, Allah extinguishes it, and they strive throughout the land to spread corruption, though Allah does not love those who spread corruption.
verses.65: Had the People of the Scripture believed and been mindful of Allah, We would have removed from them their misdeeds and admitted them into gardens of pleasure.
verses.66: Had they upheld the Torah, the Gospel, and what was revealed to them from their Lord, they would have had abundant provision from above them and from beneath their feet; among them is a moderate community, though how evil is what many of them continue to do.
verses.67: O Messenger, convey what has been revealed to you from your Lord — if you do not, then you will not have conveyed His message at all; Allah will protect you from the people, for Allah does not guide a disbelieving people.
verses.68: Say: 'O People of the Scripture, you have nothing to stand on until you uphold the Torah, the Gospel, and what has been revealed to you from your Lord' — what has been revealed to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in transgression and disbelief, so do not grieve over a disbelieving people.
verses.69: Indeed, the believers, the Jews, the Sabians, and the Christians — whoever truly believes in Allah, the Last Day, and does righteousness, no fear will come upon them, nor will they grieve.
verses.70: We took a covenant from the Children of Israel and sent them messengers; every time a messenger came to them with what they themselves did not desire, they denied a group of them and killed another group.
verses.71: They assumed there would be no trial, so they became blind and deaf; then Allah accepted their repentance, but afterward many of them again became blind and deaf — Allah sees fully everything they do.
verses.72: Those who say, 'Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary,' have certainly disbelieved, even though the Messiah himself said, 'O Children of Israel, worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord' — whoever associates partners with Allah, Paradise has truly been forbidden to him, and his refuge is the Fire, and the wrongdoers will have no helpers.
verses.73: Those who say, 'Allah is the third of three,' have certainly disbelieved — there is no god except one God; if they do not desist from what they say, a painful punishment will surely befall those among them who disbelieve.
verses.74: Will they not turn to Allah in repentance and ask His forgiveness? Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful. Even the sharpest theological rebuke ends by leaving the door to repentance open.
verses.75: The Messiah, son of Mary, was only a messenger; messengers had certainly passed away before him, and his mother was a woman of truth, and both of them ate food like any other mortals — see how We make the signs clear to them, and then see how deluded they still become.
verses.76: Say: 'Do you worship, besides Allah, something that has no power to harm or benefit you at all?' Allah alone is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.77: Say: 'O People of the Scripture, do not exceed the limits of truth in your religion, and do not follow the desires of a people who went astray before, misled many others, and strayed from the right way.'
verses.78: Those among the Children of Israel who disbelieved were cursed by the tongue of David and of Jesus, son of Mary — that was because they disobeyed and habitually transgressed.
verses.79: They did not prevent one another from any wrongdoing they committed — how evil indeed was what they used to do.
verses.80: You see many of them taking the disbelievers as allies — how evil indeed is what they have sent ahead for themselves, in that Allah became angry with them, and in the punishment they will abide therein forever.
verses.81: Had they truly believed in Allah, in the Prophet, and in what was revealed to him, they would not have taken them as allies — but many of them are defiantly disobedient.
verses.82: You will surely find the strongest of people in enmity toward the believers to be the Jews and those who associate partners with Allah, and you will surely find the closest of them in affection toward the believers to be those who say, 'We are Christians' — that is because among them are priests and monks, and because they are not arrogant.
verses.83: When they hear what has been revealed to the Messenger, you see their eyes overflowing with tears because of the truth they have recognized — they say, 'Our Lord, we believe, so register us among the witnesses.'
verses.84: 'And why should we not believe in Allah and in the truth that has come to us, while we hope our Lord will admit us with the righteous people?'
verses.85: So Allah rewarded them, for what they said, with gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever — that is the reward of those who do good.
verses.86: But those who disbelieve and deny Our verses are the companions of the Blaze.
verses.87: O believers, do not prohibit the good things that Allah has made lawful to you, and do not transgress — Allah does not love transgressors.
verses.88: And eat of what Allah has provided you that is lawful and good, and be mindful of Allah, in whom you are believers.
verses.89: Allah does not blame you for what is unintentional in your oaths, but He blames you for what your hearts have deliberately intended; its expiation is feeding ten needy people from the average of what you feed your own families, or clothing them, or freeing a slave, and whoever cannot find the means should fast three days — that is the expiation for oaths when you have sworn them; so guard your oaths — thus Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so that you may be grateful.
verses.90: O believers, wine, gambling, sacrificial stone altars, and divination arrows are simply an abomination from the handiwork of Satan, so avoid them, that you may succeed.
verses.91: Satan only wants to stir up enmity and hatred among you through wine and gambling, and to keep you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer — so will you not desist?
verses.92: Obey Allah, obey the Messenger, and beware — and if you turn away, know that the duty of Our Messenger is only to clearly convey the message.
verses.93: There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they ate previously, so long as they are mindful of Allah, believe, and do righteousness, and then remain mindful of Allah and believe, and then remain mindful of Allah and do good — Allah loves those who do good.
verses.94: O believers, Allah will surely test you with something of the hunting your hands and spears can reach, so that Allah may make evident who fears Him in private — whoever transgresses after that will have a painful punishment.
verses.95: O believers, do not kill game while you are in the state of pilgrim sanctity; whoever among you kills it intentionally, the compensation is an equivalent from your livestock, as judged by two just people among you, offered as a sacrifice reaching the Ka'bah, or as expiation by feeding the needy or its equivalent in fasting, so he may taste the consequence of his conduct — Allah has pardoned what is past, but whoever returns to it, Allah will take retribution from him, for Allah is Exalted in Might, Owner of Retribution.
verses.96: Lawful to you is the hunting of the sea and its food, as provision for you and for travelers, but forbidden to you is the hunting of land while you are in the state of pilgrim sanctity — and be mindful of Allah, to whom you will all be gathered.
verses.97: Allah has made the Ka'bah, the Sacred House, a standing point for all people, along with the sacred month, the sacrificial animal, and the garlands — that you may know that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and that Allah has full knowledge of all things.
verses.98: Know that Allah is severe in punishment, and that Allah is also Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.99: Nothing is required of the Messenger except to deliver the message — Allah knows whatever you reveal and whatever you conceal.
verses.100: Say: 'The evil and the good are not equal, even if the abundance of evil impresses you' — so be mindful of Allah, O people of understanding, that you may succeed.
verses.101: O believers, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would only distress you; but if you ask about them while the Quran is being revealed, they will be made clear to you — Allah has pardoned what has already passed, for Allah is Forgiving, Forbearing.
verses.102: A people before you asked such questions, and then became disbelievers because of them. A caution against needless prying is grounded here in the fate of an earlier generation.
verses.103: Allah has not ordained anything like the pagan practices concerning certain livestock — but the disbelievers invent lies about Allah, and most of them do not use reason.
verses.104: When it is said to them, 'Come to what Allah has revealed and to the Messenger,' they say, 'What we found our forefathers doing is sufficient for us' — even if their forefathers knew nothing at all and were not guided?
verses.105: O believers, you are responsible for your own souls — no one who is astray can harm you if you yourselves are guided; to Allah you will all return together, and He will inform you of everything you used to do.
verses.106: O believers, when death approaches one of you and you are making a bequest, let there be testimony between you of two just people from among you, or two others from outside if you are traveling and the affliction of death strikes you; detain them after prayer if you have any doubt, and let them swear by Allah, 'We will not sell our testimony for any price, even for a close relative, and we will not conceal the testimony required by Allah, for then we would surely be among the sinners.'
verses.107: But if it is discovered that both were guilty of a sin, let two others stand in their place from among those wronged, and let them swear by Allah, 'Our testimony is truer than the testimony of the two before us, and we have not transgressed — for then we would truly be among the wrongdoers.'
verses.108: That is more likely to make people give testimony correctly, or to make them fear that their oaths will be refuted after they have sworn them — so be mindful of Allah, and listen, for Allah does not guide a defiantly disobedient people.
verses.109: On a Day Allah will gather the messengers together and ask, 'What response did you receive?' They will say, 'We have no knowledge — it is You who are the Knower of all that is unseen.'
verses.110: Allah will say, 'Jesus, son of Mary, remember My favor upon you and upon your mother, when I supported you with the Pure Spirit, so you could speak to people in the cradle and in maturity, and when I taught you writing, wisdom, the Torah, and the Gospel, and when you designed a bird-like shape from clay by My permission and breathed into it and it became a bird by My permission, and healed the blind from birth and the leper by My permission, and brought forth the dead by My permission, and when I restrained the Children of Israel from harming you when you came to them with clear proofs, and the disbelievers among them said, this is nothing but clear magic.'
verses.111: 'And when I inspired the disciples: believe in Me and My messenger — they said, we believe, and bear witness that we are submitted to You.'
verses.112: The disciples said, 'Jesus, son of Mary, can your Lord send down to us a table spread with food from heaven?' He said, 'Be mindful of Allah, if you are truly believers.'
verses.113: They said, 'We wish to eat from it, so our hearts may be satisfied, so we may know that you have spoken the truth to us, and so we may be among the witnesses to it.'
verses.114: Jesus, son of Mary, said, 'O Allah, our Lord, send down to us a table spread with food from heaven, that it may be a festival for the first of us and the last of us, and a sign from You — and provide for us, for You are the best of providers.'
verses.115: Allah said, 'I will surely send it down to you, but whoever among you disbelieves after that, I will punish him with a punishment I will not inflict on anyone else among all the worlds.'
verses.116: Allah will say, 'Jesus, son of Mary, did you say to people, take me and my mother as gods besides Allah?' He will say, 'Exalted are You, it is not for me to say what I have no right to say — had I said it, You would certainly have known it; You know what is within myself, though I do not know what is within Yourself, for You are the Knower of all that is unseen.'
verses.117: 'I only said to them what You commanded me to say — worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord; I was a witness over them as long as I remained among them, but when You took me up, You became the Watcher over them, and You are Witness over all things.'
verses.118: 'If You punish them, they are truly Your servants; and if You forgive them, You are the Exalted in Might, the Wise.'
verses.119: Allah will say, 'This is a Day on which the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness' — for them are gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever; Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him — that is the great attainment.
verses.120: To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and everything within them, and He is capable of all things. The surah closes, as it opened, on the sheer scope of Allah's authority — over contracts, over history, and finally over everything that exists.

verses.1: Praise belongs to Allah, who created the heavens and the earth and made darkness and light — yet those who disbelieve still set up equals to their Lord. The surah opens by naming the very thing it will spend the rest of its length arguing against.
verses.2: It is He who created you from clay, then decreed a term for you, and there is another appointed term known only to Him — yet you continue to doubt.
verses.3: He is Allah in the heavens and on the earth; He knows what you conceal and what you make known, and He knows what you earn.
verses.4: Not a single sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord without them turning away from it.
verses.5: They have already denied the truth when it came to them, but news will soon come to them of what they used to mock.
verses.6: Have they not seen how many generations We destroyed before them, whom We had established in the land as We have not established you, sending down abundant rain and making rivers flow beneath them — yet We destroyed them for their sins, and raised up other generations after them.
verses.7: Had We sent down to you a written scripture on actual paper and they had touched it with their own hands, the disbelievers would still have said, 'This is nothing but obvious magic.' No amount of physical proof, the verse suggests, would overcome a mind already determined to reject.
verses.8: They say, 'Why has no angel been sent down to him?' — had We sent down an angel, the matter would have already been decided, and they would have been granted no further respite.
verses.9: And had We made him an angel, We would still have made him appear as a man, and We would have confused them just as they are confusing themselves now.
verses.10: Messengers were mocked before you, but those who ridiculed them were surrounded by the very thing they mocked.
verses.11: Say: 'Travel through the land, and see what the end of the deniers was.'
verses.12: Say: 'To whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth?' Say: 'To Allah' — He has decreed mercy upon Himself; He will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt; those who have lost their own souls do not believe.
verses.13: To Him belongs whatever dwells in the night and the day, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.14: Say: 'Shall I take as protector anyone other than Allah, the Creator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds and is not fed?' Say: 'I have been commanded to be the first who submits, and not to be among those who associate partners with Him.'
verses.15: Say: 'I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.'
verses.16: Whoever is spared from it on that Day — Allah has surely bestowed mercy upon him, and that is a clear attainment.
verses.17: If Allah touches you with harm, no one can remove it except Him; and if He touches you with good, then He is capable of all things.
verses.18: He is the Dominant over His servants, and He is the Wise, the Aware.
verses.19: Say: 'What thing is the greatest in testimony?' Say: 'Allah is Witness between me and you, and this Quran has been revealed to me so that I may warn you with it, and whoever it reaches — do you truly testify that there are other gods with Allah?' Say: 'I do not testify to that.' Say: 'He is only one God, and I am free of what you associate with Him.'
verses.20: Those to whom We gave the Scripture recognize him as they recognize their own sons — those who have lost their own souls do not believe.
verses.21: Who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah or denies His signs? The wrongdoers will never succeed.
verses.22: On the Day We gather them all together, We will say to those who associated partners, 'Where are your so-called partners that you used to claim?'
verses.23: Then their only excuse will be to say, 'By Allah, our Lord, we were never polytheists.'
verses.24: See how they will lie about themselves, and how what they used to invent will abandon them entirely. A moment of complete self-exposure closes what began as pure denial.
verses.25: Among them are some who listen to you, though We have placed coverings over their hearts, preventing them from understanding, and deafness in their ears — even if they were to see every sign, they still would not believe in it, so that when they come to argue with you, the disbelievers say, 'This is nothing but legends of former peoples.'
verses.26: They prevent others from listening to him, and they themselves stay away from him — they destroy nothing but their own selves, though they do not perceive it.
verses.27: If only you could see when they are made to stand before the Fire, they will say, 'If only we could be returned, we would not deny the signs of our Lord, and we would be among the believers.'
verses.28: Rather, what they used to conceal before has become evident to them — and even if they were returned, they would only repeat what they were forbidden from before, for they are truly liars.
verses.29: And they say, 'There is nothing but our life in this world, and we will not be resurrected.'
verses.30: If only you could see when they are made to stand before their Lord, He will say, 'Is this not the truth?' They will say, 'Yes, by our Lord.' He will say, 'Then taste the punishment for what you used to deny.'
verses.31: Lost indeed are those who deny the meeting with Allah, until, when the Hour comes upon them suddenly, they say, 'How we regret our neglect of it,' as they carry their burdens on their backs — how evil is what they carry.
verses.32: The life of this world is nothing but amusement and diversion, while the home of the Hereafter is far better for those who are mindful of Allah — will you not reason?
verses.33: We know well that what they say saddens you — it is not you they are truly denying, but it is the signs of Allah that the wrongdoers deny.
verses.34: Messengers before you were certainly denied, yet they endured with patience the denial and harm they suffered, until Our help came to them — there is no changing the words of Allah, and news of the messengers before you has already come to you.
verses.35: If their turning away is difficult for you to bear, then if you are able to seek a tunnel into the ground or a ladder into the sky to bring them a sign — but had Allah willed, He could have gathered them all upon guidance, so do not be among the ignorant.
verses.36: Only those who truly listen will respond; as for the dead, Allah will resurrect them, and then to Him they will be returned.
verses.37: They say, 'Why has no sign been sent down to him from his Lord?' Say, 'Allah is surely capable of sending down a sign, but most of them do not know.'
verses.38: There is no creature on earth, nor a bird that flies on its wings, except that they are communities like you — We have not neglected anything in the Book — then to their Lord they will be gathered.
verses.39: Those who deny Our signs are deaf and mute, dwelling in darkness — Allah sends astray whom He wills and puts on a straight path whom He wills.
verses.40: Say: 'Consider — if the punishment of Allah came to you, or the Hour came to you, would you call upon anyone other than Allah, if you are being truthful?'
verses.41: No, it is Him alone you would call upon, and He would remove what you called upon Him for, if He willed, and you would forget whatever you associate with Him now.
verses.42: We sent messengers to nations before you, and We seized them with hardship and adversity, so that they might humble themselves.
verses.43: If only, when Our hardship came to them, they had humbled themselves — but their hearts became hardened instead, and Satan made pleasing to them what they were doing.
verses.44: So when they forgot what they had been reminded of, We opened for them the doors of everything, until, just as they were rejoicing in what they had been given, We seized them suddenly, and they were left in utter despair.
verses.45: So the roots of the wrongdoing people were cut off — and praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
verses.46: Say: 'Consider — if Allah took away your hearing and your sight and sealed your hearts, what god other than Allah could bring them back to you?' See how We vary the signs, yet still they turn away.
verses.47: Say: 'Consider — if the punishment of Allah came to you suddenly or openly, would anyone be destroyed except the wrongdoing people?'
verses.48: We do not send messengers except as bearers of good news and as warners — whoever believes and reforms himself, no fear will come upon them, nor will they grieve.
verses.49: But those who deny Our signs will be touched by punishment for their persistent defiance.
verses.50: Say: 'I do not tell you that I possess the treasures of Allah, nor do I know the unseen, nor do I tell you that I am an angel — I only follow what has been revealed to me.' Say: 'Are the blind and the seeing equal? Will you not reflect?'
verses.51: Warn by this those who fear being gathered to their Lord, having no protector or intercessor besides Him, so that they may become mindful of Allah.
verses.52: Do not send away those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking only His presence — you are not at all accountable for them, nor are they at all accountable for you, so do not send them away and thereby become one of the wrongdoers.
verses.53: We test some of them by means of others, so that they say, 'Are these the ones Allah has favored among us?' Is Allah not most knowing of those who are grateful?
verses.54: When those who believe in Our signs come to you, say, 'Peace be upon you — your Lord has decreed mercy upon Himself, so that whoever among you does evil out of ignorance and then repents afterward and reforms will find Him Forgiving, Merciful.'
verses.55: This is how We make the signs clear, so that the way of the guilty may become evident.
verses.56: Say: 'I have been forbidden to worship those you call upon besides Allah.' Say: 'I do not follow your desires — if I did, I would have gone astray, and I would not be among the guided.'
verses.57: Say: 'I stand on a clear proof from my Lord, yet you have denied it — what you seek to hasten is not within my power; the judgment belongs only to Allah, He relates the truth, and He is the best of deciders.'
verses.58: Say: 'If what you seek to hasten were within my power, the matter between us would have already been decided — Allah knows best the wrongdoers.'
verses.59: With Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them except Him; He knows what is in the land and in the sea; not a leaf falls without His knowledge, nor a grain in the darkness of the earth, nor anything moist or dry, except that it is written in a clear record.
verses.60: It is He who takes your souls by night and knows what you have done by day, then resurrects you within it that a specified term may be fulfilled — then to Him is your return, and He will inform you of everything you used to do.
verses.61: He is the Dominant over His servants, and He sends guardians to watch over you, until, when death approaches one of you, Our messengers take his soul, and they do not fail in their duty.
verses.62: Then they are returned to Allah, their true Master — His alone is the judgment, and He is swift in reckoning.
verses.63: Say: 'Who saves you from the darknesses of the land and the sea, when you call upon Him humbly and secretly, saying, if He saves us from this, we will surely be among the grateful'?
verses.64: Say: 'Allah saves you from that and from every distress — and still you associate partners with Him.'
verses.65: Say: 'He is fully capable of sending punishment upon you from above you or from beneath your feet, or of confusing you into opposing factions and making some of you taste the violence of others.' See how We vary the signs, so that they may understand.
verses.66: Yet your people have denied it, though it is the truth — say, 'I am not a disposer of your affairs.'
verses.67: Every piece of news has its fixed time, and you will come to know it in due course.
verses.68: When you see those who engage in mockery of Our verses, turn away from them until they engage in some other conversation — and should Satan cause you to forget, do not sit, after remembering, with the wrongdoing people.
verses.69: Those who are mindful of Allah are not at all accountable for the wrongdoers' account, but only for a reminder, so that perhaps they too might become mindful.
verses.70: Leave alone those who take their religion as mere amusement and diversion, and who are deluded by the life of this world — remind by this Book, lest a soul be given up to ruin for what it has earned, having no protector or intercessor besides Allah; and even if it offers every possible ransom, it will not be accepted — such people are given up to ruin for what they earned, and theirs will be a drink of boiling water and a painful punishment for their disbelief.
verses.71: Say: 'Shall we call upon, besides Allah, something that can neither benefit us nor harm us, and be turned back on our heels after Allah has guided us — like one Satan has lured into confusion, wandering the earth while his friends call him to guidance, saying, come to us?' Say: 'The guidance of Allah is the only true guidance, and we have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.72: 'And to establish prayer and to be mindful of Him — it is to Him that you will all be gathered.'
verses.73: It is He who created the heavens and the earth in truth; the Day He says, 'Be,' it is; His word is truth, and His is the dominion on the Day the trumpet is blown; He is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, and He is the Wise, the Aware.
verses.74: Abraham said to his father, Azar, 'Do you take idols as gods? I see you and your people in clear error.'
verses.75: And thus We showed Abraham the dominion of the heavens and the earth, so that he might be among those with certainty.
verses.76: When the night covered him, he saw a star and said, 'This is my Lord' — but when it set, he said, 'I do not love things that set.'
verses.77: Then when he saw the moon rising, he said, 'This is my Lord' — but when it set, he said, 'If my Lord does not guide me, I will surely be among the people who go astray.'
verses.78: Then when he saw the sun rising, he said, 'This is my Lord, this is greater' — but when it set, he said, 'O my people, I am free of everything you associate with Allah.'
verses.79: 'Indeed, I have turned my face toward the One who created the heavens and the earth, inclining toward truth, and I am not among those who associate partners with Him.'
verses.80: His people argued with him — he said, 'Do you argue with me concerning Allah while He has already guided me? I do not fear what you associate with Him unless my Lord wills something — my Lord encompasses all things in knowledge, so will you not reflect?'
verses.81: 'And how should I fear what you associate, when you yourselves do not fear that you have associated with Allah something for which He has sent down no authority to you? So which of the two parties is more deserving of security, if you have any knowledge?'
verses.82: Those who believe and do not mix their belief with injustice — for them is security, and they are the ones who are truly guided.
verses.83: That was the argument We gave Abraham against his people — We raise in degrees whom We will, for your Lord is Wise, Knowing.
verses.84: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob, each of whom We guided, just as We had guided Noah before, and among his descendants were David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron — thus do We reward the doers of good.
verses.85: And Zechariah, John, Jesus, and Elias — every one of them was among the righteous.
verses.86: And Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot — each of them We preferred above the worlds.
verses.87: And of their fathers, their descendants, and their brothers — We chose them and guided them to a straight path.
verses.88: That is the guidance of Allah, by which He guides whomever He wills of His servants — had they associated partners with Him, everything they used to do would have been worthless.
verses.89: Those are the ones to whom We gave the Scripture, the judgment, and prophethood; but if these people disbelieve it, We have already entrusted it to a people who will not disbelieve in it.
verses.90: Those are the ones Allah has guided, so follow their guidance — say, 'I do not ask you for any reward for it, it is only a reminder for all the worlds.'
verses.91: They have not appreciated Allah with true appreciation when they say, 'Allah has not revealed anything to any human being' — say, 'Who then revealed the Book that Moses brought, a light and guidance for the people, which you make into separate sheets, revealing some and concealing much, and by which you were taught what neither you nor your fathers knew?' Say, 'Allah,' and then leave them to amuse themselves in their idle talk.
verses.92: This is a blessed Book We have sent down, confirming what came before it, so that you may warn the Mother of Cities and everyone around it — those who believe in the Hereafter believe in it, and they are ever mindful of their prayer.
verses.93: Who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah, or says, 'It has been revealed to me,' when nothing has been revealed to him, or one who says, 'I will reveal something like what Allah has revealed'? If only you could see when the wrongdoers are in the agonies of death, and the angels extend their hands, saying, 'Discharge your souls — today you will be recompensed with a punishment of humiliation for what you used to say untruthfully about Allah, and your arrogance toward His signs.'
verses.94: You have certainly come to Us alone, just as We created you the first time, leaving behind everything We bestowed upon you — We do not see with you your intercessors, whom you claimed were partners with you; the bond between you has been severed, and what you claimed has abandoned you.
verses.95: Indeed, Allah is the One who splits the grain and the date seed; He brings the living out of the dead, and He brings the dead out of the living — that is Allah, so how are you deluded?
verses.96: He is the One who causes the dawn to break, and He made the night for rest, and the sun and the moon for the reckoning of time — that is the design of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
verses.97: It is He who made the stars for you, so that you may be guided by them in the darknesses of the land and the sea — We have detailed the signs for people who have knowledge.
verses.98: And it is He who produced you from a single soul, then gave you a dwelling place and a repository — We have detailed the signs for people who understand.
verses.99: And it is He who sends down water from the sky, and by it We bring forth the growth of every kind of plant, from which We bring forth something green, from which We bring forth compounded grain, and from the palm tree, from its pollen, come clusters hanging low, and gardens of grapes and olives and pomegranates, similar yet dissimilar — look at their fruit when it yields and as it ripens; in that are truly signs for people who believe.
verses.100: And they have made the jinn partners with Allah, though He created them, and they invent sons and daughters for Him without any knowledge — exalted and far removed is He from what they describe.
verses.101: Originator of the heavens and the earth — how could He have a child, when He has no companion, and He created all things, and He has full knowledge of all things?
verses.102: That is Allah, your Lord — there is no god except Him, Creator of all things, so worship Him, for He is the Disposer of all affairs.
verses.103: Vision cannot perceive Him, but He perceives all vision, for He is the Subtle, the Aware.
verses.104: There has come to you enlightenment from your Lord — whoever sees it does so for the benefit of his own soul, and whoever remains blind to it does so to his own detriment; and I am not a guardian over you.
verses.105: And thus We vary the signs, so that they may say, 'You have studied,' and so that We may make it clear to a people who know.
verses.106: Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord — there is no god except Him — and turn away from those who associate partners with Him.
verses.107: Had Allah willed, they would not have associated partners with Him — We have not made you a guardian over them, nor are you a disposer of their affairs.
verses.108: Do not insult those they call upon besides Allah, lest they insult Allah out of hostility and without knowledge — thus We have made pleasing to every community its own deeds; then to their Lord is their return, and He will inform them of everything they used to do.
verses.109: They swear their most solemn oaths by Allah that if a sign came to them, they would surely believe in it — say, 'Signs are only with Allah,' and what would make you perceive that even if it came to them, they still would not believe?
verses.110: We turn away their hearts and their vision, just as they did not believe in it the first time, and We leave them to wander blindly in their transgression.
verses.111: And even if We sent down to them the angels, and the dead spoke to them, and We gathered together everything in front of them, they still would not believe, unless Allah willed it — but most of them are simply ignorant.
verses.112: And thus We made for every prophet an enemy — devils from among mankind and jinn, inspiring each other with elaborate speech in deception — had your Lord willed, they would not have done it, so leave them and what they invent.
verses.113: Let the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter incline toward it, and let them be satisfied with it, and let them acquire whatever they are going to acquire.
verses.114: Shall I seek a judge other than Allah, when it is He who has revealed to you the Book explained in detail? Those to whom We gave the Scripture know that it has been revealed from your Lord in truth, so do not be among the doubters.
verses.115: The word of your Lord has been fulfilled in truth and in justice — there is no changing His words, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.116: If you were to obey most of those on earth, they would lead you astray from Allah's way — they follow nothing but assumption, and they do nothing but guess.
verses.117: Your Lord knows best who strays from His way, and He knows best who are the rightly guided.
verses.118: So eat of that over which Allah's name has been mentioned, if you are truly believers in His signs.
verses.119: And why should you not eat of that over which Allah's name has been mentioned, when He has already explained in detail what is forbidden to you, except that which you are compelled to? Indeed, many people mislead others through their own desires without any knowledge — your Lord knows best the transgressors.
verses.120: Leave behind sin, whether apparent or concealed — those who earn sin will be recompensed for exactly what they used to earn.
verses.121: Do not eat of that over which Allah's name has not been mentioned, for that is truly defiant disobedience — the devils inspire their allies to argue with you, and if you obey them, you would truly become polytheists.
verses.122: Is one who was dead, and We gave him life, and made for him a light by which he walks among people, like one who is in complete darkness from which he cannot emerge? Thus it has been made pleasing to the disbelievers, what they used to do.
verses.123: And thus We placed in every city its greatest criminals, so they might plot therein — but they only plot against their own selves, and they do not perceive it.
verses.124: When a sign comes to them, they say, 'We will not believe until we are given something like what was given to the messengers of Allah' — Allah knows best where to place His message; disgrace before Allah and a severe punishment will befall the criminals for their plotting.
verses.125: Whoever Allah wills to guide, He expands his breast to Islam; and whoever He wills to leave astray, He makes his breast tight and constricted, as if he were ascending into the sky — thus Allah places uncleanness upon those who do not believe.
verses.126: This is the straight path of your Lord — We have detailed the signs for people who remember.
verses.127: For them is the Home of Peace with their Lord, and He will be their ally because of what they used to do.
verses.128: On the Day He gathers them all together, He will say, 'O company of jinn, you have sought to mislead many of mankind' — and their allies among mankind will say, 'Our Lord, we benefited from one another, but we have reached the term You appointed for us' — He will say, 'The Fire is your home, abiding therein except as Allah wills,' for your Lord is Wise, Knowing.
verses.129: And thus We make some wrongdoers allies of others, because of what they used to earn.
verses.130: 'O company of jinn and mankind, did there not come to you messengers from among you, relating to you My signs and warning you of the meeting of this Day of yours?' They will say, 'We bear witness against ourselves' — the life of this world had deluded them, and they will bear witness against themselves that they were disbelievers.
verses.131: That is because your Lord would not destroy cities for their wrongdoing while their people were unaware.
verses.132: For everyone are degrees according to what they have done — your Lord is not unaware of what they do.
verses.133: Your Lord is Free of need, Owner of mercy — if He wills, He can remove you and give succession after you to whomever He wills, just as He produced you from the descendants of another people.
verses.134: Indeed, what you have been promised is certainly coming, and you will not be able to escape it.
verses.135: Say: 'O my people, work according to your position, for I too am working — you will soon know for whom will be the home of the good outcome; indeed, the wrongdoers will not succeed.'
verses.136: They allot to Allah a share of the crops and cattle He has created, saying, 'This is for Allah,' and, 'This is for our partners' — but what is for their partners does not reach Allah, while what is for Allah does reach their partners; how evil is what they judge.
verses.137: And thus their partners have made pleasing to many of the polytheists the killing of their own children, in order to bring about their ruin and to confuse them in their own religion — had Allah willed, they would not have done it, so leave them and what they invent.
verses.138: And they say, 'These cattle and crops are forbidden — none may eat them except whom we permit,' according to their claim, along with cattle whose backs are forbidden to be used, and cattle over which they do not mention Allah's name, inventing a lie against Him — He will recompense them for what they used to invent.
verses.139: And they say, 'What is in the wombs of these cattle is exclusively for our males and forbidden to our wives' — but if it is stillborn, they all share in it; He will recompense them for their description, for He is Wise, Knowing.
verses.140: Those who killed their children out of ignorance and without knowledge have truly lost, and they forbade what Allah had provided them, inventing a lie against Allah — they have certainly strayed and have not been guided.
verses.141: And He is the One who produces gardens, both trellised and untrellised, and palm trees, and crops of different kinds of food, and olives, and pomegranates, similar and dissimilar — eat of its fruit when it yields, and give its due on the day of its harvest, and do not waste, for He does not love the wasteful.
verses.142: And of the cattle are some for carrying burdens and some for slaughter — eat of what Allah has provided you, and do not follow the footsteps of Satan, for he is a clear enemy to you.
verses.143: Eight mates — of sheep two and of goats two; say, 'Has He forbidden the two males, the two females, or what is contained in the wombs of the two females? Inform me with knowledge, if you are truthful.'
verses.144: And of camels two and of cattle two — say, 'Has He forbidden the two males, the two females, or what is contained in the wombs of the two females — or were you witnesses when Allah enjoined this upon you?' Who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah to mislead people without any knowledge? Allah does not guide a wrongdoing people.
verses.145: Say: 'I do not find in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden to eat, except carrion, flowing blood, the flesh of swine — for it is impure — or an animal defiantly dedicated to other than Allah; but whoever is forced by necessity, not desiring it nor transgressing, your Lord is Forgiving, Merciful.'
verses.146: To the Jews We forbade every animal with claws, and of cattle and sheep We forbade them their fat, except what is on their backs, entrails, or mixed with bone — that was Our recompense for their transgression, and We are truly truthful.
verses.147: If they deny you, say, 'Your Lord is a Lord of vast mercy, but His punishment cannot be repelled from the guilty people.'
verses.148: Those who associate partners with Allah will say, 'Had Allah willed, we would not have associated partners, nor would our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything' — those before them denied likewise, until they tasted Our punishment; say, 'Do you have any knowledge that you can produce for us? You follow nothing but assumption, and you do nothing but guess.'
verses.149: Say: 'With Allah is the conclusive argument — had He willed, He would have guided all of you.'
verses.150: Say: 'Bring your witnesses who will testify that Allah has forbidden this' — if they testify, do not testify with them, and do not follow the desires of those who deny Our signs and those who do not believe in the Hereafter, and who equate others with their Lord.
verses.151: Say: 'Come, I will recite what your Lord has truly forbidden you — do not associate anything with Him, be good to your parents, do not kill your children out of poverty, for We provide for you and for them, do not approach immoralities, whether apparent or concealed, and do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden, except by right — this is what He has enjoined upon you, so that you may understand.'
verses.152: 'And do not approach the property of an orphan, except in a way that is best, until he reaches maturity, and give full measure and weight in justice — We do not burden any soul beyond its capacity — and when you speak, be just, even if it concerns a close relative, and fulfill the covenant of Allah — this is what He has enjoined upon you, so that you may remember.'
verses.153: 'And this is My path, made straight — so follow it, and do not follow other paths, lest they separate you from His way — this is what He has enjoined upon you, so that you may become mindful of Him.'
verses.154: Then We gave Moses the Scripture, completing Our favor upon the doer of good and explaining everything in detail, as guidance and mercy, so that they might believe in the meeting with their Lord.
verses.155: This is a blessed Book We have sent down, so follow it and be mindful of Allah, that you may receive mercy.
verses.156: Lest you say, 'The Scripture was only sent down to two groups before us, and we were truly unaware of their study.'
verses.157: Or lest you say, 'Had the Scripture been sent down to us, we would have been better guided than they were' — a clear proof, guidance, and mercy has indeed come to you from your Lord, so who is more unjust than one who denies Allah's signs and turns away from them? We will recompense those who turn away from Our signs with an evil punishment for their turning away.
verses.158: Are they waiting only for the angels to come to them, or for your Lord to come, or for some of your Lord's signs to come? The Day some of your Lord's signs come, faith will no longer benefit a soul that did not believe before, or that earned nothing good through its faith — say, 'Wait, we too are waiting.'
verses.159: Those who have divided their religion and become sects, you have nothing to do with them at all — their matter rests only with Allah, and He will inform them of what they used to do.
verses.160: Whoever brings a good deed will have ten times its like, and whoever brings an evil deed will only be recompensed with its equivalent, and none will be wronged.
verses.161: Say: 'My Lord has guided me to a straight path, an upright religion, the way of Abraham, inclining toward truth — and he was not among the polytheists.'
verses.162: Say: 'My prayer, my sacrifice, my life, and my death all belong to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.163: 'He has no partner — this is what I have been commanded, and I am the first of the Muslims.'
verses.164: Say: 'Shall I seek a lord other than Allah, when He is the Lord of all things?' Every soul earns only against itself, and no bearer of burden will bear the burden of another — then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of that in which you used to differ.
verses.165: And it is He who has made you successors upon the earth and raised some of you above others in degrees, that He may test you in what He has given you — your Lord is swift in punishment, yet He is truly Forgiving, Merciful. The surah closes, having journeyed through Abraham's search for truth and long passages of law, on the same balance of severity and mercy it opened with.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Meem, Sad.
verses.2: This is a Book revealed to you — let there be no distress in your heart because of it — so that you may warn by it, and as a reminder for the believers.
verses.3: Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord, and do not follow any allies besides Him — little do you remember.
verses.4: How many a city have We destroyed — Our punishment came upon them by night, or while they rested at midday.
verses.5: Their only cry when Our punishment came was to say, 'We were indeed wrongdoers.'
verses.6: We will surely question those to whom messengers were sent, and We will surely question the messengers themselves.
verses.7: Then We will surely relate to them with knowledge, for We were never absent.
verses.8: The weighing on that Day is the truth — those whose scales are heavy will be the successful ones.
verses.9: And those whose scales are light are the ones who have lost their own souls for the injustice they did to Our signs.
verses.10: We have established you upon the earth and made for you therein a means of livelihood — yet little are you grateful.
verses.11: We created you, then formed you, then said to the angels, 'Prostrate to Adam' — they all prostrated except Iblis, who was not among those who prostrated.
verses.12: Allah asked, 'What prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?' He said, 'I am better than him — You created me from fire and created him from clay.'
verses.13: Allah said, 'Descend from this place — it is not for you to be arrogant here, so get out, for you are among the debased.'
verses.14: He said, 'Grant me respite until the Day they are resurrected.'
verses.15: Allah said, 'You are among those granted respite.'
verses.16: He said, 'Because You have put me in error, I will surely lie in wait for them on Your straight path.'
verses.17: 'Then I will come to them from before them and behind them, from their right and their left, and You will not find most of them grateful.'
verses.18: Allah said, 'Get out of this place, blamed and driven away — whoever among them follows you, I will surely fill Hell with you all.'
verses.19: 'Adam, dwell with your wife in Paradise and eat freely from wherever you wish, but do not approach this tree, lest you be among the wrongdoers.'
verses.20: Satan whispered to them so as to expose to them what had been concealed of their nakedness, saying, 'Your Lord only forbade you this tree so you would not become angels or become immortal.'
verses.21: And he swore to them, 'Indeed, I am among your sincere advisors.'
verses.22: So he lured them by deception, and when they tasted the tree, their nakedness became apparent to them, and they began fastening leaves from Paradise together over themselves; their Lord called to them, 'Did I not forbid you from that tree, and tell you that Satan is a clear enemy to you?'
verses.23: They said, 'Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves — if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will surely be among the losers.'
verses.24: He said, 'Descend, being enemies to one another — you will have a settlement on earth and provision for a time.'
verses.25: 'Therein you will live, therein you will die, and from it you will be brought forth.'
verses.26: O children of Adam, We have given you clothing to cover your nakedness and as adornment, but the clothing of righteousness is best — that is one of the signs of Allah, so that people may remember.
verses.27: O children of Adam, do not let Satan tempt you as he removed your parents from Paradise, stripping them of their clothing to expose their nakedness — he and his tribe see you from where you cannot see them; We have made devils allies of those who do not believe.
verses.28: When they commit an immorality, they say, 'We found our fathers doing it, and Allah has commanded us to do it' — say, 'Allah does not command immorality — do you say about Allah what you do not know?'
verses.29: Say, 'My Lord has commanded justice, and that you turn your faces toward Him at every place of worship, and call upon Him sincerely in devotion — as He brought you into being, so you will return.'
verses.30: A group He guided, and a group deserved to go astray, for they took devils as allies instead of Allah while thinking they were rightly guided.
verses.31: O children of Adam, dress well at every place of worship, and eat and drink, but do not be excessive — He does not love those who commit excess.
verses.32: Say, 'Who has forbidden the beautiful garments and good provisions Allah has produced for His servants?' Say, 'These are for the believers during worldly life, and exclusively for them on the Day of Resurrection' — thus We detail the signs for people who know.
verses.33: Say, 'My Lord has only forbidden immoralities, apparent or concealed, sin, unjustified aggression, associating with Allah what He has sent no authority for, and saying about Allah what you do not know.'
verses.34: Every nation has an appointed term — when their term arrives, they cannot delay it an hour, nor can they advance it.
verses.35: O children of Adam, if messengers come to you from among yourselves relating My signs, then whoever is mindful of Allah and reforms — no fear will there be for them, nor will they grieve.
verses.36: But those who deny Our signs and are arrogant toward them — those are the companions of the Fire, abiding therein forever.
verses.37: Who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah or denies His signs? Such people will receive their share of what has been decreed, until Our messengers come to take their souls, asking, 'Where are those you used to call upon besides Allah?' They will say, 'They have left us,' and will testify against themselves that they were disbelievers.
verses.38: Allah will say, 'Enter, among the nations of jinn and mankind that passed before you, into the Fire' — every time a nation enters, it curses its sister nation, until, when they have all gathered there, the last of them will say of the first, 'Our Lord, these led us astray, so give them double the punishment of the Fire' — He will say, 'Each of you will have double, but you do not know.'
verses.39: The first will say to the last, 'You have no advantage over us, so taste the punishment for what you used to earn.'
verses.40: Those who deny Our signs and are arrogant toward them — the gates of heaven will not be opened for them, nor will they enter Paradise until a camel passes through the eye of a needle — thus do We recompense the criminals.
verses.41: They will have a bed of Hell beneath them and coverings of it above them — thus do We recompense the wrongdoers.
verses.42: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds — We do not burden a soul beyond its capacity — those are the companions of Paradise, abiding therein forever.
verses.43: We will remove whatever resentment is in their hearts; rivers will flow beneath them, and they will say, 'Praise be to Allah, who has guided us to this — we would never have been guided if Allah had not guided us; our Lord's messengers certainly came with truth' — and it will be proclaimed to them, 'This is Paradise, given to you as inheritance for what you used to do.'
verses.44: The companions of Paradise will call out to the companions of the Fire, 'We have found what our Lord promised us to be true — have you found what your Lord promised to be true?' They will say, 'Yes' — and a caller will announce, 'The curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers.'
verses.45: Who averted people from the path of Allah and sought to make it crooked, and who disbelieved in the Hereafter.
verses.46: Between them will be a partition, and on the heights are men who recognize everyone by their mark, calling out to the companions of Paradise, 'Peace be upon you' — they have not yet entered it, though they long to.
verses.47: And when their eyes are turned toward the companions of the Fire, they say, 'Our Lord, do not place us with the wrongdoing people.'
verses.48: The companions of the heights call to men they recognize by their mark, saying, 'What good was your accumulated wealth and your arrogance to you?'
verses.49: 'Are these the ones you swore Allah would never extend mercy to? Enter Paradise — no fear will there be for you, nor will you grieve.'
