verses.50: The companions of the Fire will call to the companions of Paradise, 'Pour upon us some water, or anything Allah has provided you' — they will reply, 'Allah has forbidden both to the disbelievers.'
verses.51: Those who took their religion as amusement and diversion, and were deluded by the life of this world — today We will forget them, just as they forgot the meeting of this Day, and just as they used to deny Our signs.
verses.52: We have certainly brought them a Book which We have detailed with knowledge, as guidance and mercy for a people who believe.
verses.53: Are they waiting for anything but its fulfillment? The Day it is fulfilled, those who had forgotten it before will say, 'Our Lord's messengers certainly came with truth — are there any intercessors now to intercede for us?'
verses.54: Indeed, your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself above the Throne; He covers the night with the day, seeking it rapidly, and He created the sun, the moon, and the stars, subjected to His command — unquestionably His is the creation and the command; blessed is Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
verses.55: Call upon your Lord humbly and privately — He does not love transgressors.
verses.56: Do not cause corruption on earth after it has been set right, and call upon Him in fear and hope — the mercy of Allah is always near to those who do good.
verses.57: It is He who sends the winds as good tidings ahead of His mercy, until, when they have carried heavy clouds, We drive them to a dead land and send down rain, bringing forth every kind of fruit — thus do We bring forth the dead, so that you may remember.
verses.58: The good land brings forth its vegetation by permission of its Lord, while the bad land brings forth nothing but scarcely — thus do We diversify the signs for a people who are grateful.
verses.59: We sent Noah to his people, and he said, 'O my people, worship Allah — you have no god other than Him; indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.'
verses.60: The eminent among his people said, 'We see you in clear error.'
verses.61: He said, 'O my people, there is no error in me — rather, I am a messenger from the Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.62: 'I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I advise you, and I know from Allah what you do not know.'
verses.63: 'Do you find it strange that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man among you, to warn you, so that you might be mindful of Allah, and perhaps be shown mercy?'
verses.64: But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who denied Our signs — they were truly a blind people.
verses.65: To the people of Aad We sent their brother Hud — he said, 'O my people, worship Allah — you have no god other than Him, so will you not be mindful of Him?'
verses.66: The eminent among his disbelieving people said, 'We see you in foolishness, and we think you are among the liars.'
verses.67: He said, 'O my people, there is no foolishness in me — rather, I am a messenger from the Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.68: 'I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I am a trustworthy advisor to you.'
verses.69: 'Do you find it strange that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man among you, to warn you? Remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you greatly in stature — remember Allah's favors, that you might succeed.'
verses.70: They said, 'Have you come to us so that we worship Allah alone and abandon what our fathers worshipped? Then bring us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful.'
verses.71: He said, 'Punishment and anger have already fallen upon you from your Lord — do you argue with me over mere names you and your fathers have invented, for which Allah has sent down no authority? Then wait, and I too am waiting with you.'
verses.72: So We saved him and those with him by mercy from Us, and We eliminated those who denied Our signs and were not believers.
verses.73: To the people of Thamud We sent their brother Salih — he said, 'O my people, worship Allah — you have no god other than Him; a clear proof has come to you from your Lord — this is the she-camel of Allah, a sign for you, so leave her to graze freely on Allah's land, and do not touch her with harm, lest a painful punishment seize you.'
verses.74: 'Remember when He made you successors after Aad and settled you in the land — you take palaces from its plains and carve homes into its mountains — remember Allah's favors, and do not go about the earth spreading corruption.'
verses.75: The eminent who were arrogant among his people said to the oppressed ones — those among them who believed — 'Do you know that Salih is truly sent from his Lord?' They said, 'Indeed, we believe in what he has been sent with.'
verses.76: The arrogant ones said, 'We disbelieve in what you believe in.'
verses.77: So they hamstrung the she-camel and defied the command of their Lord, and they said, 'O Salih, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are truly one of the messengers.'
verses.78: So the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless in their homes, fallen face down.
verses.79: He turned away from them and said, 'O my people, I certainly conveyed to you the message of my Lord and gave you sincere advice, but you do not like advisors.'
verses.80: And Lot, when he said to his people, 'Do you commit an immorality no people before you in all the worlds have ever committed?'
verses.81: 'Indeed, you approach men with desire instead of women — rather, you are a transgressing people.'
verses.82: The only response of his people was to say, 'Expel them from your town — they are people who keep themselves pure.'
verses.83: So We saved him and his family, except his wife — she was among those who stayed behind.
verses.84: And We rained upon them a rain of destruction — see how the end of the criminals was.
verses.85: To the people of Madyan We sent their brother Shuaib — he said, 'O my people, worship Allah — you have no god other than Him; a clear proof has come to you from your Lord — so give full measure and full weight, and do not deprive people of what is theirs, and do not spread corruption on earth after it has been set right — that is better for you, if you are believers.'
verses.86: 'And do not sit at every path threatening people and turning them away from Allah's way, those who believe in Him, seeking to make it crooked — remember when you were few and He increased you in number, and see how the end of the corrupters was.'
verses.87: 'If there is a group among you who has believed in what I was sent with, and a group who has not believed, then be patient until Allah judges between us — He is the best of judges.'
verses.88: The eminent who were arrogant among his people said, 'We will surely expel you, Shuaib, and those who have believed with you, from our town, unless you return to our religion' — he said, 'Even if we are unwilling?'
verses.89: 'We would surely be inventing a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion after Allah has saved us from it, and it is not for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, wills — our Lord encompasses all things in knowledge; upon Allah we have relied — our Lord, decide between us and our people in truth, for You are the best of those who decide.'
verses.90: The eminent who disbelieved among his people said, 'If you follow Shuaib, you will surely be losers.'
verses.91: So the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless in their homes, fallen face down.
verses.92: Those who denied Shuaib became as if they had never lived there at all — those who denied Shuaib were the true losers.
verses.93: He turned away from them and said, 'O my people, I certainly conveyed to you the messages of my Lord and gave you sincere advice — so how could I grieve for a disbelieving people?'
verses.94: Whenever We sent a prophet to a city, We seized its people with hardship and adversity, so that they might humble themselves.
verses.95: Then We replaced hardship with prosperity, until they multiplied and said, 'Our forefathers too experienced hardship and ease,' so We seized them suddenly while they did not perceive it coming.
verses.96: Had the people of the cities believed and been mindful of Allah, We would have opened upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth — but they denied, so We seized them for what they used to earn.
verses.97: Did the people of the cities feel secure from Our punishment coming to them by night while they were asleep?
verses.98: Or did they feel secure from Our punishment coming to them in broad daylight while they were at play?
verses.99: Did they then feel secure from Allah's plan? No one feels secure from Allah's plan except a people destined to lose.
verses.100: Has it not become clear to those who inherited the earth after its former inhabitants that, had We willed, We could have afflicted them for their sins and sealed over their hearts so they could no longer hear?
verses.101: Those cities — We relate to you some of their stories; their messengers certainly came to them with clear proofs, but they were not ones to believe in what they had already denied — thus does Allah seal over the hearts of the disbelievers.
verses.102: We found most of them without any regard for a covenant — rather, We found most of them defiantly disobedient.
verses.103: Then after them We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, but they were unjust toward them — see how the end of the corrupters was.
verses.104: Moses said, 'O Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.105: 'It is only right that I say nothing about Allah except the truth — I have come to you with a clear proof from your Lord, so send with me the Children of Israel.'
verses.106: Pharaoh said, 'If you have brought a sign, then produce it, if you are among the truthful.'
verses.107: So Moses threw down his staff, and it became a clear serpent.
verses.108: And he drew out his hand, and it appeared white to all who looked upon it.
verses.109: The eminent among Pharaoh's people said, 'This is indeed a learned magician.'
verses.110: 'He wants to drive you out of your land, so what do you advise?'
verses.111: They said, 'Delay him and his brother, and send gatherers throughout the cities.'
verses.113: The magicians came to Pharaoh and said, 'Will there truly be a reward for us if we are the ones who prevail?'
verses.114: He said, 'Yes, and you will indeed be among those brought near to me.'
verses.115: They said, 'O Moses, either you throw first, or we will be the ones to throw.'
verses.116: He said, 'You throw' — and when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people, struck terror into them, and produced a great display of magic.
verses.117: We inspired Moses, 'Throw your staff,' and it swallowed up what they had falsely produced.
verses.118: So the truth was established, and what they were doing was nullified.
verses.119: They were defeated there and turned back, utterly humbled.
verses.120: And the magicians fell down in prostration.
verses.121: They said, 'We believe in the Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.123: Pharaoh said, 'You believed in him before I gave you permission — this is only a scheme you devised in the city to drive out its people, but you will soon know.'
verses.124: 'I will surely cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then I will surely crucify you all.'
verses.125: They said, 'Indeed, to our Lord we will return.'
verses.126: 'And you resent us only because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us — our Lord, pour patience upon us, and let us die in submission to You.'
verses.127: The eminent among Pharaoh's people said, 'Will you leave Moses and his people to spread corruption in the land and abandon you and your gods?' He said, 'We will kill their sons and let their women live — indeed, we hold complete dominance over them.'
verses.128: Moses said to his people, 'Seek help through Allah and be patient — the earth truly belongs to Allah, and He gives it as inheritance to whomever He wills of His servants, and the outcome belongs to the righteous.'
verses.129: They said, 'We were harmed before you came to us, and after you came to us' — he said, 'Perhaps your Lord will destroy your enemy and grant you succession in the land, and then see how you will act.'
verses.130: We seized the people of Pharaoh with years of famine and a shortage of fruits, so that they might remember.
verses.131: When something good came to them, they said, 'This is due to us' — but when a misfortune afflicted them, they blamed Moses and those with him; yet the true cause of their fate was with Allah, though most of them did not know it.
verses.132: They said, 'Whatever sign you bring to bewitch us with, we will not believe you.'
verses.133: So We sent upon them the flood, locusts, lice, frogs, and blood as distinct signs — yet they remained arrogant and were a people immersed in crime.
verses.134: When the punishment fell upon them, they said, 'O Moses, call upon your Lord for us by the covenant He has made with you — if you remove the punishment from us, we will surely believe you and send the Children of Israel with you.'
verses.135: But when We removed the punishment from them for a term they were to reach, they broke their word at once.
verses.136: So We took retribution from them and drowned them in the sea, because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.
verses.137: We caused the oppressed people to inherit the eastern and western regions of the land, which We had blessed, and the good word of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel because of their patience — and We destroyed everything Pharaoh and his people had built and erected.
verses.138: We took the Children of Israel across the sea, and they came upon a people devoted to idols of their own — they said, 'O Moses, make for us a god just as they have gods,' and he said, 'You are truly an ignorant people.'
verses.139: 'Indeed, what these people are engaged in is destroyed, and worthless is everything they do.'
verses.140: He said, 'Shall I seek for you a god other than Allah, when He has preferred you above all the worlds?'
verses.141: 'And remember when We saved you from the people of Pharaoh, who inflicted upon you the worst torment, killing your sons and letting your women live — in that was a tremendous trial from your Lord.'
verses.142: We made an appointment with Moses for thirty nights, and completed it with ten more, so the term set by his Lord was completed at forty nights — Moses said to his brother Aaron, 'Take my place among my people, act rightly, and do not follow the way of the corrupters.'
verses.143: When Moses came to Our appointed time and his Lord spoke to him, he said, 'My Lord, show Yourself to me, that I may look at You' — He said, 'You will not see Me, but look at the mountain — if it remains in place, then you will see Me' — but when his Lord appeared to the mountain, He rendered it crushed to dust, and Moses fell down unconscious; when he awoke, he said, 'Exalted are You — I turn to You in repentance, and I am the first of the believers.'
verses.144: He said, 'Moses, I have chosen you above the people through My messages and My words — so take what I have given you, and be among the grateful.'
verses.145: We inscribed for him on the tablets something of everything — instruction and explanation for all things, saying, 'Take them with determination, and command your people to take what is best in them — I will soon show you the home of the defiantly disobedient.'
verses.146: I will turn away from My signs those who are arrogant on the earth without right — and even if they see every sign, they will not believe in it, and even if they see the way of right conduct, they will not take it as their way, but if they see the way of error, they take it as their way; that is because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.
verses.147: Those who deny Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter — their deeds have become worthless; are they recompensed except for what they used to do?
verses.148: The people of Moses, after his departure, made from their ornaments a calf — an image that made a lowing sound; did they not see that it could neither speak to them nor guide them to any way? They took it and became wrongdoers.
verses.149: When regret overtook them and they saw that they had gone astray, they said, 'If our Lord does not have mercy on us and forgive us, we will surely be among the losers.'
verses.150: When Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved, he said, 'How wretched is what you have done in my absence — did you hasten to bring about the command of your Lord?' He threw down the tablets and seized his brother by the head, pulling him toward himself — Aaron said, 'Son of my mother, the people overpowered me and nearly killed me, so do not let the enemies rejoice over me, and do not place me among the wrongdoing people.'
verses.151: Moses said, 'My Lord, forgive me and my brother, and admit us into Your mercy, for You are the most merciful of the merciful.'
verses.152: Those who took the calf as an object of worship will incur anger from their Lord and humiliation in worldly life — thus do We recompense the inventors of falsehood.
verses.153: But those who did evil deeds and then repented after them and believed — indeed, your Lord after that is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.154: When Moses's anger subsided, he took up the tablets, and in their inscription was guidance and mercy for those who fear their Lord.
verses.155: Moses chose seventy men from his people for Our appointment, and when the earthquake seized them, he said, 'My Lord, had You willed, You could have destroyed them before, and me as well — will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? This is only Your trial, by which You send astray whom You will and guide whom You will — You are our protector, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, for You are the best of forgivers.'
verses.156: 'And ordain for us good in this world and in the Hereafter — we have indeed turned to You' — He said, 'My punishment — I afflict with it whom I will, but My mercy encompasses all things, so I will decree it for those who are mindful of Allah, give charity, and believe in Our signs.'
verses.157: 'Those who follow the Messenger, the unlettered prophet, whom they find described in the Torah and the Gospel that they possess, who commands them toward what is right and forbids them from what is wrong, makes lawful for them the good things and prohibits for them what is bad, and relieves them of their burdens and the shackles that were upon them — so those who believe in him, honor him, support him, and follow the light sent down with him, it is they who will succeed.'
verses.158: Say: 'O mankind, I am truly the Messenger of Allah to all of you — to Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; there is no god except Him; He gives life and causes death — so believe in Allah and His Messenger, the unlettered prophet who believes in Allah and His words, and follow him, that you may be guided.'
verses.159: Among the people of Moses is a community that guides by truth and establishes justice through it.
verses.160: We divided them into twelve tribes as distinct communities, and We inspired Moses, when his people asked him for water, 'Strike the stone with your staff' — twelve springs gushed forth from it, and every tribe knew its own watering place; We shaded them with clouds and sent down manna and quails upon them — 'Eat of the good things We have provided you' — but they did not wrong Us; rather, they were wronging themselves.
verses.161: It was said to them, 'Dwell in this town and eat from it wherever you wish, and say a word of repentance, and enter the gate bowing humbly — We will forgive you your sins, and We will increase the reward of the doers of good.'
verses.162: But those among them who did wrong substituted a different word from the one they had been given, so We sent upon them a punishment from the sky for what they were wrongfully doing.
verses.163: Ask them about the town that was by the sea, when its people transgressed regarding the Sabbath — when the fish came to them clearly visible on their Sabbath day, but did not come on the day they had no Sabbath — thus did We test them for their persistent defiance.
verses.164: When a group among them said, 'Why do you advise a people whom Allah is going to destroy or punish with a severe punishment?' They said, 'As justification before your Lord, and so that they might perhaps become mindful of Him.'
verses.165: When they forgot what they had been reminded of, We saved those who had forbidden evil, and We seized the wrongdoers with a wretched punishment for their persistent defiance.
verses.166: When they insolently persisted in what they had been forbidden, We said to them, 'Be apes, despised.'
verses.167: Your Lord declared that He would surely send against them, until the Day of Resurrection, those who would afflict them with the worst torment — your Lord is swift in punishment, yet He is truly Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.168: We dispersed them throughout the earth as separate communities — some among them were righteous, and some were otherwise, and We tried them with times of prosperity and times of hardship, so that they might return.
verses.169: Then successors came after them who inherited the Scripture, taking the fleeting gains of this lower life, saying, 'It will be forgiven us,' and if similar gains came to them again, they would take them just the same — had not a covenant of the Scripture been taken from them, that they would say nothing about Allah except the truth, and had they not studied what was in it? The home of the Hereafter is better for those who are mindful of Allah — will you not use reason?
verses.170: And those who hold firmly to the Book and establish prayer — indeed, We will never let the reward of the reformers be lost.
verses.171: When We raised the mountain above them as if it were a shading canopy, and they were certain it would fall upon them — 'Hold firmly to what We have given you, and remember what is in it, that you may be mindful of Allah.'
verses.172: When your Lord took from the children of Adam, from their loins, their descendants, and made them testify about themselves, 'Am I not your Lord?' They said, 'Yes, we testify' — lest you say on the Day of Resurrection, 'We were unaware of this.'
verses.173: Or lest you say, 'Our fathers associated others with Allah before us, and we were only descendants after them — would You then destroy us for what those who dealt in falsehood used to do?'
verses.174: And thus do We detail the signs, so that perhaps they will return.
verses.175: Recite to them the account of the one to whom We gave Our signs, but who detached himself from them, so Satan pursued him, and he became one of those who went astray.
verses.176: Had We willed, We could have elevated him by these signs, but he clung to the earth and followed his own desire — his likeness is that of a dog: whether you chase it away or leave it alone, it pants regardless — such is the likeness of the people who deny Our signs, so relate this account, that perhaps they will reflect.
verses.177: How evil is the example of the people who denied Our signs and used to wrong themselves.
verses.178: Whoever Allah guides is truly guided, and whoever He leaves astray — those are the losers.
verses.179: We have certainly created many jinn and mankind for Hell — they have hearts with which they do not understand, eyes with which they do not see, and ears with which they do not hear; those are like livestock, or even more astray — those are the heedless.
verses.180: To Allah belong the most beautiful names, so call upon Him by them, and leave those who deviate concerning His names — they will be recompensed for what they used to do.
verses.181: Among those We have created is a community that guides by truth and establishes justice through it.
verses.182: As for those who deny Our signs, We will progressively draw them toward destruction from where they do not know.
verses.183: I will grant them respite — indeed, My plan is firm.
verses.184: Do they not reflect that their companion is not afflicted by madness? He is only a clear warner.
verses.185: Have they not looked into the realm of the heavens and the earth, and everything Allah has created, and considered that perhaps their own time is drawing near? So in what message after this will they believe?
verses.186: Whoever Allah leaves astray has no guide — He leaves them to wander blindly in their transgression.
verses.187: They ask you about the Hour, when it will come to pass; say, 'Its knowledge is only with my Lord — none can reveal its timing except Him; it weighs heavily upon the heavens and the earth, and it will not come upon you except suddenly' — they ask you as if you were well informed about it; say, 'Its knowledge is only with Allah,' though most people do not know this.
verses.188: Say: 'I hold no power for myself over benefit or harm, except what Allah wills — had I known the unseen, I would have accumulated much good, and no harm would have touched me; I am only a warner and a bringer of good tidings to a people who believe.'
verses.189: It is He who created you from one soul, and from it made its mate, so that he might find comfort in her — when he covered her, she carried a light burden and went about with it, and when it grew heavy, they both prayed to Allah, their Lord, 'If You give us a righteous child, we will surely be among the grateful.'
verses.190: Yet when He gave them a healthy child, they attributed partners to Him regarding what He had given them — exalted is Allah above what they associate with Him.
verses.191: Do they associate with Him things that create nothing, while they themselves are created?
verses.192: Such things can neither offer any help to them, nor can they help themselves.
verses.193: And if you invite them to guidance, they will not follow you — it makes no difference whether you invite them or remain silent.
verses.194: Those you call upon besides Allah are servants like yourselves — so call upon them and let them answer you, if you are truthful.
verses.195: Do they have feet with which to walk, hands with which to strike, eyes with which to see, or ears with which to hear? Say, 'Call upon your partners, then plot against me, and give me no respite.'
verses.196: 'Indeed, my protector is Allah, who has revealed the Book, and He is an ally to the righteous.'
verses.197: 'And those you call upon besides Him are unable to help you, nor can they help themselves.'
verses.198: If you invite them to guidance, they do not hear — and you see them looking at you, though they do not truly see.
verses.199: Take what is given freely, enjoin what is right, and turn away from the ignorant.
verses.200: And if an evil suggestion from Satan provokes you, then seek refuge in Allah — indeed, He is Hearing, Knowing.
verses.201: Indeed, those who are mindful of Allah — when an impulse from Satan touches them, they remember Him, and at once they truly see clearly again.
verses.202: But as for their brothers, the devils draw them further into error, and then they do not relent.
verses.203: When you do not bring them a miracle, they say, 'Why have you not produced one?' Say, 'I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord — this is enlightenment from your Lord, and guidance and mercy for a people who believe.'
verses.204: So when the Quran is recited, listen to it attentively and remain silent, that you may receive mercy.
verses.205: Remember your Lord within yourself, in humility and in awe, without raising your voice, in the mornings and the evenings, and do not be among the heedless.
verses.206: Indeed, those who are near your Lord are never too proud to worship Him — they glorify Him, and to Him they prostrate. The surah closes with the same posture of humility before Allah with which it began, describing angels bowing exactly as Iblis once refused to.

verses.1: They ask you about the spoils of war — say, 'Their distribution belongs to Allah and the Messenger,' so be mindful of Allah, settle disputes among yourselves, and obey Allah and His Messenger, if you are truly believers.
verses.2: The believers are only those whose hearts tremble with awe when Allah is mentioned, whose faith increases when His verses are recited to them, and who rely upon their Lord.
verses.3: Those who establish prayer and spend from what We have provided them.
verses.4: Those are the true believers — they will have high ranks with their Lord, forgiveness, and generous provision.
verses.5: Just as your Lord brought you out of your home in truth, though a group of the believers were averse to it.
verses.6: They argued with you about the truth after it had become clear, as if they were being driven toward death while looking on.
verses.7: Remember when Allah promised you that one of the two groups would be yours, and you wished that the unarmed one would be yours, but Allah intended to establish the truth by His words and to cut off the roots of the disbelievers.
verses.8: That He might establish the truth and abolish falsehood, even though the criminals disliked it.
verses.9: Remember when you sought help from your Lord, and He answered you, 'I will reinforce you with a thousand angels, one after another.'
verses.10: Allah made it not except as good news, so your hearts might be at rest through it — victory comes only from Allah, for Allah is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.11: He caused drowsiness to overcome you as a reassurance from Him, and sent down water from the sky to purify you, remove Satan's suggestions from you, strengthen your hearts, and make your feet firm.
verses.12: Your Lord inspired the angels, 'I am with you, so make firm those who believe — I will cast terror into the hearts of the disbelievers, so strike above their necks and strike from them every fingertip.'
verses.13: That was because they opposed Allah and His Messenger — whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger, Allah is severe in punishment.
verses.14: 'That is your penalty, so taste it, and know that for the disbelievers is the punishment of the Fire.'
verses.15: O believers, when you meet those who disbelieve advancing for battle, do not turn your backs to them.
verses.16: Whoever turns his back on them that day, unless maneuvering for battle or joining another group, has certainly incurred wrath from Allah, and his refuge is Hell — a truly wretched destination.
verses.17: You did not kill them, but it was Allah who killed them, and you did not throw when you threw, but it was Allah who threw, that He might test the believers with a good test — indeed, Allah is Hearing, Knowing.
verses.18: That was the outcome, and that Allah will weaken the plot of the disbelievers.
verses.19: If you sought victory, victory has already come to you; and if you cease, that is better for you, but if you return, We will return, and your force will not avail you at all, no matter how great its number — Allah is with the believers.
verses.20: O believers, obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him while you hear him speak.
verses.21: And do not be like those who say, 'We have heard,' while they do not truly hear.
verses.22: The worst of moving creatures in Allah's sight are the deaf and the mute who do not use reason.
verses.23: Had Allah known any good in them, He would have made them hear — and even if He had made them hear, they would have turned away, averse.
verses.24: O believers, respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life, and know that Allah intervenes between a person and his heart, and that to Him you will be gathered.
verses.25: Beware of a trial that will not strike only the wrongdoers among you exclusively, and know that Allah is severe in punishment.
verses.26: Remember when you were few and considered weak in the land, fearing that people might snatch you away, but He sheltered you, supported you with His help, and provided you with good things, that you might be grateful.
verses.27: O believers, do not betray Allah and the Messenger, or betray your trusts while you know their importance.
verses.28: And know that your wealth and your children are but a trial, and that with Allah is a great reward.
verses.29: O believers, if you are mindful of Allah, He will grant you a criterion, remove your misdeeds from you, and forgive you — Allah is the Owner of great bounty.
verses.30: Remember when the disbelievers plotted against you, to restrain you, kill you, or expel you — they plotted, and Allah planned as well, and Allah is the best of planners.
verses.31: When Our verses are recited to them, they say, 'We have heard — if we willed, we could say something like this; this is nothing but legends of the ancient peoples.'
verses.32: Remember when they said, 'O Allah, if this is the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.'
verses.33: But Allah would not punish them while you were among them, nor would He punish them while they sought His forgiveness.
verses.34: Why should Allah not punish them, when they have obstructed people from the Sacred Mosque and are not even its rightful guardians? Its true guardians are only those who are mindful of Allah, though most of them do not know it.
verses.35: Their prayer at the House was nothing but whistling and clapping of hands — so taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve.
verses.36: Indeed, those who disbelieve spend their wealth to obstruct people from Allah's way; they will continue spending it, but then it will become a source of regret for them, and eventually they will be overcome — and those who disbelieve will be gathered to Hell.
verses.37: That Allah may separate the wicked from the good, and place the wicked one upon another, heap them together entirely, and put them into Hell — those are the truly lost.
verses.38: Say to those who disbelieve, if they cease, what has already passed will be forgiven them, but if they return to hostility, then the precedent of the earlier peoples has already passed.
verses.39: Fight them until there is no more persecution and until the religion, all of it, belongs to Allah; but if they cease, then indeed, Allah sees fully what they do.
verses.40: But if they turn away, then know that Allah is your protector — an excellent protector and an excellent helper.
verses.41: Know that whatever spoils of war you obtain, a fifth belongs to Allah, the Messenger, near relatives, orphans, the needy, and the traveler, if you truly believe in Allah and what We revealed to Our servant on the day of criterion, the day the two armies met — Allah is capable of all things.
verses.42: Remember when you were on the near side of the valley, and they were on the far side, and the caravan was lower than you — had you made an appointment to meet, you would have differed about the timing, but this was so that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined, that whoever perished would perish by clear evidence, and whoever lived might live by clear evidence — Allah is Hearing, Knowing.
verses.43: Remember when Allah showed them to you in your dream as few — had He shown them to you as many, you would have lost heart and disputed over the matter, but Allah saved you from that, for He knows well what is within every heart.
verses.44: Remember when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes, and made you appear as few in their eyes, so that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined — to Allah all matters are returned.
verses.45: O believers, when you encounter an opposing force, stand firm and remember Allah often, that you may succeed.
verses.46: Obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute with one another, lest you lose courage and your strength depart — be patient, for Allah is with the patient.
verses.47: And do not be like those who left their homes boastfully and to be seen by people, and who obstructed others from the way of Allah — Allah fully encompasses everything they do.
verses.48: Remember when Satan made their deeds appear pleasing to them and said, 'No one can overcome you today, and I am truly your protector' — but when the two forces came in sight of each other, he turned on his heels and said, 'I am free of you — I see what you do not see, and I fear Allah, for Allah is severe in punishment.'
verses.49: Remember when the hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts said, 'Their religion has deluded these people' — but whoever relies upon Allah will find Allah Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.50: If only you could see when the angels take the souls of the disbelievers, striking their faces and their backs, saying, 'Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.'
verses.51: 'That is for what your own hands have sent forth, and Allah is never unjust to His servants.'
verses.52: Like the behavior of Pharaoh's people and those before them — they disbelieved in Allah's signs, so Allah seized them for their sins, for Allah is Powerful and severe in punishment.
verses.53: That is because Allah would never change a favor He has bestowed upon a people until they change what is within themselves, and indeed Allah is Hearing, Knowing.
verses.54: Like the behavior of Pharaoh's people and those before them — they denied the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned Pharaoh's people, for they were all wrongdoers.
verses.55: Indeed, the worst of moving creatures in Allah's sight are those who disbelieve and will not believe.
verses.56: Those with whom you made a treaty, but who then break their pledge every time, and are not even mindful of it.
verses.57: So if you gain the advantage over them in war, deal with them decisively enough to disperse those who would follow them, so that they may take heed.
verses.58: If you fear treachery from a people, throw their treaty back to them equally — Allah does not love the treacherous.
verses.59: Let not those who disbelieve suppose that they have outrun anything — they will never cause failure.
verses.60: Prepare against them whatever force and steeds of war you are able, to deter thereby the enemies of Allah and your enemies, and others besides them whom you may not know but whom Allah knows — whatever you spend in Allah's cause will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.
verses.61: If they incline toward peace, then incline toward it as well, and rely upon Allah, for He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.62: And if they intend to deceive you, then Allah is sufficient for you — it is He who supported you with His help and with the believers.
verses.63: And He brought their hearts together — had you spent everything on earth, you could not have united their hearts, but Allah brought them together, for He is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.64: O Prophet, sufficient for you is Allah, and for whoever follows you among the believers.
verses.65: O Prophet, urge the believers to fight — if there are twenty among you who are steadfast, they will overcome two hundred, and if there are a hundred among you, they will overcome a thousand of those who disbelieve, because they are a people who do not understand.
verses.66: Now Allah has lightened your burden, and He knows there is weakness within you — so if there are a hundred steadfast among you, they will overcome two hundred, and if there are a thousand, they will overcome two thousand by Allah's permission, for Allah is with the steadfast.
verses.67: It is not fitting for a prophet to hold captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land — you desire the fleeting gains of this world, while Allah desires the Hereafter for you, and Allah is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.68: Had it not been for a prior decree from Allah, a severe punishment would have touched you for what you took.
verses.69: So consume what you have gained of the spoils, as lawful and good, and be mindful of Allah, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.70: O Prophet, say to the captives in your hands, 'If Allah knows any good in your hearts, He will give you something better than what was taken from you, and He will forgive you, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.'
verses.71: But if they intend to betray you, they have already betrayed Allah before, and He gave you power over them — Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.72: Those who believed, emigrated, and strove with their wealth and their lives in Allah's cause, and those who gave shelter and aid — those are allies of one another; but those who believed and did not emigrate, you have no duty to protect them until they emigrate, though if they seek help from you in religion, you are obliged to help them, except against a people with whom you have a treaty — Allah sees fully what you do.
verses.73: As for those who disbelieve, they are allies of one another — if you do not act likewise, there will be persecution on earth and great corruption.
verses.74: Those who believed, emigrated, and strove in Allah's cause, and those who gave shelter and aid, they are the true believers — for them is forgiveness and generous provision.
verses.75: Those who believed afterward, emigrated, and strove alongside you, are of you as well — but relatives are more entitled to inheritance from one another in the Book of Allah, for Allah has full knowledge of all things. The surah closes, having opened on the spoils of a hard-won battle, by widening its concern to bonds of loyalty, kinship, and shelter that outlast any single victory.

verses.1: This is a declaration of disassociation from Allah and His Messenger toward those idolaters with whom you have made a treaty. The surah opens without its usual opening formula, matching the harder tone of the announcement it delivers.
verses.2: So travel freely throughout the land for four months, but know that you cannot escape Allah, and that Allah will disgrace the disbelievers.
verses.3: And a declaration from Allah and His Messenger to all the people on the day of the greatest pilgrimage — that Allah is disassociated from the idolaters, as is His Messenger; so if you repent, that is better for you, but if you turn away, then know that you cannot escape Allah — give tidings of a painful punishment to those who disbelieve.
verses.4: Except for those idolaters with whom you made a treaty, who have not violated anything of it nor supported anyone against you — fulfill their treaty until its term, for Allah loves the righteous.
verses.5: When the sacred months have passed, kill the idolaters wherever you find them, capture them, besiege them, and lie in wait for them at every point of ambush — but if they repent, establish prayer, and give charity, let them go on their way, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.6: If any among the idolaters seeks your protection, grant it to him so he may hear the words of Allah, then deliver him to his place of safety — that is because they are a people without knowledge.
verses.7: How can there be a treaty with Allah and His Messenger for the idolaters, except those with whom you made a treaty at the Sacred Mosque? As long as they remain true to you, remain true to them, for Allah loves the righteous.
verses.8: How could there be a treaty, when, if they gained the advantage over you, they would not observe any kinship or covenant with you? They satisfy you with their mouths while their hearts refuse, and most of them are defiantly disobedient.
verses.9: They exchanged Allah's verses for a small price and averted people from His way — how evil is what they have been doing.
verses.10: They do not observe toward a believer any tie of kinship or covenant — it is they who are the transgressors.
verses.11: But if they repent, establish prayer, and give charity, then they are your brothers in religion — thus We detail the verses for a people who know.
verses.12: If they break their oaths after their treaty and defame your religion, then fight the leaders of disbelief — indeed, their oaths mean nothing to them — so that they might cease.
verses.13: Would you not fight a people who broke their oaths, resolved to expel the Messenger, and were the ones who initiated hostility against you first? Do you fear them? Allah has more right to be feared, if you are truly believers.
verses.14: Fight them — Allah will punish them by your hands, disgrace them, give you victory over them, and heal the breasts of a believing people.
verses.15: And remove the rage of their hearts — Allah turns in mercy to whom He wills, for Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.16: Do you suppose you would be left without Allah having made evident those among you who strive and who take no one as an intimate ally besides Allah, His Messenger, and the believers? Allah is fully aware of what you do.
verses.17: It is not for the idolaters to maintain the mosques of Allah while testifying against themselves to disbelief — those are the ones whose deeds have become worthless, and they will abide eternally in the Fire.
verses.18: Only those maintain the mosques of Allah who believe in Allah and the Last Day, establish prayer, give charity, and fear none but Allah — it is expected that those will be among the rightly guided.
verses.19: Have you made providing water to pilgrims and maintaining the Sacred Mosque equal to believing in Allah and the Last Day and striving in Allah's cause? They are not equal in Allah's sight — Allah does not guide a wrongdoing people.
verses.20: Those who believed, emigrated, and strove in Allah's cause with their wealth and their lives are greater in rank with Allah, and it is they who will be the successful ones.
verses.21: Their Lord gives them good tidings of mercy from Himself, His approval, and gardens in which they will have lasting bliss.
verses.22: Abiding therein forever — with Allah is a great reward.
verses.23: O believers, do not take your fathers or your brothers as allies if they prefer disbelief over belief — whoever among you takes them as allies, those are the wrongdoers.
verses.24: Say: 'If your fathers, sons, brothers, wives, relatives, wealth you have acquired, trade you fear may decline, or dwellings you love are more beloved to you than Allah, His Messenger, and striving in His cause, then wait until Allah brings about His command' — Allah does not guide a defiantly disobedient people.
verses.25: Allah has already given you victory in many regions and on the day of Hunayn, when your great numbers pleased you, but they availed you nothing, and the earth, despite its vastness, closed in on you, and then you turned back retreating.
verses.26: Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Messenger and upon the believers, and sent down soldiers whom you did not see, and punished those who disbelieved — that is the recompense of the disbelievers.
verses.27: Then Allah accepts the repentance of whomever He wills after that, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.28: O believers, the idolaters are truly impure, so let them not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year of theirs — and if you fear poverty, Allah will enrich you from His bounty if He wills, for Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.29: Fight those who do not believe in Allah or the Last Day, nor forbid what Allah and His Messenger have forbidden, nor follow the religion of truth from among those given the Scripture, until they pay the required tax while willingly submissive.
verses.30: The Jews say, 'Ezra is the son of Allah,' and the Christians say, 'The Messiah is the son of Allah' — that is a statement from their mouths, resembling the statement of those who disbelieved before them; may Allah destroy them — how are they deluded?
verses.31: They have taken their scholars and monks as lords besides Allah, and also the Messiah, son of Mary, though they were commanded to worship only one God — there is no god except Him, exalted is He above what they associate with Him.
verses.32: They want to extinguish Allah's light with their mouths, but Allah refuses except to perfect His light, even if the disbelievers dislike it.
verses.33: It is He who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth, to make it prevail over all religion, even if the polytheists dislike it.
verses.34: O believers, indeed many scholars and monks consume people's wealth unjustly and turn people away from Allah's way — those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it in Allah's cause, give them tidings of a painful punishment.
verses.35: The Day it will be heated in the fire of Hell and used to brand their foreheads, their sides, and their backs — 'This is what you hoarded for yourselves, so taste what you used to hoard.'
verses.36: Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve, in the register of Allah since the day He created the heavens and the earth, of which four are sacred — that is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them; but fight the polytheists collectively, as they fight you collectively, and know that Allah is with the righteous.
verses.37: The postponement of a sacred month is only an increase in disbelief, by which the disbelievers are led further astray — they permit it one year and forbid it another, to match the number Allah has made sacred, permitting what Allah has forbidden; the evil of their deeds has been made pleasing to them, and Allah does not guide a disbelieving people.
verses.38: O believers, what is the matter with you that, when it is said to you, 'Go forth in Allah's cause,' you cling heavily to the earth? Are you content with worldly life in place of the Hereafter? Yet the enjoyment of worldly life compared to the Hereafter is only a small thing.
verses.39: If you do not go forth, He will punish you with a painful punishment and put another people in your place, and you will not harm Him at all, for Allah is capable of all things.
verses.40: If you do not aid the Prophet, then Allah has already aided him when the disbelievers drove him out, one of two, when they were both in the cave and he said to his companion, 'Do not grieve, Allah is with us' — Allah sent down His tranquility upon him, supported him with soldiers you did not see, and made the word of the disbelievers the lowest, while the word of Allah remains the highest, for Allah is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.41: Go forth, whether light or heavy, and strive with your wealth and your lives in Allah's cause — that is better for you, if you only knew.
verses.42: Had it been an easy gain and a moderate journey, they would have followed you, but the distance seemed too far to them — yet they will swear by Allah, 'If we were able, we would surely have gone forth with you,' destroying themselves, for Allah knows they are truly liars.
verses.43: May Allah pardon you — why did you give them permission before it became clear to you who was truthful and who among them were liars?
verses.44: Those who believe in Allah and the Last Day would not ask your permission to be excused from striving with their wealth and their lives — Allah knows well those who are mindful of Him.
verses.45: Only those who do not believe in Allah and the Last Day ask your permission — their hearts are in doubt, so in their doubt they waver.
verses.46: Had they truly intended to go forth, they would have made preparations for it, but Allah disliked their being sent, so He held them back, and it was said, 'Sit with those who sit.'
verses.47: Had they gone forth with you, they would have added nothing to you but confusion, and they would have hastened around among you, seeking to sow discord between you — and among you are some who would have listened to them, for Allah knows well the wrongdoers.
verses.48: They had already sought discord earlier and had upset matters for you, until the truth came and the command of Allah appeared, even though they disliked it.
verses.49: Among them is one who says, 'Grant me exemption, and do not put me to trial' — but surely they have already fallen into trial, and Hell will truly encompass the disbelievers.
verses.50: If good befalls you, it distresses them, but if a misfortune strikes you, they say, 'We already took our precaution before,' and they turn away, rejoicing.
verses.51: Say: 'Nothing will befall us except what Allah has decreed for us — He is our protector,' and upon Allah let the believers rely.
verses.52: Say: 'Do you await for us anything except one of the two best outcomes? While we await for you that Allah will afflict you with punishment from Himself or at our hands — so wait, we too are waiting with you.'
verses.53: Say: 'Spend willingly or unwillingly — it will never be accepted from you, for you have been a defiantly disobedient people.'
verses.54: Nothing prevents their contributions from being accepted, except that they disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger, they do not come to prayer except lazily, and they do not spend except reluctantly.
verses.55: So let neither their wealth nor their children impress you — Allah only intends to punish them with these things in worldly life, and that their souls should depart while they are disbelievers.
verses.56: They swear by Allah that they are truly of you, when they are not of you, but they are a people who are afraid.
verses.57: If they could find a refuge, caves, or any place to enter, they would turn to it while hastening.
verses.58: Among them is one who criticizes you concerning charitable distribution — if they are given a share of it they are satisfied, but if they are not given a share, at once they become angry.
verses.59: If only they had been satisfied with what Allah and His Messenger gave them and said, 'Sufficient for us is Allah — Allah will give us of His bounty, and so will His Messenger, and to Allah we direct our hope.'
verses.60: Charity is only for the poor, the needy, those employed to administer it, those whose hearts are to be reconciled, for freeing captives, for those in debt, in Allah's cause, and for the stranded traveler — an obligation from Allah, and Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.61: Among them are those who harm the Prophet and say, 'He is only an ear' — say, 'An ear of goodness for you, believing in Allah, having faith in the believers, and a mercy for those among you who believe' — those who harm the Messenger of Allah will have a painful punishment.
verses.62: They swear to you by Allah to please you, but Allah and His Messenger are more deserving that they should please Him, if they are truly believers.
verses.63: Do they not know that whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger will have the fire of Hell, abiding therein forever? That is the great disgrace.
verses.64: The hypocrites fear that a surah may be revealed about them, informing them of what is in their hearts — say, 'Mock as you wish; Allah will surely bring forth what you fear.'
verses.65: If you asked them, they would surely say, 'We were only conversing and playing' — say, 'Was it Allah, His verses, and His Messenger that you were mocking?'
verses.66: Do not make excuses — you have disbelieved after your belief; if We pardon one group of you, We will punish another group, because they were criminals.
verses.67: The hypocrite men and women are of one another — they enjoin what is wrong, forbid what is right, and withhold their hands from spending; they have forgotten Allah, so He has forgotten them — the hypocrites are the defiantly disobedient.
verses.68: Allah has promised the hypocrite men, the hypocrite women, and the disbelievers the fire of Hell, abiding therein forever — it is sufficient for them; Allah has cursed them, and for them is a lasting punishment.
verses.69: You are like those before you — they were stronger than you in power, more abundant in wealth and children, and they enjoyed their portion, so you have enjoyed your portion just as those before you enjoyed theirs, and you have indulged in vain talk just as they indulged — those are the ones whose deeds are worthless in this world and the Hereafter, and they are the losers.
verses.70: Has the news not reached them of those before them — the people of Noah, Aad, Thamud, Abraham's people, the companions of Madyan, and the overturned cities? Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, and Allah would never have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves.
verses.71: The believing men and women are allies of one another — they enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, establish prayer, give charity, and obey Allah and His Messenger; those, Allah will have mercy upon them, for Allah is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.72: Allah has promised the believing men and women gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever, and beautiful dwellings in the gardens of eternity — but the greatest of all is Allah's approval, for that is the great attainment.
verses.73: O Prophet, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be harsh with them — their refuge is Hell, a truly wretched destination.
verses.74: They swear by Allah that they did not say it, but they certainly said the statement of disbelief, and disbelieved after their submission, and they resolved to do something they were unable to achieve — they resented nothing except that Allah and His Messenger had enriched them from His bounty; so if they repent, it is better for them, but if they turn away, Allah will punish them with a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter, and they will have no protector or helper on earth.
verses.75: Among them is one who made a covenant with Allah, saying, 'If He gives us from His bounty, we will surely give charity and be among the righteous.'
verses.76: But when He gave them from His bounty, they withheld it and turned away in aversion.
verses.77: So He penalized them with hypocrisy in their hearts until the Day they meet Him, because they broke their promise to Allah and because they used to lie.
verses.78: Do they not know that Allah knows their secrets and their private conversations, and that Allah is the Knower of all that is unseen?
verses.79: Those who criticize the believers who give charity willingly and mock those who find nothing to give except their utmost effort — Allah will mock them, and they will have a painful punishment.
verses.80: Whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them — even if you asked forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah would never forgive them, because they disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger, and Allah does not guide a defiantly disobedient people.
verses.81: Those who remained behind rejoiced in their staying behind the Messenger of Allah, and disliked striving with their wealth and their lives in Allah's cause, and said, 'Do not go forth in this heat' — say, 'The fire of Hell is more intense in heat,' if only they understood.
verses.82: So let them laugh a little and weep much as recompense for what they used to earn.
verses.83: If Allah returns you to a group of them and they ask your permission to go forth, say, 'You will never go forth with me, nor will you fight an enemy with me — you were satisfied with staying behind the first time, so stay with those who remain behind.'
verses.84: And do not pray at the funeral of any of them who dies, ever, and do not stand at his grave — indeed, they disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger, and died while defiantly disobedient.
verses.85: And do not let their wealth or their children impress you — Allah only intends to punish them by these things in this world, and that their souls should depart while they are disbelievers.
verses.86: When a surah is revealed, saying, 'Believe in Allah and strive alongside His Messenger,' those of wealth among them ask your permission and say, 'Leave us with those who stay behind.'
verses.87: They are content to be with those who stay behind, and their hearts have been sealed so that they do not understand.
verses.88: But the Messenger and those who believed with him strove with their wealth and their lives — those will have the good things, and they are the successful ones.
verses.89: Allah has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever — that is the great attainment.
verses.90: And those with excuses among the bedouins came to be excused, while those who lied to Allah and His Messenger simply stayed behind — a painful punishment will befall those among them who disbelieve.
verses.91: There is no blame upon the weak, the ill, or those who find nothing to spend, if they are true to Allah and His Messenger — there is no cause against the doers of good, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.92: Nor upon those who, when they came to you asking you to provide them mounts, and you said, 'I cannot find anything to mount you on,' turned back with their eyes overflowing with tears out of grief that they could find nothing to spend.
verses.93: The cause is only against those who ask your permission while being wealthy — they are content to be with those who stay behind, and Allah has sealed their hearts, so they do not know.
verses.94: They will make excuses to you when you return to them — say, 'Do not make excuses; we will never believe you; Allah has already informed us of your affairs, and Allah and His Messenger will observe your deeds, and then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, and He will inform you of what you used to do.'
verses.95: They will swear to you by Allah, when you return to them, so that you will leave them alone — so leave them alone, for they are truly impure, and their refuge is Hell as recompense for what they used to earn.
verses.96: They swear to you so that you will be satisfied with them — but even if you are satisfied with them, Allah is not satisfied with a defiantly disobedient people.
verses.97: The bedouins are the more severe in disbelief and hypocrisy, and more likely not to know the limits of what Allah has revealed to His Messenger — Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.98: Among the bedouins are some who take what they spend as a loss and await misfortune for you — upon them will be a misfortune of evil, for Allah is Hearing, Knowing.
verses.99: And among the bedouins are some who believe in Allah and the Last Day, and take what they spend as means of nearness to Allah and the prayers of the Messenger — indeed, it is a means of nearness for them; Allah will admit them into His mercy, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.100: The forerunners, first among the emigrants and the helpers, and those who followed them with excellence — Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him, and He has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever — that is the great attainment.
verses.101: Among the bedouins around you are hypocrites, and among the people of Medina as well, persisting in hypocrisy — you do not know them, but We know them, and We will punish them twice, then they will be returned to a great punishment.
verses.102: And others have acknowledged their sins — they mixed a righteous deed with another that was bad — perhaps Allah will turn to them in forgiveness, for Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.103: Take from their wealth a charity by which you purify them and cause them to increase, and pray for them — indeed, your prayer is a source of tranquility for them, and Allah is Hearing, Knowing.
verses.104: Do they not know that it is Allah who accepts repentance from His servants and receives charitable offerings, and that Allah is the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful?
verses.105: Say: 'Work, for Allah will see your deeds, as will His Messenger and the believers, and you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, and He will inform you of what you used to do.'
verses.106: And others are awaiting Allah's command — either He will punish them or He will turn to them in mercy, for Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.107: As for those who took a mosque out of harm, disbelief, causing division among the believers, and as a station for whoever had warred against Allah and His Messenger before, they will surely swear, 'We intended nothing but good,' but Allah bears witness that they are truly liars.
verses.108: Never stand for prayer therein — a mosque founded on righteousness from the first day is more deserving of your standing in it, wherein are men who love to purify themselves, and Allah loves those who purify themselves.
verses.109: Is one who founded his building on mindfulness of Allah and His approval better, or one who founded it on the edge of a crumbling cliff about to collapse with him into the fire of Hell? Allah does not guide a wrongdoing people.
verses.110: Their building which they built will never cease to be a source of doubt in their hearts, unless their hearts are cut to pieces — Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.111: Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their wealth in exchange for Paradise — they fight in Allah's cause, so they kill and are killed, a true promise binding upon Him in the Torah, the Gospel, and the Quran — and who is more faithful to his covenant than Allah? So rejoice in the exchange you have made, for that is the great attainment.
verses.112: Those who repent, worship, praise Allah, travel for His cause, bow, prostrate, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and maintain the limits set by Allah — and give good tidings to the believers.
verses.113: It is not for the Prophet and those who believe to ask forgiveness for the polytheists, even if they were relatives, once it has become clear to them that they are companions of Hellfire.
verses.114: Abraham's asking forgiveness for his father was only because of a promise he had made to him — but when it became clear to him that his father was an enemy of Allah, he disassociated himself from him, for Abraham was truly tenderhearted and forbearing.
verses.115: Allah would not let a people go astray after He has guided them until He makes clear to them what they should avoid — indeed, Allah has full knowledge of all things.
verses.116: Indeed, to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth — He gives life and causes death, and you have no protector or helper besides Allah.
verses.117: Allah has already turned in mercy to the Prophet, the emigrants, and the helpers who followed him in the time of hardship, after the hearts of a portion of them had almost deviated — then He turned to them in mercy, for He is Kind, Merciful to them.
verses.118: And He turned in mercy also to the three who were left behind, until, when the earth had become constrained for them despite its vastness, and their own souls had become constrained for them, and they were certain that there was no refuge from Allah except in Him — then He turned to them in mercy, so that they might repent, for Allah is the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.
verses.119: O believers, be mindful of Allah and be with the truthful.
verses.120: It was not fitting for the people of Medina and the bedouins around them to remain behind the Messenger of Allah, nor to prefer themselves over him — that is because no thirst, fatigue, or hunger touches them in Allah's cause, nor do they step in a way that enrages the disbelievers, nor do they inflict any harm upon an enemy, without a righteous deed being recorded for them — Allah does not let the reward of the doers of good be lost.
verses.121: Nor do they spend anything, small or large, nor cross a valley, without it being recorded for them, so that Allah may reward them for the best of what they used to do.
verses.122: It is not necessary for the believers to go forth all together — but why does not a party from every group among them go forth to obtain understanding in religion and warn their people when they return, so that they may be cautious?
verses.123: O believers, fight those disbelievers who are nearest to you and let them find harshness in you — know that Allah is with the righteous.
verses.124: Whenever a surah is revealed, some of them say, 'Which of you has this increased in faith?' As for those who believe, it has increased them in faith, and they rejoice.
verses.125: But as for those in whose hearts is disease, it has only increased them in wickedness added to their wickedness, and they die while they are disbelievers.
verses.126: Do they not see that they are tried once or twice every year? Yet they do not repent, nor do they take heed.
verses.127: Whenever a surah is revealed, they look at one another, saying, 'Does anyone see you?' Then they slip away — Allah has turned their hearts away, because they are a people who do not understand.
verses.128: There has certainly come to you a Messenger from among yourselves — grievous to him is what you suffer, full of concern for you, and to the believers he is kind and merciful.
verses.129: But if they turn away, say, 'Sufficient for me is Allah — there is no god except Him, upon Him I rely, and He is the Lord of the great Throne.' The surah closes, after a great deal of severity toward broken oaths and hidden hypocrisy, on this quiet return to reliance on Allah alone.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Ra — these are the verses of the wise Book.
verses.2: Is it strange to people that We revealed to a man among them, 'Warn mankind and give good tidings to those who believe that they will have a sure footing with their Lord'? The disbelievers say, 'This is truly a clear magician.'
verses.3: Indeed, your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself above the Throne, directing the affair — there is no intercessor except after His permission; that is Allah, your Lord, so worship Him — will you not remember?
verses.4: To Him is your return, all together — the promise of Allah is true; He begins creation, then repeats it, to justly reward those who believed and did righteous deeds, while those who disbelieved will have a drink of boiling water and a painful punishment for what they used to reject.
verses.5: It is He who made the sun a shining light and the moon a derived light, and determined for it phases, that you may know the number of years and the calculation — Allah did not create this except in truth, and He details the signs for a people who know.
verses.6: Indeed, in the alternation of night and day, and in what Allah has created in the heavens and the earth, are surely signs for a people who are mindful of Him.
verses.7: Those who do not hope for the meeting with Us and are satisfied with worldly life and content with it, and those who are heedless of Our signs.
verses.8: Those, their refuge is the Fire, because of what they used to earn.
verses.9: Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds — their Lord will guide them because of their faith; rivers will flow beneath them in gardens of pleasure.
verses.10: Their call therein will be, 'Exalted are You, O Allah,' and their greeting therein will be, 'Peace,' and the last of their call will be, 'Praise be to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.'
verses.11: If Allah were to hasten evil for people the way they hasten good, their term would already have been decided for them — but We leave those who do not hope for meeting Us to wander blindly in their transgression.
verses.12: When affliction touches a person, he calls upon Us, whether lying on his side, sitting, or standing, but when We remove his affliction from him, he continues on as if he had never called upon Us for an affliction that touched him — thus is made pleasing to the transgressors what they used to do.
verses.13: We have already destroyed generations before you when they wronged themselves, though their messengers came to them with clear proofs, and they were not going to believe — thus do We recompense the criminal people.
verses.14: Then We made you successors in the land after them, that We might observe how you would act.
verses.15: When Our verses are recited to them as clear evidence, those who do not hope for meeting Us say, 'Bring us a Quran other than this, or change it' — say, 'It is not for me to change it on my own accord — I only follow what is revealed to me; indeed, I fear, if I were to disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.'
verses.16: Say: 'Had Allah willed, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you, for I had already lived among you a lifetime before it — will you not reason?'
verses.17: Who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah or denies His signs? Indeed, the criminals will not succeed.
verses.18: They worship besides Allah what neither harms them nor benefits them, and they say, 'These are our intercessors with Allah' — say, 'Do you inform Allah of something He does not know in the heavens or on the earth? Exalted is He, and high above what they associate with Him.'
verses.19: Mankind was once one community, then they differed — had it not been for a word that had already gone forth from your Lord, the matter between them would have already been decided regarding that in which they differ.
verses.20: They say, 'Why has no sign been sent down to him from his Lord?' Say, 'The unseen belongs only to Allah, so wait — I too am among those who wait with you.'
verses.21: When We give people a taste of mercy after adversity has touched them, at once they scheme against Our signs — say, 'Allah is swifter in scheming' — indeed, Our messengers record whatever you scheme.
verses.22: It is He who enables you to travel on land and sea, until, when you are in ships and they sail with them by a good wind and they rejoice therein, a stormy wind reaches them and waves come upon them from every direction, and they assume they are surrounded, so they call upon Allah, sincere in their faith in Him, 'If You save us from this, we will surely be among the grateful.'
verses.23: Then when He has saved them, at once they commit injustice upon the earth without right — O people, your injustice is only against yourselves, an enjoyment of worldly life, then to Us is your return, and We will inform you of what you used to do.
verses.24: The example of worldly life is only like rain that We send down from the sky which mingles with the earth's produce, from which people and cattle eat, until, when the earth takes on its adornment and is beautified and its people believe they have full mastery over it, Our command comes to it by night or by day, and We make it a harvested field, as if it had not flourished the day before — thus do We detail the signs for a people who reflect.
verses.25: Allah invites to the Home of Peace and guides whom He wills to a straight path.
verses.26: For those who have done good is the best reward, and even more — no darkness or disgrace will cover their faces; those are the companions of Paradise, abiding therein forever.
verses.27: But those who have earned evil deeds — the recompense of an evil deed is its equivalent, and disgrace will cover them, with no protector for them against Allah, as if their faces were covered with pieces of the night, so dark it is — those are the companions of the Fire, abiding therein forever.
verses.28: On the Day We gather them all together, then We will say to those who associated partners, 'Stay in your place, you and your partners' — We will separate them, and their partners will say, 'It was not us you used to worship.'
verses.29: Allah is sufficient as a witness between us and you — indeed, we were unaware of your worship.
verses.30: There every soul will discover what it has done before, and they will be returned to Allah, their true Master, and what they used to invent will abandon them.
verses.31: Say: 'Who provides for you from the sky and the earth, or who controls hearing and sight, and who brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living, and who arranges every matter?' They will say, 'Allah' — so say, 'Will you not then be mindful of Him?'
verses.32: That is Allah, your true Lord — so what could be beyond truth except error? So how are you diverted?
verses.33: Thus the word of your Lord has come into effect upon those who defiantly disobeyed — that they will not believe.
verses.34: Say: 'Are there any among your partners who begins creation and then repeats it?' Say: 'Allah begins creation and then repeats it — so how are you deluded?'
verses.35: Say: 'Are there any among your partners who guides to the truth?' Say: 'Allah guides to the truth — is He who guides to the truth more worthy to be followed, or one who cannot guide unless he himself is guided? What is the matter with you? How do you judge?'
verses.36: Most of them follow nothing but assumption — indeed, assumption avails nothing against the truth; Allah is fully aware of what they do.
verses.37: This Quran could not have been produced by anyone other than Allah, but is a confirmation of what came before it, and a detailed explanation of the Book, about which there is no doubt, from the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.38: Or do they say, 'He has invented it'? Say, 'Then bring a surah like it, and call upon whoever you can besides Allah, if you are truthful.'
verses.39: Rather, they have denied what they could not encompass in knowledge, and whose true meaning has not yet come to them — thus did those before them also deny; see how the end of the wrongdoers was.
verses.40: Among them are some who believe in it, and among them are some who do not believe in it — your Lord knows best the corrupters.
verses.41: If they deny you, say, 'For me are my deeds, and for you are your deeds — you are disassociated from what I do, and I am disassociated from what you do.'
verses.42: Among them are some who listen to you, but can you make the deaf hear, even if they will not use reason?
verses.43: And among them are some who look at you, but can you guide the blind, even if they cannot see?
verses.44: Indeed, Allah does not wrong people at all, but it is people who wrong themselves.
verses.45: On the Day He gathers them, it will be as if they had not remained more than an hour of a day, recognizing one another — those will have truly lost who denied the meeting with Allah and were not guided.
verses.46: Whether We show you some of what We promise them or We take you in death, their return is to Us, and Allah is a witness over what they do.
verses.47: For every nation is a messenger — when their messenger comes, judgment is made between them in fairness, and they are not wronged.
verses.48: They say, 'When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?'
verses.49: Say: 'I have no power for myself over any harm or benefit except what Allah wills — for every nation is a term; when their term comes, they cannot delay it an hour, nor can they advance it.'
verses.50: Say: 'Consider — if His punishment came to you by night or by day, what would the criminals be in such a hurry for?'
verses.51: Then, is it that when it has actually occurred that you will believe in it? Now? And you were certainly trying to hasten it before.
verses.52: Then it will be said to those who wronged, 'Taste the punishment of eternity — are you recompensed except for what you used to earn?'
verses.53: They ask you to inform them, 'Is it true?' Say, 'Yes, by my Lord, it is truly true, and you will not be able to escape it.'
verses.54: If every soul that wronged had everything on earth, it would offer it in ransom — they will conceal their regret when they see the punishment, and judgment will be made between them in fairness, and they will not be wronged.
