verses.55: Indeed, to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth — indeed, the promise of Allah is true, but most of them do not know.
verses.56: He gives life and causes death, and to Him you will be returned.
verses.57: O mankind, there has come to you an instruction from your Lord, a healing for what is in the hearts, guidance, and mercy for the believers.
verses.58: Say: 'In the bounty of Allah and in His mercy — in that let them rejoice; it is better than what they accumulate.'
verses.59: Say: 'Have you seen what Allah has sent down for you of provision, some of which you have made unlawful and some lawful?' Say: 'Has Allah permitted you, or do you invent a lie about Allah?'
verses.60: What will those who invent lies about Allah think on the Day of Resurrection? Indeed, Allah is full of bounty to the people, but most of them are not grateful.
verses.61: In whatever matter you may be, and whatever portion you may recite of the Quran, and whatever deed you people may do, We are witness over you when you are engaged in it — not even an atom's weight escapes your Lord, whether on earth or in the heaven, nor anything smaller than that or greater, except that it is in a clear record.
verses.62: Indeed, the allies of Allah — no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.
verses.63: Those who believed and were ever mindful of Allah.
verses.64: For them are good tidings in the worldly life and in the Hereafter — there is no changing the words of Allah — that is the great attainment.
verses.65: Let not their speech grieve you — indeed, honor belongs to Allah entirely; He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.66: Unquestionably, to Allah belongs whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth — those who call upon partners besides Allah do not truly follow the partners; they follow nothing but assumption, and they do nothing but guess.
verses.67: It is He who made for you the night to rest in and the day to give sight — indeed, in that are signs for a people who listen.
verses.68: They say, 'Allah has taken a son' — exalted is He, He is Free of need; to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth; you have no authority for this — do you say about Allah what you do not know?
verses.69: Say: 'Indeed, those who invent a lie about Allah will not succeed.'
verses.70: A brief enjoyment in this world, then to Us is their return, then We will make them taste the severe punishment for their disbelief.
verses.71: Recite to them the news of Noah, when he said to his people, 'O my people, if my stay among you and my reminding you of Allah's signs has been hard on you, then upon Allah I rely — so resolve upon your plan and gather your partners, then do not let your plan confuse you, then carry it out against me and do not delay.'
verses.72: 'But if you turn away, I have not asked you for any reward — my reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be among the Muslims.'
verses.73: They denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We made them successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs — so see how the end of those who were warned was.
verses.74: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples, and they came to them with clear proofs, but they were not going to believe in what they had already denied before — thus do We seal the hearts of the transgressors.
verses.75: Then We sent after them Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his establishment with Our signs, but they were arrogant, and they were a criminal people.
verses.76: When the truth came to them from Us, they said, 'This is truly clear magic.'
verses.77: Moses said, 'Do you say this about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? But magicians will not succeed.'
verses.78: They said, 'Have you come to us to turn us away from what we found our fathers doing, and so that greatness in the land may belong to you two? We do not believe in you.'
verses.79: Pharaoh said, 'Bring me every learned magician.'
verses.80: When the magicians came, Moses said to them, 'Throw down whatever you are going to throw.'
verses.81: When they had thrown, Moses said, 'What you have brought is magic — Allah will surely make it worthless, for Allah does not amend the work of corrupters.'
verses.82: And Allah establishes the truth by His words, even if the criminals dislike it.
verses.83: None believed in Moses except a few descendants among his people, out of fear that Pharaoh and his establishment would persecute them — indeed, Pharaoh was truly a tyrant in the land, and he was truly among the transgressors.
verses.84: Moses said, 'O my people, if you truly believe in Allah, then rely upon Him, if you are truly Muslims.'
verses.85: They said, 'Upon Allah we rely — our Lord, do not make us a trial for the wrongdoing people.'
verses.86: 'And save us, by Your mercy, from the disbelieving people.'
verses.87: We inspired to Moses and his brother, 'Settle your people in Egypt in houses, and make your houses places of worship, and establish prayer, and give good tidings to the believers.'
verses.88: Moses said, 'Our Lord, You have given Pharaoh and his establishment splendor and wealth in worldly life, our Lord, that they may lead people astray from Your way — our Lord, obliterate their wealth and harden their hearts, so they will not believe until they see the painful punishment.'
verses.89: He said, 'Your supplication has been answered, so remain firm, and do not follow the way of those who do not know.'
verses.90: We took the Children of Israel across the sea, and Pharaoh and his soldiers pursued them in tyranny and enmity, until, when drowning overtook him, he said, 'I believe that there is no god except the one in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am among the Muslims.'
verses.91: 'Now? And you had already disobeyed before and were among the corrupters?'
verses.92: 'So today We will save your body, that you may be a sign for those after you — and indeed, many among the people are heedless of Our signs.'
verses.93: We settled the Children of Israel in a place of honor and provided them with good things — they did not differ until knowledge came to them; your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that in which they used to differ.
verses.94: If you are in doubt about what We have revealed to you, then ask those who have been reading the Scripture before you — the truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so do not be among the doubters.
verses.95: And do not be among those who deny the signs of Allah, lest you become among the losers.
verses.96: Indeed, those upon whom the word of your Lord has come into effect will not believe.
verses.97: Even if every sign should come to them, until they see the painful punishment.
verses.98: Then had there been a city that believed and its faith benefited it, except the people of Jonah — when they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in worldly life and gave them enjoyment for a time.
verses.99: Had your Lord willed, all those on earth would have believed, all of them together — so will you then compel people until they become believers?
verses.100: It is not for a soul to believe except by permission of Allah, and He places uncleanness upon those who do not use reason.
verses.101: Say: 'Look at what is in the heavens and the earth' — but no signs or warners avail people who do not believe.
verses.102: So do they wait for anything except the days like those of the people who passed on before them? Say, 'Then wait — I too am among those who wait with you.'
verses.103: Then We save Our messengers and those who believe — thus, as an obligation upon Us, We save the believers.
verses.104: Say: 'O people, if you are in doubt about my religion, I do not worship those you worship besides Allah, but I worship Allah, who will take your souls, and I have been commanded to be among the believers.'
verses.105: 'And direct your face toward the religion, inclining toward truth, and never be among the polytheists.'
verses.106: 'And do not call upon, besides Allah, that which neither benefits you nor harms you — for if you did, then you would surely be among the wrongdoers.'
verses.107: If Allah touches you with harm, there is no remover of it except Him, and if He intends good for you, there is no repeller of His bounty — He grants it to whom He wills of His servants, and He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
verses.108: Say: 'O people, the truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so whoever is guided is only guided for the benefit of his own soul, and whoever goes astray only goes astray to his own detriment — and I am not a guardian over you.'
verses.109: Follow what is revealed to you, and be patient until Allah issues His judgment, for He is the best of judges. The surah closes on the same quiet trust in Allah's timing that runs through Noah, Moses, and Jonah's stories that fill it.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Ra — a Book whose verses are made firm and then explained in detail, from One who is Wise and Acquainted with all things.
verses.2: Its message is simple: worship none but Allah — the Prophet is sent to you as both a warner and a bringer of good news.
verses.3: Seek forgiveness from your Lord and turn back to Him, and He will let you enjoy a good provision until an appointed time, giving His bounty to everyone who has earned it — but if you turn away, a great day's punishment is feared for you.
verses.4: To Allah is your return, and He has power over all things — nothing about that return is ever in doubt.
verses.5: They fold up their chests trying to hide their true feelings from Him, yet even when they cover themselves with their garments, He knows exactly what they conceal and what they reveal, for He knows what lies within every heart.
verses.6: There is no creature on earth whose provision is not owed by Allah — He knows where it dwells and where it will finally be laid to rest, all recorded in a clear register.
verses.7: He created the heavens and the earth in six days, His throne upon the water, in order to test which of you is best in deed — yet when told they will be raised after death, the disbelievers dismiss it as nothing but obvious magic.
verses.8: If Allah delays their punishment for a time, they ask mockingly what is holding it back — but on the day it finally arrives, it cannot be turned away, and what they used to mock will surround them completely.
verses.9: If Allah gives a person a taste of mercy and then withdraws it, that person turns despairing and ungrateful, as if the mercy had never been real at all.
verses.10: And if He gives that same person ease after hardship, he says, 'The evils have left me,' and becomes exultant and boastful — swinging between two extremes with nothing steady in between.
verses.11: Except for those who are patient and do righteous deeds — for them is forgiveness and a great reward, a steadiness that neither despair nor pride can shake.
verses.12: Perhaps you would leave out part of what is revealed to you, and your heart is distressed that they say, 'Why has no treasure been sent down to him, or an angel come with him?' You are only a warner, and Allah is the disposer of all affairs.
verses.13: Or do they say he invented it? Say: then bring ten surahs like it, invented, and call upon whoever you can besides Allah, if you are truthful.
verses.14: And if they do not respond to you, then know that it was sent down with the knowledge of Allah, and that there is no deity except Him — so will you submit?
verses.15: Whoever desires the life of this world and its adornment, their deeds will be repaid to them in full therein, with no diminishment.
verses.16: Those are the ones who will have nothing in the Hereafter but the Fire — everything they built will prove useless, and everything they used to do will be worthless.
verses.17: But is such a person like one who relies on clear evidence from his Lord, recited by a witness from Him, preceded by the Book of Moses as a guide and a mercy? Whoever disbelieves in it among the various factions is promised the Fire, so let there be no doubt about it.
verses.18: Who does more wrong than one who invents a lie about Allah? Such people will be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, 'These are the ones who lied about their Lord' — the curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers.
verses.19: Those who hinder others from the path of Allah and seek to make it appear crooked, and who disbelieve in the Hereafter.
verses.20: They will not cause failure on earth, nor do they have any protectors besides Allah — their punishment will be doubled, for they were unable to hear and could not see.
verses.21: Those are the ones who have lost themselves, and everything they used to invent has abandoned them.
verses.22: Without any doubt, it is they who will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.
verses.23: As for those who believe, do righteous deeds, and are humbly devoted to their Lord, they are the companions of Paradise, abiding therein eternally.
verses.24: The example of the two groups is like the blind and deaf compared to the seeing and hearing — are they equal in comparison? Will you not then take heed?
verses.25: Noah was sent to his people, saying, 'I am to you a clear warner.'
verses.26: 'Worship none but Allah — indeed I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.'
verses.27: The leaders of his people who disbelieved replied, 'We see you as nothing but a man like ourselves, and we see that only the lowest among us have followed you, without real reflection — we do not see in you any merit over us; rather, we think you are liars.'
verses.28: He said, 'My people, consider — if I am upon clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Himself which has been hidden from you, should we compel you to accept it while you are averse to it?'
verses.29: 'And my people, I do not ask you for wealth in return for it — my reward is only upon Allah, and I will not drive away those who have believed, for they will surely meet their Lord; but I see that you are a people acting out of ignorance.'
verses.30: 'And my people, who would help me against Allah if I drove them away? Will you not take heed?'
verses.31: 'I do not say to you that I possess the treasures of Allah, nor do I know the unseen, nor do I say that I am an angel, nor do I say of those your eyes look down upon that Allah will never grant them good — Allah knows best what is within their souls; indeed, I would then be among the wrongdoers.'
verses.32: They said, 'Noah, you have disputed with us and prolonged the dispute — so bring us what you threaten us with, if you are among the truthful.'
verses.33: He said, 'Allah will bring it to you if He wills, and you will not cause Him failure.'
verses.34: 'And my advice will not benefit you, even if I wish to advise you, if Allah intends to keep you astray — He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned.'
verses.35: Or do they say he invented it? Say: if I have invented it, then upon me is my crime, but I am innocent of what crimes you commit.
verses.36: It was revealed to Noah: 'None of your people will believe except those who have already believed, so do not despair over what they have been doing.'
verses.37: 'Construct the ark under Our observation and Our inspiration, and do not address Me concerning those who have wronged — indeed they are about to be drowned.'
verses.38: As he was constructing the ark, whenever the leaders of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him — he said, 'If you ridicule us, we will ridicule you just as you ridicule us.'
verses.39: 'You will soon know who will be visited by a punishment that disgraces him, and upon whom will descend a lasting punishment.'
verses.40: When the command came and water gushed forth from the earth, Noah was told, 'Load upon it a pair of every kind, and your family, except those against whom the decree has already been passed, and those who have believed' — but only a few had believed with him.
verses.41: He said, 'Embark in it; in the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage — indeed my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful.'
verses.42: The ark sailed with them through waves like mountains, and Noah called out to his son, who had isolated himself, 'My son, embark with us and do not be with the disbelievers.'
verses.43: He replied, 'I will take refuge on a mountain that will protect me from the water,' but Noah said, 'There is no protector today from the command of Allah, except for whom He gives mercy' — and the waves came between them, and he was among the drowned.
verses.44: It was said, 'Earth, swallow your water, and sky, withhold your rain' — the water subsided, the matter was concluded, and the ark came to rest on Mount Judi, and it was said, 'Away with the wrongdoing people.'
verses.45: Noah called out to his Lord and said, 'My Lord, my son is of my family, and indeed Your promise is true, and You are the most just of judges.'
verses.46: Allah said, 'Noah, he is not of your family — it is an unrighteous deed; so do not ask Me for that of which you have no knowledge, lest you be among the ignorant.'
verses.47: Noah said, 'My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking You for that which I have no knowledge of, and if You do not forgive me and have mercy upon me, I will be among the losers.'
verses.48: It was said, 'Noah, descend in peace from Us, and blessings upon you and upon nations descending from those with you — but other nations will enjoy pleasures for a time, then a painful punishment will touch them from Us.'
verses.49: This is from the news of the unseen, revealed to you — neither you nor your people knew it before this. So be patient, for the outcome belongs to the righteous.
verses.50: To the tribe of Aad was sent their brother Hud, who said, 'My people, worship none but Allah — you are but inventors of lies.'
verses.51: 'My people, I do not ask you for a reward for this — my reward is only upon the One who created me; will you not use reason?'
verses.52: 'My people, seek forgiveness from your Lord and repent to Him, and He will send rain upon you in abundance and increase you in strength added to your strength — and do not turn away as criminals.'
verses.53: They said, 'Hud, you have not brought us clear evidence, and we will not leave our gods based on your words alone, nor will we believe in you.'
verses.54: 'We say only that some of our gods have possessed you with evil.' He replied, 'I call Allah to witness, and you witness too, that I am free of what you associate with Him.'
verses.55: 'Besides Him, plot against me all together, then do not give me respite.'
verses.56: 'I have relied upon Allah, my Lord and your Lord — there is no creature He does not hold by its forelock; my Lord is upon a straight path.'
verses.57: 'If you turn away, then I have already conveyed to you what I was sent with — my Lord will replace you with another people, and you cannot harm Him at all, for my Lord is a guardian over all things.'
verses.58: When Our command came, We saved Hud and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and We saved them from a severe punishment.
verses.59: That was Aad — they denied the signs of their Lord, disobeyed His messengers, and followed the order of every obstinate tyrant.
verses.60: They were pursued by a curse in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection — Aad indeed disbelieved in their Lord, so away with Aad, the people of Hud.
verses.61: To the tribe of Thamud was sent their brother Salih, who said, 'My people, worship none but Allah — He produced you from the earth and settled you in it, so seek His forgiveness and turn to Him, for my Lord is near and responsive.'
verses.62: They said, 'Salih, you were among us one in whom hope was placed before this — do you forbid us to worship what our forefathers worshipped? Indeed we are in disquieting doubt about what you invite us to.'
verses.63: He said, 'My people, consider — if I am upon clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me a mercy from Himself, who would help me against Allah if I disobeyed Him? You would only increase me in loss.'
verses.64: 'My people, this she-camel of Allah is a sign for you, so leave her to eat on Allah's earth and do not touch her with harm, lest a near punishment seize you.'
verses.65: But they hamstrung her, so he said, 'Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days — this is a promise that will not be denied.'
verses.66: When Our command came, We saved Salih and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and from the disgrace of that day — indeed your Lord is the Powerful, the Exalted in Might.
verses.67: The thunderous blast seized the wrongdoers, and they became fallen face down in their homes by morning.
verses.68: As if they had never prospered therein — indeed Thamud disbelieved in their Lord, so away with Thamud.
verses.69: Our messengers came to Abraham with good tidings and said, 'Peace' — he replied, 'Peace,' and did not delay in bringing a roasted calf.
verses.70: When he saw their hands not reaching for it, he found them strange and felt fear from them — they said, 'Do not fear; we have been sent to the people of Lot.'
verses.71: His wife, standing nearby, laughed, and so We gave her good tidings of Isaac, and after Isaac, of Jacob.
verses.72: She said, 'Woe to me — shall I bear a child while I am an old woman and this husband of mine is an old man? This is truly a strange thing.'
verses.73: They said, 'Do you wonder at the command of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of this house — indeed He is Praiseworthy and Glorious.'
verses.74: When the fear left Abraham and the good tidings had reached him, he began to argue with Us concerning the people of Lot.
verses.75: Indeed Abraham was forbearing, imploring, and repentant.
verses.76: 'Abraham, turn away from this matter — the command of your Lord has already come, and a punishment that cannot be averted is coming to them.'
verses.77: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account and felt his strength was too small to protect them, and he said, 'This is a difficult day.'
verses.78: His people came to him, rushing toward him, as they had habitually done evil deeds before — he said, 'My people, here are my daughters, purer for you if you would marry, so fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests; is there not among you a right-minded man?'
verses.79: They said, 'You know well that we have no need or right to your daughters, and indeed you know what we want.'
verses.80: He said, 'If only I had strength against you, or could take refuge in a strong support.'
verses.81: The messengers said, 'Lot, we are messengers of your Lord — they will never reach you, so set out with your family during a portion of the night, and let none of you look back except your wife, for what will strike her will strike them; their appointed time is the morning — is the morning not near?'
verses.82: When Our command came, We turned the town upside down and rained upon it stones of baked clay, layered one upon another.
verses.83: Marked from your Lord, and it is not far from the wrongdoers.
verses.84: To Madyan was sent their brother Shu'ayb, who said, 'My people, worship none but Allah, and do not decrease in measure and weight — I see you in a good state, but I fear for you the punishment of an encompassing day.'
verses.85: 'My people, fulfill the measure and the weight with justice, and do not deprive people of their due things, and do not commit corruption on the earth, spreading it.'
verses.86: 'What remains lawfully with Allah is better for you, if you are believers, and I am not a guardian over you.'
verses.87: They said, 'Shu'ayb, does your prayer command you that we leave what our fathers worshipped, or that we not do with our wealth as we please? Indeed you are the forbearing, right-minded one.'
verses.88: He said, 'My people, consider — if I am upon clear evidence from my Lord, and He has provided me with a good provision from Himself — I do not intend to differ from you concerning what I forbid; I only intend reform as much as I am able, and my success is only through Allah — upon Him I rely, and to Him I turn.'
verses.89: 'My people, let not your dissent from me lead you to a crime like what befell the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih — and the people of Lot are not far from you.'
verses.90: 'Seek forgiveness from your Lord and repent to Him — my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate.'
verses.91: They said, 'Shu'ayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and indeed we see you among us as weak — were it not for your clan, we would have stoned you, for you are not powerful against us.'
verses.92: He said, 'My people, is my clan more dear to you than Allah, whom you have taken behind your backs, forgotten entirely? Indeed my Lord encompasses everything you do.'
verses.93: 'My people, work on your position — I too am working; you will soon know upon whom will come a punishment that disgraces him, and who is a liar — and watch, for I am watching with you.'
verses.94: When Our command came, We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us, and the thunderous blast seized the wrongdoers, and they became fallen face down in their homes.
verses.95: As if they had never prospered therein — away with Madyan, just as Thamud was made to vanish away.
verses.96: We sent Moses with Our signs and clear authority.
verses.97: To Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not one of right guidance.
verses.98: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire — wretched is the place to which they are led.
verses.99: They were pursued by a curse in this life and on the Day of Resurrection — wretched is the gift that was given them.
verses.100: That is from the news of the cities which We relate to you — some of them are still standing, and some have been mown down.
verses.101: We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves — their gods whom they called upon besides Allah did not benefit them at all when the command of your Lord came, and they increased them in nothing but ruin.
verses.102: Such is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the cities while they are committing wrong — indeed His seizure is painful and severe.
verses.103: In that is a sign for whoever fears the punishment of the Hereafter — that is a day for which mankind will be gathered, and that is a day that will be witnessed.
verses.104: We do not delay it except for a specified, numbered term.
verses.105: The day it comes, no soul will speak except by His permission — some among them will be wretched and some will be prosperous.
verses.106: As for those who are wretched, they will be in the Fire, wherein for them is sighing and wailing.
verses.107: They will abide therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord wills — indeed your Lord is an effecter of what He intends.
verses.108: As for those who are prosperous, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord wills — an uninterrupted gift.
verses.109: So do not be in doubt as to what these people worship — they worship not except as their fathers worshipped before, and We will surely pay them their share undiminished.
verses.110: We gave Moses the Scripture, and they differed over it — and if not for a word that had already preceded from your Lord, the matter would have been settled between them, though they are indeed in disquieting doubt about it.
verses.111: Your Lord will fully repay each of them for their deeds — indeed He is fully Acquainted with what they do.
verses.112: So remain steadfast as you have been commanded, and those who repent with you, and do not transgress — indeed He sees what you do.
verses.113: Do not incline toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire touch you — you have no protectors besides Allah, and then you would not be helped.
verses.114: Establish prayer at the two ends of the day and during the early hours of the night — indeed good deeds remove evil deeds; that is a reminder for those who remember.
verses.115: Be patient, for indeed Allah does not let the reward of the doers of good be lost.
verses.116: If only there had been among the generations before you people of virtue who forbade corruption on the earth, except for a few whom We saved — while the wrongdoers pursued what luxury they were given, and they became criminals.
verses.117: Your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reforming themselves.
verses.118: If your Lord had willed, He could have made mankind one single nation — yet they continue to differ.
verses.119: Except for those upon whom your Lord has bestowed mercy, and for that He created them — and the word of your Lord will be fulfilled: 'I will surely fill Hell with jinn and mankind, all together.'
verses.120: We relate to you from the news of the messengers that with which We make firm your heart — and there has come to you in this the truth, an admonition, and a reminder for the believers.
verses.121: Say to those who do not believe, 'Act according to your position — indeed we are acting too.'
verses.122: 'And wait — indeed we are waiting as well.'
verses.123: To Allah belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth, and to Him the whole matter will be returned — so worship Him and rely upon Him, for your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Ra — these are the verses of the clear Book.
verses.2: It has been sent down as an Arabic Qur'an, so that you might understand it.
verses.3: The best of stories is related through this revelation — before it, Joseph was among those unaware of what would unfold.
verses.4: Joseph said to his father, 'I saw eleven stars, and the sun and the moon, and I saw them prostrating to me.'
verses.5: His father said, 'My son, do not relate your vision to your brothers, lest they devise a plan against you — Satan is a clear enemy to mankind.'
verses.6: 'Thus your Lord will choose you and teach you the interpretation of events, and complete His favor upon you and upon the family of Jacob, as He completed it upon your forefathers Abraham and Isaac — indeed your Lord is Knowing and Wise.'
verses.7: In the story of Joseph and his brothers are certainly signs for those who ask.
verses.8: The brothers said, 'Joseph and his brother are dearer to our father than we are, though we are a group — indeed our father is in clear error.'
verses.9: 'Kill Joseph or cast him out to some land, so that your father's attention will be only for you, and after that you can be righteous people.'
verses.10: One of them said, 'Do not kill Joseph — throw him into the bottom of a well; some travelers will pick him up, if you must do something.'
verses.11: They said, 'Father, why do you not trust us with Joseph, when we are indeed sincere advisors to him?'
verses.12: 'Send him with us tomorrow to enjoy himself and play, and we will surely be his guardians.'
verses.13: He said, 'It truly grieves me that you should take him, and I fear a wolf would eat him while you are unaware of him.'
verses.14: They said, 'If a wolf were to eat him while we are a group of strength, then indeed we would be losers.'
verses.15: So they took him, and agreed to put him at the bottom of the well — and We revealed to Joseph, 'You will surely inform them of this deed of theirs, while they do not perceive it.'
verses.16: They came to their father in the evening, weeping.
verses.17: They said, 'Father, we went racing and left Joseph with our belongings, and a wolf ate him — but you will not believe us, even if we are truthful.'
verses.18: They brought his shirt stained with false blood, and their father said, 'Rather, your souls have made a matter seem alright to you — patience is beautiful, and Allah is the one sought for help against what you describe.'
verses.19: A caravan came and sent a water drawer, who let down his bucket and said, 'Good news — a boy!' They hid him as merchandise, but Allah was fully aware of what they did.
verses.20: They sold him for a low price, a few counted coins, showing so little interest in keeping him.
verses.21: The Egyptian who bought him said to his wife, 'Make his residence honorable — perhaps he will benefit us, or we will take him as a son.' Thus Joseph was established in the land, and We began teaching him the interpretation of events, for Allah is predominant over His affair, though most people do not know it.
verses.22: When he reached his full strength, We gave him judgment and knowledge — thus do We reward the doers of good.
verses.23: The woman in whose house he was living sought to seduce him, closing the doors and saying, 'Come, you.' He said, 'I seek refuge in Allah — he is my master, who has made good my residence; indeed wrongdoers do not succeed.'
verses.24: She certainly inclined toward him, and he would have inclined toward her had he not seen the proof of his Lord — thus did We turn away from him evil and immorality, for he was among Our sincere servants.
verses.25: They both raced to the door, and she tore his shirt from the back, and they found her husband at the door — she said, 'What is the recompense for one who intended evil for your wife, except imprisonment or a painful punishment?'
verses.26: Joseph said, 'It was she who sought to seduce me.' A witness from her family testified, 'If his shirt is torn from the front, she has told the truth and he is a liar.'
verses.27: 'But if his shirt is torn from the back, then she has lied, and he is truthful.'
verses.28: When her husband saw the shirt torn from the back, he said, 'Indeed, this is from the scheming of you women — your scheming is truly great.'
verses.29: 'Joseph, turn away from this — and you, wife, ask forgiveness for your sin, for you are indeed among the sinful.'
verses.30: Women in the city said, 'The governor's wife is seeking to seduce her slave boy — he has captivated her with love; indeed we see her in clear error.'
verses.31: When she heard of their scheming, she sent for them and prepared a banquet, giving each of them a knife, and said to Joseph, 'Come out before them.' When they saw him, they were amazed and cut their hands, saying, 'Allah be exalted — this is no human being; this is nothing but a noble angel.'
verses.32: She said, 'That is the one you blamed me concerning — I did seek to seduce him, but he refused; and if he does not do what I order him, he will surely be imprisoned and be among the humiliated.'
verses.33: Joseph said, 'My Lord, prison is more beloved to me than what they invite me to — and if You do not turn their plan away from me, I might incline toward them and be among the ignorant.'
verses.34: His Lord responded to him and turned their plan away from him — indeed He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.35: It then seemed appropriate to them, even after they had seen the signs of his innocence, to imprison him for a time.
verses.36: Two young men entered the prison with him — one said, 'I saw myself pressing wine,' and the other said, 'I saw myself carrying bread on my head, from which birds were eating — inform us of the interpretation, for we see you as a doer of good.'
verses.37: Joseph said, 'No food will come to nourish you but that I will inform you of its interpretation before it arrives — this is from what my Lord has taught me, for I have left the religion of a people who do not believe in Allah, and it is they who disbelieve in the Hereafter.'
verses.38: 'And I have followed the religion of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — it was not for us to attribute anything to Allah; that is from the favor of Allah upon us and upon mankind, but most of the people are not grateful.'
verses.39: 'My fellow prisoners, are separate lords better, or Allah, the One, the Prevailing?'
verses.40: 'You worship nothing besides Him except names you have named, you and your forefathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority — judgment belongs to none but Allah, who has commanded that you worship none but Him; that is the correct religion, but most people do not know it.'
verses.41: 'My fellow prisoners, as for one of you, he will give his master wine to drink, and as for the other, he will be crucified and birds will eat from his head — the matter about which you inquired has been decreed.'
verses.42: Joseph said to the one he thought would be released, 'Mention me before your master' — but Satan caused him to forget to mention Joseph to his master, so he remained in prison for several more years.
verses.43: The king said, 'I saw seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean cows, and seven green ears of grain and other dry ones — eminent ones, explain to me my vision, if you are able to interpret dreams.'
verses.44: They said, 'It is a confused set of dreams, and we are not knowledgeable in the interpretation of such dreams.'
verses.45: The one who had been released, remembering Joseph after a long time, said, 'I will inform you of its interpretation, so send me forth.'
verses.46: 'Joseph, man of truth, explain to us about seven fat cows eaten by seven lean cows, and seven green ears of grain and other dry ones, that I may return to the people so they may know.'
verses.47: He said, 'You will plant for seven consecutive years as usual, and what you harvest leave in its ears, except a little from which you will eat.'
verses.48: 'Then will come after that seven hard years which will consume what you saved for them, except a little that you will store.'
verses.49: 'Then will come after that a year in which the people will be given rain and in which they will press oil and wine.'
verses.50: The king said, 'Bring him to me.' But when the messenger came to him, Joseph said, 'Return to your master and ask him about the case of the women who cut their hands — indeed my Lord is fully aware of their scheme.'
verses.51: The king asked the women, 'What was your affair when you sought to seduce Joseph?' They said, 'Allah forbid — we know of no evil concerning him.' The governor's wife said, 'Now the truth has become clear — it was I who sought to seduce him, and he is indeed among the truthful.'
verses.52: This was so that my husband would know I did not betray him in his absence, and that Allah does not guide the plan of betrayers.
verses.53: 'And I do not acquit myself — the soul indeed inclines toward evil, except when my Lord bestows mercy; indeed my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful.'
verses.54: The king said, 'Bring him to me — I will select him exclusively for myself.' When he had spoken with him, he said, 'Indeed, today you are with us established firmly in position and trusted.'
verses.55: Joseph said, 'Appoint me over the storehouses of the land — I am indeed a knowledgeable guardian.'
verses.56: Thus We established Joseph in the land, settling in it wherever he willed — We reach with Our mercy whom We will, and We do not allow the reward of the doers of good to be lost.
verses.57: And the reward of the Hereafter is better for those who have believed and used to fear Allah.
verses.58: Joseph's brothers came and entered upon him, and he recognized them, while they were unaware of who he was.
verses.59: When he had furnished them with their provisions, he said, 'Bring me a brother of yours from your father — do you not see that I give full measure and that I am the best of hosts?'
verses.60: 'But if you do not bring him to me, there will be no measure for you from me, nor will you approach me.'
verses.61: They said, 'We will attempt to secure his release from his father — and we will surely do it.'
verses.62: Joseph said to his servants, 'Put their merchandise back into their saddlebags, so that they might recognize it when they return to their family — perhaps they will come back.'
verses.63: When they returned to their father, they said, 'Father, we have been denied further measure, so send our brother with us and we will get our measure — indeed we will be his guardian.'
verses.64: He said, 'Should I entrust you with him except as I entrusted you with his brother before? Allah is the best guardian, and He is the most merciful of the merciful.'
verses.65: When they opened their baggage, they found their merchandise returned to them — they said, 'Father, what more could we desire? This is our merchandise returned to us. We will get provision for our family, protect our brother, and add a camel's measure — that is an easy measure.'
verses.66: He said, 'I will not send him with you until you give me a promise before Allah that you will bring him back to me, unless you are surrounded.' When they had given him their promise, he said, 'Allah is a guardian over what we have said.'
verses.67: He also said, 'My sons, do not enter from one gate, but enter from separate gates — though I cannot avail you against anything from Allah at all; the decision is only for Allah, upon Him I have relied, and let those who would rely, rely upon Him.'
verses.68: When they entered as their father had commanded them, it did not avail them at all against Allah, except that it satisfied a need in the soul of Jacob, which he then carried out — indeed he was possessor of knowledge because of what We had taught him, but most people do not know.
verses.69: When they entered upon Joseph, he took his brother to himself and said, 'Indeed I am your brother, so do not despair over what they used to do.'
verses.70: When he had furnished them with their provisions, he placed the drinking cup in his brother's bag — then a caller called out, 'Travelers, you are indeed thieves.'
verses.71: They said, turning toward them, 'What is it you are missing?'
verses.72: They said, 'We are missing the measure of the king, and for whoever produces it is the load of a camel, and I am responsible for that.'
verses.73: They said, 'By Allah, you certainly know we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we are not thieves.'
verses.74: The Egyptians said, 'Then what would be the recompense for it, if you should be liars?'
verses.75: They said, 'The recompense is that whoever it is found in his bag, he himself will be the recompense — thus do we recompense the wrongdoers.'
verses.76: Joseph began with their bags before his brother's bag, then he extracted the cup from his brother's bag — thus did We plan for Joseph, for he would not have taken his brother according to the king's law, unless Allah willed it. We raise in degrees whom We will, and above every possessor of knowledge is one more knowing.
verses.77: They said, 'If he steals, a brother of his had stolen before.' But Joseph kept it within himself and did not reveal it to them — he said, 'You are in a worse position, and Allah is most knowing of what you describe.'
verses.78: They said, 'Eminent one, he has an elderly father, so take one of us in his place — indeed we see you as a doer of good.'
verses.79: He said, 'Allah forbid that we take except him with whom we found our property — indeed we would then be wrongdoers.'
verses.80: When they had despaired of him, they conferred privately — the eldest among them said, 'Do you not know that your father has taken a promise upon you before Allah, and before that how you failed concerning Joseph? I will not leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah judges for me, for He is the best of judges.'
verses.81: 'Return to your father and say, Father, your son has stolen, and we testify only to what we know, and we could not have guarded against the unseen.'
verses.82: 'Ask the town in which we were, and the caravan with which we came — indeed we are truthful.'
verses.83: Jacob said, 'Rather, your souls have made a matter seem alright to you — patience is beautiful; perhaps Allah will bring them all to me together, for He is the Knowing, the Wise.'
verses.84: He turned away from them and said, 'How great is my sorrow over Joseph,' and his eyes became white from grief, and he was suppressing his sorrow.
verses.85: They said, 'By Allah, you will continue to remember Joseph until you become fatally ill, or become of those who perish.'
verses.86: He said, 'I only complain of my grief and sorrow to Allah, and I know from Allah that which you do not know.'
verses.87: 'My sons, go and find out about Joseph and his brother, and do not despair of relief from Allah — indeed no one despairs of relief from Allah except a disbelieving people.'
verses.88: When they entered upon Joseph, they said, 'Eminent one, hardship has touched us and our family, and we have brought merchandise of poor quality, so give us full measure and be charitable to us — indeed Allah rewards the charitable.'
verses.89: He said, 'Do you know what you did with Joseph and his brother while you were ignorant?'
verses.90: They said, 'Are you indeed Joseph?' He said, 'I am Joseph, and this is my brother — Allah has certainly favored us; indeed whoever fears Allah and is patient, Allah does not allow the reward of the doers of good to be lost.'
verses.91: They said, 'By Allah, certainly Allah has preferred you over us, and we have indeed been sinners.'
verses.92: He said, 'No blame will there be upon you today — Allah will forgive you, and He is the most merciful of the merciful.'
verses.93: 'Take this shirt of mine and cast it over the face of my father, and he will become seeing again — and bring me your family, all together.'
verses.94: As the caravan departed, their father said, 'Indeed I find the scent of Joseph, if you would not think me weakened in mind.'
verses.95: They said, 'By Allah, you are indeed in your old error.'
verses.96: When the bearer of good tidings arrived, he cast the shirt over Jacob's face, and he became seeing again — he said, 'Did I not tell you that I know from Allah what you do not know?'
verses.97: They said, 'Father, ask for us forgiveness of our sins — we have indeed been sinners.'
verses.98: He said, 'I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord — indeed it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful.'
verses.99: When they entered upon Joseph, he took his parents to himself and said, 'Enter Egypt, if Allah wills, in security.'
verses.100: He raised his parents upon the throne, and they all fell down in prostration to him — he said, 'Father, this is the interpretation of my vision from before — my Lord has made it come true, and He was certainly good to me when He took me out of prison and brought you from the desert after Satan had sown discord between me and my brothers. Indeed my Lord is Gentle to whom He wills, and He is the Knowing, the Wise.'
verses.101: 'My Lord, You have given me a portion of sovereignty and taught me the interpretation of events — Originator of the heavens and the earth, You are my protector in this world and the Hereafter; take my soul in submission and join me with the righteous.'
verses.102: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you — you were not present with them when they determined their affair while they were scheming.
verses.103: Most of the people, even if you desire it eagerly, will not be believers.
verses.104: You do not ask them for it any reward — it is not except a reminder to the worlds.
verses.105: How many a sign within the heavens and the earth do they pass over, while they turn away from it!
verses.106: Most of them do not believe in Allah except while they associate others with Him.
verses.107: Do they feel secure from the coming of an overwhelming punishment from Allah, or that the Hour will come upon them suddenly while they do not perceive?
verses.108: Say: this is my way — I invite to Allah with insight, I and those who follow me; and glory be to Allah, and I am not among those who associate others with Him.
verses.109: We did not send before you except men to whom We revealed, from among the people of the towns — have they not traveled through the earth and observed the end of those before them? The home of the Hereafter is best for those who fear Allah; will they not then reason?
verses.110: Until, when the messengers despaired and were certain that they had been denied, Our help came to them, and whoever We willed was saved — and Our punishment cannot be repelled from the criminal people.
verses.111: In their stories was certainly a lesson for people of understanding — it was not a narration invented, but a confirmation of what came before it, and a detailed explanation of all things, and a guidance and mercy for a people who believe.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Mim, Ra — these are the verses of the Book; what has been revealed to you from your Lord is the truth, but most of the people do not believe.
verses.2: It is Allah who raised the heavens without pillars that you can see, then established Himself above the Throne, and subjected the sun and the moon, each running for a specified term — He directs the affair, explaining the signs in detail, that you might be certain of meeting your Lord.
verses.3: It is He who spread out the earth and placed within it firm mountains and rivers, and from all the fruits He made two mates — He covers the night with the day; indeed in that are signs for a people who reflect.
verses.4: On the earth are neighboring tracts, gardens of grapes, crops, and palm trees, some in clusters and some not, watered with the same water, yet some are made to excel others in taste — indeed in that are signs for a people who use reason.
verses.5: If you should wonder, then wondrous is their saying, 'When we are dust, will we truly be in a new creation?' Those are the ones who have disbelieved in their Lord, and those are the ones who will have shackles upon their necks, and those are the companions of the Fire, abiding therein eternally.
verses.6: They urge you to hasten the bad before the good, although punishing examples have already occurred before them — your Lord is full of forgiveness for the people despite their wrongdoing, yet your Lord is severe in penalty.
verses.7: Those who disbelieve say, 'Why has no sign been sent down to him from his Lord?' You are only a warner, and for every people there is a guide.
verses.8: Allah knows what every female carries and what the wombs fall short of or exceed — everything with Him is measured precisely.
verses.9: He is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Grand, the Exalted.
verses.10: It is the same to Him whether one of you conceals his speech or speaks it publicly, whether he hides by night or appears in public by day.
verses.11: For each person are angels succeeding one another, before him and behind him, protecting him by the command of Allah — indeed Allah will not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves, and if Allah intends for a people ill, there is no turning it back, nor do they have any protector besides Him.
verses.12: It is He who shows you lightning, causing fear and hope, and produces the heavy clouds.
verses.13: The thunder exalts His praise, and the angels do so too, in awe of Him — He sends thunderbolts and strikes with them whom He wills, while they dispute about Allah, though He is severe in assault.
verses.14: To Him alone belongs the true supplication — those they invoke besides Him do not respond to them with anything, except like one who stretches his hands toward water so it will reach his mouth, but it never does; the supplication of the disbelievers is nothing but error.
verses.15: To Allah prostrates whoever is in the heavens and earth, willingly or by compulsion, as do their shadows in the mornings and evenings.
verses.16: Say: who is Lord of the heavens and earth? Say: Allah. Say: have you then taken besides Him protectors who do not possess for themselves any benefit or harm? Say: is the blind equal to the seeing, or is darkness equal to light? Or have they ascribed partners to Allah who created similar to His creation, so that creation seemed alike to them? Say: Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is the One, the Prevailing.
verses.17: He sends down rain from the sky, and the valleys flow according to their capacity, and the torrent carries a rising foam — and from what they heat in the fire seeking ornament or utensil comes a similar foam. Thus does Allah present the truth and falsehood: the foam vanishes, discarded, while that which benefits the people remains on the earth. Thus does Allah present examples.
verses.18: For those who have responded to their Lord is the best reward — but those who did not respond to Him, if they had all that is in the earth entirely and the like of it with it, they would surely offer it as ransom; those will have the worst account, and their refuge is Hell, and wretched is that resting place.
verses.19: Is one who knows that what has been revealed to you from your Lord is the truth like one who is blind? Only they will remember, the possessors of understanding.
verses.20: Those who fulfill the covenant of Allah and do not break their pact.
verses.21: Those who join what Allah has ordered to be joined, and fear their Lord and are afraid of the evil of the account.
verses.22: Those who are patient, seeking the countenance of their Lord, establish prayer, spend from what We have provided them secretly and publicly, and repel evil with good — those will have the best outcome of the home.
verses.23: Gardens of eternity, which they will enter along with the righteous among their fathers, spouses, and descendants — and the angels will enter upon them from every gate.
verses.24: 'Peace be upon you for what you patiently endured — excellent indeed is the final home.'
verses.25: Those who break the covenant of Allah after its ratification, and sever that which Allah has ordered to be joined, and cause corruption on earth — those will have the curse, and they will have the worst home.
verses.26: Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts it — and they rejoice in the life of this world, while the life of this world compared to the Hereafter is nothing but a brief enjoyment.
verses.27: Those who disbelieve say, 'Why has no sign been sent down to him from his Lord?' Say: indeed Allah sends astray whom He wills and guides to Himself whoever turns back.
verses.28: Those who have believed and whose hearts are assured by the remembrance of Allah — unquestionably, by the remembrance of Allah hearts are assured.
verses.29: Those who have believed and done righteous deeds — a good state is theirs, and a good place of return.
verses.30: Thus have We sent you to a nation before which nations have passed away, so that you might recite to them what We revealed to you, while they disbelieve in the Most Merciful — say: He is my Lord, there is no deity except Him; upon Him I rely, and to Him is my return.
verses.31: If there were a Qur'an by which mountains were moved, or by which the earth were broken apart, or by which the dead were made to speak, the matter still belongs entirely to Allah — do the believers not know that if Allah had willed, He could have guided all of mankind together? Those who disbelieve will not cease to be struck by calamity for what they have done, or it will descend near their homes, until the promise of Allah comes — indeed Allah does not fail in His promise.
verses.32: Messengers before you were certainly ridiculed, but I gave respite to those who disbelieved, and then I seized them — how severe was My punishment.
verses.33: Then is He who stands over every soul, aware of everything it has earned, like other deities? Yet they ascribe partners to Allah — say: name them; do you inform Him of what He does not know in the earth, or is this merely superficial talk? Rather, their plotting is made attractive to the disbelievers, and they are averted from the right path — whoever Allah sends astray has no guide.
verses.34: For them is punishment in the life of this world, but the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe, and there is no protector for them against Allah.
verses.35: The example of Paradise promised to the righteous is that beneath it rivers flow, its food is lasting and its shade is continual — that is the end result of the righteous, while the end result of the disbelievers is the Fire.
verses.36: Those to whom We gave the Scripture rejoice in what has been revealed to you, while some of the factions deny part of it — say: I have only been commanded to worship Allah and not associate anything with Him; to Him I invite, and to Him is my return.
verses.37: Thus We have sent it down as an Arabic judgment — if you were to follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you would have no ally or protector against Allah.
verses.38: We sent messengers before you and gave them wives and descendants — it was not for any messenger to bring a sign except by the permission of Allah; for every term there is a decree recorded.
verses.39: Allah erases what He wills and confirms what He wills, and with Him is the Mother of the Book.
verses.40: Whether We show you some of what We promise them, or We cause you to die before that, upon you is only the conveyance of the message, and upon Us is the account.
verses.41: Do they not see that We are reducing the land from its borders? Allah judges, and there is no adjuster of His judgment — He is swift in account.
verses.42: Those before them also plotted, but the plan belongs entirely to Allah — He knows what every soul earns, and the disbelievers will know for whom is the final home.
verses.43: Those who disbelieve say, 'You are not a messenger.' Say: sufficient as a witness between me and you is Allah, and whoever has knowledge of the Scripture.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Ra — a Book We have revealed to you, so that you may bring mankind out of darkness into light, by permission of their Lord, to the path of the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy.
verses.2: Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth — woe to the disbelievers from a severe punishment.
verses.3: Those who prefer the life of this world over the Hereafter and avert people from the path of Allah, seeking to make it crooked, are in extreme error.
verses.4: We did not send any messenger except in the language of his people, so he could make things clear for them — Allah then sends astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.5: We certainly sent Moses with Our signs, saying, 'Bring your people out of darkness into light, and remind them of the days of Allah' — indeed in that are signs for everyone who is patient and grateful.
verses.6: Moses said to his people, 'Remember the favor of Allah upon you when He saved you from the people of Pharaoh, who were afflicting you with the worst torment, slaughtering your sons and letting your women live — in that was a great trial from your Lord.'
verses.7: 'And remember when your Lord proclaimed, if you are grateful, I will surely increase you, but if you deny, indeed My punishment is severe.'
verses.8: Moses said, 'If you disbelieve, you and everyone on earth entirely, indeed Allah is Free of need and Praiseworthy.'
verses.9: Has not the news come to you of those before you — the people of Noah, Aad, Thamud, and those after them, whom none knows but Allah? Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they returned their hands to their mouths and said, 'We disbelieve in that with which you have been sent, and we are indeed in disquieting doubt about that to which you invite us.'
verses.10: Their messengers said, 'Can there be doubt about Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth? He invites you so He may forgive you your sins and delay you until a specified term.' They said, 'You are but men like us who wish to avert us from what our fathers used to worship — so bring us a clear authority.'
verses.11: Their messengers said to them, 'We are only men like you, but Allah confers favor upon whom He wills of His servants — it is not for us to bring you an authority except by permission of Allah, and upon Allah let the believers rely.'
verses.12: 'And why should we not rely upon Allah, when He has guided us to our ways? We will surely be patient against whatever harm you cause us — and let those who would rely, rely upon Allah.'
verses.13: Those who disbelieved said to their messengers, 'We will surely drive you out of our land, or you must return to our religion.' So their Lord revealed to them, 'We will surely destroy the wrongdoers.'
verses.14: 'And We will surely settle you in the land after them — that is for whoever fears standing before Me and fears My threat.'
verses.15: They sought victory, and every obstinate tyrant was disappointed.
verses.16: Beyond that person is Hell, and he will be given a drink from purulent water.
verses.17: He will gulp it but will hardly be able to swallow it, and death will come to him from everywhere, but he will not die — beyond that is a harsh punishment.
verses.18: The example of those who disbelieve in their Lord is that their deeds are like ashes blown about intensely by wind on a stormy day — they have no power over anything they earned; that is extreme error.
verses.19: Do you not see that Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth? If He wills, He could remove you and bring a new creation.
verses.20: That is not difficult at all for Allah.
verses.21: They will come out to Allah all together, and the weak will say to those who were arrogant, 'Indeed we were your followers, so can you avail us anything against the punishment of Allah?' They will say, 'Had Allah guided us, we would have guided you — it is the same for us whether we panic or endure patiently; there is for us no place of escape.'
verses.22: Satan will say, when the matter has been concluded, 'Indeed Allah had promised you the promise of truth, and I promised you but I betrayed you — I had no authority over you except that I invited you and you responded to me; so do not blame me, but blame yourselves — I cannot save you, nor can you save me; indeed I deny your association of me with Allah before' — indeed for the wrongdoers is a painful punishment.
verses.23: Those who believed and did righteous deeds will be admitted into gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding eternally therein by permission of their Lord — their greeting therein will be 'Peace.'
verses.24: Do you not see how Allah presents an example — a good word is like a good tree, its root firmly fixed and its branches in the sky.
verses.25: It gives its fruit at every time by permission of its Lord — Allah presents examples for the people so they might remember.
verses.26: The example of a bad word is like a bad tree, uprooted from the surface of the earth, having no stability.
verses.27: Allah keeps firm those who believe with the firm word in worldly life and in the Hereafter, but Allah sends astray the wrongdoers — Allah does what He wills.
verses.28: Do you not see those who have exchanged the favor of Allah for disbelief, and led their people to the home of ruin.
verses.29: Hell — they will burn in it, and how wretched a settlement it is.
verses.30: They set up rivals to Allah to mislead others from His path — say: enjoy yourselves, for indeed your destination is the Fire.
verses.31: Tell My servants who have believed to establish prayer and spend from what We have provided them, secretly and publicly, before a day comes in which there will be no exchange nor any friendship.
verses.32: Allah is the one who created the heavens and the earth, sent down rain from the sky and produced with it fruits as provision for you, and subjected for you the ships to sail through the sea by His command, and subjected for you the rivers.
verses.33: He subjected for you the sun and the moon, continuously running, and subjected for you the night and the day.
verses.34: He gave you from all that you asked Him — if you should count the favor of Allah, you would not be able to enumerate it; indeed mankind is truly unjust and ungrateful.
verses.35: Abraham said, 'My Lord, make this city secure, and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.'
verses.36: 'My Lord, indeed they have led astray many among mankind — so whoever follows me is of me, and whoever disobeys me, indeed You are Forgiving and Merciful.'
verses.37: 'Our Lord, I have settled some of my descendants in an uncultivated valley near Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may establish prayer — so make hearts among the people incline toward them, and provide them with fruits so they might be grateful.'
verses.38: 'Our Lord, indeed You know what we conceal and what we declare — nothing is hidden from Allah on earth or in the heaven.'
verses.39: 'Praise be to Allah, who has granted me in old age Ishmael and Isaac — indeed my Lord is the Hearer of supplication.'
verses.40: 'My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and from my descendants — our Lord, and accept my supplication.'
verses.41: 'Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and the believers, the Day the account is established.'
verses.42: Never think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do — He only delays them for a day when eyes will stare in horror.
verses.43: Racing ahead, their heads raised up, their gaze not returning to them, and their hearts empty.
verses.44: Warn the people of a day when the punishment will come to them, and those who did wrong will say, 'Our Lord, delay us for a short term — we will answer Your call and follow the messengers.' 'Had you not sworn before that there would be no cessation for you?'
verses.45: 'And you settled in the homes of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, and We presented for you examples.'
verses.46: They had already plotted their plan, but with Allah is their plan, even if their plan was such that mountains would be moved by it.
verses.47: Never think that Allah will fail in His promise to His messengers — indeed Allah is Exalted in Might, the Owner of Retribution.
verses.48: On the Day the earth will be replaced by another earth, and the heavens as well, and they will come forth before Allah, the One, the Prevailing.
verses.49: You will see the criminals that Day bound together in shackles.
verses.50: Their garments will be of tar, and the Fire will cover their faces.
verses.51: This is so Allah may repay every soul for what it earned — indeed Allah is swift in account.
verses.52: This is a message for the people, that they may be warned thereby, and that they may know that He is but one deity, and that those of understanding may remember.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Ra — these are the verses of the Book and a clear Qur'an.
verses.2: Perhaps those who disbelieve will wish they had been Muslims.
verses.3: Leave them to eat and enjoy themselves and be diverted by false hope, for they are going to know.
verses.4: We have not destroyed any city but that it had a known decree.
verses.5: No nation can precede its term, nor can it delay it.
verses.6: They said, 'You to whom the message has been revealed, you are indeed mad.'
verses.7: 'Why do you not bring us the angels, if you should be among the truthful?'
verses.8: We do not send down the angels except with truth, and then the disbelievers would not be reprieved.
verses.9: Indeed it is We who sent down the reminder, and indeed it is We who will preserve it.
verses.10: We have already sent messengers before you among the sects of former peoples.
verses.11: No messenger came to them except that they used to ridicule him.
verses.12: Thus do We insert denial into the hearts of the criminals.
verses.13: They will not believe in it, while the way of the former peoples has already passed before them as example.
verses.14: Even if We opened to them a gate into heaven and they continued to ascend through it.
verses.15: They would say, 'Our eyes have only been dazzled — rather, we are a people who have been bewitched.'
verses.16: We have placed within the heaven great stars and have beautified it for the observers.
verses.17: And We have protected it from every accursed devil.
verses.18: Except one who steals a hearing, and he is pursued by a clear flame.
verses.19: The earth We have spread out and placed within it firm mountains and caused to grow therein all things in balanced measure.
verses.20: We made for you therein means of living, and for those whom you do not provide.
verses.21: There is nothing but that with Us are its depositories, and We do not send it down except according to a known measure.
verses.22: We send the fertilizing winds and cause water to descend from the sky, and provide you with drink, and you are not the ones who store it.
verses.23: Indeed it is We who give life and cause death, and We are the inheritors.
verses.24: We certainly know those of you who hasten forward, and We certainly know those who remain behind.
verses.25: Indeed your Lord will gather them, for He is Wise and Knowing.
verses.26: We created man from clay, changed and formed, altered from mud.
verses.27: And the jinn We created before, from a scorching, smokeless fire.
verses.28: Your Lord said to the angels, 'I am creating a human being from clay, changed and formed.'
verses.29: 'When I have fashioned him and breathed into him of My spirit, then fall down to him in prostration.'
verses.30: So the angels prostrated, all of them together.
verses.31: Except Iblis — he refused to be among those who prostrated.
verses.32: Allah said, 'Iblis, what is your reason for not being among those who prostrated?'
verses.33: He said, 'I would not prostrate to a human whom You created from clay, changed and formed.'
verses.34: Allah said, 'Then depart from it, for indeed you are expelled.'
verses.35: 'And indeed upon you is the curse until the Day of Recompense.'
verses.36: He said, 'My Lord, then reprieve me until the Day they are raised.'
verses.37: Allah said, 'You are indeed of those reprieved.'
verses.38: 'Until the Day of the known time.'
verses.39: Iblis said, 'My Lord, because You sent me astray, I will surely make wrong appealing to them on earth, and I will mislead them all.'
verses.40: 'Except, among them, Your sincere servants.'
verses.41: Allah said, 'This is a path leading straight to Me.'
verses.42: 'Indeed My servants — no authority will you have over them, except whoever follows you of the deviators.'
verses.43: 'And indeed Hell is the promised place for all of them.'
verses.44: It has seven gates, and each gate has an assigned portion of them.
verses.45: Indeed the righteous will be within gardens and springs.
verses.46: 'Enter it in peace, safe and secure.'
verses.47: We will have removed whatever is within their breasts of resentment, as brothers, on thrones facing one another.
verses.48: No fatigue will touch them therein, nor will they be expelled from it.
verses.49: Inform My servants that indeed I am the Forgiving, the Merciful.
verses.50: And that My punishment is indeed the painful punishment.
verses.51: Inform them about the guests of Abraham.
verses.52: When they entered upon him and said, 'Peace,' he said, 'Indeed we are afraid of you.'
verses.53: They said, 'Do not be afraid — we give you good tidings of a knowledgeable boy.'
verses.54: He said, 'Do you give me good tidings although old age has touched me? Then of what do you give good tidings?'
verses.55: They said, 'We have given you good tidings in truth, so do not be among the despairing.'
verses.56: He said, 'Who despairs of the mercy of his Lord except those astray?'
verses.57: He said, 'What then is your business, messengers?'
verses.58: They said, 'Indeed we have been sent to a criminal people.'
verses.59: 'Except the family of Lot — indeed we will save them all.'
verses.60: 'Except his wife — We have decreed that she is truly of those who remain behind.'
verses.61: When the messengers came to the family of Lot.
verses.62: He said, 'Indeed you are people unknown to me.'
verses.63: They said, 'Rather, we have come to you with that about which they were disputing.'
verses.64: 'And we have come to you with truth, and indeed we are truthful.'
verses.65: 'So set out with your family during a portion of the night and follow behind them, and let not any among you look back, and continue on to where you are commanded.'
verses.66: We conveyed to him that matter — that the roots of those people would be cut off by morning.
verses.67: The people of the city came rejoicing at the news.
verses.68: Lot said, 'Indeed these are my guests, so do not disgrace me.'
verses.69: 'And fear Allah and do not shame me.'
verses.70: They said, 'Have we not forbidden you from the worlds?'
verses.71: He said, 'These are my daughters, if you would do such a thing.'
verses.72: By your life, they were truly lost in their bewilderment, wandering blindly.
verses.73: So the blast seized them at sunrise.
verses.74: We made the highest part of the city its lowest, and We rained upon them stones of baked clay.
verses.75: Indeed in that are signs for those who discern.
verses.76: It still stands on an established road.
verses.77: Indeed in that is a sign for the believers.
verses.78: The companions of the thicket, too, were certainly wrongdoers.
verses.79: So We took retribution from them, and both cities are on a clear register.
verses.80: The companions of Al-Hijr also denied the messengers.
verses.81: We gave them Our signs, but they were turning away from them.
verses.82: They used to carve homes into the mountains, feeling secure.
verses.83: So the blast seized them in the morning.
verses.84: And nothing availed them of what they used to earn.
verses.85: We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except in truth — and indeed the Hour is coming, so forgive with gracious forgiveness.
verses.86: Indeed your Lord is the Creator, the Knowing.
verses.87: We have certainly given you seven of the oft-repeated verses and the great Qur'an.
verses.88: Do not extend your eyes toward that which We have given pairs of them to enjoy, and do not grieve over them, and lower your wing to the believers.
verses.89: And say, 'Indeed I am the clear warner.'
verses.90: Just as We sent down upon the dividers.
verses.91: Who have made the Qur'an into portions.
verses.92: So by your Lord, We will surely ask them all.
verses.93: About what they used to do.
verses.94: So proclaim what you have been commanded and turn away from the polytheists.
verses.95: Indeed We are sufficient for you against the mockers.
verses.96: Those who set up another deity with Allah — they are going to know.
verses.97: We already know that your breast is distressed by what they say.
verses.98: So glorify the praise of your Lord and be among those who prostrate to Him.
verses.99: And worship your Lord until certainty, meaning death, comes to you.

verses.1: The command of Allah is coming, so do not seek to hasten it — exalted is He, and high above what they associate with Him.
verses.2: He sends down the angels with the inspiration by His command to whom He wills of His servants, saying, 'Warn that there is no deity except Me, so fear Me.'
verses.3: He created the heavens and earth in truth — exalted is He above what they associate with Him.
verses.4: He created man from a sperm-drop, and behold, he is a clear disputant.
verses.5: And cattle He has created for you, in them is warmth and benefits, and from them you eat.
verses.6: There is beauty in them for you when you bring them in the evening and when you send them out to pasture.
verses.7: They carry your loads to a land you could not otherwise reach except with great hardship to yourselves — indeed your Lord is Kind and Merciful.
verses.8: And horses, mules, and donkeys He created for you to ride and as an adornment — He creates what you do not know.
verses.9: Upon Allah is the direction of the right way, but among the ways are some deviating — had He willed, He would have guided you all.
verses.10: It is He who sends down water from the sky, from which you drink, and from which grows the vegetation in which you pasture your livestock.
verses.11: He causes crops to grow for you thereby, and olives, palm trees, grapevines, and all kinds of fruit — indeed in that is a sign for a people who reflect.
verses.12: He has subjected for you the night and day, and the sun and moon, and the stars are subjected by His command — indeed in that are signs for a people who reason.
verses.13: Whatever He has multiplied for you on the earth of varying colors, in that is a sign for a people who remember.
verses.14: It is He who subjected the sea for you to eat from it tender meat and to extract ornaments you wear, and you see the ships cleaving through it so you may seek His bounty, and that you might be grateful.
verses.15: He cast into the earth firm mountains so it would not shift with you, and rivers and roads that you might be guided.
verses.16: And landmarks — and by the stars, they are guided.
verses.17: Then is He who creates like one who does not create? Will you not then remember?
verses.18: And if you should count the favors of Allah, you could not enumerate them — indeed Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.19: And Allah knows what you conceal and what you declare.
verses.20: Those they invoke besides Allah create nothing, and they are themselves created.
verses.21: They are dead, not alive, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected.
verses.22: Your God is one God — for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, their hearts refuse to know, and they are arrogant.
verses.23: Undoubtedly Allah knows what they conceal and what they declare — indeed He does not love the arrogant.
verses.24: When it is said to them, 'What has your Lord sent down?' they say, 'Legends of former peoples.'
verses.25: They will bear their own burdens in full on the Day of Resurrection, and some of the burdens of those they misled without knowledge — how evil is that which they bear.
verses.26: Those before them plotted, so Allah came at their building from the foundations, and the roof fell upon them from above, and the punishment came to them from where they did not perceive.
verses.27: On the Day of Resurrection He will disgrace them and say, 'Where are My partners for whose sake you used to oppose?' Those given knowledge will say, 'Indeed disgrace today and evil are upon the disbelievers.'
verses.28: Those the angels take in death while wronging themselves, and then they offer submission, saying, 'We were not doing any evil.' But Allah is fully aware of what they used to do.
verses.29: 'So enter the gates of Hell, abiding therein — how evil is the residence of the arrogant.'
verses.30: It is said to the righteous, 'What has your Lord sent down?' They say, 'Good' — for those who do good in this world is good, and the home of the Hereafter is even better, and excellent indeed is the home of the righteous.
verses.31: Gardens of eternity, which they will enter, beneath which rivers flow, in which they will have whatever they wish — thus does Allah reward the righteous.
verses.32: Those the angels take in death while they are good, saying, 'Peace be upon you, enter Paradise for what you used to do.'
verses.33: Do they await only that the angels should come to them, or that the command of your Lord should come? Thus did those before them, and Allah did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.
verses.34: So the evils of what they did struck them, and they were surrounded by what they used to mock.
verses.35: Those who associated others with Allah say, 'Had Allah willed, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him, neither we nor our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything without His command.' Thus did those before them do — is there any duty on the messengers except clear conveyance?
