verses.36: We certainly sent to every nation a messenger, saying, 'Worship Allah and avoid false deities' — among them were some Allah guided, and among them were some upon whom error was justified; so travel through the earth and observe how was the end of the deniers.
verses.37: Even if you are eager for their guidance, indeed Allah does not guide whom He sends astray, and they will have no helpers.
verses.38: They swore by Allah their strongest oaths that Allah would not resurrect one who dies — yes indeed, it is a true promise binding upon Him, but most people do not know.
verses.39: This is so He may make clear to them that in which they differ, and so those who disbelieved may know that they were liars.
verses.40: Our word to a thing when We intend it is only that We say to it 'Be,' and it is.
verses.41: Those who emigrated for the cause of Allah after they had been wronged, We will surely settle them in this world in a good place, but the reward of the Hereafter is greater, if only they knew.
verses.42: Those who are patient and upon their Lord rely.
verses.43: We did not send before you except men to whom We revealed — so ask the people of the message if you do not know.
verses.44: With clear proofs and scriptures — and We revealed to you the message that you may make clear to the people what was sent down to them, and that they might reflect.
verses.45: Do those who plot evil deeds feel secure that Allah will not cause the earth to swallow them, or that the punishment will not come upon them from where they do not perceive?
verses.46: Or that He would not seize them during their movement about, and they could not escape?
verses.47: Or that He would not seize them gradually amid their fear — indeed your Lord is Kind and Merciful.
verses.48: Have they not looked at what Allah has created, of anything, how its shadows incline to the right and to the left, prostrating to Allah while they are humbled?
verses.49: To Allah prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth of creatures, and the angels, and they are not arrogant.
verses.50: They fear their Lord above them, and they do what they are commanded.
verses.51: Allah has said, 'Do not take two deities — He is but one deity, so fear only Me.'
verses.52: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and His is the constant obedience — will you then fear other than Allah?
verses.53: Whatever you have of favor is from Allah — then when adversity touches you, it is to Him you cry for help.
verses.54: Then when He removes the adversity from you, at once a party of you associates others with their Lord.
verses.55: So as to deny what We have given them — enjoy yourselves, for you are going to know.
verses.56: They assign a portion of what We have provided them to what they do not even know — by Allah, you will surely be questioned about what you used to invent.
verses.57: And they assign to Allah daughters — exalted is He — while for themselves is what they desire.
verses.58: When one of them is given news of a female, his face becomes dark, and he suppresses grief.
verses.59: He hides himself from the people because of the ill of what he has been informed — should he keep it in humiliation or bury it in the earth? How evil is what they judge.
verses.60: For those who do not believe in the Hereafter is the description of evil, while for Allah is the highest attribute — He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.61: If Allah were to seize the people for their wrongdoing, He would not leave upon the earth any creature — but He delays them for a specified term, and when their term comes, they cannot delay it an hour, nor can they advance it.
verses.62: They attribute to Allah that which they dislike for themselves, and their tongues assert the lie that they will have the best — undoubtedly for them is the Fire, and they will be hastened into it, left behind.
verses.63: By Allah, We certainly sent messengers before you to nations, but Satan made their deeds attractive to them — he is their ally today, and they will have a painful punishment.
verses.64: We have not sent down to you the Book except that you may make clear to them what they differ over, and as guidance and mercy for a people who believe.
verses.65: Allah sends down rain from the sky, giving life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness — indeed in that is a sign for a people who listen.
verses.66: In cattle too is a lesson for you — We give you to drink from what is within their bellies, between excretion and blood, pure milk, palatable to drinkers.
verses.67: From the fruits of palm trees and grapevines you take intoxicant and good provision — indeed in that is a sign for a people who use reason.
verses.68: Your Lord revealed to the bee, 'Take for yourself among the mountains, trees, and what people construct.'
verses.69: 'Then eat from all the fruits and follow the ways of your Lord made easy for you' — there emerges from their bellies a drink of varying colors, in which there is healing for people; indeed in that is a sign for a people who think.
verses.70: Allah created you, then He causes you to die, and among you is he who is returned to the most feeble age, so that after having known he knows nothing — indeed Allah is Knowing and Competent.
verses.71: Allah has favored some of you over others in provision, and those favored would not give their provision to those bound by their right hand so they would be equal — then it is the favor of Allah that they deny.
verses.72: And Allah has made for you from yourselves mates, and has made for you from your mates sons and grandchildren, and has provided you with good things — then in falsehood do they believe while the favor of Allah they deny.
verses.73: They worship besides Allah that which does not possess for them any provision from the heavens or the earth at all, nor are they able to do so.
verses.74: So do not strike similitudes for Allah — indeed Allah knows and you do not know.
verses.75: Allah strikes a similitude — a slave owned by another with no power over anything, and one whom We have provided a good provision from Us, so he spends from it secretly and publicly — are they equal? Praise be to Allah, but most of them do not know.
verses.76: Allah strikes a similitude of two men, one of them dumb, having no power over anything, and he is a burden to his guardian — wherever he directs him, he does not bring any good; is he equal to one who commands justice while he is on a straight path?
verses.77: To Allah belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth — the matter of the Hour is not except as a glance of the eye, or even nearer, for indeed Allah is over all things competent.
verses.78: Allah has brought you out of the wombs of your mothers not knowing anything, and He made for you hearing, sight, and hearts, that you might be grateful.
verses.79: Do they not see the birds controlled in the atmosphere of the sky? None holds them except Allah — indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.
verses.80: Allah has made for you from your homes a place of rest, and made for you from the hides of animals tents, and made from their wool, fur, and hair furnishings and enjoyment for a time.
verses.81: Allah has made for you shade from what He has created, and made for you shelters in the mountains, and made for you garments that protect you from the heat and garments that protect you in battle — thus does He complete His favor upon you, so that you might submit.
verses.82: But if they turn away, then upon you is only the clear conveyance.
verses.83: They recognize the favor of Allah, then they deny it, and most of them are disbelievers.
verses.84: The Day We resurrect from every nation a witness, then permission will not be given to those who disbelieved, nor will they be asked to seek pleasure.
verses.85: When those who did wrong see the punishment, it will not be lightened for them, nor will they be given respite.
verses.86: When those who associated others with Allah see their partners, they will say, 'Our Lord, these are our partners whom we used to invoke besides You,' but they will throw back at them the word, 'Indeed you are liars.'
verses.87: They will offer to Allah that Day their submission, and lost from them is what they used to invent.
verses.88: Those who disbelieved and averted others from the path of Allah, We increased them in punishment upon punishment for what corruption they used to cause.
verses.89: The Day We resurrect from every nation a witness against them from themselves, and We bring you as a witness against your people — We have sent down to you the Book as clarification for everything, and as guidance, mercy, and good tidings for the Muslims.
verses.90: Indeed Allah orders justice, good conduct, and giving to relatives, and He forbids immorality, bad conduct, and oppression — He admonishes you that you might remember.
verses.91: Fulfill the covenant of Allah when you have taken it, and do not break oaths after their confirmation while you have made Allah a surety over you — indeed Allah knows what you do.
verses.92: Do not be like the woman who undoes her spun thread after it has become strong, by taking your oaths as deception between you because one nation is more numerous than another — Allah only tests you thereby, and He will surely make clear to you on the Day of Resurrection that in which you differed.
verses.93: Had Allah willed, He could have made you one nation, but He sends astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills — you will surely be questioned about what you used to do.
verses.94: Do not take your oaths as deception between you, lest a foot slip after it was firmly planted, and you taste evil for having averted from the path of Allah, and you would have a great punishment.
verses.95: Do not exchange the covenant of Allah for a small price — indeed what is with Allah is better for you, if only you knew.
verses.96: What you have depletes, but what is with Allah remains — We will surely give those who were patient their reward according to the best of what they used to do.
verses.97: Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while being a believer, We will surely cause him to live a good life, and We will surely give them their reward according to the best of what they used to do.
verses.98: When you recite the Qur'an, seek refuge in Allah from Satan, the expelled.
verses.99: Indeed he has no authority over those who believe and rely upon their Lord.
verses.100: His authority is only over those who take him as an ally and who associate others with Allah.
verses.101: When We substitute a verse in place of a verse, and Allah is most knowing of what He sends down, they say, 'You are but an inventor' — rather, most of them do not know.
verses.102: Say, the Holy Spirit has brought it down from your Lord in truth, to make firm those who believe, as guidance and good tidings to the Muslims.
verses.103: We already know that they say, 'It is only a human being who teaches him' — the tongue of the one they refer to is foreign, while this is a clear Arabic language.
verses.104: Those who do not believe in the verses of Allah, Allah will not guide them, and for them is a painful punishment.
verses.105: It is only those who do not believe in the verses of Allah who invent falsehood, and it is they who are the liars.
verses.106: Whoever disbelieves in Allah after his belief, except for one who is forced while his heart is secure in faith, but those who open their breast to disbelief, upon them is wrath from Allah, and for them is a great punishment.
verses.107: That is because they preferred the life of this world over the Hereafter, and because Allah does not guide a disbelieving people.
verses.108: Those are the ones upon whose hearts, hearing, and sight Allah has sealed, and it is they who are the heedless.
verses.109: Undoubtedly, in the Hereafter, it is they who will be the losers.
verses.110: Then indeed your Lord, to those who emigrated after they had been persecuted and then strove and were patient — indeed your Lord, after that, is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.111: On the Day every soul will come disputing for itself, and every soul will be paid in full for what it did, and they will not be wronged.
verses.112: Allah presents an example of a city that was secure and content, its provision coming to it in abundance from every location, but it denied the favors of Allah — so Allah made it taste the garment of hunger and fear for what they used to do.
verses.113: There had certainly come to them a messenger from among themselves, but they denied him, so the punishment seized them while they were wrongdoers.
verses.114: So eat of what Allah has provided you, lawful and good, and be grateful for the favor of Allah, if it is Him you worship.
verses.115: He has only forbidden you carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah — but whoever is forced by necessity, without desiring it or transgressing, indeed Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.116: Do not say what your tongues falsely describe, 'This is lawful and this is unlawful,' to invent falsehood about Allah — indeed those who invent falsehood about Allah will not succeed.
verses.117: A brief enjoyment, and for them is a painful punishment.
verses.118: And to those who are Jews We have forbidden what We related to you before — We did not wrong them, but they were wronging themselves.
verses.119: Then indeed your Lord, to those who committed evil out of ignorance and then repented after that and corrected themselves — indeed your Lord, after that, is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.120: Indeed Abraham was a comprehensive leader, devoutly obedient to Allah, and inclining toward truth, and he was not among those who associated others with Allah.
verses.121: He was grateful for His favors — Allah chose him and guided him to a straight path.
verses.122: We gave him good in this world, and indeed in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous.
verses.123: Then We revealed to you, 'Follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth, and he was not among those who associated others with Allah.'
verses.124: The sabbath was only appointed for those who differed over it — indeed your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
verses.125: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best — indeed your Lord is most knowing of who has strayed from His way, and He is most knowing of who is guided.
verses.126: If you punish, then punish with an equivalent of that with which you were harmed — but if you are patient, that is certainly best for those who are patient.
verses.127: Be patient, and your patience is not except through Allah, and do not grieve over them, and do not be in distress over what they plot.
verses.128: Indeed Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are doers of good.

verses.1: Exalted is He who took His servant by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him of Our signs — indeed He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
verses.2: We gave Moses the Scripture and made it a guidance for the Children of Israel, saying, 'Do not take besides Me a disposer of affairs.'
verses.3: O descendants of those We carried with Noah — indeed he was a grateful servant.
verses.4: We decreed for the Children of Israel in the Scripture that they would surely cause corruption on the earth twice and would rise to great haughtiness.
verses.5: When the promise of the first of the two events came, We sent against you servants of Ours with great military force, and they probed into your homes — it was a promise fulfilled.
verses.6: Then We gave you back the return over them, and We reinforced you with wealth and sons and made you more numerous in soldiers.
verses.7: If you do good, you do good for yourselves, and if you do evil, it is likewise for yourselves — then when the second promise came, We sent your enemies to sadden your faces and to enter the mosque as they had entered it the first time, and to destroy what they had taken over with utter destruction.
verses.8: Perhaps your Lord will have mercy upon you — but if you return to defiance, We will return to punishment, and We have made Hell a prison for the disbelievers.
verses.9: Indeed this Qur'an guides to that which is most suitable, and gives good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a great reward.
verses.10: And that those who do not believe in the Hereafter — We have prepared for them a painful punishment.
verses.11: Man supplicates for evil as he supplicates for good, and man is ever hasty.
verses.12: We have made the night and the day two signs — We erase the sign of the night and make the sign of the day visible, that you might seek bounty from your Lord and know the number of years and the account, and We have explained everything in detail.
verses.13: Every man's deeds We have fastened around his neck, and We will produce for him on the Day of Resurrection a record which he will find spread open.
verses.14: 'Read your record — sufficient is yourself today as accountant against you.'
verses.15: Whoever is guided is only guided for the benefit of his own soul, and whoever strays only strays to his own detriment — no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another, and We would never punish until We sent a messenger.
verses.16: When We intend to destroy a city, We command its affluent ones, but they defiantly disobey therein, so the word comes into effect upon it, and We destroy it with complete destruction.
verses.17: How many generations have We destroyed after Noah — sufficient is your Lord, concerning the sins of His servants, as Acquainted and Seeing.
verses.18: Whoever wishes for the immediate world, We hasten for him therein what We will, to whom We intend — then We have made for him Hell, in which he will burn, disgraced and expelled.
verses.19: Whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with the effort it deserves, while being a believer — it is those whose effort is ever appreciated.
verses.20: To each of the two We extend, from the gift of your Lord — and the gift of your Lord has never been restricted.
verses.21: See how We have favored some of them over others, and the Hereafter is greater in degrees and greater in preference.
verses.22: Do not make with Allah another deity, or you will sit blamed and insufficient.
verses.23: Your Lord has decreed that you worship none but Him, and to parents, good treatment — should one or both of them reach old age with you, do not say to them a word of contempt, nor repel them, but speak to them a noble word.
verses.24: Lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy, and say, 'My Lord, have mercy upon them as they raised me when I was small.'
verses.25: Your Lord is most knowing of what is within yourselves — if you should be righteous, then indeed He is ever, to the often returning, Forgiving.
verses.26: Give the relative his right, and the poor and the traveler, and do not spend wastefully.
verses.27: Indeed the wasteful are brothers of the devils, and Satan is ever ungrateful to his Lord.
verses.28: If you turn away from them seeking mercy from your Lord which you hope for, then speak to them a gentle word.
verses.29: Do not make your hand chained to your neck, nor extend it completely, so that you become blamed and destitute.
verses.30: Indeed your Lord extends provision for whom He wills and restricts it — indeed He is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing.
verses.31: Do not kill your children for fear of poverty — We provide for them and for you; indeed their killing is ever a great sin.
verses.32: Do not approach adultery — indeed it is an immorality and an evil way.
verses.33: Do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden, except by right — whoever is killed unjustly, We have given his heir authority, but let him not exceed limits in taking life, for he is ever supported by law.
verses.34: Do not approach the wealth of the orphan except in the best way until he reaches maturity — and fulfill every covenant, for the covenant will be questioned about.
verses.35: Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with an even balance — that is the best way and best in result.
verses.36: Do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge — indeed the hearing, sight, and heart, about all those one will be questioned.
verses.37: Do not walk upon the earth exultantly — indeed you will never tear the earth apart, nor will you reach the mountains in height.
verses.38: All that is evil, hated by your Lord.
verses.39: That is from what your Lord has revealed to you of wisdom — do not make with Allah another deity, or you will be cast into Hell, blamed and banished.
verses.40: Has your Lord favored you with sons and taken from among the angels daughters? Indeed you say a tremendous statement.
verses.41: We have diversified the explanation in this Qur'an that they might remember, but it increases them only in aversion.
verses.42: Say: if there had been with Him gods as they say, they would then have sought a way to the possessor of the Throne.
verses.43: Exalted is He and high above what they say by great exaltation.
verses.44: The seven heavens and earth and whatever is in them exalt Him, and there is nothing that does not exalt Him with praise, but you do not understand their way of exalting — indeed He is ever Forbearing and Forgiving.
verses.45: When you recite the Qur'an, We place between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter a hidden veil.
verses.46: We have placed over their hearts coverings, lest they understand it, and in their ears deafness — and when you mention your Lord alone in the Qur'an, they turn back in aversion.
verses.47: We are most knowing of how they listen when they listen to you and when they conspire, when the wrongdoers say, 'You follow but a bewitched man.'
verses.48: Look how they strike similitudes for you and thus have strayed, and they cannot find a way.
verses.49: They say, 'When we are bones and crumbled particles, will we truly be raised as a new creation?'
verses.50: Say: be you stones or iron.
verses.51: Or a creation of what is great in your minds — they will say, 'Who will restore us?' Say: He who created you the first time. They will shake their heads at you and say, 'When is that?' Say: perhaps it is near.
verses.52: The Day He calls you and you respond with His praise, you will think you had remained but a little.
verses.53: Tell My servants to say that which is best — indeed Satan sows discord among them; indeed Satan is ever a clear enemy to mankind.
verses.54: Your Lord is most knowing of you — if He wills, He will have mercy upon you, or if He wills, He will punish you; and We have not sent you as a guardian over them.
verses.55: Your Lord is most knowing of whoever is in the heavens and earth — We have made some prophets exceed others, and to David We gave the book of Psalms.
verses.56: Say: call upon those you claim besides Him, for they do not possess the removal of adversity from you, nor its transfer.
verses.57: Those they call upon seek means of nearness to their Lord, whichever of them is nearest, and hope for His mercy and fear His punishment — indeed the punishment of your Lord is ever feared.
verses.58: There is no city but that We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection or punish it with a severe punishment — that is written in the Register.
verses.59: Nothing has prevented Us from sending signs except that the former peoples denied them — We gave Thamud the she-camel as a visible sign, but they wronged her, and We do not send signs except as a warning.
verses.60: We told you that your Lord has encompassed the people — and We did not make the vision We showed you except as a trial for people, as well as the accursed tree in the Qur'an; We threaten them, but it increases them only in great transgression.
verses.61: When We said to the angels, 'Prostrate to Adam,' they prostrated except for Iblis, who said, 'Should I prostrate to one You created from clay?'
verses.62: He said, 'Do you see this one You have honored above me? If You give me respite until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely destroy his descendants, except for a few.'
verses.63: Allah said, 'Go, for whoever follows you among them, indeed Hell will be the recompense for you all, an ample recompense.'
verses.64: 'And incite whoever you can among them with your voice, and assault them with your cavalry and infantry, and share with them in wealth and children, and make promises to them' — but Satan does not promise them except delusion.
verses.65: 'Indeed over My servants there is for you no authority — sufficient is your Lord as a guardian.'
verses.66: Your Lord is the one who drives the ship for you through the sea that you might seek His bounty — indeed He is ever Merciful to you.
verses.67: When adversity touches you at sea, lost are those you call upon except for Him — but when He delivers you to land, you turn away, for man is ever ungrateful.
verses.68: Do you feel secure that He would not cause a side of the land to swallow you, or send against you a storm of stones? Then you would not find for yourselves a guardian.
verses.69: Or do you feel secure that He would not return you into the sea another time and send upon you a hurricane of wind and drown you for what you denied? Then you would not find for yourselves against Us an avenger.
verses.70: We have certainly honored the children of Adam and carried them on land and sea and provided them with good things and preferred them over much of what We have created, with definite preference.
verses.71: The Day We will call every people by their leader, so whoever is given his record in his right hand, those will read their records, and injustice will not be done to them even so much as a thread.
verses.72: Whoever is blind in this world will be blind in the Hereafter, and more astray in way.
verses.73: They were about to tempt you away from what We revealed to you, that you might invent something else in Our name, and then they would have taken you as an intimate friend.
verses.74: Had We not made you firm, you could have inclined toward them a little.
verses.75: Then We would have made you taste double punishment in life and double after death, and you would not find for yourself against Us any helper.
verses.76: They were about to drive you out of the land to expel you from it, and then they would not have remained after you except for a little.
verses.77: That was Our precedent for those We sent before you of Our messengers, and you will not find in Our precedent any change.
verses.78: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night, and observe the recitation of dawn — indeed the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
verses.79: During part of the night, pray with it as additional worship for you — it is expected that your Lord will raise you to a praised station.
verses.80: Say: my Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit, and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority.
verses.81: Say: the truth has come, and falsehood has departed — indeed falsehood is ever bound to depart.
verses.82: We send down of the Qur'an that which is healing and mercy for the believers, but it does not increase the wrongdoers except in loss.
verses.83: When We bestow favor upon man, he turns away and distances himself, but when evil touches him, he is ever despairing.
verses.84: Say: everyone works according to his disposition, and your Lord is most knowing of who is best guided in way.
verses.85: They ask you about the soul — say: the soul is of the affair of my Lord, and you have not been given of knowledge except a little.
verses.86: If We willed, We could surely take away that which We revealed to you — then you would not find for yourself any guardian against Us.
verses.87: Except as a mercy from your Lord — indeed His favor upon you has ever been great.
verses.88: Say: if mankind and the jinn gathered together to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like of it, even if they were to each other assistants.
verses.89: We have certainly diversified for people in this Qur'an every kind of example, but most of the people refuse except disbelief.
verses.90: They said, 'We will not believe you until you cause a spring to gush from the earth for us.'
verses.91: 'Or you have a garden of palm trees and grapevines, and you cause rivers to gush forth within them abundantly.'
verses.92: 'Or you make the sky fall upon us in fragments, as you have claimed, or you bring Allah and the angels before us.'
verses.93: 'Or you have a house of gold ornament, or you ascend into the sky — and we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book we may read.' Say: exalted is my Lord — was I ever except a human messenger?
verses.94: Nothing has prevented people from believing when guidance came to them, except that they said, 'Has Allah sent a human messenger?'
verses.95: Say: if there were upon the earth angels walking about peacefully, We would have sent down to them from the heaven an angel as messenger.
verses.96: Say: sufficient is Allah as a witness between me and you — indeed He is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing.
verses.97: Whoever Allah guides, he is the guided, and whoever He sends astray, you will not find for them protectors besides Him — We will gather them on the Day of Resurrection on their faces, blind, dumb, and deaf; their refuge is Hell, whenever it subsides We increase them in blazing fire.
verses.98: That is their recompense because they disbelieved in Our signs and said, 'When we are bones and crumbled particles, will we truly be resurrected as a new creation?'
verses.99: Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and earth, is able to create the like of them? He has appointed for them a term of which there is no doubt — but the wrongdoers refuse except disbelief.
verses.100: Say: if you possessed the depositories of the mercy of my Lord, then you would withhold out of fear of spending, for man is ever stingy.
verses.101: We certainly gave Moses nine clear signs, so ask the Children of Israel — when he came to them, Pharaoh said to him, 'I think you are, Moses, bewitched.'
verses.102: He said, 'You have already known that none has sent these signs down except the Lord of the heavens and the earth, as insight, and I think you, Pharaoh, are destroyed.'
verses.103: So he intended to drive them from the land, and We drowned him and those with him all together.
verses.104: We said after him to the Children of Israel, 'Dwell in the land, and when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We will bring you as a gathered crowd.'
verses.105: In truth We sent the Qur'an down, and in truth it descended — and We sent you only as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.
verses.106: We have divided the Qur'an into portions that you might recite it to the people over an extended period, and We have sent it down in gradual stages.
verses.107: Say: believe in it or do not believe — indeed those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, fall upon their faces in prostration.
verses.108: They say, 'Exalted is our Lord — indeed the promise of our Lord has been fulfilled.'
verses.109: They fall upon their faces weeping, and it increases them in humility.
verses.110: Say: call upon Allah or call upon the Most Merciful — whichever you call, to Him belong the best names; do not recite too loudly in your prayer, nor too quietly, but seek a way between that.
verses.111: And say: praise be to Allah, who has not taken a son and has no partner in sovereignty, nor is He in need of any protector out of weakness — and glorify Him with great glorification.

verses.1: Praise be to Allah, who has sent down to His servant the Book and has not made therein any deviance.
verses.2: He made it straight, to warn of severe punishment from Him and to give good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a good reward.
verses.3: In which they will remain forever.
verses.4: And to warn those who say, 'Allah has taken a son.'
verses.5: They have no knowledge of it, nor did their forefathers — how great is the word that comes out of their mouths; they say nothing but a lie.
verses.6: Perhaps you would kill yourself with grief over them, out of sorrow, if they do not believe in this message.
verses.7: Indeed We have made what is on the earth as adornment for it, that We may test them as to which of them is best in deed.
verses.8: And indeed We will make what is on it a barren ground.
verses.9: Or do you think that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, among Our signs, a wonder?
verses.10: When the young men took refuge in the cave, they said, 'Our Lord, grant us mercy from Yourself and facilitate for us our affair in the right way.'
verses.11: So We cast slumber over their ears within the cave for a number of years.
verses.12: Then We awakened them to know which of the two groups would best calculate the time they had remained.
verses.13: We relate to you their story in truth — they were young men who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.
verses.14: We made firm their hearts when they stood up and said, 'Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth — we will never call upon any deity besides Him, for then we would have spoken an outrageous transgression.'
verses.15: 'These, our people, have taken besides Him deities — why do they not bring for them a clear authority? Who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah?'
verses.16: 'When you have withdrawn from them and that which they worship except Allah, retreat to the cave — your Lord will spread out for you His mercy and will prepare for you facility in your affair.'
verses.17: You would see the sun, when it rose, inclining away from their cave to the right, and when it set, passing away from them to the left, while they were within an open space thereof — that was from the signs of Allah; whoever Allah guides is the rightly guided, and whoever He sends astray, you will never find for him a protecting guide.
verses.18: You would think them awake while they were asleep, and We turned them on their right and their left sides, while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance — had you looked at them, you would have surely turned from them in flight and been filled with terror.
verses.19: Similarly, We awakened them to question one another among themselves — one of them said, 'How long have you remained?' They said, 'We have remained a day or part of a day.' They said, 'Your Lord is most knowing of how long you remained; so send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him look at which is the best food and bring you provision from it, and let him be cautious and let no one be aware of you.'
verses.20: 'Indeed if they come to know of you, they will stone you or return you to their religion, and never then will you succeed, ever.'
verses.21: Thus We made known about them so the people would know that the promise of Allah is truth and that the Hour, there is no doubt about it — when they disputed among themselves about their affair, they said, 'Construct over them a structure — their Lord is most knowing about them.' Those who prevailed in the matter said, 'We will surely take a mosque over them.'
verses.22: Some will say they were three, their dog being the fourth, and some will say they were five, their dog being the sixth, guessing at the unseen, and others will say seven, and their dog was the eighth — say: my Lord is most knowing of their number, none knows them except a few; so do not argue about them except with an obvious argument, and do not inquire about them among any of them.
verses.23: Never say about anything, 'Indeed I will do that tomorrow.'
verses.24: Except when adding, 'If Allah wills' — and remember your Lord when you forget, and say, 'Perhaps my Lord will guide me to what is nearer than this to right conduct.'
verses.25: They remained in their cave three hundred years and added nine.
verses.26: Say: Allah is most knowing of how long they remained — to Him belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth; how clearly He sees and hears; they have no protector besides Him, and He does not share His judgment with anyone.
verses.27: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord — none can change His words, and you will never find besides Him a refuge.
verses.28: Keep yourself patient with those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking His face, and let not your eyes pass beyond them, desiring the adornment of worldly life, and do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance and who follows his desire and whose affair is ever beyond bounds.
verses.29: Say: the truth is from your Lord, so whoever wills, let him believe, and whoever wills, let him disbelieve — indeed We have prepared for the wrongdoers a fire whose walls will surround them, and if they call for relief, they will be relieved with water like murky oil, which scalds their faces — how terrible is the drink, and how evil the resting place.
verses.30: Indeed those who believe and do righteous deeds — indeed We will not let the reward of one who does good deeds go to waste.
verses.31: Those will have gardens of eternity beneath which rivers flow, adorned therein with bracelets of gold, and wearing green garments of fine silk and brocade, reclining therein on thrones — excellent is the reward, and how good is the resting place.
verses.32: Present to them an example of two men — for one of them We made two gardens of grapevines, surrounded them with palm trees, and placed between them crops.
verses.33: Each of the two gardens produced its fruit and did not fall short thereof at all, and We caused a river to gush forth within them.
verses.34: He had abundant fruits, so he said to his companion while conversing with him, 'I am greater than you in wealth and stronger in respect of men.'
verses.35: He entered his garden while he was unjust to himself, saying, 'I do not think this will ever perish.'
verses.36: 'And I do not think the Hour will occur, and even if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely find better than this as a return.'
verses.37: His companion said to him while conversing with him, 'Have you disbelieved in He who created you from dust and then from a sperm-drop and then proportioned you as a man?'
verses.38: 'But as for me, He is Allah, my Lord, and I will not associate with my Lord anyone.'
verses.39: 'If only you had said when you entered your garden, what Allah wills — there is no power except by Allah — although you see me less than you in wealth and children.'
verses.40: 'It may be that my Lord will give me better than your garden and send upon it a calamity from the sky, and it will become a slippery ground.'
verses.41: 'Or its water will become sunken into the earth, so you would never be able to seek it.'
verses.42: His fruits were encompassed by ruin, and he began turning his hands about over what he had spent on it while it had collapsed on its trellises, and he said, 'Oh, I wish I had not associated with my Lord anyone.'
verses.43: He had no group to help him besides Allah, nor could he defend himself.
verses.44: There, protection is only from Allah, the Truth — He is best in reward and best in outcome.
verses.45: Present to them the example of the life of this world — like rain which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mixes with it, and it becomes dry remnants, scattered by the winds; Allah is ever, over all things, Perfect in ability.
verses.46: Wealth and children are but adornment of the worldly life, but the enduring good deeds are better in the sight of your Lord for reward and better for hope.
verses.47: The Day We will remove the mountains, and you will see the earth as a leveled plain, and We will gather them and not leave out any of them.
verses.48: They will be presented before your Lord in rows, and it will be said, 'You have certainly come to Us just as We created you the first time — although you claimed We would never make for you a meeting.'
verses.49: The record will be placed, and you will see the criminals fearful of what is in it, saying, 'How terrible is this record — it does not leave a small thing or a great thing except that it has enumerated it.' They will find present what they had done, and your Lord does not wrong anyone.
verses.50: When We said to the angels, 'Prostrate to Adam,' they prostrated except for Iblis — he was of the jinn and departed from the command of his Lord; then will you take him and his descendants as protectors besides Me while they are enemies to you? Wretched it is for the wrongdoers as an exchange.
verses.51: I did not make them witness to the creation of the heavens and earth or the creation of themselves, and I would not have taken the misleaders as assistants.
verses.52: The Day He will say, 'Call My partners whom you claimed,' and they will invoke them, but they will not respond to them — We will make between them a valley of destruction.
verses.53: The criminals will see the Fire and will be certain that they are to fall into it, and they will not find a way to avoid it.
verses.54: We have certainly diversified in this Qur'an for the people every kind of example — man is ever, most of anything, contentious.
verses.55: Nothing has prevented people from believing when guidance came to them, and from asking forgiveness of their Lord, except that there befall them the precedent of the former peoples or that the punishment should come face to face with them.
verses.56: We send the messengers not except as bringers of good tidings and warners, and those who disbelieve dispute using falsehood to invalidate the truth thereby, and they take My verses and warnings as mockery.
verses.57: Who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord but turns away from them and forgets what his hands have sent forth? Indeed We have placed over their hearts coverings lest they understand it, and in their ears deafness — and if you call them to guidance, they will never be guided, then, ever.
verses.58: Your Lord is the Forgiving, full of mercy — if He should punish them for what they have earned, He would have hastened for them the punishment; but for them is an appointment from which they will never find an escape.
verses.59: Those cities We destroyed when they wronged, and We made for their destruction an appointed time.
verses.60: Moses said to his servant, 'I will not cease until I reach the junction of the two seas, even if I go for a long time.'
verses.61: When they reached the junction between them, they forgot their fish, and it took its course into the sea, slipping away.
verses.62: When they had passed beyond it, Moses said to his servant, 'Bring us our morning meal — we have certainly suffered fatigue in this our journey.'
verses.63: He said, 'Did you see when we retired to the rock? I forgot the fish, and none made me forget it except Satan, that I should mention it, and it took its course into the sea, amazingly.'
verses.64: Moses said, 'That is what we were seeking' — so they returned, following their footprints.
verses.65: They found one of Our servants to whom We had given mercy from Us and had taught him from Us knowledge.
verses.66: Moses said to him, 'May I follow you on the condition that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgment?'
verses.67: He said, 'Indeed you will not be able to be patient with me.'
verses.68: 'And how can you be patient about that of which you have not encompassed any knowledge?'
verses.69: Moses said, 'You will find me, if Allah wills, patient, and I will not disobey you in any order.'
verses.70: He said, 'Then if you follow me, do not ask me about anything until I make to you about it mention.'
verses.71: So they set out until, when they had embarked on the ship, he made a hole in it — Moses said, 'Have you made a hole in it to drown its people? You have certainly done a grave thing.'
verses.72: He said, 'Did I not say that you would never be able to have patience with me?'
verses.73: Moses said, 'Do not blame me for what I forgot, and do not cover me in my matter with difficulty.'
verses.74: So they set out until, when they met a boy, he killed him — Moses said, 'Have you killed a pure soul for other than a soul killed? You have certainly done a deplorable thing.'
verses.75: He said, 'Did I not tell you that you would never be able to have patience with me?'
verses.76: Moses said, 'If I ask you about anything after this, then do not keep me as a companion — you have obtained from me an excuse.'
verses.77: So they set out until, when they came to the people of a town, they asked its people for food, but they refused to offer them hospitality — they found therein a wall about to collapse, so he repaired it. Moses said, 'If you wished, you could have taken payment for it.'
verses.78: He said, 'This is parting between me and you — I will inform you of the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience.'
verses.79: 'As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea, so I intended to cause defect in it, as there was after them a king who seized every ship by force.'
verses.80: 'As for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared he would burden them by transgression and disbelief.'
verses.81: 'So we intended that their Lord would substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.'
verses.82: 'As for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and beneath it was a treasure for them, and their father had been righteous — so your Lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure as mercy from your Lord; and I did not do it upon my own command — this is the interpretation of that about which you could not have patience.'
verses.83: They ask you about Dhul-Qarnayn — say: I will recite to you about him a report.
verses.84: Indeed We established him upon the earth, and We gave him means of everything.
verses.85: So he followed a path.
verses.86: Until, when he reached the setting of the sun, he found it setting in a spring of dark, murky water, and he found near it a people — We said, 'Dhul-Qarnayn, you may punish them or treat them with kindness.'
verses.87: He said, 'As for one who wrongs, we will punish him, then he will be returned to his Lord, and He will punish him with a terrible punishment.'
verses.88: 'But as for one who believes and does righteousness, he will have a reward of the best, and we will speak to him from our command with ease.'
verses.89: Then he followed a path.
verses.90: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun, he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.
verses.91: Thus We knew what was ahead of him in knowledge.
verses.92: Then he followed a path.
verses.93: Until, when he reached between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand speech.
verses.94: They said, 'Dhul-Qarnayn, indeed Gog and Magog are causing corruption on earth — so should we assign you tribute so that you make between us and them a barrier?'
verses.95: He said, 'That in which my Lord has established me is better, so assist me with strength — I will make between you and them a dam.'
verses.96: 'Bring me sheets of iron' — until, when he had leveled them between the two cliffs, he said, 'Blow' — until, when he had made it a fire, he said, 'Bring me molten copper to pour over it.'
verses.97: So Gog and Magog were unable to pass over it, nor were they able to tunnel through it.
verses.98: He said, 'This is a mercy from my Lord — but when the promise of my Lord comes, He will make it level, and the promise of my Lord is ever true.'
verses.99: We will leave them that day surging over one another, and the trumpet will be blown, and We will assemble them all together.
verses.100: We will present Hell that day to the disbelievers, on display.
verses.101: Those whose eyes had been within a cover from My remembrance, and they were unable to hear.
verses.102: Do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants as protectors besides Me? Indeed We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging.
verses.103: Say: shall we inform you of the greatest losers as to their deeds?
verses.104: Those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well in work.
verses.105: Those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord and in the meeting with Him, so their deeds have become worthless, and We will not assign them on the Day of Resurrection any weight.
verses.106: That is their recompense — Hell — for what they denied and took My verses and My messengers in ridicule.
verses.107: Indeed those who believe and do righteous deeds will have the gardens of Paradise as a lodging.
verses.108: In which they abide eternally — they will not desire from it any transfer.
verses.109: Say: if the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it as a supplement.
verses.110: Say: I am only a man like you, to whom it has been revealed that your God is one God — so whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord, let him do righteous work and not associate in the worship of his Lord anyone.

verses.1: Kaf, Ha, Ya, Ain, Sad.
verses.2: This is a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zachariah.
verses.3: When he called upon his Lord with a private call.
verses.4: He said, 'My Lord, indeed my bones have weakened, and my head has filled with white hair, and I have never been unhappy in my supplication to You, my Lord.'
verses.5: 'I fear for my successors after me, and my wife has been barren, so grant me from Yourself an heir.'
verses.6: 'Who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob — and make him, my Lord, pleasing.'
verses.7: 'Zachariah, indeed We give you good tidings of a boy whose name will be John — We have not assigned to anyone before, his name.'
verses.8: He said, 'My Lord, how will I have a boy when my wife has been barren and I have reached extreme old age?'
verses.9: He said, 'Thus your Lord has said, it is easy for Me, for I created you before, while you were nothing.'
verses.10: He said, 'My Lord, make for me a sign.' He said, 'Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, while being sound.'
verses.11: He came out to his people from the prayer chamber and signaled to them to exalt Allah morning and evening.
verses.12: 'John, take the Scripture with determination' — and We gave him judgment while yet a child.
verses.13: And tenderness from Us and purity, and he was fearing of Allah.
verses.14: And dutiful to his parents, and he was not a disobedient tyrant.
verses.15: Peace be upon him the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he is raised alive.
verses.16: Mention in the Book, Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.
verses.17: She took a veil apart from them — then We sent to her Our spirit, and he appeared to her as a man in complete form.
verses.18: She said, 'Indeed I seek refuge from you in the Most Merciful, if you should be fearing of Allah.'
verses.19: He said, 'I am only the messenger of your Lord to give you a pure boy.'
verses.20: She said, 'How can I have a boy while no man has touched me, and I have not been unchaste?'
verses.21: He said, 'Thus your Lord has said, it is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign for the people and a mercy from Us — and it is a matter already decreed.'
verses.22: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.
verses.23: The pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree — she said, 'I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten.'
verses.24: One called out to her from beneath her, 'Do not grieve — your Lord has provided beneath you a stream.'
verses.25: 'Shake the trunk of the palm tree toward you, and it will drop ripe, fresh dates upon you.'
verses.26: 'So eat and drink and be at ease — and if you see any human being, say, I have vowed a fast to the Most Merciful, so I will not speak today to any human.'
verses.27: She came to her people carrying him — they said, 'Mary, you have certainly done a strange thing.'
verses.28: 'Sister of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste.'
verses.29: She pointed to him — they said, 'How can we speak to one who is in the cradle, a child?'
verses.30: The baby spoke, 'Indeed I am the servant of Allah — He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet.'
verses.31: 'He has made me blessed wherever I am, and has enjoined upon me prayer and charity as long as I remain alive.'
verses.32: 'And dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a tyrant, unhappy.'
verses.33: 'Peace be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised alive.'
verses.34: That is Jesus, the son of Mary, a word of truth about which they are in doubt.
verses.35: It is not befitting for Allah to take a son — exalted is He; when He decrees a matter, He only says to it, 'Be,' and it is.
verses.36: 'Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him — this is a straight path.'
verses.37: The factions differed among themselves, so woe to those who disbelieved from the scene of a great day.
verses.38: How they will hear and how they will see the Day they come to Us — but the wrongdoers today are in clear error.
verses.39: Warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded, while they are in heedlessness and they do not believe.
verses.40: Indeed it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is upon it, and to Us they will be returned.
verses.41: Mention in the Book, Abraham — indeed he was a man of truth and a prophet.
verses.42: He said to his father, 'My father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all?'
verses.43: 'My father, indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me, I will guide you to a level path.'
verses.44: 'My father, do not worship Satan — indeed Satan is ever disobedient to the Most Merciful.'
verses.45: 'My father, indeed I fear that a punishment from the Most Merciful will touch you, and you would become to Satan a friend.'
verses.46: He said, 'Have you turned away from my gods, Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so leave me for a long time.'
verses.47: He said, 'Peace will be upon you — I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord; indeed He is ever gracious to me.'
verses.48: 'I will leave you and what you invoke besides Allah, and I will invoke my Lord — I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy.'
verses.49: When he left them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each We made a prophet.
verses.50: We granted them of Our mercy, and We made for them a mention of high honor and truth.
verses.51: Mention in the Book, Moses — indeed he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
verses.52: We called him from the right side of the mount and brought him near, privately conversing.
verses.53: We granted him from Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet.
verses.54: Mention in the Book, Ishmael — indeed he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
verses.55: He used to enjoin upon his family prayer and charity, and he was pleasing to his Lord.
verses.56: Mention in the Book, Idris — indeed he was a man of truth and a prophet.
verses.57: We raised him to a high station.
verses.58: Those are the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favor from among the prophets, of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and chose — when the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping.
verses.59: But there came after them a generation who lost prayer and followed desires, so they will meet evil.
verses.60: Except those who repent, believe, and do righteous work — those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged at all.
verses.61: Gardens of eternity, which the Most Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen — indeed His promise is ever coming.
verses.62: They will not hear therein ill speech, only peace — and they will have their provision therein morning and evening.
verses.63: That is Paradise, which We cause those of Our servants to inherit who were fearing of Allah.
verses.64: The angels do not descend except by the order of your Lord — to Him belongs what is before us and what is behind us and what is between them, and your Lord is not forgetful.
verses.65: Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, so worship Him and be steadfast in worship of Him — do you know of any equal to Him?
verses.66: Man says, 'When I have died, will I be brought forth alive?'
verses.67: Does man not remember that We created him before, while he was nothing?
verses.68: By your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils, then We will bring them to kneel around Hell.
verses.69: Then We will surely extract from every sect those who were worst against the Most Merciful in insolence.
verses.70: Then indeed We are most knowing of those most worthy of burning therein.
verses.71: There is none of you except that he will come to it — this is upon your Lord an inevitability decreed.
verses.72: Then We will save those who feared Allah and leave the wrongdoers therein, on their knees.
verses.73: When Our verses are recited to them as clear evidences, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, 'Which of the two parties is best in position and best in assembly?'
verses.74: How many generations before them have We destroyed, who were better in possessions and outward appearance?
verses.75: Say: whoever is in error, let the Most Merciful extend for him an extension of time, until, when they see what they were promised, either the punishment or the Hour, they will know who is worse in position and weaker in soldiers.
verses.76: Allah increases those who were guided in guidance, and the enduring righteous deeds are better with your Lord for reward and better for returns.
verses.77: Have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said, 'I will surely be given wealth and children'?
verses.78: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken a covenant with the Most Merciful?
verses.79: No — We will record what he says and extend for him from the punishment extensively.
verses.80: And We will inherit from him what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.
verses.81: They have taken besides Allah gods that they might be for them a source of honor.
verses.82: No — those gods will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents.
verses.83: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them to commotion?
verses.84: So do not hasten against them — We only count for them a limited number of days.
verses.85: The Day We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful as a delegation.
verses.86: And drive the criminals to Hell in thirst.
verses.87: None will have the power of intercession except one who has taken from the Most Merciful a covenant.
verses.88: They say, 'The Most Merciful has taken a son.'
verses.89: You have certainly come up with an atrocious thing.
verses.90: The heavens almost tear apart therefrom, and the earth splits, and the mountains collapse in devastation.
verses.91: That they have attributed to the Most Merciful a son.
verses.92: It is not befitting for the Most Merciful that He should take a son.
verses.93: There is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the Most Merciful as a servant.
verses.94: He has enumerated them and counted them a precise counting.
verses.95: Each of them will come to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.
verses.96: Indeed those who have believed and done righteous deeds — the Most Merciful will bestow upon them affection.
verses.97: We have only made it easy in your tongue that you may give good tidings thereby to the righteous and warn thereby a hostile people.
verses.98: How many have We destroyed before them of generations — do you perceive of them anyone, or hear from them the slightest sound?

verses.1: Ta-Ha. Two lone letters open the surah, sounds without a settled meaning, a reminder that this speech comes from beyond ordinary language — a seal of authenticity placed before the story even begins.
verses.2: The revelation was not sent to exhaust or torment the one who carries it. Whatever weight Muhammad feels in delivering this message, it was never meant to break him — it was meant to reach him gently.
verses.3: Instead, it is a reminder — medicine, not burden — offered to anyone whose heart still trembles at the thought of standing before their Lord.
verses.4: It descends from the One who spread out the earth beneath every footstep and raised the heavens beyond every horizon — a Being of such scale that a single revelation costs Him nothing, yet He sends it anyway, out of care.
verses.5: The Most Merciful is established over all creation, sovereign and unshaken, not distant but directly governing everything that exists.
verses.6: Everything in the skies, everything on earth, everything hidden between them, even what lies beneath the soil — all of it belongs to Him alone. Nothing escapes His ownership.
verses.7: Speak your prayer aloud or bury it in silence — it makes no difference to Him. He already knows the secret, and He knows what is even more hidden than the secret: the thought behind the thought.
verses.8: This is Allah. No other deity shares His reality. Every name of beauty and majesty belongs to Him, and none of them contradicts another — mercy and justice, gentleness and power, all held together in one being.
verses.9: Has the story reached you — the story of Moses? What follows is not just history; it is a mirror held up for anyone who has ever felt small before a great mission.
verses.10: He saw a fire in the distance and told his family to wait: 'Stay here — I see a fire. Perhaps I can bring back an ember, or find someone there to point us toward the road.' He was searching for something ordinary and found something that would change everything.
verses.11: When he reached it, a voice called out to him by name: 'O Moses.' No preamble, no ritual — just his name, spoken directly into the dark.
verses.12: 'I am your Lord. Remove your sandals — you are standing in the sacred valley of Tuwa.' Even the ground beneath his feet had to be approached differently now; holiness reshapes the smallest gestures.
verses.13: 'I have chosen you. Now listen closely to what is about to be revealed.' A shepherd fleeing exile is suddenly told he has been selected — not for comfort, but for a task.
verses.14: 'I am Allah. There is no god but Me. So worship Me alone, and establish prayer to keep My remembrance alive within you.' The very first command is not conquest or confrontation — it is worship, the root from which everything else will grow.
verses.15: 'The Hour is coming — I keep it almost hidden — so that every soul will be repaid exactly for what it strove toward.' Uncertainty about the timing is itself a mercy, keeping every heart alert rather than complacent.
verses.16: 'So do not let anyone who denies it, chasing their own desires, pull you away from this truth — or you will be lost.' A warning placed before the mission even starts, because the road ahead will be full of people offering easier paths.
verses.17: 'And what is that in your right hand, Moses?' A question so simple it almost seems unnecessary — until you realize where it is about to lead.
verses.18: 'It is my staff,' he answered. 'I lean on it, I knock down leaves for my flock with it, and I have other uses for it besides.' An ordinary tool, described in ordinary, practical terms — the ground before the miracle.
verses.19: 'Throw it down, Moses.'
verses.20: He threw it — and it became a snake, moving with a life of its own. The staff he had leaned on his whole life suddenly slipped its familiar shape entirely.
verses.21: 'Take hold of it, and do not be afraid. We will return it to what it was.' Courage was demanded before comfort was granted — he had to reach for the very thing that frightened him.
verses.22: 'And press your hand to your side; it will come out shining white, without any illness — another sign.' A second wonder, gentler than the first, given as reassurance rather than shock.
verses.23: 'This is so We may show you some of Our greatest signs' — not the whole of what Allah can do, but enough to steady a man about to walk into a tyrant's court.
verses.24: 'Go to Pharaoh. He has crossed every boundary.' The mission is named plainly: not diplomacy for its own sake, but confronting a man who believes himself beyond correction.
verses.25: Moses answered with a prayer, not a boast: 'My Lord, open up my chest for me.' Before facing Pharaoh, he asks first to be able to bear the weight of the task internally.
verses.26: 'And make my task easy for me.'
verses.27: 'And loosen the knot from my tongue' — an honest admission of a real limitation, spoken directly to the One who chose him anyway.
verses.28: 'So that they may understand what I say.' The goal was never eloquence for its own sake, but simply being understood.
verses.29: 'And appoint for me a helper from my own family' — he does not ask to face this alone, and he is not expected to.
verses.30: 'Aaron, my brother.'
verses.31: 'Strengthen me through him.'
verses.32: 'And let him share in my task' — partnership requested and granted, a rare tenderness within a story about confronting empire.
verses.33: 'So that we may glorify You much,'
verses.34: 'and remember You much.' Even the purpose of the partnership circles back to worship, not personal relief.
verses.35: 'You are always watching over us.' The prayer ends not with a demand but with trust — an acknowledgment that he is seen no matter what happens next.
verses.36: 'You have been granted what you asked, Moses.' The request, offered so humbly, is answered in full.
verses.37: 'And We had already shown you favor once before, long ago' — Allah reminds him gently that this is not the first time he has been carried by unseen hands.
verses.38: 'When We inspired your mother with what she needed to know' — the story rewinds to his infancy, to a mother facing an impossible choice.
verses.39: 'Place him in the chest and cast it into the river; the river will carry him to the shore, where an enemy of Mine and his will take him in. I wrapped you in My own love, so that you would be raised under My watchful eye.' The child destined to confront Pharaoh was first placed, defenseless, into Pharaoh's own household — by design, not accident.
verses.40: 'Your sister followed you and said, “Shall I lead you to someone who can nurse him?” And so We returned you to your mother, that her eyes might be soothed and her grief lifted. Later you killed a man, and We saved you from the consequences, testing you thoroughly along the way. Then you lived years among the people of Madyan, and now you have arrived here exactly as it was written, Moses.' A whole hidden life — exile, labor, love, guilt, refuge — summarized in a single breath, each piece revealed as purposeful.
verses.41: 'I have shaped you for Myself' — every wandering year, it turns out, was formation, not waste.
verses.42: 'Go, you and your brother, carrying My signs, and do not grow slack in remembering Me' — the mission is given, but so is the warning against spiritual fatigue along the way.
verses.43: 'Go, both of you, to Pharaoh — he has truly transgressed.'
verses.44: 'Speak to him gently — perhaps he will reflect, or perhaps he will feel fear.' Even facing the most arrogant ruler of his age, the instruction is softness first, not scorn.
verses.45: 'Our Lord,' they said, 'we're afraid he may act rashly against us, or that he will go too far.' Their fear is not hidden or shameful — it is spoken honestly, straight to God.
verses.46: 'Do not be afraid,' He answered. 'I am with you both — I hear, and I see.' Presence promised in the most direct terms possible, before a single word is spoken to Pharaoh.
verses.47: 'Go to him and say: We are messengers of your Lord. Send the Children of Israel away with us, and do not torment them any longer. We have brought you a sign from your Lord, and peace belongs to whoever follows guidance.' Their opening words are an offer of peace laid beside a demand for justice.
verses.48: 'It has been revealed to us that punishment awaits whoever denies and turns away' — a warning delivered plainly, without threat of their own making, only of what has already been decreed.
verses.49: Pharaoh sneered: 'So who is this Lord of yours, Moses?' A question meant to belittle, tossed out as though the answer could not possibly matter.
verses.50: Moses answered without flinching: 'Our Lord is the One who gave every single thing its form, then guided it.' Not an abstract deity, but the very source of shape and direction in all creation.
verses.51: Pharaoh pressed further: 'And what about the earlier generations, then?' — trying to trap him in a question about the fate of ancestors.
verses.52: 'Knowledge of that belongs to my Lord, recorded fully. My Lord neither makes mistakes nor forgets anything.' A calm redirection — some knowledge belongs to God alone, and that is not a weakness in the argument but its strength.
verses.53: 'He is the One who laid out the earth for you like a bed, wove roadways through it, and sent down rain from the sky, bringing forth through it pairs of every kind of plant.' Moses turns the conversation from abstraction to the ground beneath Pharaoh's own throne.
verses.54: 'Eat, and graze your livestock — in all of this are signs for anyone who truly thinks.' An invitation, not a lecture: look at what feeds you and ask where it came from.
verses.55: 'From the earth We created you, into it We will return you, and from it We will bring you out once more.' Life, death, and resurrection are compressed into a single cycle drawn from the dust itself.
verses.56: Allah had shown Pharaoh every kind of sign, yet he denied them all and refused to yield. Evidence alone, it turns out, was never going to be enough for a heart already closed.
verses.57: 'Have you come,' Pharaoh said, 'to drive us from our own land with your magic, Moses?' He reframes truth as threat, revelation as trickery.
verses.58: 'Then we will meet you with magic just as powerful. Set an appointment between us that neither of us will break.' A challenge issued from wounded pride rather than honest inquiry.
verses.59: Moses answered: 'Let it be on the day of the festival, and let the people gather in the late morning, when everyone can see clearly.' No secrecy, no ambush — he wanted the truth witnessed in daylight, by everyone.
verses.60: Pharaoh withdrew, gathered his full scheme, and returned. Behind the confidence of a king was the frantic assembling of every trick he could muster.
verses.61: Moses warned the magicians directly: 'Woe to you — do not fabricate lies against Allah, or He will wipe you out with a devastating punishment. Whoever invents falsehood already stands defeated.' Compassion even for his opponents, offered before the contest begins.
verses.62: They argued quietly among themselves, murmuring their private disagreement about what to do next. Doubt had already crept into the camp meant to defeat him.
verses.63: They convinced themselves: 'These two are just magicians, trying to drive us from our land with their tricks and dismantle our most cherished way of life.' Fear disguised itself as certainty.
verses.64: 'So gather your strategy and come forward in a single line. Today, whoever prevails will have succeeded.' Pharaoh's magicians rally their courage, unaware of what is about to unfold.
verses.65: 'Moses,' they offered, 'either you throw first, or we will.' Even in confrontation, there is a strange politeness — a final choice extended to him.
verses.66: 'You throw first,' he said. And suddenly, through their trickery, their ropes and staffs appeared to writhe and move as if alive. For one unsettling instant, illusion looked exactly like truth.
verses.67: Moses felt a flicker of apprehension rise within him — even a chosen prophet is allowed a human moment of fear before the ground steadies again.
verses.68: 'Do not be afraid,' came the reassurance. 'You are the one who will prevail.' Fear met instantly with certainty from the only source that could offer it.
verses.69: 'Throw what is in your right hand — it will swallow up everything they have made. What they have made is only a magician's trick, and a trickster never truly succeeds, wherever he goes.' Truth does not merely outshine illusion here — it consumes it entirely.
verses.70: And the magicians — the very men hired to defeat Moses — fell to the ground in prostration, declaring: 'We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.' The staged spectacle of royal power collapses into the most sincere surrender in the whole scene.
verses.71: Pharaoh raged: 'You believed him before I gave you permission? I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides and crucify you on palm trunks — then you will know which of us holds the more severe and lasting punishment.' Threats escalate the instant control slips from his hands.
verses.72: They answered without hesitation: 'We will never choose you over the clear truth that has come to us, and over the One who created us. Decide whatever you intend to decide — your power only reaches this fleeting life.' Torture no longer held any leverage over hearts already transformed.
verses.73: 'We have believed in our Lord, hoping He will forgive our sins, including the magic you forced us to perform. Allah is better, and He endures.' Their final words reorder everything — eternity now outweighs any threat a king could make.
verses.74: 'Whoever comes before his Lord guilty will have Hell — in it he will neither die nor live.' The stakes of the whole confrontation are stated with stark clarity.
verses.75: 'But whoever comes to Him believing, having done what is right — those will hold the highest ranks.'
verses.76: 'Gardens of lasting residence, rivers flowing beneath them, where they remain forever. That is the reward of whoever purifies himself.' The magicians' sudden transformation becomes the living proof of this very promise.
verses.77: Allah instructed Moses: 'Travel by night with My servants, and strike out a dry path for them through the sea. Do not fear being overtaken, and do not be afraid.' Deliverance, when it finally comes, arrives as a direct, personal command.
verses.78: Pharaoh pursued them with his army — and the sea closed over them exactly as it was destined to. Pursuit driven by pride ends in total ruin.
verses.79: Pharaoh led his people astray; he did not guide them toward anything worth reaching. A ruler entrusted with a nation steered it instead toward its own destruction.
verses.80: 'Children of Israel, We rescued you from your enemy, made an appointment with you on the mountain's right side, and sent down manna and quail for you.' Liberation is followed immediately by sustenance — freedom paired with provision.
verses.81: 'Eat from the good things We have given you, but do not overstep — or My anger will fall upon you, and whoever My anger falls upon has truly fallen.' Even freedom comes with limits meant to protect, not restrict for its own sake.
verses.82: 'Yet I am endlessly forgiving toward whoever repents, believes, acts righteously, and then stays the course.' The door back is always described as wide open, for anyone willing to walk through it.
verses.83: 'What made you hurry ahead of your people, Moses?' A question asked while Moses is up on the mountain, his people left behind.
verses.84: 'They are close behind me,' he answered, 'and I hurried to You, my Lord, so that You would be pleased with me.' Even his haste was an act of devotion, a longing to please rather than impatience.
verses.85: 'We have indeed tested your people in your absence,' came the reply, 'and the Samiri has led them astray.' The mountaintop intimacy is interrupted by news of betrayal below.
verses.86: Moses returned to his people angry and grieved. What he found waiting for him was nothing like what he had left behind.
verses.87: They explained: 'We did not break our promise to you by our own choice — we were carrying heavy loads of jewelry from that other people, so we threw them into the fire, and the Samiri did the same.' Excuses offered quickly, each one shifting blame elsewhere.
verses.88: And the Samiri shaped from it a calf — a body that produced a lowing sound — and they declared, 'This is your god, and the god of Moses,' though he himself had forgotten the truth. An entire nation, freshly rescued from slavery, bowed to something they had made with their own hands.
verses.89: Could they not see that it never answered them with a single word, and had no power to harm or help them at all? The emptiness of the idol was obvious to anyone willing to actually look.
verses.90: Aaron had already warned them before Moses returned: 'My people, this is only a test for you. Your true Lord is the Most Merciful — so follow me, and obey what I ask of you.' A brother's plea, spoken into a crowd already losing itself.
verses.91: But they answered: 'We will not stop devoting ourselves to it until Moses comes back to us.' Loyalty misplaced so completely that even a prophet's own brother could not turn it back.
verses.92: Moses turned to Aaron: 'What kept you, when you saw them going astray,'
verses.93: 'from following after me? Did you disobey what I asked of you?' Grief and anger tangled together in a single confrontation between brothers.
verses.94: 'Son of my mother,' Aaron pleaded, 'do not grab me by my beard or my head. I was afraid you would say I had split the Children of Israel apart and failed to honor your word.' Aaron's answer reveals he stayed to prevent worse division, not out of neglect.
verses.95: Moses turned instead to the source: 'And you, Samiri — what is your story?'
verses.96: 'I saw something they did not see,' he answered. 'I took a handful of dust from the messenger's footprint and threw it in — that is what my own soul enticed me to do.' A vague, almost mystical excuse for a very deliberate act of deception.
verses.97: 'Then go,' Moses told him. 'For the rest of your life you will only be able to say, “Do not touch me.” And there is still an appointment awaiting you that you will not escape. Now look at your god, the one you stayed so devoted to — we will burn it completely and scatter its ashes into the sea.' Isolation as punishment, and the idol's destruction made total and public.
verses.98: 'Your only true God is Allah — there is no deity but Him. His knowledge surrounds everything that exists.' The story's central lesson, stated plainly at last.
verses.99: This is how the accounts of what has already passed are related to you — and We have given you, from Our own presence, a message meant just for this moment. The story of Moses becomes, in the end, a message handed directly to Muhammad and to everyone listening after him.
verses.100: Whoever turns away from it will carry a heavy burden on the Day of Resurrection.
verses.101: They will remain under that weight forever — and what a terrible load to carry on that Day.
