verses.102: On the Day the Horn is blown, the guilty will be gathered together, their eyes dulled with dread.
verses.103: They will whisper among themselves, unable to agree: 'We stayed no longer than ten days.' Even memory itself will fail them in that moment.
verses.104: Allah knows best what they will say, when the most perceptive among them admits: 'You stayed no more than a single day.' Whole lifetimes will feel, in hindsight, like almost nothing.
verses.105: They ask you about the mountains — say: 'My Lord will scatter them completely, like dust carried off by wind.'
verses.106: 'And He will leave the earth a flat, empty plain,'
verses.107: 'with no valley and no hill left standing anywhere upon it.' The familiar landscape of the world dissolves entirely on that Day.
verses.108: That Day, everyone will follow the summoning voice without deviation, and every sound will be hushed before the Most Merciful — nothing heard but a faint whisper. The loudest crowd imaginable, reduced to near silence.
verses.109: On that Day, no intercession will help anyone except from the one to whom the Most Merciful has granted permission and whose words He accepts. Even pleading on another's behalf requires prior permission from the only One who can grant it.
verses.110: He knows what lies ahead of them and what lies behind them, while their own knowledge can never fully surround Him.
verses.111: Every face will bow low before the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence — and whoever carries injustice with them will have truly failed.
verses.112: But whoever does what is right while believing will fear neither being wronged nor being shortchanged.
verses.113: This is how We sent it down — an Arabic recitation — weaving warnings through it in every form, so that people might hold back from wrong, or so it might stir something like remembrance within them.
verses.114: Exalted is Allah, the true Sovereign. Do not rush ahead with the recitation before its revelation to you is complete — and say instead, 'My Lord, increase me in knowledge.' Even the Prophet is taught patience, and the humility to keep asking for more understanding.
verses.115: Long before, We had made a covenant with Adam, but he forgot it — We did not find in him firm resolve. Forgetfulness, it turns out, is written into the human story from its very first chapter.
verses.116: And when We told the angels, 'Bow down before Adam,' they all bowed — except Iblis, who refused.
verses.117: So We warned him: 'Adam, this one is truly an enemy to you and to your wife. Do not let him drive the two of you out of the garden, or you will suffer for it.'
verses.118: 'Here, you will never go hungry, and you will never be left without clothing.'
verses.119: 'And here you will never be thirsty, nor will you ever suffer from the heat of the sun.' A description of a life with every basic need already resolved.
verses.120: But Satan whispered temptation to him: 'Adam, shall I show you the tree of everlasting life, and a kingdom that never fades?' The oldest lie in existence, offered in the guise of a gift.
verses.121: So the two of them ate from it, and their nakedness became apparent to them, and they began covering themselves with leaves from the garden. Adam disobeyed his Lord, and he lost his way. A single act of forgetting unravels an entire state of ease.
verses.122: But afterward, his Lord chose him, turned to him in forgiveness, and guided him once more. The story does not end in exile without hope — it ends in restoration.
verses.123: He said: 'Go down from here, all of you, as enemies to one another. But whenever guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows it will neither lose their way nor fall into misery.'
verses.124: 'But whoever turns away from remembering Me will live a life narrowed and constrained, and We will raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.' Forgetting God, it turns out, shrinks even a life that looks outwardly full.
verses.125: He will ask, 'My Lord, why have You raised me blind, when I could once see clearly?'
verses.126: The answer will come: 'Just as Our signs came to you and you chose to forget them, so today you too are forgotten.' A mirror held up to the very forgetfulness that defined his life.
verses.127: And this is how We repay anyone who transgresses and refuses to believe in the signs of his Lord — and the punishment of the next life is even harsher, and it does not end.
verses.128: Has it not become clear to them, how many generations before them We destroyed — people whose empty dwellings they now walk through themselves? In that there are signs for anyone who still thinks.
verses.129: And were it not for a word already decreed by your Lord, and a time already appointed, the punishment would have arrived without delay.
verses.130: So bear patiently what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise before the sun rises and before it sets, and through parts of the night, and at both ends of the day — so that you may find contentment. Patience is paired here with a rhythm of remembrance stretched across the whole day.
verses.131: And do not let your eyes linger on the fleeting comforts We have given some of them to enjoy — just a flower of this worldly life, meant only to test them. What your Lord provides for you is better, and it lasts.
verses.132: Command your family to pray, and be steady in it yourself. We do not ask you for provision — We provide for you. And the best outcome belongs to a life lived in awareness of God.
verses.133: They ask, 'Why doesn't he bring us a sign from his Lord?' — as if clear evidence had not already reached them in the scriptures that came before.
verses.134: Had We destroyed them with punishment before this messenger came, they would have said, 'Our Lord, why didn't You send us a messenger, so we could have followed Your signs before we were humiliated and disgraced?' A complaint that only exists because it has already been answered by the very messenger sent to them.
verses.135: Say: 'Everyone is waiting — so wait as well. Soon enough you will know who has walked the even path, and who has truly been guided.' The surah closes not with a verdict, but with an open horizon, everyone left to see how their own story ends.

verses.1: Mankind's reckoning has drawn near, yet people carry on turned away, heedless — as if the horizon holds nothing waiting for them at all.
verses.2: No fresh reminder ever reaches them from their Lord without them receiving it half-listening, treating it almost like entertainment.
verses.3: Their hearts wander elsewhere while they whisper among themselves, the wrongdoers muttering, 'Isn't this just a man like us? Would you really fall for magic with your own eyes open?' Familiarity bred contempt instead of recognition.
verses.4: He answered them plainly: 'My Lord knows every word spoken in heaven and on earth — He is the Hearing, the Knowing.' No secret conversation was ever truly hidden from the One being mocked.
verses.5: Still they scrambled for an explanation — 'confused dreams,' or 'he made it up,' or 'he's just a poet' — and demanded he produce a sign like the prophets before him, as if truth owed them a spectacle.
verses.6: Not one city that We destroyed before them ever believed even after seeing such signs — so why would this generation be any different?
verses.7: Every messenger sent before this one was simply a man who received revelation — nothing more exotic than that. Ask those who carry earlier scripture, if you doubt it.
verses.8: None of the prophets were given bodies that never needed food, and none of them lived forever — they were fully human, carrying a message far greater than themselves.
verses.9: In the end, We fulfilled every promise made to them: We rescued them, and whoever else We willed to save, and We wiped out those who had crossed every line.
verses.10: We have sent down to you a Book that carries your own remembrance within it — will you still not use your reason?
verses.11: How many a wrongdoing city did We shatter, only to raise up an entirely different people in its place.
verses.12: And the moment they finally sensed Our punishment arriving, they turned and fled — too late, running from something they had ignored for years.
verses.13: 'Do not run,' came the mocking reply. 'Go back to the luxuries you were given, back to your homes — perhaps you'll be asked about them.' Their comfort, once a source of pride, became the very thing pointed to in their downfall.
verses.14: 'Oh, ruin has come upon us,' they cried out. 'We really were wrongdoers.'
verses.15: That cry never stopped until We reduced them to something like a harvested field, stripped bare and silent — a civilization mown down to nothing.
verses.16: We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything between them as some kind of idle amusement.
verses.17: Had We ever wanted a mere diversion, We could have found one within Ourselves — but that was never what any of this was for.
verses.18: Instead, We hurl truth against falsehood, and truth shatters it completely, and falsehood simply vanishes. Ruin awaits anyone still describing Us falsely.
verses.19: Everyone in the heavens and on earth belongs to Him. Even those nearest to Him — the angels — are never too proud to worship Him, and they never grow tired of it.
verses.20: They glorify Him endlessly, through night and day, without a single pause.
verses.21: Have these people instead taken gods from the earth capable of raising the dead? A serious claim, asked of powerless things.
verses.22: Had there been more than one god ruling the heavens and the earth, the whole system would have collapsed into ruin. So exalted is Allah, Lord of the Throne, far above whatever they imagine.
verses.23: He is never questioned about what He does — yet everyone else will be questioned about what they did.
verses.24: Have they taken other gods besides Him? Say: 'Bring your proof.' This very book carries the same message as everything revealed before it — yet most of them don't even understand that much, and simply turn away.
verses.25: Every single messenger sent before you received the same core revelation: 'There is no god but Me, so worship Me alone.' One message, echoing across every era.
verses.26: And still they claim, 'The Most Merciful has taken a son.' Exalted far above that! In truth, even the angels they imagine as His offspring are nothing more than honored servants.
verses.27: They never speak ahead of Him, and they act only according to His command — utterly obedient, nothing like children asserting independence.
verses.28: He knows everything before them and everything behind them, and they cannot intercede for anyone except whoever He approves — and even they tremble in awe of Him.
verses.29: Any one of them who dared to claim, 'I am a god besides Him' — that one We would repay with Hell. This is how We deal with wrongdoers, without exception.
verses.30: Have those who deny not considered that the heavens and the earth were once joined as a single mass, and We split them apart, and from water We brought forth every living thing? Will they still not believe?
verses.31: We placed firm mountains on the earth so it would not shake beneath them, and We carved out wide passes between them as roads, so people could find their way.
verses.32: We made the sky a protected ceiling overhead — and still they turn away from every sign contained within it.
verses.33: It is He who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon — each one gliding along its own path, swimming through the vastness like a swimmer through water.
verses.34: We never granted eternal life on earth to any man before you — so if you were to die, would they suddenly become immortal themselves?
verses.35: Every soul will taste death. We test all of you with hardship and with ease alike, and in the end, every one of you returns to Us.
verses.36: When the disbelievers see you, they treat you only as an object of mockery: 'Is this the one who talks about your gods?' — while denying the very mention of the Most Merciful in the same breath.
verses.37: Man was created out of haste. 'I will show you My signs,' He says, 'so do not rush Me.' Impatience, it turns out, is woven into human nature itself.
verses.38: 'When will this promise come, if you're telling the truth?' they demand — as if urgency could be summoned on command.
verses.39: If only those who deny could glimpse the moment when they will be unable to keep the Fire from their faces or their backs, and no one will come to help them.
verses.40: It will arrive without warning, catching them completely off guard — they won't be able to push it back, and no delay will be granted.
verses.41: Messengers before you were mocked too, and the very thing those mockers laughed at ended up surrounding them entirely.
verses.42: Say: 'Who could protect you, night or day, from the Most Merciful?' — yet they still turn away from any reminder of their own Lord.
verses.43: Do they have gods able to defend them instead of Us? Those gods cannot even help themselves, let alone shield anyone from Us.
verses.44: We granted good things to these people, and to their forefathers before them, until their lives stretched on comfortably. Do they not notice how We are steadily reducing the land at its edges? So who is really winning here?
verses.45: Say: 'I warn you only through what has been revealed to me.' But the deaf cannot hear a call, no matter how clearly the warning is given.
verses.46: If even the faintest breath of your Lord's punishment touched them, they would cry out at once, 'Oh, ruin has come upon us — we really were wrongdoers.'
verses.47: We will set up the scales of true justice for the Day of Resurrection, and no soul will be wronged in the slightest — even something as small as a mustard seed will be brought forward and weighed. We are more than sufficient as the One who accounts for everything.
verses.48: We had already given Moses and Aaron the Criterion, a light, and a reminder for those mindful of God.
verses.49: Those who hold their Lord in awe though they cannot see Him, and who tremble at the thought of the Hour.
verses.50: And this Qur'an is itself a blessed reminder that We have sent down — will you really remain unfamiliar with it?
verses.51: Long before this, We gave Abraham sound judgment, and We knew him thoroughly, inside and out.
verses.52: He said to his father and his people, 'What are these statues you're so devoted to?'
verses.53: 'We found our fathers worshipping them,' they replied — tradition offered as though it were proof.
verses.54: 'Then you, and your fathers before you, have clearly been lost the whole time,' he told them plainly.
verses.55: 'Have you come to us with the truth,' they scoffed, 'or are you just joking around?'
verses.56: 'No,' he answered, 'your true Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the One who created them — and I stand as a witness to that truth.'
verses.57: And privately, he made a resolve: 'By Allah, I will scheme against your idols the moment your backs are turned.'
verses.58: So he smashed them all into pieces, except for the largest one — leaving it standing so that the questions would inevitably return to it.
verses.59: 'Who has done this to our gods?' they demanded upon returning. 'Whoever it was, he is truly a wrongdoer.'
verses.60: 'We heard a young man mention them,' someone offered. 'They call him Abraham.'
verses.61: 'Then bring him out in front of everyone,' they decided, 'so people can witness it themselves.'
verses.62: 'Was it you who did this to our gods, Abraham?' they asked directly.
verses.63: 'No — it was that one, the largest of them,' he answered. 'Ask them yourselves, if they're able to speak.' A trap set with perfect logic.
verses.64: They turned inward, arguing among themselves: 'It really is you who are the wrongdoers here.'
verses.65: Then, just as quickly, they flipped back: 'You already know full well these idols cannot speak!' — caught between their own conscience and their pride.
verses.66: 'Then why,' he pressed, 'do you worship, instead of Allah, something that can neither help you nor harm you at all?'
verses.67: 'Shame on you, and on everything you worship besides Allah. Will you not finally use your reason?'
verses.68: 'Burn him,' they decided, 'and stand up for your gods, if you're going to do anything at all.' Violence as a substitute for an answer they could not give.
verses.69: But Allah commanded: 'Fire, be coolness and safety for Abraham.' The very element meant to destroy him became his shelter instead.
verses.70: They had intended real harm for him, but instead We turned them into the greatest losers of all.
verses.71: And We delivered him, along with Lot, to a land We had blessed for all the worlds.
verses.72: We also granted him Isaac, and Jacob as an additional gift — and We made every one of them righteous.
verses.73: We made them leaders, guiding others by Our command, and We inspired them toward good deeds, the establishment of prayer, and the giving of charity — they were devoted worshippers, through and through.
verses.74: To Lot We gave sound judgment and knowledge, and We rescued him from a city sunk in vile practices — truly a wicked, defiantly disobedient people.
verses.75: We welcomed him into Our mercy; he was among the righteous.
verses.76: And remember Noah, who called out long before, and We answered him, saving him and his family from the great calamity.
verses.77: We saved him from the people who rejected Our signs — truly an evil people, so We drowned every last one of them.
verses.78: And remember David and Solomon, when they were judging a case about a field — someone's sheep had wandered into it overnight and grazed it bare — and We witnessed the judgment they each reached.
verses.79: We granted Solomon the deeper understanding of that particular case, and to each of them We gave judgment and knowledge. We even made the mountains join David in glorifying Us, along with the birds — a choir of creation itself, orchestrated by Us.
verses.80: We taught him how to craft coats of armor, to protect all of you in times of battle. Will you not, then, be grateful for that?
verses.81: For Solomon, We subjected the wind, blowing forcefully wherever he commanded, carrying him toward the land We had blessed — for We have complete knowledge of all things.
verses.82: And among the devils, some dove for him beneath the sea and carried out other tasks besides — all of them kept firmly under Our watch.
verses.83: And remember Job, when he cried out to his Lord: 'Hardship has truly touched me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.'
verses.84: So We answered him and lifted every hardship that had struck him, and We restored his family to him, doubling it as a mercy from Us — a reminder for anyone who worships sincerely.
verses.85: And remember Ishmael, Idris, and Dhul-Kifl — every one of them among the patient.
verses.86: We welcomed them into Our mercy; they were truly among the righteous.
verses.87: And remember the man of the whale, who once stormed off in anger, assuming We would never take him to task for it. Then, in the utter darkness, he cried out: 'There is no god but You — glory be to You. I have truly wronged myself.'
verses.88: So We answered him and rescued him from that suffocating distress. This is how We save those who truly believe.
verses.89: And remember Zachariah, when he called out to his Lord: 'My Lord, do not leave me without an heir — though You are the best of all inheritors.'
verses.90: So We answered him, and We gave him John, and We made his wife capable of bearing a child once more. They were always quick toward good deeds, calling on Us in both hope and fear, forever humble before Us.
verses.91: And remember the one who guarded her chastity so completely — We breathed into her from Our own spirit, and We made her and her son a sign for all the worlds.
verses.92: This community of yours is truly one single community, and I am your one Lord — so worship Me alone.
verses.93: Yet people split their religion into fragments among themselves — but every fragment, in the end, returns to Us.
verses.94: So whoever does what is right while holding true belief will find their effort never denied — We are recording every bit of it.
verses.95: There is a firm decree upon any city We have destroyed: it will never return again.
verses.96: Until Gog and Magog are finally let loose, pouring down from every high point on the horizon.
verses.97: And when the true promise draws close, the eyes of the disbelievers will suddenly freeze wide with horror: 'We were so heedless of this — we really were the wrongdoers.'
verses.98: Truly, you and everything you worship besides Allah will become fuel for Hell — that is exactly where you are headed.
verses.99: Had those idols actually been gods, they would never have entered it — yet all of them will remain there forever.
verses.100: In there they will do nothing but groan endlessly, unable to hear anything at all.
verses.101: But those for whom We had already decreed the best outcome will be kept entirely far removed from it.
verses.102: They won't hear even the faintest sound of it, and they will live forever exactly as their hearts once longed to.
verses.103: The greatest terror of that Day will not trouble them in the least, and the angels will greet them warmly: 'This is your Day — the one you were always promised.'
verses.104: On the Day We fold up the heavens the way a scroll is rolled closed around its writing — just as We began the first creation, We will bring it back again. A promise We have bound Ourselves to. We will surely carry it out.
verses.105: We had already written in the Psalms, after the earlier reminder, that the righteous among Our servants would inherit the earth.
verses.106: There is truly enough in this for any people devoted to worship.
verses.107: And We did not send you except as a mercy poured out over all the worlds.
verses.108: Say: 'It has only ever been revealed to me that your God is One — so will you submit?'
verses.109: But if they turn away, say: 'I have delivered this message equally to all of you. I do not know whether what you've been promised is near or still far off.'
verses.110: 'He knows every word spoken aloud, and He knows everything you keep hidden.'
verses.111: 'And I do not know — perhaps this delay is simply a test for you, and a brief enjoyment meant to run its course.'
verses.112: He said: 'My Lord, judge with the truth. And our Lord is the Most Merciful, the One whose help is sought against everything you falsely describe.'

verses.1: O humanity, be mindful of your Lord — the convulsion of that Hour will be a truly terrible thing.
verses.2: On the Day you witness it, every nursing mother will forget the infant at her breast, every pregnant woman will lose what she carries, and you will see people staggering as if drunk, though they are not drunk at all — it is simply that Allah's punishment is severe beyond anything they've known.
verses.3: Among people there is one who argues about Allah without any real knowledge, following instead every rebellious devil down whatever path it leads.
verses.4: It has been decreed for that devil: whoever takes him as a companion will be led astray and steered straight toward the punishment of the Blaze.
verses.5: O people, if you doubt the Resurrection, consider this: We created you from dust, then from a drop, then from a clot, then from a lump of flesh, some formed and some unformed — so that We might make Our power clear to you. We settle whatever We will in the womb for an appointed time, then bring you out as an infant, until you reach maturity. Some of you die young, and some are returned to the most feeble old age, until they forget everything they once knew. And you see the earth lying lifeless — then We send rain upon it, and it stirs, swells, and bursts into every kind of beautiful growth.
verses.6: All of that is because Allah is the Truth, because He gives life to the dead, and because He has power over absolutely everything.
verses.7: And because the Hour is coming — there is no doubt in that — and Allah will raise up everyone in their graves.
verses.8: Among people there is also one who argues about Allah without knowledge, without guidance, and without any illuminating book to lean on.
verses.9: He turns his head away in arrogance, trying to lead others off the path of Allah. Disgrace follows him in this life, and on the Day of Resurrection We will make him taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.
verses.10: It will be said to him: 'This is what your own hands have earned — Allah is never unjust to His servants.'
verses.11: And among people is one who worships Allah only on the edge of belief — content when good things come his way, but the instant hardship strikes, he spins around and abandons it entirely. He has lost both this world and the next; that is the clearest kind of loss there is.
verses.12: He calls instead on things that can neither harm nor help him — the furthest a person can wander from the truth.
verses.13: He calls on something whose harm is nearer to him than any benefit it could ever offer — how wretched a protector, and how wretched a companion.
verses.14: But those who believe and act righteously — Allah will admit them into gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. Allah does exactly what He intends.
verses.15: Whoever thinks Allah will never support His Messenger in this life or the next — let him stretch a rope up to the ceiling and cut off his own breath, and see whether that desperate act erases what enrages him. Rage without power to change anything is only self-destruction.
verses.16: This is how We sent the Qur'an down: as clear, unmistakable verses — and Allah guides whoever He intends.
verses.17: Those who believe, the Jews, the Sabians, the Christians, the Magians, and those who associate others with Allah — Allah will judge between every one of them on the Day of Resurrection. He is witness over everything.
verses.18: Do you not see that everything in the heavens and everything on earth bows before Allah — the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, every moving creature, and many among people too? Yet for many others the punishment has already been justified. Whoever Allah humbles, no one else can honor — He does exactly what He wills.
verses.19: These are two opposing groups, disputing over their Lord. For those who disbelieve, garments of fire have already been cut out for them, and scalding water will be poured over their heads,
verses.20: melting away everything within their bodies, and their skin along with it.
verses.21: And for them there are hooked rods of iron.
verses.22: Every time they try to claw their way out of that torment, driven by anguish, they are forced back in: 'Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.'
verses.23: But Allah will admit those who believe and do what is right into gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, adorned there with bracelets of gold and pearl, dressed in silk.
verses.24: They were guided in this life toward good speech, and guided toward the path of the One deserving all praise.
verses.25: Those who disbelieve, who turn people away from Allah's path and from the Sacred Mosque — which We made equally for everyone, resident and visitor alike — and whoever intends wrongdoing or deviation there, We will make them taste a painful punishment.
verses.26: And remember when We showed Abraham the site of the House, telling him: 'Associate nothing with Me, and keep My House pure for those who circle it, those who stand in prayer, and those who bow and prostrate.'
verses.27: 'And announce the pilgrimage to all people — they will come to you on foot and on every worn-out camel, arriving from every distant, far-off path.'
verses.28: So that they might witness benefits meant for themselves, and mention Allah's name on the appointed days over the livestock He has provided them — so eat from it, and feed the poor and those in real need.
verses.29: Then let them tend to their unkempt appearance, fulfill their vows, and circle the ancient House once more.
verses.30: That is the command — and whoever honors the sacred limits set by Allah, it is better for him before his Lord. Livestock has been made lawful for you, except what has already been made clear — so avoid the filth of idols, and avoid speaking falsehood entirely,
verses.31: turning only toward Allah, associating nothing with Him. Whoever associates anything with Allah is like someone who has fallen from the sky, only to be snatched by birds or swept away by the wind into some distant, ruinous place.
verses.32: That is the truth — and whoever honors the symbols set by Allah, that honoring comes straight from the piety within their hearts.
verses.33: In these sacrificial animals you find benefits for a set time, until their place of offering is reached at the ancient House.
verses.34: For every community We appointed a rite of sacrifice, so they might mention Allah's name over the livestock He provided them. Your God is One God, so submit to Him alone — and give good news to those who remain humble,
verses.35: whose hearts tremble at the mention of Allah, who bear patiently whatever befalls them, who keep up prayer, and who give generously from what We have provided them.
verses.36: The camels and cattle We have made part of the sacred symbols of Allah — there is good for you in them. So mention Allah's name over them as they stand in line for sacrifice, and once they have fallen, eat from them and feed both the needy who ask and the needy who do not. This is how We placed them at your service, so that you might be grateful.
verses.37: Their meat never reaches Allah, and neither does their blood — what reaches Him is the sincerity of heart behind the act. This is how We placed them at your service, so that you might glorify Allah for guiding you. Give good news to those who do what is right.
verses.38: Allah defends those who believe — He has no love at all for anyone who is treacherous and ungrateful.
verses.39: Permission to fight has been given to those who are being attacked, because they have been wronged — and Allah is fully capable of granting them victory.
verses.40: Those who were driven from their homes without any justification, only because they said, 'Our Lord is Allah.' And were it not for Allah restraining some people by means of others, monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques where His name is constantly remembered would have been torn down long ago. Allah will surely support whoever supports His cause — He is Powerful, Mighty.
verses.41: Those who, if We gave them power in the land, would establish prayer, give charity, encourage what is right, and forbid what is wrong. To Allah belongs the outcome of every affair.
verses.42: If they deny you, remember that the people of Noah denied their prophet before them, as did 'Aad and Thamud,
verses.43: and the people of Abraham, and the people of Lot,
verses.44: and the dwellers of Madyan too — and Moses was denied as well. I gave every one of those deniers time before I finally seized them — and how terrible My rejection of them turned out to be.
verses.45: How many a city did We destroy while it was steeped in wrongdoing — now lying collapsed into ruin, alongside abandoned wells and towering palaces gone silent.
verses.46: Have they never traveled through the land with hearts capable of understanding, or ears capable of truly hearing? It isn't the eyes that go blind — it's the hearts inside the chest that lose their sight.
verses.47: They push you to hurry the punishment along, but Allah never breaks His promise. A single day with your Lord is like a thousand years by the way you count them.
verses.48: How many a city did I allow to carry on in comfort while it was steeped in wrongdoing — then I finally seized it, and to Me is where everything returns.
verses.49: Say: 'O people, I am nothing to you but a clear warner.'
verses.50: Those who believe and do what is right will receive forgiveness and generous provision.
verses.51: But those who work relentlessly against Our verses, trying to undermine them — they are the companions of the blazing Fire.
verses.52: We never sent a messenger or prophet before you without Satan attempting to slip something into what he recited — but Allah wipes away whatever Satan throws in, and then firmly establishes His verses. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
verses.53: This happens so that Satan's interference becomes a trial for those whose hearts carry disease and those whose hearts have hardened — the wrongdoers are truly deep in opposition.
verses.54: And so that those given real knowledge would recognize it as truth from their Lord, believe in it, and let their hearts humbly settle into it. Allah surely guides those who believe onto a straight path.
verses.55: But those who disbelieve will not stop doubting it until either the Hour catches them off guard, or the punishment of a desolate, barren Day arrives.
verses.56: On that Day, all authority belongs to Allah alone, and He will judge between them. Those who believed and did what was right will find themselves in gardens of pure delight.
verses.57: But those who disbelieved and rejected Our verses — for them there is a humiliating punishment.
verses.58: Those who left their homes for Allah's sake and then were killed or died — Allah will surely grant them a fine provision. Allah is truly the best of all providers.
verses.59: He will surely welcome them into a place they will be pleased with; Allah is All-Knowing, endlessly patient.
verses.60: That is the promise. And whoever answers injustice with an equal response, and is then wronged again — Allah will surely support him. Allah is Pardoning, Forgiving.
verses.61: That is because Allah folds the night into the day and folds the day into the night, and because Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
verses.62: That is because Allah is the Truth, while whatever they call upon besides Him is nothing but falsehood, and because Allah is the Most High, the Most Great.
verses.63: Do you not see how Allah sends rain down from the sky, and the earth turns green in response? Allah is Subtle in His workings, fully Aware.
verses.64: Everything in the heavens and everything on the earth belongs to Him. Allah is truly Free of any need, worthy of all praise.
verses.65: Do you not see how Allah has placed everything on earth at your service, along with ships sailing the sea by His command? He holds the sky back from falling upon the earth, except by His permission — Allah is Kind and Merciful toward people.
verses.66: It is He who gave you life, then causes you to die, and will bring you back to life once more — and yet mankind remains so ungrateful.
verses.67: For every community We set rites for them to follow — so do not let them draw you into pointless argument over this. Invite them instead to your Lord; you are firmly upon straight guidance.
verses.68: And if they still argue with you, say: 'Allah knows best everything you do.'
verses.69: Allah will judge between all of you on the Day of Resurrection concerning everything you used to disagree about.
verses.70: Do you not know that Allah knows everything in the heavens and the earth? All of it is contained in a Record — that is effortless for Allah.
verses.71: They worship, besides Allah, things for which He never sent down any authority, things about which they have no real knowledge — and the wrongdoers will find no helper at all.
verses.72: When Our clear verses are recited to them, you can see the disapproval written on the faces of the disbelievers — they nearly lunge at those who recite Our verses to them. Say: 'Shall I tell you of something even worse than that? It is the Fire that Allah has promised the disbelievers — and what a miserable destination.'
verses.73: O people, here is an example, so listen closely: those you call upon besides Allah could never create even a single fly, even if they all joined forces to try. And if a fly snatched something away from them, they could not even take it back. How feeble both the seeker and the sought truly are.
verses.74: They have not valued Allah with the reverence He truly deserves — Allah is Powerful, Mighty beyond comparison.
verses.75: Allah chooses messengers from among the angels and from among people; Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
verses.76: He knows everything before them and everything that will come after them, and every matter returns, in the end, to Allah.
verses.77: O you who believe, bow, prostrate, worship your Lord, and do good — so that you might succeed.
verses.78: And strive for Allah as He truly deserves to be striven for. He has chosen you, and He has placed no real hardship upon you in this religion — it is the faith of your father Abraham. He is the One who named you 'Muslims,' both before, in earlier scriptures, and in this revelation — so that the Messenger may bear witness over you, and you may bear witness over people. So establish prayer, give charity, and hold firmly to Allah. He is your true Protector — the finest Protector, and the finest Helper there is.

verses.1: Success is declared here as a certainty for one particular kind of person — the believers — and what follows are not vague hopes but the exact qualities that define them, laid out like a checklist for anyone wondering what genuine faith actually looks like in practice.
verses.2: The first mark is humility in prayer — not merely standing and bowing on schedule, but a stillness of heart so complete that the outside world fades and only the conversation with God remains.
verses.3: The second mark is turning away from idle talk — the empty chatter, mockery, and meaningless noise that fills so many hours, deliberately avoided because a mind cluttered with nonsense has less room for what actually matters.
verses.4: The third mark is active in giving — purifying wealth through zakat, not as a tax grudgingly paid but as a cleansing act, proof that what is owned was never meant to be hoarded.
verses.5: The fourth mark is guarding one's chastity — treating desire not as something to indulge without boundary but as something sacred enough to protect.
verses.6: An exception is carved out immediately — what is shared with a spouse or those rightfully theirs carries no blame, because the guarding spoken of here is about discipline, not denial of what is lawful.
verses.7: But anyone who seeks satisfaction beyond those bounds has stepped past the line — the verse names them transgressors, not out of harshness, but because the boundary itself was drawn for their own protection.
verses.8: The fifth mark is faithfulness — keeping trusts intact and honoring covenants, whether spoken aloud or simply understood, because a believer's word is meant to be as solid as their prayer.
verses.9: The sixth mark returns to prayer, but this time to its consistency — guarding the five daily prayers not as an occasional habit but as an unbroken rhythm structuring the whole of life.
verses.10: These are the ones — the verse says — who will inherit al-Firdaus, singled out from among every other consequence, because their qualities were not performance but substance.
verses.11: They will inherit it and abide there forever, a promise made not to reward wealth or lineage but to reward exactly the six qualities just described — humility, restraint, generosity, chastity, faithfulness, and consistency.
verses.12: The scene shifts entirely now, from the believer's character to the believer's origin — man, the verse says, was created from an extract of clay, a substance as humble as the earth itself, the starting point for every person who has ever lived.
verses.13: That extract was placed as a drop of fluid in a secure resting place, hidden and protected, the earliest, most fragile beginning of a life that would one day stand before its Creator.
verses.14: From that drop, a clinging clot forms, then flesh, then bone, then flesh wrapping the bone again — each stage named deliberately, as if daring anyone paying attention to call this process accidental. Then a soul is breathed in, and a completely new creation stands where a drop once was. Blessed is Allah, the verse exclaims, the best of creators.
verses.15: After all of this — the layered, deliberate architecture of a human life — the verse states plainly: then you will die. The same care that shaped a person out of clay guarantees an equally certain end.
verses.16: And then, on the Day of Resurrection, you will be raised again — the very creation described moments ago repeated in reverse, proof that the One who could build a person from nothing can just as easily rebuild one from dust.
verses.17: Above all of this stands the architecture of the heavens themselves — seven layered paths, and never once has Allah been unmindful of any part of what He created, not the smallest human cell nor the vastest expanse of sky.
verses.18: Rain falls from the sky in a measure that is neither too much nor too little, and it is made to settle in the earth — yet the verse adds a quiet warning: He is just as capable of taking it all away.
verses.19: With that measured rain, gardens of palm trees and grapevines spring up, heavy with fruit for eating, a harvest so abundant it becomes its own quiet proof of a Provider paying close attention.
verses.20: A particular tree is singled out — one that grows from Mount Sinai, producing oil and a relish for those who eat, a small, specific detail meant to root the whole discussion of divine provision in something tangible and familiar.
verses.21: Even livestock become a lesson: within their bodies is milk to drink, and many other benefits besides, and from them comes food — ordinary animals turned, on reflection, into evidence of design too consistent to be chance.
verses.22: And on both livestock and ships you are carried — across land and across sea — two different modes of travel, both made possible by the same hand that shaped rain, fruit, and cattle.
verses.23: The story now turns to history itself, beginning with Noah, sent to his people with the same message every messenger would carry: worship Allah alone, for you have no god besides Him — will you not be mindful?
verses.24: The leaders among his people, the ones who had refused to believe, dismissed him instantly: this is nothing but a man like yourselves, seeking superiority over you; had Allah willed, He would have sent angels — we never heard of such a thing among our forefathers.
verses.25: Their verdict was blunt: he is only a man possessed by madness, so watch him for a while and see what becomes of it — a charge repeated against nearly every prophet who ever tried to wake a sleeping people.
verses.26: Noah, met with mockery instead of belief, turned to his Lord with a plea stripped of theatrics: my Lord, help me, for they have denied me.
verses.27: Allah's answer came as instruction, precise and practical: build the ship under Our watch and by Our revelation, and when Our command arrives and water gushes from the earth's very oven, take aboard a pair from every kind, along with your family — except those already destined to drown — and do not plead for the wrongdoers, for they are about to be overwhelmed.
verses.28: Once aboard, Noah was told exactly what to say — praise be to Allah who saved us from the wrongdoing people — a moment of survival transformed instantly into an act of gratitude rather than relief alone.
verses.29: He was taught to pray further still: my Lord, let me land at a blessed landing place, for You are the best to provide such landings — even deliverance, the verse suggests, should be met with continued dependence, not self-congratulation.
verses.30: In all of this, the verse says, there are signs — the ark, the flood, the rescue — and We are always testing, always placing proof in front of people to see who will actually look.
verses.31: After Noah's generation, another was raised in its place, a new people given their own chance at the same message.
verses.32: A messenger from among them was sent, saying the identical words Noah had said: worship Allah, you have no other god — will you not be mindful?
verses.33: But the elite of that people, comfortable in the life they were living and denying the meeting of the Hereafter, gave the same tired dismissal: this is only a man like you, eating what you eat and drinking what you drink.
verses.34: They warned each other: obey a man just like yourselves, and you will surely be losers — as if shared humanity disqualified someone from carrying truth, rather than being the very reason he could deliver it.
verses.35: They mocked his message about resurrection outright: does he promise you that when you die and become dust and bones, you will be brought out again?
verses.36: Far, far off, they said, is what you are promised — impossible, unbelievable, a fantasy dressed up as prophecy.
verses.37: There is nothing, they insisted, but this worldly life; we die, we live, and we will never be resurrected — a flat denial of anything beyond what the eyes could see.
verses.38: He is only a man who has invented a lie about Allah, they concluded, and we will never believe him — certainty built entirely on assumption.
verses.39: The messenger, worn down by their rejection, prayed as Noah had: my Lord, help me, for they have denied me.
verses.40: Allah's response carried both patience and inevitability: in a little while, they will surely become regretful — the mockery they wore so confidently would not last.
verses.41: The blast seized them in truth, and We made them like scattered debris — so away with the wrongdoing people, a sentence closing an entire civilization in a single breath.
verses.42: Then, after them, We raised up other generations — the pattern repeating, one people replacing another across the sweep of history.
verses.43: No nation can hasten its appointed time nor delay it — every civilization, however powerful it feels in its own moment, moves on a clock it does not control.
verses.44: Messenger after messenger followed, one after another, and every time a messenger came to a nation, they denied him — so We made them follow one another into ruin and turned them into cautionary tales; away with a people who do not believe.
verses.45: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs and clear authority.
verses.46: To Pharaoh and his council — but they responded with arrogance, for they were a haughty people, unwilling to bow to a message that threatened their power.
verses.47: They said: shall we believe two men like ourselves, whose people are enslaved to us? — using Moses and Aaron's very humanity, once again, as an excuse to dismiss them.
verses.48: So they denied them both and became of those destroyed — the same fate that met every people before them who chose pride over truth.
verses.49: And We gave Moses the Scripture, so that his people might be guided — the very confrontation with Pharaoh was never the end of the story, only the doorway to a fuller revelation.
verses.50: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, and sheltered them on high ground, level and with a flowing spring — a quiet scene of provision and protection extended to two people the world had tried to isolate.
verses.51: O messengers, eat from the good and lawful things and do righteous deeds — the address widens now, speaking to every messenger across every era, as if gathering their entire mission into a single instruction.
verses.52: This community of yours is one single community, and I am your Lord, so be mindful of Me — one message, one thread, running underneath every prophet ever sent.
verses.53: Yet people tore their unity into fragments, each faction delighting in whatever piece of the truth it happened to be holding — division born not from confusion but from preference.
verses.54: So leave them, the verse instructs, in their confusion for a time — not abandonment out of indifference, but the space given to those who have chosen not to listen.
verses.55: Do they think that the wealth and children We extend to them are proof of Our favor toward them? A question meant to unsettle the comfortable assumption that ease is always a sign of blessing.
verses.56: No — they simply do not perceive that these are only means, hurried along to them, testing them rather than rewarding them.
verses.57: Contrast that now with those who are ever mindful out of awe of their Lord, hearts trembling not from fear of loss but from reverence for the One they answer to.
verses.58: Who believe in the signs of their Lord, whatever form they take, never dismissing them as coincidence.
verses.59: Who never associate partners with their Lord, keeping worship undivided and undiluted.
verses.60: Who give whatever they give — charity, kindness, sacrifice — while their hearts still tremble, aware that they are returning to their Lord and that no good deed guarantees comfort in judgment.
verses.61: It is these who are racing toward good deeds, and they will be the ones who reach them first — urgency reframed here not as anxiety but as devotion in motion.
verses.62: No soul is burdened beyond what it can bear, and with Us is a record that speaks the truth — no one, in the end, will be wronged in the reckoning.
verses.63: But the hearts of the wrongdoers are in confusion over all of this, distracted from it by other concerns, and their deeds trail far short of what such confusion allows them to see clearly.
verses.64: Until — when those living comfortably are finally seized by punishment — they cry out in sudden distress, the ease that once numbed them offering no protection at all.
verses.65: Do not cry out today, comes the answer, for no help will reach you from Us — a closing of the very door they once assumed would always stay open.
verses.66: My verses were recited to you, and you used to turn back on your heels — truth offered directly, and rejected just as directly.
verses.67: Arrogant toward it, gathering at night to talk against it, treating the very words meant to save them as something to be mocked in private conversation.
verses.68: Have they never reflected on the Word itself, weighing it honestly rather than dismissing it on reflex?
verses.69: Or has something come to them that never came to their forefathers, some unprecedented demand that excuses their refusal? The question exposes that no such excuse actually exists.
verses.70: Or do they say he is possessed? No — he has brought them the truth, but most of them despise the truth the moment it actually arrives at their door.
verses.71: And if the truth had followed their own desires instead of what it actually says, the heavens, the earth, and everyone in them would have fallen into ruin — yet still, given their own reminder, they turn away from it.
verses.72: Are you asking them for payment for this message? The provision of your Lord is better than anything they could offer, and He is the best of providers.
verses.73: You call them to a straight path, laid out clearly and without hidden cost.
verses.74: Yet those who do not believe in the Hereafter keep swerving away from that very path, unwilling to walk toward a destination they refuse to believe exists.
verses.75: Even if We showed them mercy and lifted every hardship they currently face, they would only persist further in their transgression, blind and wandering — relief would not have changed them.
verses.76: We seized them with punishment, and still they did not surrender to their Lord nor humble themselves before Him.
verses.77: Not until We opened upon them a gate of severe punishment did they finally arrive at despair — sudden, total, and far too late to change anything.
verses.78: He is the One who made for you hearing, sight, and hearts — and how little you give thanks for gifts so constant they go almost unnoticed.
verses.79: He is the One who multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will all be gathered in the end, however scattered your life has taken you.
verses.80: He is the One who gives life and causes death, and His alone is the alternation of night and day — will you not use your reason and finally connect the pattern?
verses.81: Yet instead, they say what their ancestors said before them, repeating an old denial as if it had never been answered.
verses.82: When we die and turn to dust and bones, will we really be raised again? — spoken as though the question itself were proof enough of impossibility.
verses.83: We were promised this before, and our forefathers before us — this is nothing but ancient tales, they say, dismissing continuity of message as mere repetition of myth.
verses.84: Say to them: to whom belongs the earth and everyone in it, if you truly know the answer?
verses.85: They will say, to Allah — so say in return, will you not then take heed of what you yourselves just admitted?
verses.86: Say: who is Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Mighty Throne?
verses.87: They will say, Allah — so say, will you not then be mindful of Him, having already conceded who holds it all?
verses.88: Say: in whose hand is the dominion of all things, the One who protects but is never protected, if you truly know?
verses.89: They will say, Allah's — so say, then how are you so deluded, believing one thing and living as though you believed another?
verses.90: We brought them the truth, plainly and repeatedly, and indeed they are the ones speaking falsely, not He.
verses.91: Allah has never taken a son, nor is there any god along with Him — for if there were, each god would have taken what he created for himself, and some would have tried to overpower others; exalted is Allah far above what they describe.
verses.92: Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, He is exalted far above whatever they associate with Him — a closing statement on divinity as absolute and undivided as the argument that preceded it.
verses.93: Say: my Lord, if You should show me what they are promised.
verses.94: My Lord, do not place me among the wrongdoing people — a prayer not for comfort but for protection from ever standing among those he has spent his whole mission warning.
verses.95: Indeed We are able to show you exactly what We have promised them — the punishment is neither uncertain nor delayed by any lack of power.
verses.96: Repel the evil deed with one that is better — a striking instruction to answer harm not with matching harm but with something finer, disarming cruelty through decency rather than escalation. We are most knowing of what they describe.
verses.97: And say: my Lord, I seek refuge in You from the whisperings of devils.
verses.98: And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, from their being present near me — an admission that even a messenger needs protection from what cannot be seen.
verses.99: Then, when death finally comes to one who denied all of this, he says: my Lord, send me back.
verses.100: That I might do righteousness in what I left behind — but no, it is only a word he speaks that will never come to pass; before them now stands a barrier until the very Day they are resurrected, a gate that has already closed.
verses.101: When the Trumpet is blown, no ties of kinship will remain between them that Day, and they will not even turn to ask about one another — the bonds that once defined an entire life dissolve in a single blast.
verses.102: Those whose scales are heavy with good deeds — these are the successful ones, exactly as promised in the opening verses of this surah.
verses.103: But those whose scales are light have lost their own souls, and in Hell they will remain.
verses.104: The Fire will burn their faces, and they will grimace within it, teeth bared in a permanent, involuntary expression of agony.
verses.105: Were My verses not recited to you, and did you not deny them? — the question that requires no further answer than the record itself already provides.
verses.106: They will say: our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a people astray, offering an explanation that changes nothing about what comes next.
verses.107: Our Lord, remove us from here — if we return to what we did, we would truly be wrongdoers, a final promise made too late to matter.
verses.108: He will say: remain in it, despised, and do not speak to Me — a silence imposed after every excuse has already been heard and rejected.
verses.109: There was a group among My servants who used to say: our Lord, we believe, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of the merciful.
verses.110: But you mocked them until it made you forget My remembrance entirely, and you used to laugh at them, treating sincerity itself as a joke.
verses.111: Today I have rewarded them for their patience, for they are the ones who have truly succeeded — the very people once ridiculed for their faith are the ones vindicated in the end.
verses.112: He will ask: how many years did you remain on earth? — a question meant to expose how small a lifetime looks once it is over.
verses.113: They will answer: we remained a day, or part of a day — ask those who kept count, the length of an entire life shrinking to almost nothing in memory.
verses.114: He will say: you stayed only a little; if only you had known it at the time, you might have lived differently.
verses.115: Did you think that We created you without purpose, and that you would never be returned to Us? — the question that undercuts every excuse offered throughout the entire surah.
verses.116: Exalted is Allah, the True King; there is no god but Him, Lord of the Noble Throne — the final declaration returning, as always, to the singularity of God.
verses.117: Whoever invokes another god alongside Allah has no proof for it whatsoever, and his reckoning is with his Lord alone; indeed, the disbelievers will never succeed.
verses.118: And say: my Lord, forgive and have mercy, for You are the best of the merciful — the surah that opened with the qualities of successful believers closes with the very words a believer might say in the end, humility and hope offered in the same breath.

verses.1: This surah is sent down and made obligatory, not offered as suggestion. Clear signs are placed inside it so that anyone who reflects might actually remember, not merely read and move on.
verses.2: For the man and woman who commit adultery, a hundred lashes each is prescribed, and pity for either is not permitted to soften a law meant to protect the whole community. A group of believers is told to witness the punishment, so the deterrent is felt collectively, not hidden away in private.
verses.3: A person given to this sin, the verse notes, tends to draw toward someone of similar character or an idolater, and the reverse holds true as well. For believers, such pairing is placed off-limits, since a settled, righteous home cannot easily share space with habitual sin.
verses.4: Anyone who accuses a chaste woman of adultery without producing four witnesses faces eighty lashes and permanent loss of credibility as a witness — because an accusation this serious, thrown carelessly, becomes its own form of harm.
verses.5: An exception is carved out for those who repent afterward and reform themselves. Forgiveness reaches even here, refusing to let one reckless accusation define a person forever.
verses.6: When a husband accuses his own wife and has no other witness, the process shifts: he swears four times by Allah that he is telling the truth.
verses.7: A fifth oath follows, calling Allah's curse upon himself if he is lying — a self-imposed weight meant to keep the whole process honest.
verses.8: The punishment is lifted from her if she, in turn, swears four times that he is lying.
verses.9: Her fifth oath calls Allah's wrath upon herself if he had been truthful — mutual, solemn oath-taking replacing raw accusation with real accountability on both sides.
verses.10: Were it not for Allah's grace and mercy, and His readiness to accept repentance, a system this delicate would have collapsed long ago under ordinary human weakness.
verses.11: A slander was invented and passed hand to hand by a cluster of people close to the community. The verse insists this ordeal was not pure hardship — it exposed hearts, and good came out of it despite the malice behind it. Each person who spread the story carries their own share of the sin, and whoever set it in motion carries the heaviest weight of all.
verses.12: The believers who heard the rumor are gently rebuked: why did you not assume good of one another and say plainly, 'this is an obvious lie,' instead of letting suspicion take root?
verses.13: Why did the accusers not bring four witnesses, as truth demands? Since they could not, the verse settles the matter bluntly — in Allah's judgment, they are simply liars.
verses.14: Had it not been for Allah's grace and mercy shielding the community, in this life and the next, the damage from rushing into that rumor would have been severe.
verses.15: The story passed from tongue to tongue and mouth to mouth, repeated as trivial gossip, when in Allah's sight it was an enormous matter.
verses.16: Why, upon hearing it, did no one say, 'it is not right for us to even speak of this — glory be to You, this is a monstrous slander'?
verses.17: Allah warns the believers plainly never to let something like this happen again, if faith means anything to them at all.
verses.18: The verses are spelled out in such detail here precisely because Allah is All-Knowing and All-Wise — nothing about this teaching is left vague by accident.
verses.19: Those who take pleasure in seeing immorality spread among believers are promised a painful reckoning, in this life and the next. Allah knows the full truth of every rumor in ways people simply cannot.
verses.20: Again the refrain returns: were it not for Allah's grace, mercy, and gentleness, this community would have been undone by its own gossip long before healing was possible.
verses.21: Believers are warned not to trail behind Satan's footsteps, since his whole agenda is to normalize immorality and wrongdoing. Without Allah's grace, not a single person would ever purify themselves — purification is a gift extended, not an achievement earned alone.
verses.22: Those blessed with wealth and standing are urged not to swear off helping relatives, the poor, or those who left home for faith's sake, even after being hurt by them. Forgive and overlook instead — does anyone here not want Allah's own forgiveness in return?
verses.23: Those who accuse chaste, unsuspecting believing women face a curse in this world and the next, and a tremendous punishment awaits them.
verses.24: On that Day, it will not be witnesses or lawyers speaking — their own tongues, hands, and feet will testify against them, describing exactly what they did.
verses.25: On that Day, Allah will settle every account in full, in perfect truth, so plainly that no one is left doubting that His judgment is the only judgment that ever mattered.
verses.26: Corrupt words gravitate toward corrupt people, and pure words toward pure people. The pure are declared innocent of whatever ugly things are said about them, and forgiveness and generous provision wait for them instead.
verses.27: Believers are told never to enter a home other than their own without first asking permission and greeting its residents — a small courtesy that protects everyone's dignity and is, the verse notes, simply better for all involved.
verses.28: If no one answers, wait for permission rather than entering, and if asked to leave, leave gracefully — it is the more dignified path, and Allah is fully aware of everything anyone does.
verses.29: An exception is made for spaces that aren't lived in but hold some benefit for those entering, since Allah knows exactly what is revealed and what stays hidden.
verses.30: Believing men are instructed to lower their gaze and guard their modesty — a discipline that keeps them pure at the level of the eyes long before it ever becomes a matter of action.
verses.31: Believing women receive a matching instruction — lower the gaze, guard modesty, and let a covering fall over what is usually left exposed — while this full covering is meant chiefly for strangers rather than the intimate circle of family. The verse closes by inviting everyone, without exception, to turn back to Allah, so that real success might still be reached.
verses.32: Believers are urged to help the unmarried among them, and the righteous among those in their care, find marriage, without letting poverty stand in the way — Allah's provision has room to expand for those who take this step in good faith.
verses.33: Those unable to marry are told to remain patient and chaste until Allah opens a way; those seeking to buy their own freedom are to be supported if good is seen in them; and no one is permitted to push a person into degrading work for profit when that person only wants to remain chaste — coercion like that poisons the very idea of provision.
verses.34: Clarifying verses have been sent down, alongside the stories of communities that came before, all serving as guidance for anyone determined to live conscientiously.
verses.35: Allah is described here as the Light of everything that exists — pictured as a lamp glowing inside a niche, the glass around it so clear it looks like a radiant star, fed by oil from a blessed olive tree belonging to neither east nor west, an oil so pure it seems to glow before any flame even touches it. Light upon light — and Allah guides toward this Light whoever He wills, offering images like this because He knows all things.
verses.36: This Light shines brightest, the verse says, in houses Allah has honored, where His name is remembered morning and evening by those who gather there in devotion.
verses.37: These are people no transaction, however profitable, can pull away from remembering Allah, keeping up prayer, and giving in charity — hearts anchored against a Day when even the steadiest hearts and eyes will tremble.
verses.38: Their reward will match the very best of what they did, with even more added from Allah's own bounty — a giving with no ceiling for whoever He chooses.
verses.39: For those who reject the truth, their good deeds are compared to a mirage shimmering across a desert — the thirsty traveler races toward it expecting water, only to find nothing there at all, except Allah waiting to settle the account in full, swiftly and completely.
verses.40: Or their state is like darkness stacked upon darkness inside a deep, storm-tossed sea — wave over wave, cloud over both — so thick a person can barely see their own hand held up before them. Whoever Allah withholds light from will simply have no light at all.
verses.41: Everything in the heavens and earth, even birds mid-flight with wings outstretched, is shown glorifying Allah, each creature already knowing its own way of prayer and praise. Allah is fully aware of everything they do.
verses.42: To Allah alone belongs the entire dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to Him alone everything eventually returns.
verses.43: Picture clouds gently driven, then joined together, then piled into towering masses from which rain finally breaks free; picture hail falling from mountains of cloud in the sky, striking some and sparing others by His will, with lightning so intense it could almost steal a person's sight.
verses.44: Night gives way to day and day gives way to night, endlessly alternating — and in that steady rhythm alone, the verse says, there is a lesson for anyone willing to actually see it.
verses.45: Every living creature was made from water, yet each moves differently — some crawl on their bellies, some walk on two legs, some move on four — a reminder that Allah creates however He wills, with total power over everything that exists.
verses.46: Clarifying verses were sent down for exactly this reason, and Allah guides whoever He wills toward a path that is straight and unmistakable.
verses.47: Some claim belief in Allah and His Messenger and promise obedience, yet a portion of them quietly turns away right after saying it — proof that the claim of faith and the reality of faith are not always the same thing.
verses.48: Even when called to let Allah and His Messenger judge between them, some among them still turn aside.
verses.49: Yet if the judgment happens to favor them, they suddenly come forward eager and compliant.
verses.50: Is there sickness in their hearts, or doubt clouding their judgment, or fear that Allah and His Messenger would somehow be unfair? The verse answers plainly — no, they themselves are the ones acting unjustly.
verses.51: True believers, when called to let Allah and His Messenger judge between them, have only one response available: 'we hear, and we obey' — and it is precisely these people who go on to flourish.
verses.52: Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, holds Allah in awe, and stays mindful of Him — these are the ones who actually attain what they were seeking all along.
verses.53: Some swear their strongest oaths that they would march out immediately if commanded. The response given is simple — stop swearing, obedience already known is worth more than words, and Allah is fully aware of everything regardless.
verses.54: The instruction is to obey Allah and obey the Messenger; if people turn away, the Messenger still carries out what was asked of him, and each person remains responsible for their own share — his task was only ever to deliver the message clearly.
verses.55: Allah promises those who believe and act righteously that He will establish them firmly in the land, just as He established those before them, secure their way of life, and replace their fear with peace — on the single condition that worship remains devoted to Him alone. Anyone who turns to disbelief after all this has stepped outside the bounds of loyalty.
verses.56: Establish prayer, give charity, and obey the Messenger, so that mercy might reach you in return.
verses.57: Disbelievers should not imagine they can outrun Allah anywhere on this earth — the Fire is described as their true and final destination, the worst place any soul could end up.
verses.58: Even within one's own household, those not yet mature and those under one's care are asked to seek permission before entering a private space at three particular times of day — moments of natural undress and rest — after which the household returns comfortably to shared, open living. This is how Allah makes His verses clear, and He is both Knowing and Wise.
verses.59: Once children reach maturity, the same courtesy of asking permission that applied to the adults before them now applies to them as well — clarity, once again, laid out plainly.
verses.60: Older women who no longer expect marriage are permitted some ease in modest dress, so long as they aren't putting adornment on display, though choosing greater modesty is still described as the better path.
verses.61: No blame falls on the blind, the lame, the sick, or on anyone eating in the homes of close relatives or trusted friends, whether together or separately. And whenever entering any house, the instruction is to greet those inside with a blessed, good greeting straight from Allah, so people might genuinely understand what is being asked of them.
verses.62: True believers, when gathered with the Messenger over a shared concern, do not simply leave without asking permission first. Those who do ask are the ones whose belief is genuine, and permission should be granted to whoever needs it, along with a prayer asking Allah's forgiveness on their behalf.
verses.63: Believers are told not to treat a summons from the Messenger the same casual way they'd treat a call from one another. Allah is fully aware of those who slip quietly away using others as cover, and anyone who defies his guidance should be wary of the trial or painful punishment that could follow.
verses.64: The surah closes by returning to where its authority always rested — everything in the heavens and the earth belongs to Allah alone. He already knows exactly what state everyone is in, and on the Day all are returned to Him, He will inform every soul, in full, of everything it once did.

verses.1: Blessed is the One who sent down the Criterion — the standard for separating truth from falsehood — to His servant, so that he could warn every people on earth, not just his own.
verses.2: This is the One who owns the heavens and earth entirely, has no child, shares dominion with no one, and shaped every single thing with exact precision and purpose.
verses.3: Yet people still choose gods that create nothing and are themselves created — powerless even over their own harm or benefit, let alone anyone else's life, death, or resurrection.
verses.4: Disbelievers call the Quran a fabrication he invented with others' help — an accusation the verse names plainly as injustice dressed up as skepticism.
verses.5: They dismiss it as old legends he had copied down, read back to him morning and evening as if memorized homework.
verses.6: The response is direct: it was sent by the One who knows every secret hidden in the heavens and earth — a Lord who is Forgiving and Merciful even to those mocking His words.
verses.7: They scoff — what kind of messenger eats food and walks through markets like anyone else? Why wasn't an angel sent alongside him instead?
verses.8: Or why hasn't treasure been dropped in his lap, or a private garden given to sustain him? The wrongdoers conclude, absurdly, that he must be under some spell.
verses.9: Look, the verse says, at the wild comparisons they invent trying to explain him away — and see how thoroughly lost they've become in the process, unable to find their way back.
verses.10: Blessed is the One who could, if He willed, give something far better than their imagined treasures — gardens with flowing rivers, even palaces, all within His power to grant.
verses.11: But their real objection runs deeper: they deny the Hour itself. For those who deny it, a blazing fire has already been prepared.
verses.12: When that fire catches sight of them from far away, they will already hear its fury and roaring before it even reaches them.
verses.13: Bound and thrown into some narrow corner of it, they will cry out, longing for their own destruction as a mercy.
verses.14: They'll be told plainly: do not wish for just one destruction today — wish for many, because one ending will not be enough to capture what awaits.
verses.15: Ask them, the verse suggests, which is truly better — that, or the eternal Garden promised to the righteous as their reward and final home?
verses.16: There they will have whatever they desire, living forever — a promise from their Lord that is always worth striving toward.
verses.17: On the Day everyone is gathered, along with whatever they worshipped besides Allah, He will ask those false gods directly: did you lead these servants astray, or did they wander off on their own?
verses.18: They will answer, astonished at even being asked — it was never right for us to be taken as allies; You simply let them and their ancestors enjoy comfort until they forgot Your reminder entirely and ruined themselves.
verses.19: The response comes back to the idolaters: your gods have denied everything you claimed, so no escape and no help remains for you now; whoever did wrong will taste a great punishment for it.
verses.20: Every messenger before this one also ate food and walked through markets like everyone else — people are made a trial for one another, testing whether patience can hold; your Lord sees everything regardless.
verses.21: Those who don't expect ever to meet Allah ask why angels haven't been sent to them, or why they can't simply see their Lord — pure arrogance dressed up as a demand for proof.
verses.22: On the Day they finally do see the angels, there will be no good news waiting for the guilty — only the angels declaring that comfort is now forbidden to them entirely.
verses.23: Whatever deeds they thought counted for something will be scattered like dust caught in the wind, weightless and meaningless in the end.
verses.24: Meanwhile the people of Paradise that same Day will find themselves in the finest possible place, resting somewhere truly worth resting.
verses.25: Picture the sky splitting apart with clouds, and angels descending in wave after wave — that is the scene painted for this Day.
verses.26: On that Day, true authority belongs to the Most Merciful alone, and it will be an especially hard day for anyone who spent their life disbelieving.
verses.27: That Day the wrongdoer will bite down on his own hands in regret, wishing he had only walked the same path as the Messenger.
verses.28: Oh, how he'll wish he had never taken a certain someone as his closest friend.
verses.29: That friend led him away from the message right after it had reached him — Satan, the verse notes, is always ready to abandon the very person he misled.
verses.30: The Messenger himself will say, O my Lord, my own people treated this Quran as something to be discarded and ignored.
verses.31: This is nothing new — every prophet has faced an enemy drawn from among the guilty — but your Lord is more than sufficient as guide and helper.
verses.32: Disbelievers ask why the Quran wasn't sent down all at once; it was revealed gradually instead, precisely so the heart receiving it could be steadied and strengthened piece by piece.
verses.33: Whatever argument they bring is always already answered — the truth arrives paired with its own clearest explanation, leaving no real objection standing.
verses.34: Those dragged to Hell face-down are in the very worst position, further from the path than anyone else could imagine being.
verses.35: Moses was given the Scripture, and his brother Aaron was appointed alongside him as support and assistance.
verses.36: Both were sent to a people who had denied Our signs, and that whole people was ultimately destroyed completely for it.
verses.37: The people of Noah, too, once they denied the messengers sent to them, were drowned and turned into a lasting sign — a painful punishment prepared for wrongdoers throughout history.
verses.38: Ad, Thamud, the companions of the well, and many generations in between all met similar ends.
verses.39: To every one of them examples were given and warnings made clear, yet each was eventually ruined in its own particular way.
verses.40: They have already passed by the town destroyed under a rain of devastation — have they truly never noticed it? The truth is, they simply don't expect resurrection at all.
verses.41: Whenever they see the Messenger, all they can manage is ridicule — is this really the one Allah sent to us?
verses.42: He nearly led us away from our gods, they say, had we not held firm — but they will learn exactly who was truly astray once the punishment is finally in front of them.
verses.43: Consider someone who makes his own desire his god — could anyone realistically be held responsible for guiding a person like that?
verses.44: Do you really think most of them listen or reason at all? The verse compares them to grazing cattle — except, it adds, even more lost than that.
verses.45: Look at how your Lord extends the shadow across the earth — and had He willed, He could have simply left it fixed in place forever.
verses.46: Instead He draws it back toward Himself gradually, little by little, a quiet daily reminder of change and return.
verses.47: He is the One who made night a covering for rest, sleep itself a kind of pause, and day a waking that feels almost like a small resurrection.
verses.48: He is the One who sends winds ahead of His mercy as good news, and sends down pure, cleansing water from the sky.
