verses.49: By that water, dead land is brought back to life, and countless creatures — livestock and people alike — are given something to drink.
verses.50: Rain is deliberately distributed across different peoples so they might remember and be grateful, yet most choose ingratitude over gratitude every time.
verses.51: Had He willed, a dedicated warner could have been sent to every single town on earth.
verses.52: So do not give in to the disbelievers — instead, strive against their arguments using this Quran itself, a serious and demanding effort in its own right.
verses.53: He is the One who set two seas loose beside each other — one fresh and sweet, one salty and bitter — and placed an invisible barrier between them that neither ever crosses.
verses.54: He created humanity from water and wove people together through bloodline and marriage — a Lord fully capable of anything He wills.
verses.55: Yet people still worship things that can neither help nor harm them — and the disbeliever, in doing so, becomes an ally against his very own Lord.
verses.56: The Messenger was sent for one clear reason: to bring good news and to warn — nothing more was ever asked of him.
verses.57: Say to them: I ask no payment for this, only that whoever wishes may choose a path leading back to their Lord.
verses.58: Place your trust in the Ever-Living One who never dies, and glorify Him constantly — He is more than enough as the One fully aware of every servant's failings.
verses.59: He created the heavens, the earth, and everything between them in six days, then established His authority over all of it — ask anyone truly informed and they will tell you the same.
verses.60: When told to bow down to the Most Merciful, they scoff — what is this 'Most Merciful' you keep asking us to bow to? — and the mere suggestion only pushes them further away.
verses.61: Blessed is the One who placed great constellations in the sky and set there a radiant lamp and a glowing moon.
verses.62: He made night and day chase each other in endless succession, for anyone who wants to remember or wants to feel grateful.
verses.63: The true servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk the earth without arrogance, and when confronted rudely by the ignorant, simply respond with peace and move on.
verses.64: They are the ones who spend the night bowing and standing before their Lord in devotion.
verses.65: They plead, our Lord, turn the punishment of Hell away from us — its torment, they know, clings and never lets go.
verses.66: It is, without question, the worst possible place to settle and remain.
verses.67: When they spend, they neither waste extravagantly nor hold back stingily — their giving sits comfortably in the balance between the two.
verses.68: They call on no other god beside Allah, take no life Allah made sacred except by right, and stay clear of unlawful intimacy — anyone who does otherwise will meet a real penalty for it.
verses.69: Punishment for that is multiplied on the Day of Resurrection, and it lasts, disgracing whoever earns it, unless they change course.
verses.70: Except those who repent, truly believe, and act righteously — for them, Allah replaces their past wrongs with good deeds instead, since He is endlessly Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.71: Whoever repents and lives righteously has genuinely turned back to Allah in the fullest sense of the word.
verses.72: They are the ones who never bear false witness, and when they pass by empty, idle talk, they simply walk on with quiet dignity.
verses.73: When reminded of their Lord's verses, they don't collapse into them deaf and unmoved — they actually listen and take them in.
verses.74: They pray, our Lord, grant us spouses and children who bring us genuine comfort, and make us a model for those striving to live rightly.
verses.75: These are the ones granted the highest chambers of Paradise for their patient endurance, welcomed there with greetings of peace.
verses.76: They remain there forever — truly the finest place anyone could ever call home.
verses.77: Say to them: what would my Lord care about any of you if you never called on Him at all? You have denied the truth, and that denial will stay with you, inescapable.

verses.1: Ta, Seen, Meem — mysterious letters standing at the threshold of the surah, unexplained.
verses.2: These, the verse says, are the verses of a Book that leaves nothing about its message unclear.
verses.3: The Messenger is pictured almost grieving himself to death over people who simply will not believe — such is the weight he carries for them.
verses.4: Had Allah willed, He could send down a single overwhelming sign that would force every neck to bow in submission — but forced belief was never the point.
verses.5: Yet no fresh reminder ever comes to them from the Most Merciful without them turning away from it.
verses.6: They have already rejected the truth, and news of exactly what they used to mock is coming for them regardless.
verses.7: Have they never looked at the earth — how many noble, varied kinds of life have sprung up from it?
verses.8: There is a sign in that alone, yet most of them still refuse to believe.
verses.9: And your Lord remains, through all of it, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.10: Recall when Allah called out to Moses: go to the people who are doing wrong —
verses.11: the people of Pharaoh. Will they truly never come to fear Him?
verses.12: Moses answered, my Lord, I fear they will simply call me a liar,
verses.13: and that my chest will tighten and my words won't come easily — so send my brother Aaron with me.
verses.14: And they hold a crime against me; I fear they may kill me for it.
verses.15: Allah reassured him — no. Go, both of you, with My signs; I am with you, listening to everything.
verses.16: Go to Pharaoh and tell him plainly: we are messengers from the Lord of all the worlds —
verses.17: release the Children of Israel to come with us.
verses.18: Pharaoh answered, did we not raise you among us as a child, and did you not spend years of your life here?
verses.19: And then you did that deed of yours — you were an ungrateful man.
verses.20: Moses admitted, yes, I did it then, when I was still lost and unaware.
verses.21: So I fled from you out of fear, and my Lord granted me wisdom and made me one of His messengers.
verses.22: And is enslaving the entire Children of Israel really the favor you keep throwing back at me?
verses.23: Pharaoh demanded, and what exactly is this 'Lord of the worlds' you speak of?
verses.24: Moses answered, the Lord of the heavens and earth and everything between them — if only you had certainty.
verses.25: Pharaoh turned to those around him — do you hear what he's saying?
verses.26: Moses continued, your Lord, and the Lord of your very first ancestors.
verses.27: Pharaoh declared, this messenger sent to you must be out of his mind.
verses.28: Moses pressed on, Lord of the east and west and everything between, if only you would reason it through.
verses.29: Pharaoh threatened, if you take any god besides me, I will have you thrown among the imprisoned.
verses.30: Moses asked, even if I showed you something undeniably clear?
verses.31: Pharaoh answered, then bring it, if you're telling the truth.
verses.32: So Moses threw down his staff, and it became, before everyone's eyes, a real, moving serpent.
verses.33: He drew out his hand, and it shone bright white for everyone watching.
verses.34: Pharaoh turned to his advisors — this is clearly a skilled sorcerer,
verses.35: who wants to drive you out of your own land through his magic — what do you suggest?
verses.36: They advised, delay him and his brother, and send messengers to gather people from the cities —
verses.37: bring every skilled, learned magician to you.
verses.38: So the magicians were gathered at the appointed time, on a well-known day.
verses.39: The people were asked, will you all come together to watch,
verses.40: so we might follow the magicians if they turn out to be the stronger side?
verses.41: When the magicians arrived, they asked Pharaoh, will there be a reward for us if we win?
verses.42: He answered, yes, and you would even be counted among those closest to me.
verses.43: Moses told them simply, throw down whatever you intend to throw.
verses.44: So they threw their ropes and staffs, declaring, by Pharaoh's might, we will surely be victorious.
verses.45: Then Moses threw his own staff, and it instantly swallowed up everything they had faked.
verses.46: The magicians, witnessing this, fell down at once in prostration.
verses.47: They declared, we believe in the Lord of all the worlds —
verses.48: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.
verses.49: Pharaoh raged, you believed him before I gave permission? He must be your teacher who trained you in magic — you will soon see; I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides and crucify all of you.
verses.50: They answered calmly, no real harm done — to our Lord we are returning regardless.
verses.51: We truly hope our Lord will forgive our sins, since we are the very first to believe here today.
verses.52: Allah then instructed Moses to travel by night with His servants, warning that they would be pursued.
verses.53: So Pharaoh sent messengers out to gather people from the cities,
verses.54: saying, these people are only a small band,
verses.55: and yet they infuriate us,
verses.56: and we remain a vigilant, watchful multitude.
verses.57: So Allah removed Pharaoh's people from their gardens and springs,
verses.58: their treasures and their honored standing —
verses.59: and gave all of it as inheritance to the Children of Israel instead.
verses.60: Pharaoh's forces pursued them at the break of dawn.
verses.61: When the two groups came within sight of each other, Moses' followers cried out, we are certainly about to be caught.
verses.62: Moses answered, never — my Lord is with me, and He will guide me through this.
verses.63: Allah then instructed Moses to strike the sea with his staff, and it split apart, each side rising like a towering mountain.
verses.64: Pharaoh's army was drawn right into that same path behind them.
verses.65: Moses and everyone with him were saved, every single one.
verses.66: And then the others were drowned completely.
verses.67: There is a sign in that, yet most people still refuse to believe it.
verses.68: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.69: Recount to them the story of Abraham,
verses.70: when he asked his father and his people plainly, what is it you worship?
verses.71: They answered, we worship idols, and we remain devoted to them.
verses.72: He asked, do they hear you at all when you call out to them,
verses.73: or do they benefit you, or harm you, in any way?
verses.74: They shrugged, we simply found our fathers doing the very same thing.
verses.75: He said, then have you really considered what it is you've been worshipping —
verses.76: you and your ancestors before you?
verses.77: All of them are my enemies, he declared, except the Lord of all the worlds,
verses.78: who created me, and who guides me through everything.
verses.79: It is He who feeds me and gives me drink,
verses.80: and when I fall ill, it is He alone who heals me,
verses.81: and He who will cause my death and then bring me back to life,
verses.82: and the One I truly hope will forgive my failings on the Day of Recompense.
verses.83: My Lord, grant me sound judgment, and join me with the righteous,
verses.84: and let me be remembered with honor by generations still to come,
verses.85: and count me among the heirs of the Garden of Bliss,
verses.86: and forgive my father — he has clearly wandered far astray —
verses.87: and do not let me be disgraced on the Day everyone is raised again,
verses.88: the Day when neither wealth nor children will be of any use to anyone,
verses.89: except for the one who comes before Allah with a heart that is whole and sincere.
verses.90: Paradise on that Day will be drawn close to the righteous, no distance left between them,
verses.91: while the Hellfire will be brought forward plainly for those who strayed —
verses.92: and they will be asked, where now are those you used to worship
verses.93: instead of Allah? Can they help you, or even help themselves?
verses.94: So they will be hurled into it, they and everyone who led them astray,
verses.95: along with the ranks of Iblis, all thrown in together.
verses.96: They will argue among themselves there, saying, by Allah, we were clearly and completely wrong,
verses.97: when we treated you as equal to the Lord of the worlds.
verses.98: No one misled us except those hardened in their own wrongdoing.
verses.99: Now we have no intercessors left to plead for us,
verses.100: and not one devoted friend remains either.
verses.101: If only we had one more chance to go back, we would be among the believers this time.
verses.102: There is a sign in that whole exchange, yet most people still refuse to believe.
verses.103: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.104: The people of Noah, too, denied the messengers sent to them,
verses.105: when their own brother Noah asked them, will you truly never come to fear Allah?
verses.106: I am, to all of you, a trustworthy messenger,
verses.107: so hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.108: And I ask no payment from you for any of this — my reward comes only from the Lord of the worlds.
verses.109: So hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.110: They scoffed, should we really believe you when it's only the lowest among us who follow you?
verses.111: He answered, what would I even know about what they used to do before?
verses.112: Their account rests with my Lord alone, if only you could see that clearly.
verses.113: And I am certainly not one to drive believers away simply for who they are.
verses.114: I am nothing more than a clear and honest warner.
verses.115: They threatened, if you don't stop this, Noah, you will surely be stoned.
verses.116: He said, my Lord, my own people have rejected me completely.
verses.117: So judge decisively between me and them, and save me along with the believers who stand with me.
verses.118: So Allah saved him, and those with him, aboard the heavily laden ship,
verses.119: and afterward drowned everyone else who remained.
verses.120: There is a sign in that, yet most of them still refused to believe.
verses.121: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.122: The people of Ad, too, denied the messengers sent to them,
verses.123: when their brother Hud asked them, will you truly never come to fear Allah?
verses.124: I am, to all of you, a trustworthy messenger,
verses.125: so hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.126: And I ask no payment from you for this — my reward comes only from the Lord of the worlds.
verses.127: Do you build monuments on every high ground just to amuse yourselves,
verses.128: and construct palaces and fortresses as though you'll live in them forever?
verses.129: And whenever you strike out at others, you strike like tyrants without restraint.
verses.130: So hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.131: And fear the One who has given you everything you rely on —
verses.132: who gave you livestock and children,
verses.134: I genuinely fear for you the punishment of a terrible day.
verses.135: They answered, it makes no difference to us whether you warn us or not.
verses.136: This is simply the way our forefathers always lived,
verses.137: and we are not going to be punished for it.
verses.138: So they rejected him, and Allah destroyed them entirely. There is a sign in that, yet most of them still refused to believe.
verses.139: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.140: The people of Thamud, too, denied the messengers sent to them,
verses.141: when their brother Salih asked them, will you truly never come to fear Allah?
verses.142: I am, to all of you, a trustworthy messenger,
verses.143: so hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.144: And I ask no payment from you for this — my reward comes only from the Lord of the worlds.
verses.145: Will you really be left secure forever in what you have here —
verses.147: and fields of crops, and palm trees heavy with tender, ripening fruit,
verses.148: carving your homes skillfully out of the very mountains themselves?
verses.149: So hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say,
verses.150: and do not obey those who go beyond all bounds,
verses.151: who spread ruin across the land instead of setting things right.
verses.152: They accused him, you're merely someone under some kind of spell.
verses.153: You're just a man like the rest of us — bring us a sign, if you're telling the truth.
verses.154: He answered, here is a she-camel — she has her turn to drink, and you have yours, each on an appointed day.
verses.155: Do not lay a hand on her in harm, or a terrible day's punishment will seize you.
verses.156: But they hamstrung her anyway, and soon after were filled with regret.
verses.157: So the punishment overtook them. There is a sign in that, yet most of them still refused to believe.
verses.158: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.159: The people of Lot, too, denied the messengers sent to them,
verses.160: when their brother Lot asked them, will you truly never come to fear Allah?
verses.161: I am, to all of you, a trustworthy messenger,
verses.162: so hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.163: And I ask no payment from you for this — my reward comes only from the Lord of the worlds.
verses.164: Do you seek out men among all the peoples of the world,
verses.165: leaving aside the mates your Lord created for you? You have gone entirely beyond what's natural.
verses.166: They threatened, if you don't stop this, Lot, you will surely be driven out.
verses.167: He said, I regard your practice with nothing but revulsion.
verses.168: My Lord, save me and my family from whatever harm they intend to do.
verses.169: So Allah saved him and his entire family,
verses.170: except one old woman among those who stayed behind.
verses.171: Then Allah destroyed everyone else who remained,
verses.172: raining destruction down upon them — a truly terrible rain for those who had been warned.
verses.173: There is a sign in that, yet most of them still refused to believe.
verses.174: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.175: The companions of the wooded thicket, too, denied the messengers sent to them,
verses.176: when Shu'ayb asked them, will you truly never come to fear Allah?
verses.177: I am, to all of you, a trustworthy messenger,
verses.178: so hold Allah in awe, and follow what I say.
verses.179: And I ask no payment from you for this — my reward comes only from the Lord of the worlds.
verses.180: Give full and honest measure, and never be among those who cheat others.
verses.181: Weigh things out with an accurate, even balance,
verses.182: and never deprive people of what is rightfully theirs, and never spread corruption across the land.
verses.183: And hold in awe the One who created you and every generation before you.
verses.184: They accused him, you're merely someone under some kind of spell.
verses.185: You're just a man like the rest of us, and frankly, we think you're a liar.
verses.186: So bring pieces of the sky crashing down on us, if you're telling the truth.
verses.187: He answered, my Lord knows best exactly what you are doing.
verses.188: But they rejected him, and the punishment of a dark, shadowed day overtook them — truly the punishment of a terrible day.
verses.189: There is a sign in that, yet most of them still refused to believe.
verses.190: And your Lord remains, even here, both Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful.
verses.191: And truly, this Quran is a revelation sent down from the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.192: The Trustworthy Spirit brought it down,
verses.193: settling it upon your heart, so that you might become one of the warners,
verses.194: delivered in a clear, unmistakable Arabic tongue.
verses.195: And it is already mentioned within the scriptures of earlier peoples.
verses.196: Isn't it enough of a sign that the scholars among the Children of Israel already recognize it?
verses.197: Had it been revealed instead to someone outside the Arabic-speaking world,
verses.198: and had he recited it to them, they still would not have believed in it.
verses.199: This is how deeply Allah has let disbelief take root in the hearts of the guilty.
verses.200: They will not truly believe until they are staring the painful punishment directly in the face,
verses.201: and it will arrive suddenly, catching them completely off guard.
verses.202: Only then will they cry out, will we be given any reprieve at all?
verses.203: So is it really Our punishment they were in such a hurry to dismiss?
verses.204: Consider this — even if they were given years of comfort and enjoyment,
verses.205: and only afterward the promised reckoning finally arrived,
verses.206: what good would all that earlier comfort have done for them in the end?
verses.207: Allah has never destroyed a single town without first sending it a warning,
verses.208: a reminder given as an act of justice, since He is never unjust to anyone.
verses.209: And no, this revelation was never brought down by devils —
verses.210: that was never something suited to them, nor something within their power.
verses.211: In truth, they are kept entirely shut out from even hearing it.
verses.212: So do not call on any other god alongside Allah, or you risk joining those who are punished.
verses.213: And warn your own closest family first, before anyone else.
verses.214: Lower your wing gently over the believers who follow you, treating them with humility and care.
verses.215: And if they disobey you regardless, simply say, I have nothing to do with what you're doing.
verses.216: Place your trust in the One who is Exalted in Might and endlessly Merciful,
verses.217: who watches you when you rise for prayer,
verses.218: and sees your movements among those who bow down in worship.
verses.219: He is, after all, the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.220: Shall I tell you exactly who it is the devils descend upon?
verses.221: They descend on every sinful liar,
verses.222: who pass along whatever fragments they've overheard — and most of what they say is simply untrue.
verses.223: As for the poets, it is the aimless and the deceptive who tend to follow them,
verses.224: wandering through every valley of exaggeration without direction,
verses.225: speaking things they have no intention of ever actually living out —
verses.226: except for those poets who believe, live righteously, remember Allah often, and use their words to defend the wronged rather than deceive; those who caused harm will one day discover exactly what fate awaits them in return.
verses.227: And that discovery — the reckoning owed to everyone who chose to wrong others — is the closing note this surah leaves ringing in the air.

verses.1: Ta, Seen — these are verses of the Quran and of a Book that leaves nothing unclear.
verses.2: Guidance and good news, offered specifically to those who truly believe,
verses.3: who keep up prayer, give in charity, and hold certainty about the life still to come.
verses.4: As for those who don't believe in the Hereafter, their own deeds have been made to look appealing to them, leaving them wandering blindly.
verses.5: These are the ones facing the harshest punishment, and in the next life they will be the greatest losers of all.
verses.6: And you, Muhammad, are receiving this Quran from One who is both Wise and All-Knowing.
verses.7: Recall when Moses told his family, I've spotted a fire — let me go bring back news, or at least a burning torch to warm us.
verses.8: When he reached it, a voice called out — blessed is whoever is near this fire and all around it; glory to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
verses.9: O Moses, it is truly I — Allah, Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.10: Throw down your staff. And when he saw it moving like a living serpent, he turned and fled without looking back. O Moses, do not be afraid — messengers never need to fear in My presence.
verses.11: Except someone who has wronged himself and later replaces that evil with good — for such a person, I am truly Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.12: Place your hand inside your garment, and it will come out shining white, unharmed — one of nine signs meant for Pharaoh and his defiantly disobedient people.
verses.13: Yet when these clear signs reached them, all they could say was, this is nothing but obvious magic.
verses.14: They rejected them outright, even while privately convinced they were true — sheer arrogance and injustice. See how the story of such corrupters always ends.
verses.15: David and Solomon were both given real knowledge, and they responded, praise be to Allah, who has favored us above so many of His believing servants.
verses.16: Solomon inherited from David, and announced, people, we have been taught the language of birds, and given a share of everything — this truly is an evident gift.
verses.17: Solomon's forces were gathered before him — jinn, humans, and birds alike — all organized and arranged in ranks.
verses.18: When they reached the valley of the ants, one ant called out, ants, hurry into your homes, or Solomon and his army will crush you without even realizing it.
verses.19: Solomon smiled, delighted by what he'd heard, and prayed, my Lord, help me stay grateful for the blessings You've given me and my parents, let me do what pleases You, and count me among Your righteous servants by Your mercy.
verses.20: He took a roll call of the birds and asked, why don't I see the hoopoe — is he simply absent?
verses.21: I will punish him severely, or even have him killed, unless he brings me a clear excuse.
verses.22: The hoopoe didn't stay away long, and returned saying, I've learned something you haven't, and I've brought certain news from Sheba.
verses.23: I found a woman ruling there, given a share of everything, and seated upon a magnificent throne.
verses.24: I found her and her people bowing to the sun instead of Allah — Satan has dressed up their ways to look good to them and turned them from the right path, leaving them unguided,
verses.25: so that they never bow instead to Allah, who brings to light everything hidden in the heavens and earth, and who knows exactly what people hide and what they show.
verses.26: Allah — there is no god beside Him, Lord of the mighty Throne.
verses.27: Solomon said, we'll soon find out whether you were being truthful or one of the liars.
verses.28: Take this letter of mine, deliver it to them, then step back and see how they respond.
verses.29: The queen announced, my council, a noble letter has just been delivered to me.
verses.30: It comes from Solomon, and it opens in the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful —
verses.31: do not rise up against me in arrogance, but come to me in submission.
verses.32: She said, my council, advise me on this matter — I won't decide anything without your counsel.
verses.33: They answered, we possess strength and real military power, but the decision is yours; tell us what you wish.
verses.34: She said, when kings conquer a city, they tend to ruin it and humiliate its most honored people — that's simply what they do.
verses.35: So instead, I'll send them a gift and see what response the messengers bring back.
verses.36: When the delegation reached Solomon, he said, are you trying to increase my wealth? What Allah has given me is far better than what He's given you — it's you who should be pleased with your own gift.
verses.37: Go back to them, he said, because we will come with an army they cannot possibly resist, and we will drive them out disgraced and humbled.
verses.38: Solomon then asked his council, which of you can bring me her throne before they arrive here in submission?
verses.39: A powerful jinn spoke up — I can bring it to you before you even rise from your seat; I'm strong and trustworthy enough for this.
verses.40: But one who had knowledge from the Scripture said, I can bring it to you before your glance even returns to you. When Solomon saw it already placed before him, he said, this is a favor from my Lord, testing whether I'll be grateful or ungrateful — whoever is grateful benefits only himself, and whoever isn't, my Lord needs nothing from anyone and is endlessly generous regardless.
verses.41: He said, alter her throne slightly and see whether she recognizes it, or whether she remains unable to see clearly.
verses.42: When she arrived, she was asked, is your throne like this? She answered, it's as if it were the very same one. [The verse notes] we had already been given understanding before her, and had submitted to Allah.
verses.43: What had kept her from Allah was what she used to worship instead — she had come from a people who disbelieved.
verses.44: She was invited into the palace, and seeing its glass floor, she thought it was open water and lifted her skirts to cross it. Solomon explained, it's simply a palace paved smooth with glass. She said, my Lord, I have wronged myself — I now submit, together with Solomon, to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
verses.45: To Thamud, Allah sent their brother Salih, telling them to worship Allah alone — and at once the people split into two quarreling factions.
verses.46: He asked them, why are you rushing toward harm instead of good? Why not seek Allah's forgiveness so that mercy might reach you?
verses.47: They answered, we see you and your followers as an omen of misfortune. He replied, whatever befalls you comes from Allah — really, you are simply a people being tested.
verses.48: In that city were nine ringleaders who spread corruption across the land and refused to reform.
verses.49: They conspired, swearing to one another by Allah, we'll kill him and his family by night, then tell his heirs we witnessed nothing — and we will insist we're telling the truth.
verses.50: They schemed their scheme, and Allah countered with a plan of His own, all while they remained completely unaware.
verses.51: See how their scheme turned out in the end — Allah destroyed them entirely, along with their whole people.
verses.52: Their homes stand empty now because of the wrong they committed — a sign, surely, for anyone willing to understand.
verses.53: And Allah saved those who believed and who lived in awareness of Him.
verses.54: Recall Lot, who asked his people, are you really committing this immorality with your eyes wide open?
verses.55: Do you actually pursue men out of desire instead of women? You are behaving with sheer ignorance.
verses.56: His people's only answer was, drive Lot's family out of our city — they insist on staying pure, after all.
verses.57: So Allah saved him and his family, except his wife, who was destined to remain behind with the others.
verses.58: And Allah rained destruction down upon them — a truly evil rain for those who had already been warned.
verses.59: Say, praise belongs to Allah, and peace be upon the servants He has chosen — is Allah better, or the things people set up as His rivals?
verses.60: Is it not He who created the heavens and earth, and sends down rain that grows breathtaking gardens no human power could ever have grown? Could there really be another god beside Allah? No — these are simply people who invent equals for Him.
verses.61: Is it not He who made the earth a stable home, set rivers running through it, anchored it with firm mountains, and placed a barrier between two seas? Could there be another god beside Allah? Most people simply don't understand.
verses.62: Is it not He who answers the desperate soul when it calls out, removes hardship, and makes generation after generation inherit the earth? Could there be another god beside Allah? How little people actually reflect.
verses.63: Is it not He who guides you through the darkness of land and sea, and sends winds ahead of His mercy as good news? Could there be another god beside Allah? Allah is far above whatever they associate with Him.
verses.64: Is it not He who begins creation and then brings it back again, and provides for you from both sky and earth? Could there be another god beside Allah? Say, bring your proof, if you're telling the truth.
verses.65: Say, no one in the heavens or on earth knows the unseen except Allah, and no one even senses when they'll finally be raised again.
verses.66: In truth, their knowledge of the Hereafter falls apart completely — they are in real doubt about it, genuinely blind to it.
verses.67: The disbelievers say, once we've turned to dust, we and our ancestors both, will we really be brought back?
verses.68: We were promised this before, and so were our ancestors — it's nothing but old legends passed down from earlier peoples.
verses.69: Say, travel across the earth and see for yourselves how the story ended for the guilty.
verses.70: Do not grieve over them, and don't let their scheming weigh on you.
verses.71: They ask, when will this promised reckoning actually happen, if you're telling the truth?
verses.72: Say, perhaps what you're so eager to rush is already closer behind you than you think.
verses.73: Your Lord is truly generous toward people, yet most of them show so little gratitude for it.
verses.74: And your Lord already knows everything hearts conceal and everything they openly show.
verses.75: There is nothing hidden in the heavens or on earth that isn't already recorded in a clear register.
verses.76: This Quran settles, for the Children of Israel, most of what they've long disagreed about among themselves.
verses.77: And it stands as guidance and mercy for every believer.
verses.78: Your Lord will judge between people by His own perfect judgment — He is Exalted in Might, All-Knowing.
verses.79: So place your trust in Allah — you are standing firmly upon the clear truth.
verses.80: You cannot make the dead hear you, nor can you make the deaf hear your call once they've turned their backs and walked away.
verses.81: And you cannot lead the blind out of their error — you can only reach those who already believe in Our verses and have submitted themselves to Allah.
verses.82: When the moment finally arrives, a creature will emerge from the earth itself, speaking to people about how they failed to hold real certainty in Our signs.
verses.83: And picture the Day a company will be gathered from every single nation, made up of those who denied Our signs, brought forward in orderly rows,
verses.84: until, once they've arrived, they'll be asked, did you deny My signs without ever truly understanding them, or what exactly was it you thought you were doing?
verses.85: The sentence will fall on them because of the wrong they did, and they will find themselves utterly speechless.
verses.86: Have they never noticed that night was made for their rest, and day was made so they could see? There are real signs in that for anyone who actually believes.
verses.87: And picture the Day the Horn is blown, and everyone in the heavens and on earth is struck with terror, except whoever Allah chooses to spare — and all of them will come before Him utterly humbled.
verses.88: You look at the mountains and assume they're perfectly still, yet they will move like drifting clouds — the handiwork of Allah, who perfects everything He makes; He is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.89: Whoever arrives with a good deed will receive something even better in return, safe from the terror of that overwhelming Day.
verses.90: And whoever arrives with an evil deed will be thrown face-first into the Fire — are you being repaid for anything other than what you yourselves used to do?
verses.91: Say, I've simply been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, the One who made it sacred and to whom everything belongs, and to be among those who submit to Him,
verses.92: and to recite this Quran. Whoever accepts guidance does so purely for their own benefit, and whoever strays — say, I am only here to warn, nothing more.
verses.93: And say, praise belongs to Allah; He will show you His signs until you recognize them clearly for yourselves, and your Lord is never unaware of anything you do.

verses.1: Ta, Seen, Meem — the surah opens once more with these unexplained letters.
verses.2: These are verses of a Book that makes everything within it perfectly clear.
verses.3: We recount to you, truthfully, part of the story of Moses and Pharaoh, meant for people who actually believe.
verses.4: Pharaoh had made himself a tyrant in the land, dividing his people into factions, oppressing one group by slaughtering their sons and sparing their daughters — he was, without question, a spreader of corruption.
verses.5: Yet Allah intended to show favor to those who had been oppressed in that land, making them leaders, and making them the true inheritors,
verses.6: establishing them firmly in the land, and showing Pharaoh, Haman, and their soldiers precisely the danger they had feared from that very people.
verses.7: Allah inspired Moses' mother — nurse him, and if you fear for him, set him afloat on the river without fear or grief, for We will return him to you and make him a messenger.
verses.8: Pharaoh's household ended up picking him up themselves, though he would become an enemy and a source of grief for them — Pharaoh, Haman, and their soldiers were, after all, deeply mistaken.
verses.9: Pharaoh's wife said, he may be a comfort for us — do not kill him; perhaps he'll benefit us or we might adopt him as a son — and none of them realized what was unfolding.
verses.10: Moses' mother's heart became so anxious she nearly revealed everything, had We not strengthened her heart so she would remain a believer.
verses.11: She told his sister, follow him — and she watched him from a distance, unnoticed by anyone.
verses.12: We had already prevented him from nursing at any other breast, so his sister suggested, shall I show you a household that could raise him for you and treat him well?
verses.13: So We returned him to his mother, that her eyes might be comforted, that she wouldn't grieve, and that she might know Allah's promise is true — though most people simply do not know this.
verses.14: When he reached full maturity, We gave him sound judgment and knowledge — this is how We reward those who do good.
verses.15: He entered the city at a time its people weren't watching, and found two men fighting — one from his own people, one from his enemy's. The one from his own people asked for his help, and Moses struck the other and killed him unintentionally — this, he realized, was the work of Satan, a clear enemy leading people astray.
verses.16: He prayed, my Lord, I have wronged myself — forgive me. And Allah forgave him, for He is truly Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.17: Moses said, my Lord, because of the favor You've shown me, I will never again support the guilty.
verses.18: The next morning he moved through the city, fearful and watchful, when the man who had sought his help the day before cried out to him again. Moses said, you are clearly reckless.
verses.19: When Moses moved to strike the enemy of them both, the man cried out, Moses, do you intend to kill me as you killed a man yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land, not one who sets things right.
verses.20: A man came running from the far side of the city, warning, Moses, the leaders are conspiring to kill you — leave; I am giving you sincere advice.
verses.21: So he left, fearful and watchful, praying, my Lord, save me from this unjust people.
verses.22: And as he set out toward Madyan, he said, perhaps my Lord will guide me to the right way.
verses.23: Arriving at the well of Madyan, he found a crowd of men watering their flocks, and noticed two women holding theirs back. He asked what was wrong, and they answered, we cannot water our flock until the shepherds finish, and our father is a very old man.
verses.24: So he watered their flock for them, then withdrew to the shade and prayed, my Lord, I am truly in need of whatever good You send down to me.
verses.25: One of the two women came to him, walking with shy modesty, and said, my father invites you, to repay you for watering our flock for us. When Moses came and told him his story, the old man said, do not be afraid — you have escaped an unjust people.
verses.26: One of the daughters said, father, hire him — the best person to hire is one who is both strong and trustworthy.
verses.27: The father said, I would like to marry you to one of my daughters here, on the condition that you work for me for eight years — and if you complete ten, that would be your own choice; I do not intend to make this difficult for you, and you will find me, Allah willing, among the righteous.
verses.28: Moses said, this is settled between us — whichever of the two terms I fulfill, there should be no ill will toward me — and Allah is a witness over what we've agreed.
verses.29: When Moses had completed the term and was traveling with his family, he noticed a fire on the side of the mountain and told them, wait here, I've seen a fire — I may bring back news or a burning stick to warm us.
verses.30: When he reached it, a voice called from the tree in the blessed valley, on the right side — Moses, indeed I am Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.31: Throw down your staff. And when he saw it moving like a serpent, he turned in flight without looking back. Moses, come near and do not be afraid — you are among those who are safe.
verses.32: Put your hand into your garment; it will come out white without any harm, and draw your arm close to yourself against fear — these are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his leaders, for they have truly been a defiantly disobedient people.
verses.33: Moses said, my Lord, I killed a man among them, and I fear they will kill me.
verses.34: My brother Aaron is more eloquent in speech than I am — send him with me as support, confirming me, for I fear they will call me a liar.
verses.35: Allah answered, We will strengthen your arm through your brother, and give you both such authority that they will not be able to touch you — go, with Our signs; you two, and those who follow you, will be the victors.
verses.36: When Moses came to them with Our clear signs, they said, this is nothing but invented magic, and we never heard anything like this from our earliest ancestors.
verses.37: Moses answered, my Lord knows best who has come with true guidance from Him, and who will have the best outcome in the end — wrongdoers never truly succeed.
verses.38: Pharaoh declared, my people, I know of no god for you other than myself. Haman, kindle a fire for me over the clay and build me a lofty tower, so I might climb up and see the God of Moses — though I certainly think he is lying.
verses.39: He and his soldiers grew arrogant across the land without any right, imagining they would never be brought back to Us.
verses.40: So We seized him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea — see how the story ended for those who did wrong.
verses.41: We made them leaders inviting others to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will find no help at all.
verses.42: We placed a curse upon them in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be among the despised.
verses.43: After destroying the earlier generations, We gave Moses the Scripture as insight for people, guidance, and mercy, so they might take it to heart.
verses.44: You were not present on the western slope of the mountain when the command was given to Moses, nor were you among those who witnessed it.
verses.45: Generations rose and passed after that, their lives stretching long, and you were never a resident among the people of Madyan reciting Our verses to them — yet We kept sending messengers regardless.
verses.46: And you were not standing at the side of the mountain when Moses was called, but this story reaches you now as a mercy from your Lord, to warn a people who had never had a warner before you, so that they might finally take heed.
verses.47: Had disaster struck them because of what their own hands had done, they would surely have said, our Lord, why didn't You send us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses and become believers?
verses.48: Yet when the truth actually arrived, they complained, why wasn't he given what Moses was given? But did they not also reject what Moses received? They called both revelations coordinated magic and declared their disbelief in each.
verses.49: Say, then bring a scripture from Allah that guides better than either of these, if you're being truthful.
verses.50: If they still don't respond, know they are only chasing their own desires — and who is more lost than someone who follows desire with no guidance from Allah at all? Allah does not guide people determined to do wrong.
verses.51: This word has already reached them piece by piece, precisely so they might be reminded.
verses.52: Those who were given the Scripture before this one already recognize it as truth.
verses.53: When it's recited to them, they say, we believe in it — it is truly the truth from our Lord; we had already submitted to Allah even before it arrived.
verses.54: They will be given their reward twice over, for their patient endurance, for answering wrong with good, and for spending generously from what they've been given.
verses.55: When they hear crude speech, they simply turn away and say, our deeds belong to us and yours belong to you; peace be with you — we have no interest in ignorance.
verses.56: You cannot guide whomever you personally wish to guide — it is Allah who guides whoever He wills, and He knows best who is truly guided.
verses.57: They protest, if we followed your guidance we'd be swept out of our own land. But hasn't Allah already secured for them a safe sanctuary, supplied with fruit from everywhere as provision? Most of them simply don't realize this.
verses.58: How many towns has Allah destroyed for living in reckless excess — their homes now stand empty, barely inhabited since, and it is Allah who inherits everything left behind.
verses.59: Your Lord never destroys a town without first sending a messenger to its capital reciting Our verses, and He never destroys a town unless its people were truly doing wrong.
verses.60: Whatever you've been given is only the fleeting enjoyment of this present life and its glitter — what Allah holds is far better and far more lasting. Will you not use your reason?
verses.61: Can someone promised something truly good, destined to receive it, really be compared to someone simply given fleeting worldly pleasure, only to stand among the condemned come Resurrection Day?
verses.62: Picture the Day He calls out to them, where now are the partners you once claimed for Me?
verses.63: Those already condemned by that word will answer, our Lord, these are the ones we led astray — we misled them just as we ourselves were misled; we disown them completely before You; they were never truly worshipping us.
verses.64: They'll be told, call on your partners now — and they will call, but nothing will answer, and they will finally see the punishment before them, wishing desperately they had only been guided.
verses.65: Picture the Day He calls out to them again, asking, what answer did you give the messengers?
verses.66: That Day, every explanation will simply blur together, leaving them unable even to question one another about it.
verses.67: But whoever had repented, believed, and lived righteously can genuinely hope to be counted among the successful.
verses.68: Your Lord creates whatever He wills and chooses whatever He wills — human beings were never given that choice; Allah is exalted, far above whatever they associate with Him.
verses.69: Your Lord already knows everything hearts hide and everything they show openly.
verses.70: He is Allah — there is no god beside Him; all praise belongs to Him in this life and the next, all judgment is His, and to Him everyone will return.
verses.71: Say, consider this — if Allah made the night stretch on endlessly until the Day of Resurrection, what god besides Allah could bring you light? Will you not listen?
verses.72: Say, consider this — if Allah made the day stretch on endlessly until the Day of Resurrection, what god besides Allah could bring you a night to rest in? Will you not see?
verses.73: Out of His mercy alone, He made both night and day — one for your rest, one for seeking His provision — so that you might be grateful.
verses.74: Picture the Day He calls out to them once more, where now are the partners you once claimed for Me?
verses.75: A witness will be drawn from every single nation, and they will be told, produce your proof — and they will finally see that truth belongs entirely to Allah, while everything they invented simply vanishes.
verses.76: Qarun came from among Moses' own people, yet turned against them with arrogance. Allah had given him such treasures that the keys alone would strain a band of strong men to carry; his people warned him, do not gloat — Allah has no love for the boastful.
verses.77: Seek the home of the Hereafter through what Allah has given you, yet don't neglect your own share of this world either; do good as Allah has done good to you, and never chase corruption across the land, for Allah has no love for those who spread it.
verses.78: He answered, all this was only given to me because of knowledge I already possessed. Did he not realize Allah had already destroyed generations before him far greater in power and far richer in wealth? The guilty won't even be questioned about their sins on that Day.
verses.79: He went out before his people dressed in all his finery, and those chasing worldly life sighed, oh, if only we had what Qarun has — what tremendous fortune he holds.
verses.80: But those who had real knowledge answered, shame on you — Allah's reward is far better for whoever believes and lives righteously, and only the patient are ever granted that.
verses.81: Then Allah caused the earth to swallow him whole, along with his home, and he had no group at all to help him against Allah, nor could he even save himself.
verses.82: Those who had envied his position only the day before now said, it seems Allah extends or limits provision for whoever He wills among His servants — had Allah not shown us favor, He could have swallowed us too. It seems disbelievers truly never come out ahead.
verses.83: That home of the Hereafter is reserved for those who never sought superiority or corruption on earth — and the best outcome belongs, in the end, to the righteous.
verses.84: Whoever brings a good deed receives something even better in return, and whoever brings an evil deed will find that wrongdoers are repaid for nothing more than exactly what they did.
verses.85: The One who made the Quran binding upon you will surely bring you back to a place of return; say, my Lord knows best who carries true guidance and who is caught in plain error.
verses.86: You never expected this Scripture would reach you — it came only as a mercy from your Lord, so never become a supporter of those who reject it.
verses.87: Do not let them turn you away from Allah's verses once they've been revealed to you; keep inviting people to your Lord, and never be among those who set up partners with Him.
verses.88: And never call on any other god alongside Allah — there is no god but Him; everything will perish except His own Face; all judgment belongs to Him, and to Him you will all return.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Meem — the surah opens once again with these unexplained letters.
verses.2: Do people really imagine they'll be left alone simply for saying we believe, without ever being tested?
verses.3: Those who came before them were certainly tested too, so that Allah would clearly show who was truthful and who was lying.
verses.4: Or do those who commit evil imagine they can outrun Us? How poorly they judge.
verses.5: Whoever hopes for the meeting with Allah — Allah's appointed time is surely coming; He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.6: Whoever strives does so entirely for his own benefit — Allah has no need whatsoever of anyone in all creation.
verses.7: Those who believe and act righteously will have their sins removed and will be rewarded for the very best of what they used to do.
verses.8: We have instructed people to treat their parents well, yet if they try to make you associate with Me something you have no knowledge of, do not obey them — to Me you will all return, and I will tell you everything you used to do.
verses.9: Those who believe and act righteously will be included among the truly righteous.
verses.10: Some people say we believe in Allah, yet when harmed for His sake, they treat that harm as equal to Allah's own punishment; but if help comes from your Lord, they insist, we were with you all along. Does Allah not know best what's inside every heart?
verses.11: Allah will certainly know who the true believers are, and He will certainly know the hypocrites too.
verses.12: Disbelievers tell the believers, follow our path and we'll carry the weight of your sins — yet they cannot carry any part of another's sin at all; they are simply liars.
verses.13: They will carry their own burdens, plus additional burdens alongside their own, and they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about everything they invented.
verses.14: We sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them a thousand years minus fifty — yet the flood overtook them while they were still doing wrong.
verses.15: We saved him and those in the ship, making it a sign for all the worlds.
verses.16: And Abraham said to his people, worship Allah and hold Him in awe — that is better for you, if only you understood.
verses.17: You worship only idols besides Allah, inventing falsehood; those you worship instead of Allah have no power to provide for you at all — so seek provision from Allah, worship Him, and be grateful to Him, since to Him you will all return.
verses.18: If you deny this, communities before you also denied — the Messenger's duty is only to deliver the message clearly.
verses.19: Have they not seen how Allah originates creation and then repeats it? This is easy indeed for Allah.
verses.20: Say, travel through the earth and observe how creation first began — and then Allah will bring about the final creation, for Allah has power over absolutely everything.
verses.21: He punishes whom He wills and shows mercy to whom He wills, and to Him you will all be returned.
verses.22: You cannot escape Him anywhere on earth or in the sky, and you have no protector or helper besides Allah.
verses.23: Those who deny Allah's signs and the meeting with Him have truly lost all hope of My mercy — a painful punishment awaits them.
verses.24: Abraham's people had no answer except, kill him or burn him — yet Allah saved him from the fire; there are truly signs in that for people who believe.
verses.25: He said, you have taken idols besides Allah only out of mutual affection in this worldly life, but on the Day of Resurrection you will disown one another and curse one another, and your home will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers.
verses.26: Lot believed in him, and Abraham said, I am emigrating toward my Lord; He is truly the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.27: We granted Abraham Isaac and Jacob, placed prophethood and Scripture among his descendants, gave him his reward in this world, and in the Hereafter he will surely be among the righteous.
verses.28: Lot said to his people, you commit an indecency no community before you has ever committed.
verses.29: Do you really approach men, waylay travelers, and commit wrongdoing in your own gatherings? His people's only answer was, bring us Allah's punishment, if you're telling the truth.
verses.30: He prayed, my Lord, help me against this people who spread corruption.
verses.31: When Our messengers came to Abraham with good news, they said, we are about to destroy the people of this city — its people have truly been wrongdoers.
verses.32: He said, but Lot is there. They answered, we know best who is there; we will surely save him and his family, except his wife, who will remain among those who stay behind.
verses.33: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed and felt powerless to protect them; they reassured him, do not fear or grieve — we will save you and your family, except your wife, who will remain among those who stay behind.
verses.34: We are about to send down a punishment from the sky on the people of this town because of their persistent wrongdoing.
verses.35: And We have left of it a clear sign for people who use reason.
verses.36: To Madyan We sent their brother Shu'ayb, and he said, my people, worship Allah and look forward to the Last Day, and do not commit corruption on earth.
verses.37: But they rejected him, and the earthquake seized them, leaving them lying lifeless in their homes by morning.
verses.38: And Ad and Thamud too — this is clear to you from the ruins of their homes; Satan made their deeds appealing to them and turned them from the right path, even though they were people of insight.
verses.39: And Qarun, Pharaoh, and Haman — Moses came to them with clear proofs, and they grew arrogant on earth, yet they could not outrun Us.
verses.40: So We seized each one for their own sin — some were struck by a violent storm, some overtaken by a mighty blast, some swallowed by the earth, and some We drowned; Allah never wronged them — they wronged themselves.
verses.41: Those who take protectors besides Allah are like the spider that builds itself a home — and the spider's home is the flimsiest of all houses, if only they realized it.
verses.42: Allah knows exactly what they call upon instead of Him — He is Exalted in Might, truly Wise.
verses.43: These examples are set before people, yet only those with real understanding actually grasp them.
verses.44: Allah created the heavens and earth in perfect truth — there is a sign in that for anyone who believes.
verses.45: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, and keep up prayer — prayer restrains a person from indecency and wrongdoing, and remembering Allah is greater still; Allah knows everything you do.
verses.46: Do not argue with the People of Scripture except in the best possible way, unless they act unjustly; say, we believe in what was revealed to us and to you — our God and yours are one and the same, and to Him we submit.
verses.47: This is how the Book was revealed to you — those already given Scripture recognize it, and some among these people believe in it too; only the confirmed disbelievers reject Our signs.
verses.48: Before this, you never recited any scripture, nor wrote one with your own hand — otherwise, those determined to deny it would have had real grounds for doubt.
verses.49: Rather, these verses live clearly in the hearts of those given true knowledge, and only the wrongdoers reject Our signs.
verses.50: They ask, why haven't signs been sent down to him from his Lord? Say, signs belong to Allah alone — I am only here to give a clear warning.
verses.51: Isn't it enough for them that this Book has been revealed to you, recited to them? There is mercy and reminder in it for people who truly believe.
verses.52: Say, Allah is sufficient as witness between me and you — He knows everything in the heavens and earth; those who believe in falsehood and reject Allah are the true losers.
verses.53: They keep urging you to hurry the punishment along; had there not been an appointed time, it would already have reached them — and it will still arrive suddenly, when they least expect it.
verses.54: They keep urging you to hurry the punishment, yet Hell already surrounds the disbelievers completely.
verses.55: Picture the Day the punishment overtakes them from above and rises up beneath their very feet, and it will be said, taste now the result of what you used to do.
verses.56: My servants who believe — My earth is truly vast, so worship Me and Me alone, wherever that takes you.
verses.57: Every soul will taste death, and then all of you will be returned to Us.
verses.58: Those who believe and live righteously will be settled by Us in the highest chambers of Paradise, beneath which rivers flow, dwelling there forever — what an excellent reward for those who worked for it.
verses.59: These are the ones who remained patient and placed their trust firmly in their Lord.
verses.60: How many a creature carries no provision of its own — Allah provides for it, just as He provides for you; He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.61: If you asked them who created the heavens and earth and set the sun and moon to their tasks, they would surely say, Allah — so how do they still end up so deceived?
verses.62: Allah extends provision for whichever of His servants He wills, and limits it for others — Allah has full knowledge of everything.
verses.63: If you asked them who sends rain down from the sky and revives dead earth with it, they would say, Allah — say, praise be to Allah, then, though most of them still don't use their reason.
verses.64: This worldly life is nothing more than distraction and passing amusement — the real, lasting life is the home of the Hereafter, if only they understood.
verses.65: When they board a ship in danger, they call on Allah alone, utterly sincere — yet the moment He brings them safely to shore, they go straight back to setting up partners beside Him.
verses.66: As if determined to reject what We've given them, and simply enjoy themselves instead — but they are going to find out where that leads.
verses.67: Have they not noticed that We established a safe sanctuary for them, while all around, people elsewhere are being seized and swept away? Do they still believe in falsehood and reject Allah's own favor toward them?
verses.68: Who does greater wrong than someone who invents a lie about Allah, or denies the truth once it has reached him? Isn't there already a home prepared in Hell for people like that?
verses.69: As for those who strive earnestly for Our sake, We will surely guide them along Our ways — Allah stands, always, with those who strive to do good.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Meem — again, letters left deliberately unexplained.
verses.2: The Byzantines have been defeated,
verses.3: in the nearest part of the land — yet after their defeat, they will themselves be victorious
verses.4: within a handful of years. The matter belongs entirely to Allah, before and after, and on that day the believers will rejoice —
verses.5: in Allah's help. He gives victory to whoever He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verses.6: This is Allah's promise, and Allah never breaks His promise, though most people simply don't know it.
verses.7: They know only the outward surface of this worldly life, while remaining utterly unaware of the Hereafter.
verses.8: Have they never reflected within themselves? Allah did not create the heavens and earth, and everything between them, except in truth and for an appointed term — yet so many people deny even the meeting with their Lord.
verses.9: Have they not traveled through the land and seen how earlier peoples ended, people even stronger than them, who cultivated the earth and built it up even more than these people have? Messengers came to them with clear proofs, and Allah never wronged them — they wronged themselves.
verses.10: Then the end of those who did evil was simply evil, since they denied Allah's signs and mocked them relentlessly.
verses.11: Allah originates creation, then repeats it, and then to Him you will all be returned.
verses.12: On the Day the Hour arrives, the criminals will fall into utter despair.
verses.13: None of the partners they set up will be able to intercede for them, and they themselves will deny those very partners.
verses.14: On the Day the Hour arrives, people will be separated into distinct groups.
verses.15: Those who believed and lived righteously will find themselves delighted in a garden.
verses.16: But those who disbelieved and denied Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter will be brought forward to face punishment.
verses.17: So glorify Allah in the evening and in the morning,
verses.18: and to Him belongs all praise in the heavens and the earth, at dusk and at midday.
verses.19: He brings the living out of the dead and the dead out of the living, and revives the earth after it has died — and in that same way, you too will be brought forth.
verses.20: Among His signs is that He created you from dust, and suddenly you are human beings scattered across the earth.
verses.21: Among His signs is that He created for you mates from among yourselves, so that you might find tranquility in them, and placed between you love and mercy — there is truly a sign in that for people who reflect.
verses.22: Among His signs, too, is the creation of the heavens and earth, and the diversity of your languages and colors — there is a sign in that for those who have knowledge.
verses.23: Among His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His bounty — there is a sign in that for people who listen.
verses.24: Among His signs, He shows you lightning, both as fear and hope, and sends rain from the sky, reviving the earth after it has died — there is a sign in that for people who use reason.
verses.25: Among His signs is that the heavens and earth stand firm by His command, and when He calls you once, out of the earth, you will suddenly emerge.
verses.26: To Him belongs everyone in the heavens and the earth — all are humbly obedient to Him.
verses.27: He originates creation, then repeats it, and this is even easier for Him — His is the highest description in the heavens and earth, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.28: He presents an example drawn from yourselves — would you accept those you own as slaves as full equal partners in whatever provision We've given you, sharing it equally, fearing them as you fear each other? This is how We explain the signs to people who use their reason.
verses.29: Yet the wrongdoers follow their own desires without any real knowledge — who could possibly guide someone Allah has allowed to stray? They will have no helpers.
verses.30: So set your face firmly toward the natural religion, the very nature Allah instilled within people at creation — there is no altering Allah's creation; this is the correct, upright faith, though most people simply don't know it.
verses.31: Turn back to Him constantly, hold Him in awe, keep up prayer, and never be among those who set up partners with Him —
verses.32: among those who have splintered their religion into sects, each faction delighted simply with whatever fragment it holds onto.
verses.33: When hardship touches people, they turn to their Lord in genuine repentance, but once He lets them taste His mercy again, a portion of them immediately goes back to setting up partners with Him,
verses.34: denying what He has given them all over again. Enjoy yourselves for now, then — you are going to find out.
verses.35: Have We ever actually sent them any real authority backing up what they associate with Him? Of course not.
verses.36: When people taste Allah's mercy, they celebrate it, but when hardship strikes them from their own doing, they collapse instantly into despair.
verses.37: Have they not seen that Allah extends provision for whoever He wills, and restricts it too? There are real signs in that for people who believe.
verses.38: So give relatives their due, and the poor, and the traveler stranded far from home — that is best for anyone seeking Allah's own pleasure, and it is exactly such people who will truly succeed.
verses.39: Whatever you give through interest, hoping it will grow someone's wealth, does not actually grow in Allah's sight — but whatever you give in charity, seeking Allah's pleasure, is what truly multiplies.
verses.40: Allah is the One who created you, then provided for you, then will cause you to die, then will bring you back to life — can any of the partners you invented do any single part of that? He is exalted, far above what they associate with Him.
verses.41: Corruption has spread across land and sea because of what people's own hands have done, so that Allah lets them taste a portion of the consequences — perhaps they will still turn back.
verses.42: Say, travel through the land and see how earlier peoples ended — most of them had set up partners with Allah too.
verses.43: So set your face firmly toward the true religion before a Day arrives from Allah that nothing can hold back — on that Day people will be split apart from one another.
verses.44: Whoever disbelieves bears the weight of that disbelief alone, and whoever lives righteously is simply preparing a place for themselves.
verses.45: This is so Allah may reward those who believed and lived righteously, purely out of His own generosity — He has no love for those who reject Him.
verses.46: Among His signs is that He sends winds carrying good news, letting you taste His mercy, allowing ships to sail by His command, letting you seek His provision, so that you might be grateful.
verses.47: Messengers were sent before you, each to their own people, bringing clear proofs — and We brought consequences upon those who committed crimes, since supporting the believers was always Our responsibility.
verses.48: It is Allah who sends the winds that stir the clouds, spreading them across the sky exactly as He wills, breaking them into fragments so you see rain finally emerge from within — and when it falls on whichever of His servants He chooses, they immediately rejoice,
verses.49: even though, just moments before it fell, they had been sunk in complete despair.
verses.50: Look closely, then, at the traces of Allah's mercy — how He revives the earth after it has died; this same Lord will revive the dead too, and He has power over absolutely everything.
verses.51: Yet if He sent a wind that left their crops withered yellow, they would still turn straight back to disbelief afterward.
verses.52: You truly cannot make the dead hear you, nor can you make the deaf hear your call once they've turned their backs and walked away.
verses.53: And you cannot lead the blind out of their own error — you can only reach those who already believe in Our signs and have submitted to Allah.
verses.54: It is Allah who created you in weakness, then gave you strength after that weakness, then brought weakness and gray hair after the strength — He creates whatever He wills, and He is All-Knowing, fully Capable.
verses.55: On the Day the Hour finally arrives, the guilty will swear they had lingered only an hour — this is simply how thoroughly they had deceived themselves.
verses.56: But those given real knowledge and faith will say, no — you actually remained, by Allah's decree, until the Day of Resurrection; and this is that very Day, though you never realized it.
verses.57: That Day, no excuse will do the wrongdoers any good, and they won't even be given the chance to make amends.
verses.58: This Quran presents people every kind of example imaginable — yet even if you brought them an undeniable sign, the disbelievers would still insist you were simply making it up.
verses.59: This is exactly how Allah seals the hearts of those who refuse to understand.
verses.60: So be patient — Allah's promise is nothing but the truth, and don't let those without real certainty unsettle you.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Meem —
verses.2: These are the verses of the wise Book —
verses.3: Guidance and mercy for those who do good —
verses.4: Who establish prayer, give charity, and hold firm certainty in the Hereafter.
verses.5: Those are upon guidance from their Lord, and it is truly they who succeed.
verses.6: And among people is the one who buys idle, distracting talk, meant to lead others astray from Allah's path, without any real knowledge, treating it all as a joke; for such a person is a humiliating punishment.
verses.7: And when Our verses are recited to him, he turns away in arrogance, as though he never heard them at all, as though deafness sat in his ears; give him news of a painful punishment.
verses.8: Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds will have the Gardens of Pleasure —
verses.9: Remaining there forever; Allah's promise is true, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.10: He created the heavens without any visible pillars, and set firmly rooted mountains into the earth, so it would not shake beneath you, and scattered every kind of creature throughout it; and We sent down rain from the sky, causing every noble kind of growth to flourish.
verses.11: This is Allah's creation — so show Me what those besides Him have ever created. Rather, the wrongdoers are lost in clear error.
verses.12: And indeed, We gave Luqman wisdom, saying, be grateful to Allah; and whoever is grateful is grateful only for his own benefit, while whoever denies it — indeed, Allah is Free of need, Praiseworthy.
verses.13: And recall when Luqman said to his son, while advising him, my son, do not associate anything with Allah; indeed, associating partners with Him is a truly great injustice.
verses.14: And We have commanded man to care for his parents; his mother carried him through weakness upon weakness, and his weaning takes two full years; be grateful to Me, and to your parents — to Me is your final return.
verses.15: But if they pressure you to associate with Me something you have no knowledge of, do not obey them, yet still accompany them in this world with kindness, and follow the path of those who sincerely turn back to Me; then to Me is your return, and I will inform you of everything you did.
verses.16: My son, Luqman continued, even if a wrong were the weight of a mustard seed, hidden inside a rock, or anywhere in the heavens or the earth, Allah would bring it to light; indeed, Allah is Subtle, fully Aware.
verses.17: My son, establish prayer, encourage what is right, forbid what is wrong, and remain patient through whatever befalls you; indeed, that takes real resolve.
verses.18: And do not turn your cheek away from people in contempt, nor walk through the earth arrogantly; indeed, Allah does not love anyone excessively proud or boastful.
verses.19: And walk at a moderate pace, and lower your voice; indeed, the harshest of all sounds is the braying of a donkey — Luqman's advice closing here with this vivid, almost humorous, image of humility.
verses.20: Do you not see that Allah has made everything in the heavens and everything on earth useful to you, and has showered you generously with His favors, both seen and unseen? Yet among people is the one who argues about Allah without knowledge, guidance, or any illuminating Book.
verses.21: And when it is said to them, follow what Allah has revealed, they say, rather, we will follow what we found our fathers doing — even if Satan were inviting them straight toward the punishment of the Blaze.
verses.22: And whoever submits himself fully to Allah, while doing good, has truly grasped the most trustworthy handhold; and to Allah belongs the outcome of every matter.
verses.23: And whoever disbelieves — let his disbelief not grieve you; to Us is their return, and We will inform them of everything they did; indeed, Allah knows fully what lies within every chest.
verses.24: We let them enjoy themselves a little while, then We will force them toward a truly massive punishment.
verses.25: And if you asked them, who created the heavens and the earth? They would surely say, Allah. Say: all praise belongs to Allah — yet most of them simply do not realize it.
verses.26: To Allah belongs everything in the heavens and the earth; indeed, Allah is Free of need, Praiseworthy.
verses.27: And if every tree on earth were pens, and the sea, refilled by seven more seas after it, were ink, Allah's words would never run out; indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.28: Your creation and your resurrection are, to Him, no different than the creation of a single soul; indeed, Allah is Hearing, Seeing.
verses.29: Do you not see that Allah merges the night into the day, and the day into the night, and has made the sun and the moon subject to His command, each running its course for an appointed term, and that Allah is fully aware of everything you do?
verses.30: That is because Allah is the truth, and whatever they call upon besides Him is falsehood, and because Allah is the Most High, the Grand.
verses.31: Do you not see that ships sail across the sea by Allah's favor, so that He might show you some of His signs? Indeed, in that are signs for everyone patient and grateful.
verses.32: And when waves overwhelm them like dark canopies, they call upon Allah, sincere in their devotion to Him; yet once He delivers them safely to land, some among them settle only for moderate faith; and none rejects Our signs except every treacherous, ungrateful person.
verses.33: O mankind, hold awe of your Lord, and fear a Day when no father can help his son at all, nor can any son help his father in the slightest; indeed, Allah's promise is true, so do not let the life of this world deceive you, nor let the Deceiver mislead you about Allah.
verses.34: Indeed, with Allah alone rests knowledge of the Hour; He sends down the rain, and knows exactly what lies within every womb; and no soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die; indeed, Allah is Knowing, fully Aware — the surah closing on this quiet reminder that only Allah holds knowledge of what tomorrow truly brings.

verses.1: Alif, Lam, Meem —
verses.2: The revelation of this Book is, without any doubt, from the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.3: Or do they say, he invented it himself? Rather, it is the truth from your Lord, meant to warn a people who received no warner before you, so that perhaps they might be guided.
verses.4: It is Allah who created the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, in six days, then established Himself above the Throne; you have no protector or intercessor besides Him — so will you not take heed?
verses.5: He governs every matter from the heaven to the earth, and it ascends back to Him within a single Day, whose length equals a thousand years by your counting.
verses.6: That is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Merciful —
verses.7: Who perfected everything He created, and began the creation of man from clay —
verses.8: Then made his descendants from the extract of a humble fluid —
verses.9: Then shaped him fully, and breathed into him something of His own spirit, and gave you hearing, sight, and hearts; yet how little you show gratitude.
verses.10: And they say, once we are lost within the earth, will we truly be recreated? Rather, they simply disbelieve in meeting their Lord at all.
verses.11: Say: the angel of death, entrusted with you, will take your soul; then to your Lord you will be returned.
verses.12: If only you could see the guilty, heads lowered before their Lord, pleading, our Lord, we have now seen and heard, so send us back — we will do what is right; indeed, now we are truly certain.
verses.13: And had We willed, We could have given every soul its guidance; but My word has already been settled — I will surely fill Hell with jinn and mankind, all together.
