verses.14: So taste this, for having forgotten the meeting of this very Day; indeed, We have likewise forgotten you — taste the everlasting punishment for everything you used to do.
verses.15: Only those truly believe in Our verses who, when reminded of them, fall down in prostration, glorifying their Lord with praise, without a trace of arrogance.
verses.16: Their sides rise up from their beds, calling upon their Lord in both fear and hope, and they spend generously from what We have provided them.
verses.17: And no soul knows what joy has been hidden away for them, as a reward for everything they used to do.
verses.18: So is the one who truly believed the same as the one who was defiantly disobedient? They are never equal.
verses.19: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, for them are the Gardens of Refuge, a welcome for everything they used to do.
verses.20: But as for those who defiantly disobeyed, their refuge is the Fire; every time they try to leave it, they are dragged back, and told, taste the punishment of the Fire you once denied.
verses.21: And We will surely let them taste a lesser punishment even before the greater one, so that perhaps they might still turn back.
verses.22: And who is more unjust than the one reminded of his Lord's verses, only to turn away from them? Indeed, We will surely take retribution from the guilty.
verses.23: And indeed, We gave Moses the Scripture, so have no doubt about his meeting with Allah; and We made it a guide for the Children of Israel.
verses.24: And We appointed leaders among them, guiding by Our command, once they remained patient and held firm certainty in Our signs.
verses.25: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection, concerning everything they used to differ about.
verses.26: Has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them, whose ruins they still walk among today? Indeed, in that are real signs — will they still not listen?
verses.27: Have they not seen how We drive water to a barren land, and bring forth from it crops that both their livestock and they themselves eat? Will they still not see?
verses.28: And they ask, when will this Decision come, if you are truthful?
verses.29: Say: on the Day of Decision, the newfound belief of the disbelievers will do them no good at all, nor will they be given any further respite.
verses.30: So turn away from them, and wait — indeed, they too are waiting, the surah closing on this quiet, patient standoff between the sincere and the arrogant.

verses.1: O Prophet, hold Allah in awe, and do not obey the disbelievers and hypocrites — Allah is truly Knowing and Wise.
verses.2: Follow instead what is revealed to you from your Lord; Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.3: Place your trust in Allah — He is more than sufficient as a Guardian.
verses.4: Allah has not placed two hearts within any man's chest, nor made the wives you liken to your mothers actually your mothers, nor made adopted sons your real sons — these are just words spoken with your mouths; Allah speaks the truth and guides to the right way.
verses.5: Call adopted sons by their real fathers' names — that is more just before Allah; if you don't know their fathers, then they are your brothers in faith and companions; there's no blame for honest mistakes, only for what your hearts deliberately intend.
verses.6: The Prophet has a stronger claim on the believers than they have on themselves, and his wives are their mothers; blood relatives have a stronger claim over one another in Allah's Book than other believers and emigrants do, unless you do a kindness for your close friends — this too is written in the Book.
verses.7: Recall when Allah took a covenant from the prophets — from you, from Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus son of Mary — We took from them a solemn covenant,
verses.8: so that He might question the truthful about their truthfulness, and He has prepared a painful punishment for the disbelievers.
verses.9: Believers, remember Allah's favor upon you when armies came against you, and We sent a wind and forces you could not see against them — Allah sees everything you do.
verses.10: When they came at you from above and from below, when eyes shifted in terror and hearts rose into throats, and you thought all sorts of things about Allah.
verses.11: There the believers were truly tested, and shaken severely.
verses.12: The hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts said, Allah and His Messenger promised us nothing but delusion.
verses.13: A group of them said, people of Yathrib, there's no place for you here — retreat! Some among them even sought the Prophet's permission to leave, claiming their homes were exposed, when really they weren't exposed at all — they only wanted to flee.
verses.14: Had the enemy entered from every side and they'd been asked to turn against the faith, they would have done it almost without hesitation.
verses.15: Yet they had already pledged to Allah earlier that they would not turn their backs — and a pledge to Allah will always be questioned.
verses.16: Say, fleeing will not benefit you at all if you're trying to escape death or being killed, and even then you would enjoy this life only a little while longer.
verses.17: Say, who could protect you from Allah if He willed harm for you, or willed mercy for you? They will find no protector or helper besides Allah.
verses.18: Allah already knows exactly who among you holds others back, and who tells their companions, come join us, while contributing almost nothing to the fighting themselves.
verses.19: They are stingy toward you; when fear comes, you see them looking at you with eyes rolling like someone facing death, yet once the fear passes they lash out at you with sharp tongues, greedy even for the spoils — such people have never truly believed, and Allah has made their deeds worthless; that is easy for Allah.
verses.20: They imagine the confederates haven't even left, and if the confederates did come again, they would wish they were out in the desert among nomads instead, asking for news about you from a distance — and even if they were among you, they would barely fight at all.
verses.21: In the Messenger of Allah you truly have an excellent example, for anyone who hopes for Allah and the Last Day and remembers Allah often.
verses.22: When the believers saw the confederates, they said, this is exactly what Allah and His Messenger promised us, and Allah and His Messenger told the truth — it only increased their faith and their submission.
verses.23: Among the believers are men who have been true to what they pledged to Allah — some have fulfilled their vow through death, and some are still waiting, without changing in the slightest.
verses.24: This is so Allah may reward the truthful for their truthfulness, and punish the hypocrites if He wills, or accept their repentance — Allah is truly Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.25: Allah turned back the disbelievers in their rage, having gained no benefit at all, and He spared the believers from having to fight — Allah is Strong and Exalted in Might.
verses.26: He brought down from their fortresses those among the People of the Scripture who had supported the confederates, casting terror into their hearts — some you killed, and some you took captive.
verses.27: He made you inherit their land, their homes, their wealth, and land you had not even set foot on before — Allah has power over absolutely everything.
verses.28: O Prophet, tell your wives — if you desire this worldly life and its glitter, then come, I will provide for you and release you honorably.
verses.29: But if you desire Allah, His Messenger, and the home of the Hereafter, then Allah has surely prepared a magnificent reward for those among you who do good.
verses.30: Wives of the Prophet, whoever among you commits a clear act of gross immorality — her punishment would be doubled, and that is easy for Allah.
verses.31: Whoever among you remains devoutly obedient to Allah and His Messenger and lives righteously will receive a double reward, and We have prepared a truly generous provision for her.
verses.32: Wives of the Prophet, you are not like other women — if you hold Allah in awe, don't let your speech turn soft in a way that might tempt someone with sickness in his heart; speak instead with clear, appropriate words.
verses.33: Stay settled in your homes, and don't display yourselves the way women did in the old days of ignorance; keep up prayer, give charity, and obey Allah and His Messenger. Allah simply wants to remove impurity from you, people of this household, and purify you completely.
verses.34: Remember what is recited in your homes of Allah's verses and wisdom — Allah is truly Subtle and Aware of everything.
verses.35: Muslim men and Muslim women, believing men and believing women, obedient men and obedient women, truthful men and truthful women, patient men and patient women, humble men and humble women, charitable men and charitable women, fasting men and fasting women, men and women who guard their modesty, men and women who remember Allah often — for all of them, Allah has prepared forgiveness and a tremendous reward.
verses.36: Once Allah and His Messenger have decided a matter, no believing man or woman has any further choice in it — whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger has clearly wandered into open error.
verses.37: Recall when you told the man Allah had favored, and you yourself had favored, keep your wife and hold Allah in awe, while quietly holding back something Allah was about to reveal — you feared people, though Allah deserved that fear far more. So once he no longer needed her, We married her to you, so that believers would face no discomfort marrying the former wives of their adopted sons once that need had ended — Allah's command is always carried out.
verses.38: There is no blame on the Prophet for what Allah has assigned to him — this was always the way with the prophets who came before, and Allah's decree is a destiny already settled.
verses.39: Those who deliver Allah's messages, hold Him in awe, and fear no one else at all — Allah is more than sufficient to keep account of everything.
verses.40: Muhammad is not the father of any man among you, but he is the Messenger of Allah and the last of the prophets — Allah has full knowledge of everything.
verses.41: Believers, remember Allah often, again and again,
verses.42: and glorify Him morning and evening.
verses.43: It is He who showers blessings upon you, with His angels asking the same on your behalf, so as to lead you out of darkness into light — He is endlessly Merciful to the believers.
verses.44: Their greeting on the Day they finally meet Him will be peace itself, and He has prepared for them a truly generous reward.
verses.45: O Prophet, We have sent you as a witness, a bringer of good news, and a warner,
verses.46: one who invites people to Allah by His permission, and a shining lamp for them to follow.
verses.47: Give the believers good news — a great bounty from Allah awaits them.
verses.48: Do not give in to the disbelievers or the hypocrites, and let their harassment simply pass you by; place your trust in Allah — He is more than sufficient to manage every affair.
verses.49: Believers, if you marry believing women and then divorce them before ever touching them, there's no waiting period required of them at all — simply provide for them generously and release them with grace.
verses.50: O Prophet, We have made lawful to you your wives whose dowries you've given, those under your care from among captives Allah has granted you, cousins on both your father's and mother's side who emigrated with you, and any believing woman who offers herself to the Prophet, should he wish to marry her — this last provision belongs to you alone among the believers, since We already know what We've made obligatory for others regarding their own wives, so that no hardship falls on you; Allah is truly Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.51: You may postpone whichever of them you wish, or draw close whichever you wish, and there is no blame in bringing back one you had set aside — this makes it easier for them to remain content, free of grief, satisfied with whatever you give each of them; Allah knows exactly what is in your heart, and He is All-Knowing, endlessly Forbearing.
verses.52: No further wives are permitted to you beyond this point, nor may you exchange these for other women, even if their beauty impressed you, except those already under your care — Allah watches over everything.
verses.53: Believers, do not enter the Prophet's private rooms unless invited for a meal, and don't linger waiting for it to be prepared. When invited, come in, and once you've eaten, disperse rather than lingering to chat — this had been troubling the Prophet, though he was too gracious to say so; Allah, however, is never shy about stating the truth plainly. When you ask his wives for anything, ask from behind a screen — that is purer for everyone's hearts; and you must never harm the Messenger of Allah, nor ever marry his wives after him — that would be an enormity in Allah's sight.
verses.54: Whether you reveal something or hide it, Allah already knows all of it completely.
verses.55: There is no restriction for these women in the presence of their fathers, sons, brothers, nephews, their own women, or those under their care — but hold Allah in awe, for He witnesses everything.
verses.56: Allah showers blessings upon the Prophet, and so do His angels — believers, you too should ask Allah's blessings and peace upon him.
verses.57: Those who insult Allah and His Messenger have earned Allah's curse in this world and the next, and a humiliating punishment awaits them.
verses.58: And those who harm believing men and women for something they never actually did have taken on themselves a genuine slander and open sin.
verses.59: O Prophet, tell your wives, your daughters, and the believing women to draw their outer garments closer around themselves — this makes them more easily recognized, sparing them from harassment; Allah is truly Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.60: If the hypocrites, those with sickness in their hearts, and the rumor-spreaders in Madinah do not stop, We will surely rouse you against them, leaving them barely any time left as your neighbors there.
verses.61: Cursed wherever they're found, they will be seized and dealt with decisively —
verses.62: this has always been Allah's established way with earlier generations, and you will never find any real change in Allah's way of doing things.
verses.63: People ask you about the Hour; say, only Allah truly knows it — and who's to say the Hour isn't already close at hand?
verses.64: Allah has cursed the disbelievers and prepared for them a raging fire,
verses.65: where they will remain forever, finding no protector or helper anywhere.
verses.66: On the Day their faces are turned over and over in that Fire, they will cry, if only we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger.
verses.67: They will say, our Lord, we simply obeyed our leaders and our elders, and they were the ones who led us astray from the right path.
verses.68: Our Lord, give them double the punishment, and curse them with a truly great curse.
verses.69: Believers, do not be like those who once mistreated Moses — Allah cleared him of what they alleged, and he remained, in Allah's sight, held in high honor.
verses.70: Believers, hold Allah in awe, and speak words that are simply just and fitting.
verses.71: He will then set your deeds right for you and forgive your sins — and whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger has truly achieved a tremendous success.
verses.72: Allah offered the Trust to the heavens, the earth, and the mountains, and each declined to carry it, afraid of what it meant — yet humanity took it on, and in doing so proved itself capable of real injustice and real ignorance.
verses.73: This is so Allah may hold accountable the hypocrite men and women, and the idolatrous men and women, and accept the repentance of believing men and women — Allah is truly Forgiving and Merciful.

verses.1: All praise belongs to Allah, to whom belongs everything in the heavens and everything on the earth, and to Him belongs all praise in the Hereafter as well; He is the Wise, the Fully Aware.
verses.2: He knows what enters the earth and what emerges from it, what descends from the sky and what ascends into it; and He is the Merciful, the Forgiving.
verses.3: But the disbelievers say, the Hour will never come to us. Say: yes, by my Lord, it will surely come to you — Knower of the unseen; not even an atom's weight escapes Him, whether in the heavens or the earth, nor anything smaller or greater, without it being recorded clearly —
verses.4: So that He may reward those who believe and do righteous deeds; for them is forgiveness, and generous provision.
verses.5: But those who strive against Our verses, seeking to undermine them, will have a painful punishment of the harshest kind.
verses.6: And those given real knowledge see clearly that what has been revealed to you from your Lord is the truth, guiding toward the path of the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy.
verses.7: Yet the disbelievers say, shall we point you toward a man who claims that once you are completely scattered to dust, you will be created anew —
verses.8: Has he invented a lie about Allah, or is there some madness in him? Rather, it is those who reject the Hereafter who are truly in punishment and deep error.
verses.9: Do they not look at what surrounds them, before them and behind them, of sky and earth? Had We willed, We could make the earth swallow them, or let pieces of sky fall upon them; indeed, in that is a sign for every servant who sincerely turns back to Allah.
verses.10: And indeed, We granted David special favor from Us: mountains, join him in praise, We said, and the birds too; and We made iron soft and pliable for him —
verses.11: Make full coats of armor, and measure the links with care, and all of you, work righteousness; indeed, I see clearly everything you do.
verses.12: And for Solomon, We subjected the wind — its morning journey equal to a month, and its evening journey equal to a month — and We made a spring of molten copper flow for him; and among the jinn were those who worked under him by his Lord's permission, and whoever among them deviated from Our command, We made him taste the punishment of the Blaze.
verses.13: They made for him whatever he wished — grand chambers, statues, basins as wide as reservoirs, and firmly fixed cooking pots. Work, family of David, in gratitude — for few of My servants are truly grateful.
verses.14: And when We decreed his death, nothing revealed it to the jinn except a small creature of the earth gnawing at his staff; and when he finally fell, it became clear to the jinn that had they truly known the unseen, they would not have remained in that humiliating labor.
verses.15: There was indeed a sign for the people of Saba in their homeland — two gardens, one on the right, one on the left. Eat from your Lord's provision, and be grateful to Him; a fine land, and a forgiving Lord.
verses.16: But they turned away, so We sent upon them the flood of the dam, and replaced their two fine gardens with gardens bearing bitter fruit, tamarisk shrubs, and a few scattered lote trees.
verses.17: That is how We repaid them for their ingratitude; and do We ever repay anyone this way, except the deeply ungrateful?
verses.18: And We placed between them and the towns We had blessed other towns clearly visible, and set the distances between them for easy travel; travel between them by night or day in complete safety, We told them —
verses.19: But they said, our Lord, make the distance between our journeys longer, wronging themselves in the process; so We made them into cautionary tales, scattering them entirely. Indeed, in that are signs for everyone patient and grateful.
verses.20: And Iblees had already confirmed his suspicion about them, and they followed him — except for a group of true believers.
verses.21: And he held no real authority over them; it was only so that We might distinguish who truly believed in the Hereafter from who remained in doubt about it; and your Lord watches carefully over all things.
verses.22: Say: call upon those you claim as gods besides Allah; they own not even an atom's weight in the heavens or the earth, hold no partnership in either, and He needs no assistant among them at all.
verses.23: And intercession with Him benefits no one, except whom He permits. Until, when fear is finally lifted from their hearts, they will ask one another, what did your Lord say? They will answer, the truth; and He is the Most High, the Grand.
verses.24: Say: who provides for you from the heavens and the earth? Say: Allah. And indeed, either we or you are upon guidance, or clearly astray.
verses.25: Say: you will not be questioned about what we have done, nor will we be questioned about what you do.
verses.26: Say: our Lord will bring us together, then judge between us in truth; and He is the Knowing Judge.
verses.27: Say: show me those you have joined to Him as partners; no — rather, He alone is Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.28: And We have not sent you except as a messenger to all of humanity, bringing good news and a warning; yet most people simply do not know it.
verses.29: And they ask, when is this promise, if you are truthful?
verses.30: Say: for you is the appointment of a Day you cannot delay, even for a moment, nor bring forward.
verses.31: And the disbelievers say, we will never believe in this Quran, nor in what came before it. But if only you could see the wrongdoers standing before their Lord, hurling blame back and forth — the oppressed will say to the arrogant, had it not been for you, we would surely have believed —
verses.32: The arrogant will answer the oppressed, did we truly keep you from guidance after it had reached you? Rather, you yourselves were guilty too.
verses.33: And the oppressed will reply to the arrogant, rather, it was your scheming, night and day, ordering us to disbelieve in Allah and set up equals to Him. And they will all conceal their deep regret once they see the punishment, and We will place shackles around the necks of the disbelievers; are they repaid for anything but what they used to do?
verses.34: And We never sent a warner to any town without its wealthy elite saying, indeed, we reject what you have been sent with.
verses.35: And they said, we have more wealth and more children, and we will never be punished.
verses.36: Say: indeed, my Lord extends provision for whom He wills, and restricts it too, though most people simply do not know it.
verses.37: And it is neither your wealth nor your children that bring you closer to Us; rather, it is faith and righteous deeds; such people will have a double reward for what they did, and will dwell safely in the elevated chambers of Paradise.
verses.38: And those who strive against Our verses, seeking to undermine them — they will be brought into punishment to remain.
verses.39: Say: indeed, my Lord extends provision for whom He wills among His servants, and restricts it too; and whatever you spend in His cause, He will replace it, for He is the best of providers.
verses.40: And picture the Day He gathers them all, then asks the angels, did these people used to worship you?
verses.41: They will answer, exalted are You; You alone are our true protector, not them; rather, they used to worship the jinn — most of them believed in the jinn.
verses.42: So today, none of you holds any power to benefit or harm another; and We will say to the wrongdoers, taste the punishment of the Fire you used to deny.
verses.43: And when Our clear verses are recited to them, they say, this is just a man who wants to turn you away from what your fathers used to worship; and they say, this is nothing but an invented lie. And the disbelievers say of the truth, once it reaches them, this is nothing but plain magic.
verses.44: Yet We had given them no scriptures to study, and had sent them no warner before you.
verses.45: And those before them denied too, though these people have not received even a tenth of what We had given the earlier ones; yet they denied My messengers — so how terrible was My rejection of them.
verses.46: Say: I advise you of only one thing — that you stand before Allah, in pairs or alone, and simply reflect; there is no madness in your companion; he is only a warner to you, ahead of a severe punishment.
verses.47: Say: whatever payment I might ask of you, it is yours to keep; my reward comes only from Allah, and He is Witness over all things.
verses.48: Say: indeed, my Lord sends forth the truth; He is the Knower of the unseen.
verses.49: Say: the truth has arrived, and falsehood can neither begin anything new, nor bring anything back.
verses.50: Say: if I err, I err only against myself; but if I am rightly guided, it is because of what my Lord reveals to me; indeed, He is Hearing, ever near.
verses.51: And if only you could see them terrified, with no escape, seized suddenly from a place close by —
verses.52: They will cry, now we believe in it! But how could faith possibly be reached from so far away, at such a late hour?
verses.53: For they had already rejected it before, hurling accusations at the unseen from a place far removed from the truth.
verses.54: And a barrier will be placed between them and everything they desire, just as was done with people like them before; indeed, they had lived in deep, unsettled doubt — the surah closing on this final image of a chance, once offered freely, now permanently out of reach.

verses.1: All praise belongs to Allah, Originator of the heavens and the earth, who made the angels messengers with wings — two, three, or four; He increases in creation whatever He wills; indeed, Allah is capable of all things.
verses.2: Whatever mercy Allah opens for people, no one can withhold; and whatever He withholds, no one can release afterward; and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.3: O mankind, remember Allah's favor upon you; is there any creator besides Allah who provides for you from the sky and the earth? There is no god except Him — so how are you so easily deceived?
verses.4: And if they deny you, messengers before you were already denied too; and to Allah all matters return.
verses.5: O mankind, indeed Allah's promise is true, so do not let the life of this world deceive you, nor let the Deceiver deceive you about Allah.
verses.6: Indeed, Satan is an enemy to you, so treat him as an enemy; he only invites his followers toward becoming companions of the Blaze.
verses.7: Those who disbelieve will have a severe punishment, while those who believe and do righteous deeds will have forgiveness and a great reward.
verses.8: So is the one whose evil deeds have been made appealing to him, so he sees them as good, the same as one truly guided? Indeed, Allah lets go astray whom He wills, and guides whom He wills; so do not let yourself waste away in grief over them; indeed, Allah knows fully what they do.
verses.9: And it is Allah who sends the winds, stirring the clouds, which We then drive to a dead land, reviving the earth after its lifelessness; such is the resurrection.
verses.10: Whoever seeks honor — all honor belongs to Allah; good words rise up to Him, and righteous deeds lift them higher still; but those who plot evil will face a severe punishment, and their scheming will simply come to nothing.
verses.11: And Allah created you from dust, then from a small drop, then made you into pairs; no female conceives or gives birth without His knowledge, and no one's life is extended or shortened except that it is recorded; indeed, that is easy for Allah.
verses.12: And the two seas are not alike; one is fresh and pleasant to drink, and one is salty and bitter; yet from both you eat tender meat, and extract ornaments to wear, and you see ships cutting through them, so that you might seek His bounty, and perhaps be grateful.
verses.13: He merges the night into the day, and the day into the night, and has made the sun and moon subject to His command, each running its course for an appointed term; that is Allah, your Lord; His is the dominion; and whatever you call upon besides Him owns not even the thin skin of a date seed.
verses.14: If you call upon them, they cannot hear your call; and even if they heard, they could not answer you; and on the Day of Resurrection, they will deny your worship entirely; and none can inform you like the One who is fully Aware.
verses.15: O mankind, it is you who stand in need of Allah, while Allah is Free of need, the Praiseworthy.
verses.16: If He wills, He could remove you entirely, and bring forth a new creation —
verses.17: And that would not be difficult for Allah at all.
verses.18: And no soul bears the burden of another; and if a heavily burdened soul calls out for help with its load, none of it will be carried, even by a close relative. You can only truly warn those who fear their Lord unseen, and establish prayer; and whoever purifies himself does so only for his own benefit; and to Allah is the final return.
verses.19: Not equal are the blind and the seeing —
verses.20: Nor are the darkness and the light —
verses.21: Nor are the cool shade and the scorching heat —
verses.22: And not equal are the living and the dead; indeed, Allah causes whom He wills to hear, but you cannot make those in their graves hear at all.
verses.23: You are only a warner.
verses.24: Indeed, We have sent you with the truth, as a bringer of good news and a warner; and there was never a nation without a warner having passed among them.
verses.25: And if they deny you, those before them denied too; their messengers came with clear proofs, with scriptures, and with the illuminating Book.
verses.26: Then I seized those who disbelieved, and how terrible was My rejection of them.
verses.27: Do you not see how Allah sends down rain from the sky, and We bring forth fruits of varying colors through it? And within the mountains are streaks, white and red of differing shades, and some deeply black.
verses.28: And among people, animals, and livestock too are similarly varied colors; but only those of His servants who possess true knowledge truly hold Allah in awe; indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might, Forgiving.
verses.29: Indeed, those who recite the Book of Allah, establish prayer, and spend from what We have provided them, secretly and openly, can expect a transaction that will never fail —
verses.30: So that He may give them their reward in full, and increase them further from His bounty; indeed, He is Forgiving, ever Appreciative.
verses.31: And what We have revealed to you of the Book is the truth, confirming what came before it; indeed, Allah is fully Aware and Watchful of His servants.
verses.32: Then We gave the Book as inheritance to those We chose among Our servants; some of them wrong themselves, some are moderate, and some race ahead in good deeds by Allah's permission; that is truly the great favor —
verses.33: Gardens of eternal residence, which they will enter, adorned there with bracelets of gold and pearls, their garments made of silk.
verses.34: And they will say, praise be to Allah, who has removed all sorrow from us; indeed, our Lord is Forgiving, ever Appreciative —
verses.35: Who has settled us in this lasting home by His grace, where no exhaustion touches us, and no weariness at all.
verses.36: But for those who disbelieve is the fire of Hell; death is never decreed for them so they might escape it, nor is its torment ever lightened; this is how We repay every ungrateful soul.
verses.37: And they will cry out there, our Lord, remove us from here, and we will do good, unlike what we used to do! But had We not given you a lifespan long enough for anyone who wished to remember to remember, and had a warner not already come to you? So taste it now, for the wrongdoers have no helper at all.
verses.38: Indeed, Allah is Knower of the unseen realities of the heavens and the earth; indeed, He knows fully what lies within every chest.
verses.39: It is He who made you successors upon the earth; whoever disbelieves bears the consequence of his own disbelief; and the disbelief of the disbelievers only increases them in their Lord's contempt, and only increases them in loss.
verses.40: Say: consider your partners you call upon besides Allah; show me what they have created of the earth, or whether they share in the heavens; or have We given them a book from which they draw evidence? Rather, the wrongdoers promise one another nothing but delusion.
verses.41: Indeed, Allah holds the heavens and the earth firmly in place, so that they do not collapse; and were they to collapse, no one could hold them after Him; indeed, He is Forbearing, Forgiving.
verses.42: And they swore their strongest oaths by Allah that if a warner came to them, they would surely be better guided than any other nation; yet when a warner did come to them, it only increased them in aversion —
verses.43: Through arrogance in the land, and scheming of evil; yet evil scheming closes in only upon those who devised it. So are they waiting for anything other than the fate of earlier peoples? Yet you will never find any change in Allah's way, and you will never find any alteration in it.
verses.44: Have they not traveled through the land and seen how those before them ended up, though they were far greater in strength? Yet nothing in the heavens or the earth could ever escape Allah; indeed, He is ever Knowing, Capable.
verses.45: And if Allah were to hold people fully accountable for everything they earned, He would not leave a single living creature upon the earth; but He grants them respite for an appointed term; and when their time finally comes, then indeed, Allah is ever Watchful over His servants — the surah closing on this quiet balance between patience and eventual, certain accountability.

verses.1: Ya, Sin —
verses.2: By the Quran, full of wisdom —
verses.3: Indeed, you are truly among the messengers —
verses.4: Standing firmly on a straight path.
verses.5: This is a revelation from the Exalted in Might, the Merciful —
verses.6: So that you might warn a people whose forefathers were never warned, leaving them entirely unaware.
verses.7: The sentence has already fallen upon most of them, for they simply do not believe.
verses.8: Indeed, We have placed shackles around their necks, reaching to their chins, so their heads are forced upward —
verses.9: And We have placed a barrier before them, and a barrier behind them, and covered them entirely, so they cannot see.
verses.10: And it makes no difference to them whether you warn them or not — they simply will not believe.
verses.11: You can only truly reach the one who follows the reminder, and holds awe of the Most Merciful unseen; give him good news of forgiveness, and a generous reward.
verses.12: Indeed, it is We who bring the dead to life, recording what they sent ahead and what they left behind, with everything counted in a clear record.
verses.13: And give them the example of the people of a certain town, when messengers came to it —
verses.14: When We sent two messengers, and they were rejected, so We strengthened them with a third; and they said, indeed, we are messengers sent to you.
verses.15: The people replied, you are nothing but ordinary men like us, and the Most Merciful has revealed nothing at all; you are simply lying.
verses.16: The messengers said, our Lord knows we are truly sent to you —
verses.17: And our only duty is clear, plain delivery of the message.
verses.18: They said, indeed, we consider you a bad omen; if you do not stop, we will surely stone you, and a painful punishment from us will surely touch you.
verses.19: The messengers answered, your bad omen rests with yourselves; is it simply because you were reminded? Rather, you are truly a transgressing people.
verses.20: And a man came running from the far edge of the city, saying, my people, follow the messengers —
verses.21: Follow those who ask you for no payment at all, and who are truly guided.
verses.22: And why should I not worship the One who created me, and to whom you will all return?
verses.23: Should I take gods besides Him, whose intercession could not help me at all if the Most Merciful intended harm for me, nor could they save me?
verses.24: Indeed, I would then be in clear, unmistakable error.
verses.25: Indeed, I have believed in your Lord, so listen well to me.
verses.26: It was said to him, enter Paradise; he said, if only my people knew —
verses.27: How my Lord has forgiven me, and made me among the honored — a wish expressed even in the very moment of his own reward.
verses.28: And We sent down upon his people, after him, no army from the sky, nor did We need to.
verses.29: It was only a single blast, and instantly, they were extinguished entirely.
verses.30: How deep the regret for these servants; never did a messenger come to them without being mocked.
verses.31: Have they not considered how many generations We destroyed before them, none of whom would ever return to them again?
verses.32: And indeed, every single one of them will be brought together and presented before Us.
verses.33: And a sign for them is the dead earth; We give it life, and bring forth from it grain, from which they eat.
verses.34: And We placed within it gardens of palm trees and grapevines, and caused springs to burst forth from it —
verses.35: So that they might eat of its fruit, fruit their own hands never truly produced; will they not, then, be grateful?
verses.36: Exalted is He who created all things in pairs — from what the earth grows, from themselves, and from things they do not even know.
verses.37: And a sign for them is the night; We strip the daylight from it, and suddenly they are left in darkness.
verses.38: And the sun runs steadily toward its resting place; this is the careful design of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
verses.39: And for the moon, We have set precise phases, until it returns looking like an old, curved palm stalk.
verses.40: It is not possible for the sun to overtake the moon, nor for the night to outrun the day; each one glides gently in its own orbit.
verses.41: And a sign for them is that We carried their ancestors aboard a fully laden ship —
verses.42: And We created for them similar vessels that they ride today.
verses.43: And had We willed, We could have drowned them, leaving no one to answer their cry, and no way for them to be saved —
verses.44: Except through mercy from Us, and provision for a little while longer.
verses.45: But when it is said to them, be mindful of what is ahead of you and what is behind you, so that you might receive mercy — they turn away regardless.
verses.46: And no sign from among their Lord's signs ever reaches them without them turning away from it.
verses.47: And when it is said to them, spend from what Allah has provided you, the disbelievers say to the believers, should we feed someone Allah Himself could have fed, if He willed? You are simply in clear error.
verses.48: And they ask, when will this promise arrive, if you are truthful?
verses.49: They are waiting for nothing but a single blast, which will seize them right in the middle of their arguing.
verses.50: They will have no time even to leave instructions, nor will they be able to return to their families.
verses.51: And the Horn will be blown, and at once, from their graves, they will hasten toward their Lord.
verses.52: They will cry, woe to us — who has raised us up from our resting place? It will be answered, this is exactly what the Most Merciful promised, and the messengers spoke the truth.
verses.53: It will be only a single blast, and at once, every one of them will be brought forth and presented.
verses.54: So today, no soul will be wronged in the slightest, and you will be repaid only for exactly what you used to do.
verses.55: Indeed, the companions of Paradise, on that Day, will be joyfully occupied —
verses.56: They and their spouses, resting in shade, reclining upon adorned couches.
verses.57: There they will have fruit, and whatever else they ask for —
verses.58: And peace — a word spoken directly from a Merciful Lord.
verses.59: But it will be said, stand apart today, you who were guilty —
verses.60: Did I not command you, children of Adam, not to worship Satan, for indeed, he is a clear enemy to you —
verses.61: And that you worship Me alone? This is a straight path.
verses.62: And he had already led astray a great many of you — did you not use your reason?
verses.63: This is the very Hellfire you were once promised.
verses.64: Burn in it today, for everything you used to deny.
verses.65: That Day, We will seal their mouths shut, while their hands speak to Us, and their feet testify to everything they used to earn.
verses.66: And had We willed, We could have completely blinded their eyes, and they would race desperately to find the way — but how could they possibly see?
verses.67: And had We willed, We could have transformed them right where they stood, unable to move forward, and unable to turn back.
verses.68: And whoever We grant a long life, We gradually reverse in strength; will they not, then, understand?
verses.69: And We did not teach him poetry, nor is it fitting for him; it is nothing but a reminder, and a clear Quran —
verses.70: To warn whoever is truly alive, and to establish the case against the disbelievers.
verses.71: Do they not see that We created for them, by Our own design, grazing livestock, which they now own?
verses.72: And We made these animals gentle for them, so that some they ride, and some they eat —
verses.73: And they gain other benefits and drink from them too; will they not, then, be grateful?
verses.74: Yet they have taken gods besides Allah, hoping perhaps they might be helped.
verses.75: These gods cannot help them at all — rather, they themselves stand ready as troops in their defense, utterly powerless.
verses.76: So do not let their words grieve you; indeed, We know fully what they conceal, and what they openly declare.
verses.77: Does man not consider that We created him from a mere drop, and yet he becomes an open, argumentative adversary?
verses.78: And he presents Us an example, forgetting his own origin, asking, who can bring life to bones once they have completely decayed?
verses.79: Say: He who created them the first time will bring them to life again; and He has full knowledge of every act of creation.
verses.80: It is He who made fire for you from the green tree, from which you now kindle flame.
verses.81: Is the One who created the heavens and the earth not able to create the same again? Yes indeed — He is the Knowing Creator.
verses.82: His command, whenever He intends a thing, is simply to say to it, be — and it is.
verses.83: So exalted is He, in whose hand rests the dominion of all things, and to Him you will all return — the surah closing on this quiet, sweeping certainty.

verses.1: By those angels lined up in ranks —
verses.2: And those who drive forward with real force —
verses.3: And those who recite the message —
verses.4: Indeed, your God is truly One —
verses.5: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, and Lord of every sunrise.
verses.6: Indeed, We have adorned the nearest sky with the beauty of stars —
verses.7: And as a guard against every rebellious devil —
verses.8: So they cannot listen in on the exalted assembly above, pelted away from every direction —
verses.9: Driven off completely; and for them is a lasting punishment —
verses.10: Except the one who manages to snatch a single word, and is chased down by a piercing flame.
verses.11: So ask them: are they a tougher creation, or everything else We have made? Indeed, We created man from sticky clay.
verses.12: Rather, you marvel at this truth, while they only mock it —
verses.13: And when reminded, they simply refuse to remember.
verses.14: And when they see a sign, they turn it into ridicule —
verses.15: Saying, this is nothing but plain magic.
verses.16: When we have died and become dust and bones, will we truly be raised again —
verses.17: And our forefathers too?
verses.18: Say: yes — and you will be utterly humbled in the process.
verses.19: It will be only a single shout, and at once, they will find themselves watching it unfold.
verses.20: They will cry, woe to us — this is the Day of Recompense.
verses.21: This is the very Day of Judgment you used to deny.
verses.22: Gather up the wrongdoers, their companions, and everything they used to worship —
verses.23: Besides Allah, and guide them straight toward the path of Hellfire —
verses.24: And stop them there, for they must be questioned.
verses.25: What is wrong with you now — why are you not helping one another?
verses.26: But on that Day, they are in total, helpless surrender.
verses.27: And they will turn toward one another, trading blame back and forth.
verses.28: They will say, indeed, you used to come at us from the right, pressuring us.
verses.29: The others will answer, rather, you yourselves were simply not believers —
verses.30: And we held no real power over you at all; you were already a transgressing people.
verses.31: So the sentence of our Lord has justly fallen upon us; indeed, we must now taste it.
verses.32: And yes, we led you astray, for we ourselves were already astray.
verses.33: So on that Day, they will all share fully in the punishment together.
verses.34: Indeed, this is exactly how We deal with the guilty.
verses.35: For when it was once said to them, there is no god but Allah, they grew arrogant —
verses.36: And said, should we abandon our gods for a mad poet?
verses.37: Rather, he came with the very truth, confirming every messenger before him.
verses.38: Indeed, you disbelievers will surely taste the painful punishment —
verses.39: And you will be repaid for nothing but exactly what you used to do —
verses.40: Except the chosen, sincere servants of Allah.
verses.41: For them is a provision already determined —
verses.42: Fruit of every kind, and they will be truly honored —
verses.43: In the Gardens of Pleasure —
verses.44: Seated upon thrones, facing one another.
verses.45: A cup will be passed among them from a flowing spring —
verses.46: Pure white, and delightful to those who drink it —
verses.47: Causing no harm at all, and never leading them to intoxication.
verses.48: And with them will be companions who lower their gaze, with beautiful, wide eyes —
verses.49: As delicate and protected as hidden pearls.
verses.50: And they will turn to one another, asking questions freely.
verses.51: One of them will say, indeed, I once had a companion on earth —
verses.52: Who used to ask me, are you truly among those who believe —
verses.53: That once we have died and become dust and bones, we will really be repaid?
verses.54: He will ask, would you like to look and see for yourself?
verses.55: And he will look, and see his old companion right in the middle of the Hellfire.
verses.56: He will say, by Allah, you nearly ruined me too —
verses.57: Had it not been for my Lord's favor, I would have been dragged in right along with you.
verses.58: So — are we truly not going to die again —
verses.59: Beyond our very first death, and never to be punished after all?
verses.60: Indeed, this is truly the greatest attainment.
verses.61: For a reward like this, let every worker truly strive.
verses.62: Is this a better welcome, or the tree of Zaqqum?
verses.63: Indeed, We have made it a source of torment for the wrongdoers.
verses.64: Indeed, it is a tree rising directly from the depths of Hellfire —
verses.65: Its fruit sprouting like the heads of devils.
verses.66: And indeed, they will eat from it, filling their stomachs entirely —
verses.67: Then, on top of it, they will have a mixture of scalding water —
verses.68: And then their return will lead straight back into the Hellfire.
verses.69: Indeed, they found their fathers astray —
verses.70: So they rushed to follow right in their footsteps.
verses.71: And truly, most of the earlier peoples had already strayed before them —
verses.72: And We had already sent warners among them.
verses.73: So see how those who were warned finally ended up —
verses.74: Except the chosen, sincere servants of Allah.
verses.75: And indeed, Noah called upon Us, and We are the best of all who answer.
verses.76: And We saved him and his family from that overwhelming affliction —
verses.77: And made his descendants the ones who would remain —
verses.78: And left his name honored among every generation to follow:
verses.79: Peace be upon Noah, among all the worlds.
verses.80: Indeed, this is exactly how We reward those who do good.
verses.81: Indeed, he was truly among Our believing servants.
verses.82: Then We drowned every one of the others.
verses.83: And indeed, among his kind was Abraham too —
verses.84: When he came before his Lord with a sincere, untroubled heart —
verses.85: And said to his father and his people, what is it you worship —
verses.86: Is it falsehood you desire, gods other than Allah?
verses.87: Then what exactly do you think of the Lord of all the worlds?
verses.88: And he glanced up at the stars —
verses.89: And said, indeed, I am feeling unwell.
verses.90: So they turned away from him and left.
verses.91: Then he turned to their idols and said, will you not eat?
verses.92: What is wrong with you, that you cannot even speak?
verses.93: And he struck them decisively with his right hand.
verses.94: Then the people came rushing toward him.
verses.95: He said, do you worship what you yourselves have carved —
verses.96: While Allah created both you and everything you make?
verses.97: They said, build a great structure for him, and throw him into the blazing fire.
verses.98: And they schemed a plan against him, but We made them the true losers instead.
verses.99: And he said, indeed, I am going toward my Lord; He will guide me —
verses.100: My Lord, grant me a child among the righteous.
verses.101: So We gave him good news of a gentle, forbearing son.
verses.102: And when the boy grew old enough to walk alongside him, Abraham said, my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I must sacrifice you — what do you think? He answered, my father, do as you are commanded; you will find me, Allah willing, among the steadfast.
verses.103: And when they had both submitted, and he laid him down upon his forehead —
verses.104: We called out to him, Abraham —
verses.105: You have already fulfilled the vision; this is exactly how We reward those who do good.
verses.106: Indeed, this had truly been the clearest of trials.
verses.107: And We ransomed his son with a great sacrifice —
verses.108: And left his name honored among every generation to follow:
verses.110: Indeed, this is exactly how We reward those who do good.
verses.111: Indeed, he was truly among Our believing servants.
verses.112: And We gave him good news of Isaac too, a prophet among the righteous.
verses.113: And We blessed him and Isaac; yet among their descendants are both those who do good, and those who wrong themselves openly.
verses.114: And indeed, We conferred great favor upon Moses and Aaron —
verses.115: And saved them and their people from that great affliction —
verses.116: And gave them support, so that it was they who prevailed —
verses.117: And gave them the clear, explicit Scripture —
verses.118: And guided them along the straight path —
verses.119: And left their names honored among every generation to follow:
verses.120: Peace be upon Moses and Aaron.
verses.121: Indeed, this is exactly how We reward those who do good.
verses.122: Indeed, they were truly among Our believing servants.
verses.123: And indeed, Elias was truly among the messengers —
verses.124: When he said to his people, will you not hold awe of Allah —
verses.125: Do you call upon Ba'l, abandoning the best of all creators —
verses.126: Allah, your Lord, and the Lord of your very first ancestors?
verses.127: But they denied him, so indeed, they too will be brought forth for punishment —
verses.128: Except the chosen, sincere servants of Allah.
verses.129: And We left his name honored among every generation to follow:
verses.131: Indeed, this is exactly how We reward those who do good.
verses.132: Indeed, he was truly among Our believing servants.
verses.133: And indeed, Lot too was among the messengers.
verses.134: Recall when We saved him and his entire family together —
verses.135: Except an old woman among those who were left behind —
verses.136: Then We destroyed every one of the others.
verses.137: And indeed, you pass right by their ruins every morning —
verses.138: And every night too; will you still not use your reason?
verses.139: And indeed, Jonah too was among the messengers.
verses.140: Recall when he fled to a heavily laden ship —
verses.141: And he drew lots, and found himself among those chosen to be thrown overboard.
verses.142: Then the great fish swallowed him, while he knew he was truly at fault.
verses.143: And had he not been among those who constantly glorified Allah —
verses.144: He would have remained inside its belly until the very Day of Resurrection.
verses.145: But We cast him onto the bare shore, weak and unwell —
verses.146: And caused a gourd vine to grow up gently over him.
verses.147: And We sent him back to a hundred thousand people, or even more —
verses.148: And they believed, so We let them enjoy life for a while.
verses.149: So ask them: does your Lord have daughters, while they claim sons for themselves —
verses.150: Or did We create the angels as females right before their very eyes?
verses.151: Truly, it is out of their own fabricated falsehood that they say —
verses.152: Allah has fathered a child — and indeed, they are simply liars.
verses.153: Has He somehow chosen daughters over sons?
verses.154: What is wrong with you? How do you reach such a judgment?
verses.155: Will you not, then, take real heed?
verses.156: Or do you actually have some clear authority for this?
verses.157: Then bring forth your scripture, if you are truly telling the truth.
verses.158: And they have even invented a lineage between Him and the jinn — yet the jinn themselves already know they will be brought forth for judgment.
verses.159: Exalted is Allah, far above everything they describe —
verses.160: Except the chosen, sincere servants of Allah, who make no such claims at all.
verses.161: So indeed, you disbelievers, and everything you worship —
verses.162: You cannot tempt anyone away from Him —
verses.163: Except the one already destined to burn in the Hellfire.
verses.164: And the angels themselves say, there is not one among us without an appointed, known position —
verses.165: And indeed, we are the ones who stand lined up in worship —
verses.166: And indeed, we are the ones who glorify Allah endlessly.
verses.167: And indeed, the disbelievers used to say —
verses.168: Had we only had a reminder like those given to earlier peoples —
verses.169: We would surely have been Allah's own sincere, chosen servants.
verses.170: But now that it has come, they reject it — so they are soon going to know.
verses.171: And Our word has already been settled concerning Our servants, the messengers —
verses.172: That indeed, they would be the ones granted victory —
verses.173: And that indeed, it is Our own forces who would ultimately prevail.
verses.174: So turn away from them for a little while —
verses.175: And watch, for indeed, they too are watching, waiting to see what comes.
verses.176: Do they truly wish to hasten Our punishment upon themselves?
verses.177: But when it finally descends upon their own land, how terrible that morning will be for those who were warned.
verses.178: So turn away from them for a little while —
verses.179: And watch, for indeed, they too are watching.
verses.180: Exalted is your Lord, Lord of true might, far above everything they describe.
verses.181: And peace be upon all the messengers.
verses.182: And all praise belongs to Allah, Lord of every world — the surah closing on this same note of praise that carried through every story told within it.

verses.1: Sad. By the Quran, full of reminder —
verses.2: Yet the disbelievers remain caught up in pride and open opposition.
verses.3: How many generations did We destroy before them, crying out only when it was already too late to escape.
verses.4: And they are astonished that a warner has come from among their very own; the disbelievers say, this is a magician, a liar —
verses.5: Has he reduced all the gods to just One? This is truly a strange thing indeed.
verses.6: And their leaders set out, saying, keep going, and stay firm in defense of your gods; this is clearly something being deliberately schemed —
verses.7: We have never heard of this in the religion we know; this is nothing but an invention.
verses.8: Was the message sent down to him alone, out of all of us? Rather, they are simply in doubt about My message — they have not yet truly tasted My punishment.
verses.9: Or do they hold the treasures of your Lord's mercy, the Exalted in Might, the Bestower?
verses.10: Or does the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, belong to them? Then let them climb up by whatever means they can.
verses.11: They are nothing but a small army, destined to be defeated among the other companies of disbelievers.
verses.12: Before them, the people of Noah denied, as did 'Aad, and Pharaoh, owner of the stakes —
verses.13: And Thamud, and the people of Lot, and the companions of the thicket; these were the great opposing factions.
verses.14: Every one of them denied the messengers, so My penalty against them was fully justified.
verses.15: And these people are awaiting nothing but a single blast — one that will come without any delay at all.
verses.16: And they say, our Lord, hasten our share of the punishment even before the Day of Account — mockery disguised as impatience.
verses.17: Be patient with what they say, and remember Our servant David, possessor of real strength; indeed, he constantly turned back to Allah.
verses.18: Indeed, We made the mountains join him in glorification, both evening and morning —
verses.19: And the birds too were gathered, all echoing his praise alongside him.
verses.20: And We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom, and clarity in judgment.
verses.21: And has the story reached you of the two disputants, who climbed over the wall of his private prayer chamber —
verses.22: When they entered upon David, startling him? They said, do not be afraid; we are two disputants, one of whom has wronged the other, so judge between us fairly, without bias, and guide us to the right path.
verses.23: This man here, my brother, has ninety-nine ewes, while I have only one; yet he said, entrust her to me, and he overpowered me in argument.
verses.24: David said, he has truly wronged you by demanding your one ewe on top of his ninety-nine; indeed, many partners wrong one another, except those who believe and do righteous deeds — and how few they truly are. And David realized this had been a test for him, so he sought his Lord's forgiveness, fell down in prostration, and turned back in repentance.
verses.25: So We forgave him for that; and indeed, for him is nearness to Us, and a beautiful place of return.
verses.26: David, indeed We have made you a successor upon the earth, so judge fairly between people, and do not follow personal desire, for it will lead you astray from Allah's path; indeed, those who stray from His path face a severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Account.
verses.27: And We did not create the heaven and the earth, and everything between them, without purpose; that is merely the assumption of the disbelievers — so woe to them from the Fire.
verses.28: Should We treat those who believe and do righteous deeds the same as those who spread corruption on earth? Should the righteous be treated like the wicked?
verses.29: This is a blessed Book We have revealed to you, so that people might reflect upon its verses, and so that those of true understanding might take heed.
verses.30: And to David We gave Solomon — an excellent servant, who also constantly turned back to Allah.
verses.31: Recall when magnificent, poised horses were presented before him one afternoon —
verses.32: And he said, indeed, I let my love for these good things distract me from remembering my Lord, until the sun disappeared from view —
verses.33: Bring them back to me, he said, and began gently stroking their legs and necks, correcting his priorities immediately.
verses.34: And indeed, We tested Solomon, placing a mere body upon his throne; then he turned back to Us in devotion.
verses.35: He said, my Lord, forgive me, and grant me a kingdom unlike any granted to anyone after me; indeed, You are the Bestower.
verses.36: So We made the wind subject to him, blowing gently by his command, wherever he directed it —
verses.37: And the devils too, every skilled builder and diver —
verses.38: And others bound firmly together in chains.
verses.39: This is Our gift, We told him, so give freely, or withhold, without any account required of you.
verses.40: And indeed, for him too is nearness to Us, and a beautiful place of return.
verses.41: And remember Our servant Job, when he cried out to his Lord, indeed, Satan has afflicted me with hardship and suffering —
verses.42: Strike the ground with your foot; here is a cool spring for bathing and drinking, We told him.
verses.43: And We restored his family to him, and doubled it as well, as a mercy from Us, and a reminder for people of true understanding.
verses.44: And take in your hand a small bundle, and strike with it, and do not break your oath; indeed, We found him truly patient, an excellent servant, one who constantly turned back to his Lord.
verses.45: And remember Our servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — men of real strength and clear insight.
verses.46: Indeed, We chose them for a special quality: constant remembrance of the true home, the Hereafter.
verses.47: And indeed, in Our sight, they are among the truly chosen, the outstanding.
verses.48: And remember Ishmael, Elisha, and Dhul-Kifl too — every one of them among the outstanding.
verses.49: This is a reminder; and indeed, for the righteous is a beautiful place of return —
verses.50: Gardens of eternal residence, their gates already opened wide to welcome them —
verses.51: Reclining there, calling freely for abundant fruit and drink.
verses.52: And with them will be companions who lower their gaze, all of matching age.
verses.53: This is exactly what you are promised, for the Day of Account.
verses.54: Indeed, this is Our provision, one that never runs out or diminishes.
verses.55: That is so; but for the transgressors is a truly wretched place of return —
verses.56: Hell, where they will burn — how miserable a resting place.
verses.57: This — let them taste it — is scalding water, and foul discharge.
verses.58: And other punishments of similar kind, in various forms.
verses.59: This is a crowd being thrust in alongside you — no welcome for them; indeed, they too will burn in the Fire.
verses.60: They will reply, rather, it is you — no welcome for you either; you brought this ruin upon us, and what a wretched place to settle.
verses.61: They will say, our Lord, whoever brought this upon us — double his punishment in the Fire.
verses.62: And they will ask, why do we not see men we once counted among the worst —
verses.63: The very ones we mocked so thoroughly — or has our own sight simply missed them somehow?
verses.64: Indeed, this is the truth — the endless bickering of the people of the Fire.
verses.65: Say: I am only a warner; there is no god except Allah, the One, the Prevailing —
verses.66: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.
verses.67: Say: this is truly great news —
verses.68: One that you turn away from entirely.
verses.69: I had no knowledge of the exalted assembly of angels when they were disputing over the creation of Adam.
verses.70: It has only been revealed to me that I am nothing more than a clear warner.
verses.71: Recall when your Lord said to the angels, indeed, I am about to create a human being from clay —
verses.72: So once I have shaped him fully, and breathed into him something of My spirit, fall down in prostration before him.
verses.73: So the angels prostrated, every single one of them —
verses.74: Except Iblees, who grew arrogant, and became one of the disbelievers.
verses.75: Allah said, Iblees, what kept you from prostrating to what I created with My own hands? Were you being arrogant, or were you already among the haughty?
verses.76: He answered, I am better than him; You created me from fire, and created him from clay.
verses.77: Allah said, then get out of here, for indeed, you are truly expelled —
verses.78: And indeed, My curse rests upon you until the Day of Judgment.
verses.79: He said, my Lord, then grant me respite until the Day mankind is resurrected.
verses.80: Allah said, indeed, you are granted that respite —
verses.81: Until the well-known Day.
verses.82: Iblees said, by Your might, I will surely mislead every one of them —
verses.83: Except for Your chosen, sincere servants among them.
verses.84: Allah said, this is the truth, and I speak only the truth —
verses.85: I will surely fill Hell with you, and with every one of them who follows you, all together — the surah closing with this ancient dispute finally settled, arrogance answered decisively by truth.
verses.86: Say: I ask you no payment for this at all, and I am not one who pretends —
verses.87: It is nothing but a reminder to all the worlds.
verses.88: And you will surely come to know the truth of it, after a while.

verses.1: The revelation of this Book comes from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.2: Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book in truth, so worship Allah alone, sincere to Him in devotion.
verses.3: Truly, sincere devotion belongs to Allah alone; and those who take other protectors say, we worship them only so they might bring us nearer to Allah — yet Allah will judge between them over exactly what they disagreed about; indeed, Allah does not guide a liar, a persistent disbeliever.
verses.4: Had Allah intended to take a son, He could have chosen from His own creation whatever He willed; exalted is He far above that — He is Allah, the One, the Prevailing.
verses.5: He created the heavens and the earth in truth; He wraps the night over the day, and wraps the day over the night, and has made the sun and moon subject to His command, each running its course for an appointed term; indeed, He is the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.
verses.6: He created you from a single soul, then made from it its mate, and sent down for you eight kinds of grazing livestock in pairs; He creates you in your mothers' wombs, one stage after another, within three layers of darkness. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs all dominion; there is no god but Him — so how are you turned away?
verses.7: If you disbelieve, indeed Allah has no need of you at all, though He does not approve disbelief for His servants; and if you are grateful, He approves that for you. No soul bears another's burden; then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of everything you did; indeed, He knows fully what lies within every chest.
verses.8: And when hardship touches man, he calls upon his Lord, turning to Him sincerely; yet once He grants him a favor, he forgets the very One he had called upon, and sets up equals to Allah, leading others astray. Say: enjoy your disbelief a little while; indeed, you are among the companions of the Fire.
verses.9: Is one who spends the night devoted in prayer, prostrating and standing, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for his Lord's mercy, the same as one who does not? Say: are those who know equal to those who do not know? Only people of true understanding truly take heed.
verses.10: Say: My servants who have believed, hold awe of your Lord; for those who do good in this world is good, and Allah's earth is vast; indeed, the patient will be given their reward without limit.
verses.11: Say: I have been commanded to worship Allah alone, sincere to Him in devotion —
verses.12: And I have been commanded to be the very first among those who submit.
verses.13: Say: indeed, I fear, should I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.
verses.14: Say: Allah alone do I worship, sincere to Him in my devotion —
verses.15: So worship whatever you wish besides Him. Say: indeed, the true losers are those who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection; that, truly, is the clearest loss of all.
verses.16: They will have layers of fire above them, and layers below them; through this, Allah warns His servants — My servants, hold awe of Me.
verses.17: But those who avoided false idols, refusing to worship them, and turned back sincerely to Allah — for them is good news; so give good news to My servants —
verses.18: Those who listen closely to what is said, and follow the very best of it; those are the ones Allah has truly guided, and those are the people of real understanding.
verses.19: So is the one already deserving the sentence of punishment to be saved? Can you truly rescue someone already in the Fire?
verses.20: But those who held awe of their Lord will have elevated chambers, built one above another, with rivers flowing beneath them — the promise of Allah, and Allah never fails His promise.
verses.21: Do you not see how Allah sends down rain from the sky, and lets it flow as springs through the earth, then brings forth crops of varied colors, which then wither and turn yellow, before He reduces them to scattered debris? Indeed, in that is a reminder for people of true understanding.
verses.22: So is the one whose heart Allah has opened to Islam, walking in light from his Lord, the same as one whose heart remains sealed? Woe to those whose hearts have hardened against remembering Allah — they are in clear error.
verses.23: Allah has sent down the best of speech — a consistent Book, its themes repeated for emphasis, at which the skin of those who fear their Lord shivers; then their skin and their hearts soften at the remembrance of Allah. That is Allah's guidance, given to whom He wills; and whoever Allah leaves astray has no guide left at all.
verses.24: So is the one who must shield his own face from the worst of the punishment on Resurrection Day the same as one who is safe? It will be said to the wrongdoers, taste what you used to earn.
verses.25: Those before them denied too, and punishment reached them from directions they never anticipated.
verses.26: So Allah made them taste disgrace in this worldly life; yet the punishment of the Hereafter is far greater, if only they knew.
verses.27: And We have given people every kind of example in this Quran, so that they might take heed —
verses.28: An Arabic Quran, entirely free of any distortion, so that they might become truly righteous.
verses.29: Allah gives an example: a slave owned by several quarreling partners, compared to a slave belonging entirely to one man — are these truly equal? Praise be to Allah; but most people simply do not understand.
verses.30: Indeed, you will die, and indeed, they too will die.
verses.31: Then indeed, on the Day of Resurrection, you will all dispute before your Lord.
verses.32: So who is more unjust than one who lies about Allah, and denies the truth once it has reached him? Is there not a home in Hell reserved for the disbelievers?
verses.33: And the one who brought the truth, and those who believed in it — they are the truly righteous.
verses.34: They will have whatever they wish for with their Lord; that is the reward of those who do good —
verses.35: So that Allah may erase the worst of what they did, and reward them fully for the best of what they used to do.
verses.36: Is Allah not sufficient for His servant? Yet they still try to frighten you with those they worship besides Him; and whoever Allah leaves astray has no guide at all.
verses.37: And whoever Allah guides has no one able to mislead him; is Allah not Exalted in Might, and Owner of true Retribution?
verses.38: And if you asked them, who created the heavens and the earth? They would surely say, Allah. Say: then consider what you call upon besides Allah — if Allah intended harm for me, could they remove it? Or if He intended mercy, could they withhold it? Say: Allah is enough for me; upon Him alone do the truly wise rely.
verses.39: Say: my people, act according to your own way, for indeed, I am acting too; and you will soon come to know —
verses.40: Who will be met with disgracing torment, and who will face an enduring punishment.
verses.41: Indeed, We sent down this Book to you for humanity's sake, in truth; so whoever accepts guidance benefits only himself, and whoever strays only harms himself; you are not their keeper.
verses.42: Allah takes souls at the moment of their death, and those not yet dying, during their sleep; He keeps hold of those for whom death has been decreed, and releases the rest for an appointed term. Indeed, in that are signs for people who reflect deeply.
verses.43: Or have they taken intercessors besides Allah? Say: even though these intercessors hold no power at all, and possess no reason?
verses.44: Say: intercession belongs entirely to Allah; to Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; then to Him you will all return.
verses.45: And when Allah alone is mentioned, the hearts of those who reject the Hereafter shrink with distaste; but when others besides Him are mentioned, they light up with delight.
verses.46: Say: Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, You alone will judge between Your servants concerning everything they used to dispute over.
verses.47: And had the wrongdoers possessed everything on earth, and twice as much besides, they would surely offer it all as ransom from the worst of the punishment on Resurrection Day; yet something from Allah will appear before them that they never accounted for.
verses.48: And the evil of everything they earned will become plain to them, and what they used to mock will surround them completely.
verses.49: And when hardship touches man, he calls upon Us; but once We grant him a favor from Us, he says, I was only given this because of my own knowledge. Rather, it is simply a test, though most people do not realize it.
verses.50: Those before them said the very same thing, yet what they earned was of no use to them at all.
verses.51: So the evil of what they earned struck them; and the wrongdoers among these people too will be struck by the evil of what they earn, and they will never escape it.
verses.52: Do they not know that Allah extends provision to whom He wills, and restricts it too? Indeed, in that are signs for a people who truly believe.
verses.53: Say: My servants who have wronged themselves, do not despair of Allah's mercy; indeed, Allah forgives all sins; truly, He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
verses.54: And turn back to your Lord, and submit to Him, before the punishment reaches you — for then you will find no help at all.
verses.55: And follow the best of what has been revealed to you from your Lord, before punishment comes upon you suddenly, while you are unaware —
verses.56: Lest a soul say, how great is my regret over what I neglected concerning Allah, for I was truly among those who mocked —
verses.57: Or say, had Allah only guided me, I would surely have been among the righteous —
verses.58: Or say, upon seeing the punishment, if only I had another chance, so I could be among those who do good.
verses.59: But no — My verses had already come to you, yet you denied them, grew arrogant, and were among the disbelievers.
verses.60: And on the Day of Resurrection, you will see those who lied about Allah with their faces darkened; is there not a home in Hell reserved for the arrogant?
verses.61: And Allah will save those who held awe of Him, delivering them to their success; no harm will touch them, nor will they grieve.
verses.62: Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is the Disposer of affairs over all things.
verses.63: To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth; and those who reject the verses of Allah — they are the true losers.
verses.64: Say: is it, then, someone other than Allah you order me to worship, you who lack understanding?
verses.65: It has already been revealed to you, and to those before you, that if you associate anything with Allah, your deeds would surely become worthless, and you would surely be among the losers.
verses.66: Rather, worship Allah alone, and be among the grateful.
verses.67: And they have not valued Allah with the true value He deserves; the entire earth will be within His grip on Resurrection Day, and the heavens folded within His right hand; exalted and far above is He from whatever they associate with Him.
verses.68: And the Horn will be blown, and everyone in the heavens and on earth will fall lifeless, except whom Allah wills; then it will be blown again, and at once, they will rise, looking on.
verses.69: And the earth will shine with the light of its Lord, and the record of deeds will be set in place, and the prophets and witnesses will be brought forth, and judgment will be carried out between them in truth, without the slightest injustice.
verses.70: And every soul will be repaid in full for what it did; and He knows best everything they do.
verses.71: And the disbelievers will be driven to Hell in groups; when they reach it, its gates will open, and its keepers will ask, did messengers from among your own not come to you, reciting your Lord's verses and warning you of this very Day? They will answer, yes, but the sentence of punishment has rightly fallen upon the disbelievers.
verses.72: It will be said, enter the gates of Hell, to remain there forever; how wretched is the home of the arrogant.
verses.73: But those who held awe of their Lord will be led to Paradise in groups; when they arrive, its gates already open, its keepers will say, peace be upon you; you have become pure, so enter to remain forever.
verses.74: And they will say, praise be to Allah, who has fulfilled His promise to us, and given us this land to inherit, settling wherever we wish in Paradise; how excellent is the reward of those who labored faithfully.
verses.75: And you will see the angels surrounding the Throne, glorifying their Lord with praise; and judgment will be carried out between all in truth, and it will be said, praise be to Allah, Lord of all the worlds — the surah closing on this final, unified declaration of gratitude.

verses.1: Ha, Meem —
verses.2: The revelation of this Book comes from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Knowing —
verses.3: The Forgiver of sin, the Acceptor of repentance, severe in punishment, the Owner of abundance; there is no god but Him, and to Him is the final return.
verses.4: No one disputes the signs of Allah except those who disbelieve, so do not be misled by their freedom of movement throughout the land.
verses.5: The people of Noah denied before them, as did later factions after them; every nation schemed against its messenger to seize him, arguing with falsehood to try to defeat the truth — so I seized them, and how severe was My punishment.
verses.6: And so the word of your Lord has come true against the disbelievers, that they are companions of the Fire.
