verses.7: Those angels who carry the Throne, and those surrounding it, glorify their Lord with praise, believe in Him, and seek forgiveness for the believers: our Lord, You encompass all things in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who repent and follow Your way, and protect them from the punishment of Hellfire —
verses.8: Our Lord, admit them into the everlasting gardens You have promised them, along with the righteous among their parents, spouses, and children; indeed, You are the Exalted in Might, the Wise —
verses.9: And protect them from every evil consequence; whoever You protect that Day, You have shown him true mercy, and that is the greatest triumph.
verses.10: The disbelievers will be told, Allah's hatred for you, back when you rejected faith, was even greater than the hatred you feel for yourselves right now.
verses.11: They will say, our Lord, You gave us death twice and life twice, and we confess our sins; is there any way out at all?
verses.12: It will be said: this is because when Allah alone was invoked, you disbelieved, but when others were associated with Him, you believed; the judgment now belongs entirely to Allah, the Most High, the Grand.
verses.13: It is He who shows you His signs, and sends provision down to you from the sky; yet only the one who turns back to Him truly takes heed.
verses.14: So call upon Allah, devoted sincerely to Him alone, however much the disbelievers may dislike it.
verses.15: He is Exalted in rank, Owner of the Throne; He places His inspiration upon whom He wills of His servants, to warn of the Day of Meeting —
verses.16: The Day they emerge, when nothing about them will be hidden from Allah. To whom belongs all sovereignty that Day? To Allah, the One, the Prevailing.
verses.17: That Day, every soul will be repaid exactly for what it earned; no injustice at all — indeed, Allah is swift in reckoning.
verses.18: Warn them of the Approaching Day, when hearts rise to the throats, choked with dread; the wrongdoers will have no devoted friend, and no intercessor whose word carries weight.
verses.19: He knows the deceit hidden in every glance, and everything concealed within every chest.
verses.20: And Allah judges with truth, while whatever they call upon besides Him can judge nothing at all; indeed, Allah is the Hearing, the Seeing.
verses.21: Have they not traveled through the land and seen how those before them ended up? They were mightier in strength and left a deeper mark upon the earth, yet Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no protector against Him at all.
verses.22: That was because their messengers kept coming to them with clear proofs, yet they disbelieved, so Allah seized them; indeed, He is Powerful, severe in punishment.
verses.23: And indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs and clear authority —
verses.24: To Pharaoh, Haman, and Qarun; yet they said, a magician, and a liar.
verses.25: And when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, kill the sons of those who believe with him, and let their women live; but the scheme of the disbelievers is nothing but pure error.
verses.26: And Pharaoh said, let me kill Moses, and let him call upon his Lord; indeed, I fear he will change your religion, or spread corruption throughout the land.
verses.27: But Moses said, I have sought refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant person who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.
verses.28: And a believing man from Pharaoh's own household, who had kept his faith hidden, said, will you kill a man simply for saying, my Lord is Allah, when he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? If he is lying, his lie falls upon himself; but if he is truthful, some of what he warns you of will surely strike you; indeed, Allah does not guide a lying transgressor.
verses.29: My people, today the kingdom is yours, dominant across this land; but who will protect us from Allah's punishment, should it ever reach us? Pharaoh answered, I only show you what I myself see, and I only guide you toward the path of right conduct.
verses.30: And the believing man said, my people, indeed I fear for you a fate like that of the earlier factions —
verses.31: Like the fate of the people of Noah, and 'Aad, and Thamud, and those who came after them; and Allah desires no injustice at all for His servants.
verses.32: And my people, indeed I fear for you the Day of Calling Out —
verses.33: The Day you turn your backs, fleeing, with no protector at all from Allah; and whoever Allah lets go astray has no guide left.
verses.34: Joseph had already come to you before, with clear proofs, yet you remained in doubt about what he brought, until, once he died, you said, Allah will never send another messenger after him. This is how Allah lets a doubting transgressor go astray.
verses.35: Those who dispute the signs of Allah without any real authority reaching them are held in deep hatred, both by Allah and by the believers; this is how Allah seals the heart of every arrogant tyrant.
verses.36: And Pharaoh said, Haman, build me a tower, so that I might reach the pathways —
verses.37: The pathways to the heavens, so I may look upon the God of Moses, though I truly believe he is a liar. And so his evil deed was made appealing to Pharaoh, turning him away from the right path; and Pharaoh's scheme led to nothing but ruin.
verses.38: And the believing man said, my people, follow me — I will guide you to the path of right conduct.
verses.39: My people, this worldly life is nothing but fleeting enjoyment, while the Hereafter is truly the lasting home.
verses.40: Whoever does evil will be repaid only with its equivalent, but whoever does righteousness, male or female, while truly believing, will enter Paradise, provided for there without limit.
verses.41: And my people, how strange it is that I invite you to salvation, while you invite me to the Fire.
verses.42: You call me to disbelieve in Allah, and to associate with Him something I have no knowledge of, while I call you toward the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.
verses.43: There is no doubt that what you invite me toward has no true call in this world or the next; indeed, our return is to Allah, and the transgressors will be companions of the Fire.
verses.44: You will remember what I have said to you; and I entrust my affair completely to Allah — indeed, Allah is ever watchful of His servants.
verses.45: So Allah protected him from the evil they schemed, while Pharaoh's people were surrounded by the very worst punishment —
verses.46: The Fire, which they face morning and evening; and on the Day the Hour arrives, it will be said, admit Pharaoh's people into the most severe punishment.
verses.47: And picture them arguing within the Fire, the weak saying to those who had once been arrogant, indeed, we were merely your followers — will you now take on some share of this Fire for us?
verses.48: The arrogant will answer, indeed, we are all in this together; Allah has already judged between His servants.
verses.49: And those in the Fire will say to Hell's keepers, plead with your Lord to ease our punishment, even for just one day.
verses.50: They will answer, did your messengers not come to you with clear proofs? They will say, yes. The keepers will reply, then plead for yourselves — though the pleading of disbelievers leads only to nothing.
verses.51: Indeed, We will surely support Our messengers and the believers, both in this worldly life and on the Day the witnesses stand forth —
verses.52: The Day when no excuse will help the wrongdoers; theirs is the curse, and theirs is the worst possible home.
verses.53: And indeed, We gave Moses guidance, and caused the Children of Israel to inherit the Scripture —
verses.54: As guidance and a reminder for people of true understanding.
verses.55: So be patient — indeed, the promise of Allah is true; and seek forgiveness for your shortcomings, and glorify your Lord with praise in the evening and in the morning.
verses.56: Indeed, those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority reaching them carry nothing in their hearts but a pride they will never truly attain; so seek refuge in Allah — indeed, He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
verses.57: The creation of the heavens and the earth is truly greater than the creation of mankind, yet most people simply do not realize it.
verses.58: And the blind and the seeing are never equal, nor are the believers who do righteous deeds equal to the evildoer; how little you truly reflect.
verses.59: Indeed, the Hour is coming, with no doubt at all, yet most people simply do not believe it.
verses.60: And your Lord says, call upon Me, and I will answer you; indeed, those too proud to worship Me will enter Hell, utterly humiliated.
verses.61: It is Allah who made the night for you, to find rest in, and the day, to give you sight; indeed, Allah is full of bounty toward people, yet most people remain ungrateful.
verses.62: That is Allah, your Lord, Creator of all things; there is no god but Him — so how are you so easily deceived?
verses.63: This is exactly how earlier generations were deceived, the very ones who used to deny the signs of Allah.
verses.64: It is Allah who made the earth a place to settle, and the sky a canopy above you, who shaped you and perfected your forms, and provided you with good things; that is Allah, your Lord — so blessed is Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
verses.65: He is the Ever-Living; there is no god but Him, so call upon Him, sincerely devoted to Him alone. All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
verses.66: Say: I have been forbidden to worship what you call upon besides Allah, now that clear proofs have reached me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit fully to the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.67: It is He who created you from dust, then from a small drop, then from a clinging clot; then He brings you forth as a child, then lets you reach maturity, then old age — though some of you die before that — so that you might reach an appointed term, and perhaps come to reason clearly.
verses.68: He is the One who gives life and causes death; and when He decrees a matter, He simply says to it, be, and it is.
verses.69: Have you not considered those who dispute the signs of Allah — how far they are turned away from the truth?
verses.70: Those who deny the Book, and everything Our messengers were sent with, will soon come to know —
verses.71: When shackles and chains are placed around their necks, and they are dragged along —
verses.72: Through boiling water, then set ablaze in the Fire.
verses.73: Then it will be asked of them, where now is everything you used to associate —
verses.74: Alongside Allah? They will answer, they have vanished from us entirely; in truth, we called upon nothing real at all. This is how Allah lets the disbelievers go astray.
verses.75: It will be said, this is because you took such joy upon the earth without any right, and behaved with reckless arrogance —
verses.76: Enter the gates of Hell, to remain there forever; how wretched is the home of the arrogant.
verses.77: So be patient — indeed, the promise of Allah is true; and whether We show you some of what We promised them, or take you in death first, it is to Us they will all be returned in the end.
verses.78: And indeed, We sent messengers before you; some of their stories We have told you, and some We have not; and no messenger could ever bring a sign except by Allah's permission. So when Allah's command finally arrives, it will be settled in truth, and the deniers of truth will lose everything.
verses.79: It is Allah who made livestock for you, some to ride —
verses.80: And other benefits besides, letting you fulfill needs held deep within your hearts; and you are carried upon them, and upon ships as well.
verses.81: And He shows you His signs — so which of the signs of Allah will you deny?
verses.82: Have they not traveled through the land and seen how those before them ended up? They were more numerous, mightier in strength, and left a deeper mark upon the land, yet none of what they earned availed them at all.
verses.83: And when their messengers brought them clear proofs, they simply celebrated the knowledge they already had, until what they used to mock closed in around them completely.
verses.84: And when they finally saw Our punishment, they said, now we believe in Allah alone, and reject everything we used to associate with Him.
verses.85: But their faith was never going to benefit them once they had already seen the punishment; this is the established pattern of Allah among His servants, and so, in the end, the disbelievers were the ones truly lost.

verses.1: Ha, Meem —
verses.2: A revelation from the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful —
verses.3: A Book whose verses have been clearly detailed, an Arabic Quran for a people who truly understand —
verses.4: A bringer of good news, and a warner; yet most of them turn away, refusing even to listen.
verses.5: And they say, our hearts are wrapped in coverings against what you invite us to, our ears carry deafness, and between us and you stands a barrier; so act as you will, for indeed, we too are acting.
verses.6: Say: I am only a man like you, to whom it has been revealed that your God is One God; so take a straight path toward Him, and seek His forgiveness. And woe to those who associate partners with Him —
verses.7: Those who do not give charity, and who reject the Hereafter entirely.
verses.8: Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds will have a reward that never ends.
verses.9: Say: do you truly disbelieve in the One who created the earth in two days, and set up equals to Him? He is the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.10: And He placed firmly rooted mountains upon it, blessed it, and determined its sustenance in four days, in perfect measure — for anyone who asks.
verses.11: Then He turned to the heaven while it was still smoke, and said to it and to the earth, come into being, willingly or unwillingly; they both answered, we come willingly.
verses.12: So He completed them as seven heavens in two days, and assigned to each heaven its command; and We adorned the nearest heaven with lamps, and for protection too; that is the design of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
verses.13: But if they turn away, say: I have warned you of a thunderbolt like the one that struck 'Aad and Thamud.
verses.14: When messengers came to them, before and after, saying, worship none but Allah, they answered, had our Lord willed, He would have sent angels; so we reject what you have been sent with.
verses.15: As for 'Aad, they grew arrogant on the earth without any right, saying, who is mightier than us in strength? Did they not consider that Allah, who created them, was mightier still? Yet they kept rejecting Our signs.
verses.16: So We sent upon them a violent, howling wind, across days of misfortune, to make them taste disgrace in this worldly life; but the punishment of the Hereafter is even more disgracing, and they will find no help at all.
verses.17: And as for Thamud, We offered them guidance, but they chose blindness over guidance, so the thunderbolt of humiliating punishment seized them for what they had earned.
verses.18: And We saved those who believed, and who lived in awe of Allah.
verses.19: And on the Day the enemies of Allah are gathered toward the Fire, driven together in ranks —
verses.20: Until, when they reach it, their own hearing, sight, and skin will testify against them, about everything they used to do.
verses.21: And they will ask their skin, why have you testified against us? It will answer, Allah, who gives speech to all things, has given speech to us as well; He created you the first time, and to Him you are returned.
verses.22: And you never hid yourselves out of fear that your hearing, sight, or skin might testify against you; rather, you assumed Allah did not know much of what you were doing.
verses.23: And that very assumption you held about your Lord has brought about your ruin, and you have become among the true losers.
verses.24: So even if they endure patiently, the Fire remains their home; and even if they plead to be excused, their plea will never be accepted.
verses.25: And We assigned them companions who made everything around them, past and future, seem appealing, and so the sentence fell upon them, joining nations of jinn and humans who had already passed away; indeed, they were all losers.
verses.26: And the disbelievers say, do not listen to this Quran, and drown it out with noise, so that perhaps you might overcome it.
verses.27: But We will surely make the disbelievers taste a severe punishment, and repay them fully for the worst of what they used to do.
verses.28: That is the repayment of the enemies of Allah — the Fire; their eternal home rests there, a fitting reward for rejecting Our verses so persistently.
verses.29: And the disbelievers will say, our Lord, show us those among the jinn and men who misled us, so we may trample them underfoot, making them among the lowest of all.
verses.30: Indeed, those who say, our Lord is Allah, and then remain steadfast — the angels descend upon them, saying, do not fear, and do not grieve, but rejoice in the good news of the Paradise you were promised.
verses.31: We were your allies in worldly life, and we remain so in the Hereafter; there you will have whatever your souls desire, and there you will have whatever you ask for —
verses.32: A generous welcome from a Lord both Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.33: And who speaks more beautifully than one who invites to Allah, does what is righteous, and says, indeed, I am among those who submit?
verses.34: And good and evil deeds are never equal; repel evil with what is better, and suddenly the one between whom and you there was enmity becomes like a devoted, close friend.
verses.35: But this quality is only granted to those who are patient, and only to those blessed with an especially generous share of good.
verses.36: And if an evil suggestion from Satan ever provokes you, seek refuge in Allah; indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
verses.37: And among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon; do not prostrate to the sun or the moon, but prostrate to Allah, who created them all, if it is truly Him you worship.
verses.38: But if they remain arrogant, then those nearest to your Lord glorify Him endlessly, night and day, without ever growing weary.
verses.39: And among His signs is that you see the earth barren and still, yet when We send down rain upon it, it stirs and grows; truly, the One who revives it is also the One who revives the dead; indeed, He is capable of all things.
verses.40: Indeed, those who twist the meaning of Our verses are never hidden from Us; so is the one cast into the Fire better, or the one who arrives secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do as you wish; indeed, He sees clearly everything you do.
verses.41: Indeed, those who reject this message once it has reached them will face consequences; and truly, it is a mighty Book —
verses.42: Falsehood cannot approach it from any direction at all; it is a revelation from a Lord both Wise and Praiseworthy.
verses.43: Nothing is said to you that was not already said to the messengers before you; indeed, your Lord possesses both forgiveness and painful punishment.
verses.44: And had We made it a non-Arabic Quran, they would have said, why are its verses not explained clearly? Is it foreign speech given to an Arab messenger? Say: for the believers, it is guidance and healing; but for those who do not believe, there is deafness in their ears, and blindness over their sight, as though they are being called from somewhere very far away.
verses.45: And We had already given Moses the Scripture, yet it too became a subject of dispute; and had it not been for a decree already set by your Lord, the matter would have been settled between them; indeed, they remain in deep, troubling doubt about it.
verses.46: Whoever does righteousness does it for his own soul, and whoever does evil works against it; and your Lord is never unjust toward His servants.
verses.47: To Him alone is attributed knowledge of the Hour; no fruit emerges from its covering, nor does any female conceive or give birth, without His full knowledge. And on the Day He calls out, where are My partners? They will answer, we now declare to You that none among us can bear witness to any such thing.
verses.48: And everything they used to call upon before will vanish from them, and they will become certain that they have no way of escape left at all.
verses.49: Man never tires of asking for good things, but when hardship touches him, he grows hopeless and despairing.
verses.50: And if We let him taste mercy after some hardship that had touched him, he says, this is simply mine by right, and I do not think the Hour will ever come; and even if I am returned to my Lord, surely the best awaits me there too. But We will surely inform the disbelievers of everything they did, and make them taste a truly massive punishment.
verses.51: And when We favor man, he turns away and distances himself; but when hardship touches him, he turns to lengthy, desperate prayer.
verses.52: Say: consider this — if it truly is from Allah, and you reject it, who could be further astray than one so deep in opposition?
verses.53: We will show them Our signs across the farthest horizons, and within themselves too, until it becomes perfectly clear to them that this is the truth; is it not enough that your Lord is Witness over all things?
verses.54: Indeed, they remain in doubt about meeting their Lord; yet indeed, He truly encompasses all things — the surah closing on this final, quiet certainty, the very doubt described throughout finally met by His complete, surrounding knowledge.

verses.1: Ha, Meem —
verses.2: 'Ayn, Seen, Qaf — another set of standalone letters, opening this surah with quiet mystery.
verses.3: Thus has He revealed to you, and to those before you — Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise, connecting this revelation to every prophet who came before.
verses.4: To Him belongs everything in the heavens and everything on the earth, and He is the Most High, the Most Great.
verses.5: The heavens nearly split apart from above, out of awe of Him, while the angels glorify their Lord with praise and seek forgiveness for those on earth; truly, it is Allah who is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
verses.6: And those who take protectors besides Him — Allah remains the true Guardian over them, and you are not appointed as their keeper.
verses.7: And thus We have revealed to you an Arabic Quran, so that you might warn the Mother of Cities and everyone around it, warning of the Day of Assembly, about which there is no doubt; one group will be in Paradise, and another in the Blaze.
verses.8: And had Allah willed, He could have made them all a single community, but He admits into His mercy whom He wills; and the wrongdoers will have no protector or helper at all.
verses.9: Or have they taken protectors besides Him? Yet Allah alone is the true Protector; He gives life to the dead, and He is capable of all things.
verses.10: And whatever you differ about, its judgment rests with Allah; say, that is Allah, my Lord — upon Him I rely, and to Him I turn back.
verses.11: Creator of the heavens and the earth, He made mates for you from among yourselves, and mates among the livestock too, multiplying you through this; there is nothing at all like Him, and He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
verses.12: To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth; He extends provision for whom He wills, and restricts it too; indeed, He has full knowledge of all things.
verses.13: He has ordained for you the same religion enjoined upon Noah, and what We revealed to you, and what We enjoined upon Abraham, Moses, and Jesus — to establish the faith, and remain undivided within it; yet what you invite them to feels difficult for those who associate partners with Allah. Allah chooses for Himself whom He wills, and guides to Himself whoever sincerely turns back.
verses.14: And they only became divided after real knowledge had come to them, driven by jealous rivalry among themselves; and had it not been for a decree already set by your Lord, delaying judgment to an appointed time, the matter would have already been settled between them. And indeed, those who inherited the Scripture after them remain in deep, troubling doubt about it.
verses.15: So invite people to that faith, and remain steadfast exactly as commanded, and do not follow their desires; but say, I believe in whatever Allah has revealed of the Book, and I am commanded to act justly among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord; for us are our deeds, and for you are yours. There is no need for argument between us; Allah will gather us all together, and to Him is the final return.
verses.16: And those who argue about Allah after His message has already been answered — their argument holds no weight at all before their Lord; upon them is His wrath, and for them a severe punishment.
verses.17: It is Allah who sent down the Book in truth, along with the balance; and what could make you perceive it fully? Perhaps the Hour is already near.
verses.18: Those who do not believe in it are impatient for it, while those who believe hold real fear of it, knowing it is the truth; indeed, those who dispute about the Hour have strayed into deep error.
verses.19: Allah is Subtle and Gentle with His servants; He provides for whom He wills, and He is the Powerful, the Exalted in Might.
verses.20: Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We increase his harvest for him; and whoever desires only the harvest of this world, We give him some of it, but he will have no share at all in the Hereafter.
verses.21: Or do they have partners who have decreed for them a religion Allah never permitted? And had it not been for a decisive decree already set, the matter between them would have already been settled; indeed, the wrongdoers will have a painful punishment.
verses.22: You will see the wrongdoers fearful of what they have earned, knowing it will surely befall them; while those who believed and did righteous deeds will be in the lush meadows of the gardens, receiving whatever they wish before their Lord — that is the truly great favor.
verses.23: This is the good news Allah gives to His servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say: I ask you no payment for this message, except love for those near to me; and whoever earns a good deed, We increase its goodness for him even further; indeed, Allah is Forgiving, ever Appreciative.
verses.24: Or do they say, he has invented a lie about Allah? Yet if Allah willed, He could seal your very heart; but Allah wipes away falsehood and firmly establishes the truth through His words; indeed, He knows fully what lies within every chest.
verses.25: And it is He who accepts repentance from His servants, and pardons their misdeeds, and He knows fully everything you do.
verses.26: And He answers those who believe and do righteous deeds, increasing them from His bounty; but for the disbelievers is a severe punishment.
verses.27: And had Allah extended provision generously to all His servants, they would surely have transgressed throughout the earth; but He sends it down in the measure He wills; indeed, He is fully Aware and Watchful of His servants.
verses.28: And it is He who sends down rain after people have lost all hope, spreading His mercy widely; and He is the Protector, the Praiseworthy.
verses.29: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the living creatures He has scattered throughout them; and He is fully able to gather them all together whenever He wills.
verses.30: And whatever calamity befalls you is a result of what your own hands have earned; yet He pardons so much more than He punishes.
verses.31: And you can never escape Allah on this earth, nor do you have any protector or helper besides Him.
verses.32: And among His signs are the ships sailing the sea, tall as mountains.
verses.33: Had He willed, He could still the wind, leaving them motionless upon its surface; indeed, in that are signs for everyone patient and grateful.
verses.34: Or He could destroy them for what they earned; yet He pardons so much more than He punishes.
verses.35: And so that those who dispute over Our signs may know that they have no place to escape to at all.
verses.36: So whatever you have been given is merely the enjoyment of this worldly life; but what is with Allah is far better and more lasting, reserved for those who believe and rely upon their Lord —
verses.37: Those who avoid major sins and indecent acts, and who forgive when they are angry —
verses.38: And those who respond to their Lord, establish prayer, conduct their affairs through mutual consultation, and spend from what We have provided them —
verses.39: And those who, when oppressed, stand up and defend themselves —
verses.40: The repayment for a wrong is an equal wrong in return; but whoever pardons and seeks reconciliation, his reward rests with Allah; indeed, He does not love wrongdoers.
verses.41: And whoever defends himself after having truly been wronged — there is no blame at all upon such a person.
verses.42: Blame falls only upon those who wrong others and transgress upon the earth without right; for them is a painful punishment.
verses.43: But whoever is patient and forgives — indeed, that is truly among matters worthy of great resolve.
verses.44: And whoever Allah lets go astray has no protector left after Him; and you will see the wrongdoers, upon facing the punishment, saying, is there any way back? — a plea coming, always, far too late.
verses.45: And you will see them presented before the Fire, utterly humbled by disgrace, glancing at it from the corner of their eyes; and the believers will say, indeed, the true losers are those who lost themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection; truly, the wrongdoers remain in a lasting punishment.
verses.46: And they will have no allies at all to help them, besides Allah; and whoever Allah lets go astray has no way left to find guidance.
verses.47: Respond to your Lord before a Day comes from Allah that cannot be turned back; there will be no refuge for you that Day, nor any way to deny what you have done.
verses.48: But if they turn away, We have not sent you as a guardian over them; your duty is only to deliver the message. And indeed, when We let man taste mercy from Us, he rejoices in it; yet if hardship touches him because of what his own hands have done, then truly, man becomes deeply ungrateful.
verses.49: To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; He creates whatever He wills. He grants daughters to whom He wills, and sons to whom He wills —
verses.50: Or He grants both, male and female together, and leaves whom He wills without children at all; indeed, He is Knowing, fully Capable.
verses.51: And it is not for any human being that Allah should speak to him, except through revelation, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger to reveal, with His permission, whatever He wills; indeed, He is Most High, Wise.
verses.52: And so We have revealed to you a Spirit of Our command; you did not know before this what the Scripture was, nor what faith was, but We made it a light by which We guide whom We will among Our servants; and indeed, you guide toward a straight path —
verses.53: The path of Allah, to whom belongs everything in the heavens and everything on the earth; indeed, to Allah do all matters ultimately return, the surah closing on this final, settled certainty.

verses.1: Ha, Meem —
verses.2: By the clear Book —
verses.3: Indeed, We made it an Arabic Quran, so that you might truly understand.
verses.4: And indeed, it is held with Us in the Mother of the Book, exalted and full of wisdom.
verses.5: Then should We simply withhold this reminder from you, because you are a transgressing people?
verses.6: And how many a prophet did We send among the earlier peoples —
verses.7: Yet no prophet ever came to them without being mocked.
verses.8: So We destroyed nations far stronger than these people, and the example of those earlier peoples has already been set.
verses.9: And if you asked them, who created the heavens and the earth? They would surely answer, the Exalted in Might, the Knowing created them.
verses.10: He who made the earth a resting place for you, and set roads upon it so that you might find your way —
verses.11: And who sends down rain from the sky in careful measure, reviving a dead land through it — this is exactly how you too will be brought forth again —
verses.12: And who created all things in pairs, and made for you ships and animals to ride upon —
verses.13: So that you might settle securely upon them, then remember your Lord's favor once settled, saying, exalted is He who made this useful to us, for we could never have subdued it ourselves —
verses.14: And indeed, to our Lord we will surely return.
verses.15: Yet they have assigned to Him some of His own servants as offspring; truly, man is openly ungrateful.
verses.16: Has He taken daughters from His creation, while choosing sons for you instead? An accusation exposing its own inconsistency.
verses.17: And when one of them is given news of exactly what he attributes to the Most Merciful, his face darkens, and he holds back his grief — unwilling, even then, to accept for himself what he assigns to Allah.
verses.18: Is it one raised amid ornaments, unable to argue clearly, that they attribute to Allah?
verses.19: And they have made the angels, who are servants of the Most Merciful, into females; did they witness their creation? Their claim will be recorded, and they will be questioned about it.
verses.20: And they say, had the Most Merciful willed, we would never have worshipped them; yet they have no real knowledge of this — they are simply guessing.
verses.21: Or have We given them a book before this one, to which they are truly holding fast?
verses.22: Rather, they say, we found our fathers upon a certain way, and we are simply guided by their footsteps.
verses.23: And just the same, whenever We sent a warner to any town before you, its wealthy elite always said, we found our fathers upon a certain way, and we are following in their footsteps.
verses.24: Each warner asked, even if I brought you better guidance than what you found your fathers upon? Yet they answered, we reject whatever you were sent with.
verses.25: So We took retribution from them; see, then, how the deniers' story finally ended.
verses.26: And recall when Abraham said to his father and his people, indeed, I am free of everything you worship —
verses.27: Except for the One who created me; indeed, He will guide me.
verses.28: And he made this a lasting statement among his descendants, so that they might return to it.
verses.29: Yet I let these people, and their fathers, enjoy life, until the truth and a clear messenger finally reached them.
verses.30: But when the truth came to them, they said, this is magic, and indeed, we reject it entirely.
verses.31: And they said, why was this Quran not sent down instead upon some great man from one of the two great cities?
verses.32: Is it they who distribute the mercy of your Lord? It is We who apportion their very livelihood in this worldly life, raising some above others in rank, so that they might make use of one another; yet the mercy of your Lord is far better than anything they could ever accumulate.
verses.33: And were it not that all people might become of one disbelieving mind, We would have given the disbelievers homes with silver ceilings and silver stairways to climb —
verses.34: And silver doors and couches to recline upon —
verses.35: And gold ornaments besides; yet all of that is nothing but the passing enjoyment of this worldly life, while the Hereafter with your Lord belongs to the righteous alone.
verses.36: And whoever turns away, blind to the remembrance of the Most Merciful — We assign him a devil, who becomes his constant companion.
verses.37: And indeed, these devils turn people away from the right path, while those people believe themselves to be rightly guided —
verses.38: Until, when he finally comes before Us, he says to his companion, if only there had been between us the distance of the east and the west — what a truly wretched companion you turned out to be.
verses.39: And it will do you no good at all that Day, since you have both wronged; you are simply sharing equally in the punishment now.
verses.40: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind, or the one clearly lost in error?
verses.41: And whether We take you away in death or not, indeed, We will surely take retribution upon them.
verses.42: Or whether We show you what We have promised them, indeed, We are fully able to do so.
verses.43: So hold firmly to what has been revealed to you; indeed, you are upon a straight path.
verses.44: And indeed, it is a reminder for you and for your people, and you will all surely be questioned about it.
verses.45: And ask any of Our messengers whom We sent before you, whether We ever appointed deities to be worshipped besides the Most Merciful.
verses.46: And indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his leading men, and he said, indeed, I am the messenger of the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.47: But when he brought them Our signs, at once they laughed at every one of them.
verses.48: Yet We showed them no sign without it being greater than the one before it, and We seized them with hardship, so that they might return to faith.
verses.49: And they said, magician, call upon your Lord for us, by whatever He has promised you; we will surely be guided.
verses.50: But when We removed that hardship from them, they broke their word at once.
verses.51: And Pharaoh called out among his people, declaring, my people, does the kingdom of Egypt not belong to me, with these rivers flowing right beneath me? Can you not see?
verses.52: Am I not better than this man, insignificant, who can hardly even express himself clearly?
verses.53: Why, then, have no golden bracelets been placed upon him, or angels come with him in support?
verses.54: So he deceived his people, and they obeyed him blindly; indeed, they were a truly defiant people.
verses.55: And when they finally provoked Our anger, We took retribution from them, and drowned every one of them.
verses.56: And We made them a precedent, and an example for later generations to reflect upon.
verses.57: And when the son of Mary was mentioned as an example, at once your people erupted in mocking laughter.
verses.58: And they said, are our gods better, or is he? They raised this only to provoke argument; indeed, they are a people fond of dispute.
verses.59: He was nothing more than a servant We favored, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel.
verses.60: And had We willed, We could have made angels instead of you, succeeding one another upon the earth.
verses.61: And indeed, Jesus will be a sign for the coming of the Hour, so have no doubt about it, and simply follow Me; this is a straight path.
verses.62: And do not let Satan turn you away; indeed, he is a clear enemy to you.
verses.63: And when Jesus came with clear proofs, he said, I have brought you wisdom, and to make clear to you some of what you dispute over, so hold awe of Allah, and obey me —
verses.64: Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him alone; this is a straight path.
verses.65: Yet the factions differed among themselves; so woe to the wrongdoers, from the punishment of a truly painful Day.
verses.66: Are they simply waiting for the Hour to arrive upon them suddenly, while they remain entirely unaware?
verses.67: Close friends, on that Day, will turn against one another as enemies, except for the righteous —
verses.68: To whom it will be said, My servants, there is no fear for you today, nor will you grieve —
verses.69: Those who believed in Our verses, and lived in complete submission to Us —
verses.70: Enter Paradise, you and your loved ones, filled with joy.
verses.71: Plates and cups of gold will be passed among them, containing everything the soul desires and everything that delights the eye, and there they will remain forever.
verses.72: And that is the Paradise you have been given to inherit, for everything you used to do.
verses.73: There you will have abundant fruit, from which you will eat freely.
verses.74: Indeed, the guilty will remain in the punishment of Hell forever —
verses.75: Never eased for them; they will remain there in complete despair.
verses.76: And We never wronged them; rather, it was they who wronged themselves.
verses.77: And they will cry out, keeper of Hell, let your Lord finish us completely! He will answer, indeed, you are staying right where you are.
verses.78: We had certainly brought you the truth, yet most of you simply despised it.
verses.79: Or have they settled on some scheme? Then indeed, We too are settling on a plan.
verses.80: Or do they think We cannot hear their secrets and their private conversations? Indeed We can, and Our messengers are right there with them, recording everything.
verses.81: Say: if the Most Merciful truly had a son, I would be the very first to worship him — a statement made only to expose the absurdity of the claim.
verses.82: Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Lord of the Throne, far above everything they describe.
verses.83: So leave them to their idle talk and amusement, until they meet the very Day they were promised.
verses.84: And it is He who is God in the heaven, and God on the earth; He is the Wise, the Knowing.
verses.85: And blessed is He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them; with Him rests the knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you will all return.
verses.86: And those they call upon besides Him hold no power of intercession at all, except for those who bear witness to the truth, and who truly know what they are saying.
verses.87: And if you asked them who created them, they would surely say, Allah; so how, then, are they so easily deceived?
verses.88: And Allah is aware of the Prophet's own words, my Lord, indeed, these are a people who simply will not believe.
verses.89: So turn away from them, and say, peace — the surah closes on this gentle word, patience offered as the final response, with the certainty that they will come to know the truth in time.

verses.1: Ha, Meem —
verses.2: By the clear Book —
verses.3: Indeed, We sent it down during a blessed night; indeed, We have always been the ones to warn.
verses.4: On that night, every precise matter is made distinct and clear —
verses.5: A command issued directly from Us; indeed, We were sending a messenger —
verses.6: As a mercy from your Lord; indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing —
verses.7: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, if only you would be certain.
verses.8: There is no god but Him; He gives life and causes death — your Lord, and the Lord of your earliest forefathers.
verses.9: Yet they remain in doubt about the Hour, playing about instead of taking it seriously.
verses.10: So watch for the Day the sky brings forth a visible smoke —
verses.11: Covering the people; this is a painful torment.
verses.12: They will cry, our Lord, remove this torment from us; indeed, we now believe.
verses.13: But how will remembrance help them now, when a clear messenger had already come to them —
verses.14: And they turned away from him, saying, taught by others, a madman?
verses.15: Indeed, We will remove the torment for a little while; yet you will surely return to disbelief.
verses.16: On the Day We strike with the greatest assault, indeed, We will finally take retribution.
verses.17: And We had already tested the people of Pharaoh before them, when a noble messenger came to them —
verses.18: Saying, hand over to me the servants of Allah; indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger sent to you —
verses.19: And do not act with arrogance against Allah; indeed, I have brought you clear authority —
verses.20: And indeed, I have sought refuge in my Lord and your Lord, so that you would not stone me —
verses.21: And if you do not believe me, then simply leave me be.
verses.22: Then he called out to his Lord, these are indeed a guilty people.
verses.23: Allah answered, set out with My servants by night; indeed, you will be pursued —
verses.24: And leave the sea still and calm behind you; indeed, they are an army about to be drowned.
verses.25: How many gardens and springs they left behind —
verses.26: And fields of crops, and honored dwellings —
verses.27: And luxury in which they had once delighted.
verses.28: So it was; and We caused another people to inherit it all.
verses.29: Neither the heaven nor the earth wept for them, nor were they given any further reprieve.
verses.30: And We surely saved the Children of Israel from that humiliating torment —
verses.31: From Pharaoh, who was truly a haughty man among the transgressors.
verses.32: And We chose them, with knowledge, above the peoples of their time.
verses.33: And We gave them signs containing a clear test.
verses.34: Indeed, these disbelievers are saying —
verses.35: There is nothing but our first death, and we will never be raised again —
verses.36: So bring back our forefathers, if you are telling the truth.
verses.37: Are they somehow better than the people of Tubba and those before them? We destroyed them, for they too were truly guilty.
verses.38: And We did not create the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, as mere play —
verses.39: We created them only in truth, though most people simply do not know it.
verses.40: Indeed, the Day of Judgment is the appointed time for all of them together —
verses.41: A Day when no relative will benefit another relative at all, nor will anyone find help —
verses.42: Except for those on whom Allah has mercy; indeed, He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
verses.43: Indeed, the tree of Zaqqum —
verses.44: Is food reserved for the sinner.
verses.45: Like murky, molten oil, it boils within the stomach —
verses.46: Just as boiling water churns and seethes.
verses.47: It will be said, seize him, and drag him into the depths of Hellfire —
verses.48: Then pour over his head the torment of scalding water —
verses.49: Taste this — indeed, you were the mighty, the honored one! A bitter, mocking echo of arrogance once spoken in life.
verses.50: This is exactly what you used to argue and doubt about.
verses.51: Indeed, the righteous will dwell in a place of complete security —
verses.52: Amid gardens and flowing springs —
verses.53: Dressed in fine silk and rich brocade, seated facing one another.
verses.54: And so it will be; We will wed them to companions with beautiful, wide eyes.
verses.55: They will call for every kind of fruit there, safe and completely secure.
verses.56: They will never taste death there again, beyond that first death already behind them, and He will have shielded them fully from the punishment of Hellfire —
verses.57: As a generous favor from your Lord; that is the truly great attainment.
verses.58: And We have made this Quran easy to understand in your own tongue, so that people might take heed.
verses.59: So watch, and wait — indeed, they too are watching, and waiting, the surah closing with this quiet standoff between patience and denial.

verses.1: Ha, Meem —
verses.2: The revelation of this Quran comes from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.3: Indeed, within the heavens and the earth are signs for those who truly believe.
verses.4: And in your own creation, and in the creatures He scatters throughout the earth, are signs for people of firm certainty.
verses.5: And in the alternation of night and day, in the provision Allah sends down from the sky, reviving a lifeless earth, and in the shifting of the winds, are signs for a people who truly reason.
verses.6: These are the verses of Allah, recited to you in truth; so in what message after Allah and His verses will they still believe?
verses.7: Woe to every sinful liar —
verses.8: Who hears the verses of Allah recited to him, then persists in arrogance as though he had never heard them at all; give him news of a painful punishment.
verses.9: And when he learns anything of Our verses, he mocks them; for such people is a humiliating punishment.
verses.10: Ahead of them lies Hell, and nothing they earned will benefit them at all, nor anything they took as protectors besides Allah; for them is a tremendous punishment.
verses.11: This Quran is guidance; and for those who reject the verses of their Lord is a painful, wretched punishment.
verses.12: It is Allah who made the sea useful to you, so that ships might sail upon it by His command, and so that you might seek His bounty, and perhaps be grateful.
verses.13: And He made everything in the heavens and everything on the earth useful to you, all of it from Him; indeed, in that are signs for a people who reflect deeply.
verses.14: Tell the believers to forgive those who do not expect the days of Allah's reckoning, so that He may repay a people for what they used to earn.
verses.15: Whoever does good does it for himself, and whoever does evil does it against himself; then to your Lord you will all return.
verses.16: We had already given the Children of Israel the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood, provided them with good things, and favored them above the worlds of their time.
verses.17: And We gave them clear proofs in matters of faith, yet they only differed after knowledge had reached them, driven by jealous rivalry among themselves; indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning everything they disagreed about.
verses.18: Then We placed you upon a clear path in matters of faith; so follow it, and do not follow the desires of those without real knowledge.
verses.19: Indeed, they can never help you at all against Allah; and truly, wrongdoers are allies only of one another, while Allah is the protector of the righteous.
verses.20: This is insight for mankind, guidance, and mercy for a people of firm certainty.
verses.21: Or do those who commit evil deeds imagine We will treat them the same as those who believe and do righteous deeds, equal in life and in death? How poorly they judge.
verses.22: And Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth, so that every soul may be repaid for what it earned, without the slightest injustice.
verses.23: Have you seen the one who has taken his own desire as his god? Allah let him go astray despite knowledge, sealed his hearing and his heart, and placed a veil over his sight — so who could possibly guide him after Allah? Will you not then take heed?
verses.24: And they say, there is nothing but our life in this world; we live, we die, and nothing destroys us but time. Yet they have no real knowledge of this; they are only guessing.
verses.25: And when Our clear verses are recited to them, their only argument is, bring back our forefathers, if you are telling the truth.
verses.26: Say: Allah gives you life, then causes your death, then He will gather you together for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt — yet most people simply do not understand.
verses.27: To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; and on the Day the Hour arrives, the falsifiers of truth will be the ones who lose everything.
verses.28: And you will see every nation kneeling in fear; every nation will be called to its own record, and told, today you are repaid for exactly what you used to do.
verses.29: This record of Ours speaks about you in complete truth; indeed, We were recording everything you did.
verses.30: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy — a clear and unmistakable attainment.
verses.31: But as for those who disbelieved, it will be said, were Our verses not recited to you? Yet you were arrogant, and became a people of wrongdoers.
verses.32: And when it was said, indeed, the promise of Allah is true, and the Hour is coming with no doubt, you replied, we do not know what the Hour is; we assume, and we are not truly convinced.
verses.33: And the evil of everything they did will become clear to them, and what they used to mock will surround them completely.
verses.34: It will be said, today We forget you, just as you forgot this very meeting; your home is the Fire, and you have no helpers at all.
verses.35: That is because you took the verses of Allah in mockery, and the life of this world deceived you; so today they will not be taken out of it, nor will their excuses be accepted.
verses.36: So to Allah belongs all praise, Lord of the heavens, Lord of the earth, Lord of all the worlds —
verses.37: And to Him belongs all majesty in the heavens and the earth; He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise — the surah closes on this note of complete, sovereign praise, drawing everything that came before it back to a single source.

verses.1: Ha, Meem — letters standing alone, a quiet signature opening the surah.
verses.2: The revelation of this Book comes from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.3: We did not create the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, except in truth, and for an appointed term; yet those who disbelieve turn away from the very warning meant to reach them.
verses.4: Say: have you considered what you call upon besides Allah? Show me what they have created of the earth, or whether they share in the heavens; bring me a scripture before this one, or any trace of real knowledge, if you are truthful.
verses.5: And who is more lost than one who calls upon something that will never answer him, not even until the Day of Resurrection, entirely unaware it is even being called upon?
verses.6: And when people are gathered together, these false objects of worship will become their enemies, denying that any worship was ever truly directed at them.
verses.7: And when Our verses are recited to them clearly, the disbelievers say of the truth once it arrives, this is plainly magic.
verses.8: Or do they say, he made it up himself? Say: if I invented it, you could not shield me at all from Allah; He knows best what you are engaged in. He is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and He is Forgiving, Merciful.
verses.9: Say: I am no different from the messengers who came before me, nor do I know what will happen to me or to you; I only follow what has been revealed to me, and I am nothing but a clear warner.
verses.10: Say: consider — if this truly came from Allah and you rejected it, while a witness from the Children of Israel testified to something similar and believed, while you remained arrogant, would Allah still guide such wrongdoing people?
verses.11: And the disbelievers say of the believers, had this message held any real good, they would not have beaten us to it; and finding no guidance in it themselves, they call it an old fabrication.
verses.12: Yet before it came the scripture of Moses, a guide and a mercy; and this is a confirming Book in the Arabic tongue, meant to warn wrongdoers and bring good news to those who do good.
verses.13: Indeed, those who say, our Lord is Allah, and then remain steadfast on that path — they will have no fear, nor will they grieve.
verses.14: Those are the companions of Paradise, remaining there forever, as a direct reward for everything they used to do.
verses.15: And We have commanded man to be good to his parents; his mother carried him with hardship, and bore him with hardship, and his time of carrying and weaning spans thirty months. Until, when he reaches full maturity, at forty years, he says, my Lord, enable me to be grateful for the favor You have given me and my parents, and to do righteousness that pleases You, and make my children righteous as well; I have truly turned to You in repentance, and I am among those submitted to You.
verses.16: Such are the ones from whom We accept the best of what they did, and overlook their shortcomings, counted among the companions of Paradise — a true promise, faithfully kept.
verses.17: But there is the one who says to his parents, enough of you; are you promising me I will be raised again, when so many generations have already passed away? While his parents plead with Allah's help, crying, woe to you, believe — Allah's promise is true — yet he only answers, this is nothing but old legends —
verses.18: Those are the ones upon whom the sentence has already fallen, joining nations of jinn and humans who came before them; indeed, they were all losers.
verses.19: And for everyone there are different ranks, according to what they did, so that He may fully repay them for their deeds, without the slightest injustice.
verses.20: And on the Day the disbelievers are brought before the Fire, it will be said, you spent all your good things in your worldly life and enjoyed them fully, so today you are repaid with a punishment of utter humiliation, for your arrogance on earth without right, and for your persistent defiance.
verses.21: And recall the brother of 'Aad, who warned his people among the sand dunes — and warners had come both before and after him — saying, worship none but Allah; indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.
verses.22: They said, have you come to turn us away from our gods? Then bring us what you threaten, if you are telling the truth.
verses.23: He said, that knowledge rests only with Allah; I simply deliver what I was sent with, but I see that you are a people acting out of pure ignorance.
verses.24: And when they saw it approaching their valleys as a cloud, they said joyfully, this is a cloud bringing us rain! Rather, it was exactly what they had impatiently demanded: a wind carrying a painful punishment —
verses.25: Destroying everything by the command of its Lord, until nothing remained visible except their empty dwellings. This is how We repay a guilty people.
verses.26: We had established them with far more than We have given you, granting them hearing, sight, and intellect; yet none of it helped them at all once they persisted in rejecting Allah's signs, and what they used to mock finally closed in around them.
verses.27: And We had already destroyed the towns surrounding you, and had repeatedly shown Our signs, so that they might turn back.
verses.28: So why did those they worshipped besides Allah, seeking closeness through them, not come to their aid? Rather, they abandoned them completely — that was the full extent of their fabricated lie.
verses.29: And recall when We directed a group of jinn toward you, to listen to the Quran; when they arrived, they said, listen quietly — and once it finished, they returned to their people as warners.
verses.30: They said, our people, we have truly heard a scripture revealed after Moses, confirming what came before it, guiding to the truth and to a straight path.
verses.31: Our people, answer the caller of Allah, and believe in him; He will forgive your sins, and protect you from a painful punishment.
verses.32: And whoever does not answer the caller of Allah cannot escape Him on this earth, nor will he find any protector besides Him; such people are in clear error.
verses.33: Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth without any exhaustion, is fully able to bring the dead back to life? Yes indeed — He is capable of all things.
verses.34: And on the Day the disbelievers are exposed to the Fire, it will be asked, is this not the truth? They will answer, yes, by our Lord. He will say, then taste the punishment for your persistent disbelief.
verses.35: So be patient, just as the resolute among the messengers were patient, and do not be hasty concerning them; on the Day they finally see what they were promised, it will feel as though they had remained in this world for no more than a single hour of a single day. This is a clear notice — and will any be destroyed except a defiantly disobedient people?

verses.1: Those who disbelieve and turn others away from the path of Allah — He will render all their deeds worthless.
verses.2: But those who believe, do righteous deeds, and believe in what has been revealed to Muhammad — which is the very truth from their Lord — He will erase their misdeeds and set right their entire condition.
verses.3: That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, while those who believe follow the truth from their Lord; this is how Allah presents examples to people, so they might understand clearly.
verses.4: So when you meet the disbelievers in battle, strike firmly, until once you have subdued them, secure their bonds; then either release them graciously afterward, or free them for ransom, until the burdens of war come to rest. Had Allah willed, He could have dealt with them directly Himself, but this test exists so that some of you might be tried through others. And those who are killed in the cause of Allah — He will never let their deeds go to waste.
verses.5: He will guide them, and set right their entire condition —
verses.6: And admit them into Paradise, which He has already made known to them.
verses.7: O you who believe, if you support the cause of Allah, He will support you, and make your footing firm.
verses.8: But those who disbelieve — for them is only misery, and He will render their deeds worthless.
verses.9: That is because they disliked what Allah revealed, so He caused their deeds to come to nothing.
verses.10: Have they not traveled through the land and seen how those before them ended up? Allah brought total destruction upon them, and something similar awaits every disbeliever.
verses.11: That is because Allah is the protector of those who believe, while the disbelievers have no protector at all.
verses.12: Indeed, Allah will admit those who believe and do righteous deeds into gardens beneath which rivers flow, while the disbelievers enjoy themselves and eat as grazing cattle eat, and the Fire will be their final home.
verses.13: And how many a town far stronger than the one that drove you out did We destroy; and no one could help them at all.
verses.14: So is the one standing on clear evidence from his Lord the same as the one whose evil deeds have been made appealing to him, following nothing but his own desires?
verses.15: The description of Paradise promised to the righteous includes rivers of water forever fresh, rivers of milk whose taste never changes, rivers of wine delightful to those who drink, and rivers of pure, refined honey; within it they will have every kind of fruit, along with forgiveness from their Lord. Can this compare to those who remain forever in the Fire, given scalding water to drink that tears apart their insides?
verses.16: And among them are those who listen to you, only to ask, once they leave, what did he just say? Those are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed, following nothing but their own desires.
verses.17: But those who accept guidance, He increases them further in guidance, and grants them a deep sense of righteousness.
verses.18: So are they simply waiting for the Hour to arrive suddenly upon them? Its signs have already begun appearing; so what good will their remembrance do them once it finally arrives?
verses.19: So know that there is no god except Allah, and ask forgiveness for your shortcomings, and for believing men and women; Allah knows well your every movement and your every place of rest.
verses.20: The believers say, why has no surah been sent down commanding us to fight? But when a decisive surah is revealed mentioning fighting, you see those with hypocrisy in their hearts looking at you like someone facing death itself.
verses.21: Obedience and good words would have suited them far better; and had they truly been sincere with Allah once the matter was decided, it would have been better for them.
verses.22: So if you were to turn away, would you then spread corruption throughout the land, and sever the very ties of kinship?
verses.23: Those are the ones Allah has cursed, so He made them deaf, and blinded their sight.
verses.24: Do they not reflect deeply upon the Quran, or are there locks placed firmly upon their hearts?
verses.25: Indeed, those who turned back to disbelief after guidance had become perfectly clear to them were simply enticed by Satan, who prolonged false hope within them.
verses.26: That was because they secretly told those who disliked what Allah had revealed, we will obey you in some matters; and Allah knows fully everything they conceal.
verses.27: So how will it be when the angels take their souls, striking their faces and their backs?
verses.28: That is because they pursued what angered Allah, and disliked what would have earned His pleasure, so He rendered their deeds worthless.
verses.29: Or do those with sickness in their hearts think that Allah would never expose their hidden malice?
verses.30: And had We willed, We could show them to you directly, and you would recognize them by their very features; but you will surely recognize them by the tone of their speech. And Allah knows fully every deed you do.
verses.31: And We will surely test you, until We make clear who among you truly strives, and who remains patient; and We will examine your affairs thoroughly.
verses.32: Indeed, those who disbelieve, turn others from the path of Allah, and oppose the Messenger after guidance has become clear to them will never harm Allah in the slightest, and He will render their deeds worthless.
verses.33: O you who believe, obey Allah, obey the Messenger, and do not let your good deeds go to waste.
verses.34: Indeed, those who disbelieve, turn others from the path of Allah, and then die while still disbelieving — Allah will never forgive them.
verses.35: So do not grow weak and call for peace while you hold the upper hand; Allah is with you, and He will never deprive you of the reward for your deeds.
verses.36: This worldly life is nothing but amusement and diversion; but if you believe and hold awe of Allah, He will grant you your rewards, without ever asking you for all your wealth.
verses.37: Were He to ask you for it all, pressing you hard, you would likely withhold, and your reluctance would be exposed.
verses.38: Here you are, being invited to spend in the cause of Allah, yet among you are some who hold back out of stinginess; and whoever holds back only harms himself, for Allah is Free of need, while you are the ones truly in need. And if you turn away, He will simply replace you with another people, who will be nothing like you — the surah closes on this quiet warning, generosity offered as a genuine choice, with real consequence for whichever way it is answered.

verses.1: Indeed, We have granted you a clear victory —
verses.2: That Allah may forgive you your past and future shortcomings, complete His favor upon you, and guide you along a straight path —
verses.3: And that Allah may support you with a mighty victory.
verses.4: It is He who sent tranquility into the hearts of the believers, so that their faith might increase alongside the faith they already held; to Allah belong the forces of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.5: So that He may admit believing men and believing women into gardens beneath which rivers flow, to remain there forever, and remove their misdeeds; and that, in Allah's sight, is truly a great attainment —
verses.6: And that He may punish the hypocrite men and women, and the idolatrous men and women, who harbor evil assumptions about Allah; upon them falls a cycle of misfortune, and Allah's anger and curse rest upon them, with Hell prepared for them — a truly wretched destination.
verses.7: To Allah belong the forces of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is ever Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.8: Indeed, We have sent you as a witness, a bearer of good news, and a warner —
verses.9: So that people might believe in Allah and His Messenger, honor and support him, and glorify Allah morning and evening.
verses.10: Indeed, those who pledge allegiance to you are truly pledging allegiance to Allah; the hand of Allah is above their hands. Whoever breaks his pledge only harms himself, and whoever fulfills what he promised Allah will be given a great reward.
verses.11: Those bedouins who stayed behind will say to you, our properties and families kept us occupied, so ask forgiveness for us; they speak with their tongues what is not truly in their hearts. Say: who could hold back Allah at all, if He intended harm or benefit for you? Rather, Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.12: Rather, you assumed the Messenger and the believers would never return to their families again, and that thought was made appealing in your hearts; you harbored an evil assumption, and so you became a ruined people.
verses.13: And whoever does not believe in Allah and His Messenger — indeed, We have prepared a blazing Fire for the disbelievers.
verses.14: To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; He forgives whom He wills, and punishes whom He wills, and Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.15: Those who stayed behind will say, when you set out to gather the spoils, let us come along too; they wish to change what Allah has already decreed. Say: you will never follow us; this is exactly what Allah has already said before. Then they will say, rather, you envy us; but in truth, they understood very little.
verses.16: Say to those bedouins who stayed behind: you will soon be called to face a people of great military strength, whom you will either fight or who will surrender; if you obey, Allah will grant you a fine reward, but if you turn away as you did before, He will punish you with a painful punishment.
verses.17: There is no blame upon the blind, nor the lame, nor the sick, for staying behind; and whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger will be admitted into gardens beneath which rivers flow, while whoever turns away will be punished with a painful punishment.
verses.18: Allah was truly pleased with the believers when they pledged their loyalty to you beneath the tree; He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent tranquility upon them, and rewarded them with a near, coming victory —
verses.19: And abundant spoils that they would gather; and Allah is ever Exalted in Might, Wise.
verses.20: Allah has promised you abundant future gains, and has hastened this one for you, restraining the hands of others from harming you — so that it might become a sign for the believers, and that He might guide you along a straight path.
verses.21: And other victories too, which you have not yet achieved but which Allah has already fully encompassed; Allah is truly capable of all things.
verses.22: Had the disbelievers fought you, they would surely have fled, finding no protector or helper at all.
verses.23: This is the established way of Allah, one that has occurred before; and you will never find any change in the way of Allah.
verses.24: It is He who restrained their hands from you, and your hands from them, within Makkah, after granting you the upper hand over them; and Allah sees clearly everything you do.
verses.25: It was they who disbelieved and blocked you from the Sacred Mosque, preventing the sacrificial offerings from reaching their place; and were it not for believing men and women you did not know, whom you might have unknowingly trampled, incurring guilt without realizing it — Allah restrained you from conflict, so that He might admit into His mercy whomever He willed. Had they been clearly separated, We would have punished the disbelievers among them with a painful punishment.
verses.26: When those who disbelieved filled their hearts with fierce pride, the pride of ignorance, Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Messenger and upon the believers, and bound them to the word of righteousness, which they were more deserving and worthy of; and Allah has full knowledge of all things.
verses.27: Allah has truly fulfilled His Messenger's vision in truth: you will surely enter the Sacred Mosque, if Allah wills, in safety, with heads shaved and hair shortened, without fear; He knew what you did not know, and arranged before that a near victory.
verses.28: It is He who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth, to make it prevail over every other way of life; and Allah is sufficient as a witness.
verses.29: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, and those with him are firm against the disbelievers, yet merciful among themselves; you see them bowing and prostrating, seeking Allah's favor and pleasure. Their mark is visible on their faces, the trace of prostration. This is their description in the Torah; and in the Gospel, they are described like a seed that sends out its shoot, strengthens it, and grows thick, standing firmly on its stalk, delighting the farmers — a description meant to enrage the disbelievers through their sheer resilience. Allah has promised those among them who believe and do righteous deeds forgiveness, and a great reward, closing the surah with this striking image of quiet, patient growth.

verses.1: O you who believe, do not put yourselves ahead of Allah and His Messenger; hold awe of Allah, for indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing — a call to hold back, to let guidance lead rather than personal impulse.
verses.2: O you who believe, do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, nor speak to him loudly as you might speak to one another, lest your good deeds be nullified without your even realizing it — reverence taught here through something as ordinary as tone of voice.
verses.3: Indeed, those who lower their voices in the presence of the Messenger of Allah are the ones whose hearts Allah has tested and found devoted to righteousness; for them is forgiveness, and a great reward.
verses.4: Indeed, those who call out to you from outside your private chambers — most of them simply do not use their reason.
verses.5: Had they waited patiently until you came out to them, it would have been better for them; but Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.6: O you who believe, if a disreputable person brings you news, verify it carefully, lest you harm people unknowingly and come to regret what you have done — a principle offered here long before the term misinformation ever existed.
verses.7: And know that among you is the Messenger of Allah; were he to yield to your wishes in many matters, you would fall into real hardship. But Allah has made faith beloved to you, beautifying it in your hearts, and made disbelief, defiance, and disobedience hateful to you; those are the rightly guided —
verses.8: As a favor and blessing from Allah; and Allah is Knowing, Wise.
verses.9: And if two groups among the believers fight one another, make peace between them; but if one continues to oppress the other, fight against the oppressing side until it returns to Allah's command; and once it returns, restore peace between them with fairness, and act justly — indeed, Allah loves those who act justly.
verses.10: The believers are but brothers, so make peace between your brothers, and hold awe of Allah, so that you may receive mercy.
verses.11: O you who believe, let no people ridicule another people, for they may well be better than them; nor let women ridicule other women, for they too may be better; and do not insult one another, nor call each other by offensive nicknames. How wretched is a name earned through wrongdoing after having believed; and whoever does not repent — those are truly the wrongdoers.
verses.12: O you who believe, avoid excessive suspicion, for some suspicion is itself a sin; and do not spy on one another, nor speak ill of one another behind their backs. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his own dead brother? You would surely find that disgusting. So hold awe of Allah; indeed, Allah is Accepting of repentance, and Merciful — one of the most vivid images in the entire Quran, gossip made viscerally, physically repulsive.
verses.13: O mankind, indeed We created you from a single man and woman, and made you into nations and tribes so that you might come to know one another. Indeed, the most honored among you in the sight of Allah is the one most mindful of Him; and Allah is Knowing, fully Aware — a foundation for human equality laid out here plainly, honor tied to character, never to lineage or origin.
verses.14: The desert Arabs say, we believe. Say to them: you have not yet believed; rather say, we have submitted, for faith has not yet entered your hearts. But if you obey Allah and His Messenger, He will not diminish anything from your deeds; indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful — a careful distinction drawn between outward submission and true, inward faith.
verses.15: The true believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, and then never doubt again, striving instead with their wealth and their very lives in the cause of Allah; it is they who are truly sincere.
verses.16: Say: would you inform Allah of your religion, when Allah already knows everything in the heavens and everything on earth, and Allah has full knowledge of all things?
verses.17: They consider it a favor to you that they have accepted Islam. Say: do not count your acceptance of Islam as a favor to me; rather, it is Allah who has done you the favor, guiding you toward faith, if you are truly sincere.
verses.18: Indeed, Allah knows the unseen realities of the heavens and the earth, and Allah sees clearly everything you do — the surah closes on this quiet reminder that every private word, every hidden motive discussed throughout this chapter, was never truly private to begin with.

verses.1: Qaf. By the honored Quran —
verses.2: Yet they are astonished that a warner has come to them from among their very own; the disbelievers say, this is a truly strange thing —
verses.3: When we have died and turned to dust, will we really return? That seems like a return too far away to believe.
verses.4: We already know exactly what the earth diminishes of them, and with Us is a record that forgets nothing.
verses.5: But they denied the truth when it reached them, and so they remain in a state of deep confusion.
verses.6: Have they not looked up at the sky above them — how We built it, adorned it, and left it entirely free of any flaw?
verses.7: And the earth We spread wide, placing firmly rooted mountains within it, and causing every beautiful kind of growth to flourish there —
verses.8: Offering insight and a reminder to every servant who turns sincerely toward Allah.
verses.9: And We sent down blessed rain from the sky, causing gardens and harvest grain to grow because of it —
verses.10: And tall palm trees, their fruit stacked in generous layers —
verses.11: As provision for His servants; and by it, We bring a lifeless land back to life. This is exactly how the resurrection will happen.
verses.12: Before them, the people of Noah denied, as did the companions of the well, and Thamud —
verses.13: And 'Aad, and Pharaoh, and the brothers of Lot —
verses.14: And the companions of the thicket, and the people of Tubba; every one of them denied the messengers, so My warning was justly carried out against them.
verses.15: Did We somehow fail in the very first creation? Yet they remain in doubt over a second one.
verses.16: And indeed, We created man, and We know exactly what his own soul whispers to him; We are closer to him than his own jugular vein — a closeness beyond anything he can fully grasp.
verses.17: When the two receiving angels record everything, seated one on the right and one on the left —
verses.18: Man does not utter a single word without an ever-present observer recording it.
