verses.19: And the intoxication of death will finally bring the truth into full view — exactly what you had been trying so hard to avoid.
verses.20: And the Horn will be blown; that is the Day the warning is finally carried out.
verses.21: And every soul will come, accompanied by one who drives it forward, and one who bears witness against it.
verses.22: It will be said, you were once heedless of this, but now We have removed your veil, and your sight today is sharp.
verses.23: And his companion will say, this is what I have prepared, right here, ready.
verses.24: It will be said, throw into Hell every obstinate disbeliever —
verses.25: Every obstructer of good, every transgressor, every doubter —
verses.26: Who set up another god alongside Allah; throw him into the severest punishment.
verses.27: His companion will say, our Lord, I did not lead him astray; rather, he himself was already deep in error.
verses.28: Allah will say, do not argue in front of Me, for I had already delivered the warning to you.
verses.29: My word will never change, and I am never unjust toward My servants.
verses.30: On the Day We ask Hell, are you filled yet? It will answer, is there still more?
verses.31: And Paradise will be brought near to the righteous, no longer distant —
verses.32: This is what you were promised, for every soul devoted to returning to Allah, and to keeping His covenant —
verses.33: Who held awe of the Most Merciful even unseen, and who came with a heart turned wholly toward Him.
verses.34: Enter it in peace; this is the Day of everlasting permanence.
verses.35: They will have whatever they wish for there, and with Us there is still more.
verses.36: And how many generations before them did We destroy, far greater in strength, who had explored every corner of the land — yet was there any place they could truly escape to?
verses.37: Indeed, in this is a reminder for anyone with a truly attentive heart, or anyone who listens while fully present.
verses.38: And We created the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, in six days, and no weariness ever touched Us.
verses.39: So be patient with what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise before the rising of the sun, and before its setting —
verses.40: And glorify Him during part of the night, and after every prostration.
verses.41: And listen, on the Day the caller calls out from a place close by —
verses.42: The Day they hear the blast in absolute truth; that is the Day of emergence from the graves.
verses.43: Indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and to Us is the final return.
verses.44: On the Day the earth splits open and they emerge in haste, that gathering will be effortless for Us.
verses.45: We know best everything they say, and you are not there to compel them; so remind, through this Quran, anyone who truly fears My warning — the surah closes here, gentle in tone even after everything just described, leaving the response ultimately to the listener's own heart.

verses.1: By the winds that scatter dust far and wide —
verses.2: And by the clouds that carry a heavy burden of rain —
verses.3: And by the ships that glide with ease —
verses.4: And by the angels who apportion every matter — four oaths, each drawn from something moving with purpose and precision.
verses.5: Indeed, what you are promised is true —
verses.6: And indeed, the reckoning will surely occur.
verses.7: By the sky, woven through with pathways —
verses.8: Indeed, you are caught up in conflicting speech about this message.
verses.9: Turned away from it is whoever is truly turned away.
verses.10: Destroyed are the liars —
verses.11: Who wander lost in confusion, blind to their own condition —
verses.12: They ask, mockingly, when is this Day of Recompense?
verses.13: It is the Day they will be tormented over the Fire —
verses.14: Taste your torment; this is exactly what you were once so impatient for.
verses.15: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs —
verses.16: Receiving gladly whatever their Lord has given them; indeed, they had already been doers of good before this.
verses.17: They used to sleep only a small portion of the night —
verses.18: And in the hours before dawn, they would seek forgiveness —
verses.19: And within their wealth was a rightful share for the one who asked, and the one too dignified to ask at all.
verses.20: And on the earth are signs for those who hold firm certainty —
verses.21: And within yourselves too — will you not truly see?
verses.22: And in the sky is your provision, and everything you have been promised.
verses.23: So by the Lord of the heaven and the earth, this is truth, every bit as certain as the fact that you are speaking right now.
verses.24: Has the story reached you of Abraham's honored guests —
verses.25: When they entered upon him and said, peace. He replied, peace, to a people unfamiliar to him —
verses.26: Then he went quietly to his family and returned with a fattened, roasted calf —
verses.27: Setting it before them, he asked, will you not eat?
verses.28: And he began to feel a quiet unease toward them. They said, do not be afraid, and gave him the good news of a knowledgeable son.
verses.29: So his wife approached with a cry of astonishment, striking her face, saying, a barren old woman, like me?
verses.30: They said, this is what your Lord has decreed; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing.
verses.31: Abraham asked, so what, then, is your true purpose here, messengers?
verses.32: They said, indeed, we have been sent to a guilty people —
verses.33: To send down upon them stones made of hardened clay —
verses.34: Marked by your Lord specifically for those who overstep every bound.
verses.35: So We brought out every believer who was in those cities —
verses.36: But We found within them only a single household submitted to Allah.
verses.37: And We left there a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
verses.38: And in Moses too there is a sign, when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority —
verses.39: But he turned away with his forces and said, a magician, or simply a madman.
verses.40: So We seized him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he alone was truly blameworthy.
verses.41: And in 'Aad too, when We sent against them a barren, destructive wind —
verses.42: It left nothing it touched without turning it into disintegrated ruin.
verses.43: And in Thamud, when it was said to them, enjoy yourselves a little while longer —
verses.44: Yet they defied the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they watched it approach.
verses.45: They were unable even to rise, nor could they defend themselves in any way.
verses.46: And before that, the people of Noah — indeed, they were a deeply defiant people.
verses.47: And the sky We built with real strength, and indeed, We continue to expand it.
verses.48: And the earth We spread wide, and how excellently We prepared it.
verses.49: And from all things We created pairs, so that you might reflect and remember.
verses.50: So flee toward Allah; indeed, I come to you from Him as a clear warner.
verses.51: And do not set up any other god alongside Allah; indeed, I come to you from Him as a clear warner.
verses.52: Just the same, no messenger ever came to those before them without being met with, a magician, or simply a madman.
verses.53: Did they somehow pass this accusation down to one another? Rather, they are simply a people who transgress.
verses.54: So turn away from them, for you are not to be blamed.
verses.55: And continue to remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.
verses.56: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
verses.57: I do not want any provision from them, nor do I want them to feed Me.
verses.58: Indeed, it is Allah who is the constant Provider, the firm possessor of strength.
verses.59: And indeed, for those who did wrong, there is a share of punishment just like that of their predecessors, so let them not rush Me impatiently.
verses.60: And woe to those who disbelieved, on account of the Day they have been promised — the surah closes here, this final warning carrying the same weight as the oaths that opened it.

verses.1: By the Mount —
verses.2: And by a Book inscribed —
verses.3: Written upon unrolled parchment —
verses.4: And by the frequently visited House —
verses.5: And the raised ceiling above —
verses.6: And the sea, filled and set to overflow — a chain of oaths sworn on sacred places and vast natural forces alike.
verses.7: Indeed, the punishment of your Lord will surely occur —
verses.8: Nothing exists that could ever prevent it.
verses.9: On the Day the sky sways violently, spinning —
verses.10: And the mountains drift away, departing entirely —
verses.11: Woe, that Day, to the deniers —
verses.12: Those who amuse themselves in empty, meaningless talk.
verses.13: On the Day they are shoved violently toward the fire of Hell —
verses.14: It will be said, this is exactly the Fire you used to deny.
verses.15: Is this magic, then, or do you simply refuse to see?
verses.16: Burn in it — whether you endure patiently or lose patience entirely, it makes no difference to you now; you are only being repaid for what you used to do.
verses.17: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and true pleasure —
verses.18: Delighting in what their Lord has granted them, having been shielded by Him from the punishment of the blazing Fire.
verses.19: Eat and drink with full satisfaction, for everything you used to do —
verses.20: Reclining upon thrones arranged in rows, and We will wed them to companions with beautiful, wide eyes.
verses.21: And those who believed, whose descendants followed them in faith — We will unite them with their descendants, and We will not diminish anything from their good deeds; every soul is bound by what it earned.
verses.22: And We will provide them with fruit and meat, whatever they desire.
verses.23: They will pass a cup among themselves, causing neither idle talk nor sinful speech.
verses.24: Eternal youths will circulate around them, as though they were carefully guarded pearls.
verses.25: And they will turn to one another, asking questions —
verses.26: Saying, we used to live in awe among our own people, careful and afraid —
verses.27: So Allah showed us favor, and shielded us from the punishment of the scorching wind —
verses.28: We used to call upon Him before; indeed, He alone is the Most Kind, the Most Merciful.
verses.29: So continue to remind, for by the grace of your Lord, you are neither a soothsayer nor a madman.
verses.30: Or do they say, a poet — one for whom we simply await misfortune, over time?
verses.31: Say: wait, then, for indeed I too am waiting with you.
verses.32: Or do their own minds instruct them to say this, or are they simply a transgressing people?
verses.33: Or do they say, he has invented it himself? Rather, they simply refuse to believe.
verses.34: Let them then produce something similar to it, if they are truly telling the truth.
verses.35: Or were they created out of nothing, or are they their own creators?
verses.36: Or did they create the heavens and the earth themselves? Rather, they hold no real certainty at all.
verses.37: Or do they hold the treasures of your Lord, or do they control them?
verses.38: Or do they have a ladder by which they climb up to listen in? Then let their listener bring forth clear proof.
verses.39: Or does He have daughters, while you claim sons for yourselves?
verses.40: Or are you asking them for payment, leaving them burdened with debt?
verses.41: Or do they hold knowledge of the unseen, writing it down for themselves?
verses.42: Or do they intend some scheme? But it is the disbelievers who will find themselves outmaneuvered instead.
verses.43: Or do they have a god other than Allah? Exalted is Allah, far above whatever they associate with Him.
verses.44: And even if they saw a fragment of the sky falling down before them, they would still say, it is nothing but clustered clouds — denial so entrenched it explains away even the plainest sign.
verses.45: So leave them, until they meet their Day, when they will be struck utterly senseless —
verses.46: The Day their scheming will be of no use to them at all, and no help will come to them either.
verses.47: And indeed, for those who did wrong, there is a punishment even before that one, though most of them do not realize it.
verses.48: So be patient for the judgment of your Lord, for indeed, you remain within Our sight; and glorify your Lord with praise when you rise —
verses.49: And glorify Him during part of the night, and after every prostration — the surah closes with this quiet rhythm of remembrance, returning devotion to its proper place after such a long defense of the truth.

verses.1: By the star as it descends —
verses.2: Your companion has not strayed, nor has he erred — a direct defense of his character, sworn upon something as constant as a falling star.
verses.3: Nor does he speak from his own desire —
verses.4: It is nothing but a revelation, given to him —
verses.5: Taught to him by one of tremendous strength —
verses.6: Possessed of soundness and power, who then appeared in his true form —
verses.7: While positioned upon the highest horizon.
verses.8: Then he drew closer, and came near —
verses.9: Until the distance was no more than two bow-lengths, or even closer still.
verses.10: And he revealed to His servant exactly what he revealed.
verses.11: The heart did not lie about what it saw — perception here trusted completely, no distortion between vision and truth.
verses.12: So will you dispute with him over what he witnessed with his own eyes?
verses.13: And indeed, he saw him again, in another descent —
verses.14: At the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary —
verses.15: Near which lies the Garden of Refuge —
verses.16: When that tree was overwhelmed by something beyond ordinary description.
verses.17: His gaze did not waver, nor did it stray beyond its limit — perfect focus, even in the presence of something overwhelming.
verses.18: He truly witnessed some of the greatest signs of his Lord.
verses.19: So have you considered al-Lat and al-'Uzza —
verses.20: And Manat, that third one besides them? Idols named directly, exposed for exactly what they are.
verses.21: Is it that you have chosen sons for yourselves, and daughters for Him?
verses.22: That, indeed, would be a deeply unjust division.
verses.23: They are nothing but names you and your ancestors invented, for which Allah has sent down no authority at all; they follow only assumption and the desires of their own hearts, even though guidance had already reached them from their Lord.
verses.24: Or does man simply get whatever he happens to wish for?
verses.25: Rather, to Allah belongs both the Hereafter and this first life entirely.
verses.26: And how many angels there are in the heavens, whose intercession is of no use at all, except after Allah grants permission to whom He wills and approves.
verses.27: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter give the angels female names —
verses.28: Though they have no real knowledge of this; they follow nothing but assumption, and assumption can never replace the truth.
verses.29: So turn away from whoever turns his back on Our reminder, desiring nothing but the life of this world.
verses.30: That is the full extent of their knowledge. Indeed, your Lord knows best who has strayed from His path, and knows best who is rightly guided.
verses.31: To Allah belongs everything in the heavens and everything on earth — so that He may repay those who do evil for what they have done, and reward those who do good with what is best —
verses.32: Those who avoid major sins and shameful acts, aside from minor lapses; indeed, your Lord's forgiveness is vast. He knew you best when He formed you from the earth, and when you were still hidden within your mothers' wombs; so do not claim purity for yourselves — He alone knows best who truly holds Him in awe.
verses.33: Have you seen the one who turned away —
verses.34: Giving only a little, then withholding the rest entirely?
verses.35: Does he possess knowledge of the unseen, allowing him to see clearly?
verses.36: Or has he never been told what was written in the scriptures of Moses —
verses.37: And of Abraham, who fulfilled everything asked of him —
verses.38: That no soul bears the burden of another —
verses.39: And that man receives nothing except what he has truly strived for —
verses.40: And that his effort will surely be seen —
verses.41: And that he will be repaid for it with the fullest possible recompense —
verses.42: And that to your Lord is the final destination —
verses.43: And that it is He who causes laughter and causes tears —
verses.44: And that it is He who causes death and grants life —
verses.45: And that He creates the two mates, male and female —
verses.46: From a mere drop when it is placed —
verses.47: And that a second creation rests upon Him as well —
verses.48: And that it is He who enriches, and He who leaves in poverty —
verses.49: And that it is He who is the Lord of Sirius, the brightest star in the sky, once worshipped by some in place of Him —
verses.50: And that it is He who destroyed the earlier people of 'Aad —
verses.51: And Thamud as well, sparing none of them —
verses.52: And the people of Noah before that, who were even more unjust and oppressive still —
verses.53: And the overturned towns, which He cast down completely —
verses.54: Covering them with exactly what covered them.
verses.55: So which, then, of the favors of your Lord do you still doubt?
verses.56: This is a warner cut from the same cloth as every warner who came before him.
verses.57: The Approaching Day draws ever nearer —
verses.58: And no one besides Allah can hold it back.
verses.59: Do you truly find this message so astonishing?
verses.60: That you laugh, instead of weeping —
verses.61: While remaining distracted, indifferent, and proud?
verses.62: So prostrate to Allah, and worship Him — the surah closes with this direct call to submission, the fitting response to everything it has just described.

verses.1: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has split — a sign so extraordinary it seems to announce that time itself is running short.
verses.2: Yet if they see a sign, they turn away and say, this is nothing but passing magic — even the clearest wonder dismissed by hearts already determined to disbelieve.
verses.3: And they denied, following their own desires instead; but every matter has its appointed time of settlement.
verses.4: There has already come to them enough information to serve as a genuine deterrent —
verses.5: Profound wisdom, and yet warning does no good for those unwilling to listen.
verses.6: So leave them be — on the Day the Caller summons them to something dreadful —
verses.7: Their eyes downcast, they will emerge from their graves like scattered locusts —
verses.8: Rushing toward the Caller, while the disbelievers say, this is truly a difficult Day.
verses.9: The people of Noah denied before them; they rejected Our servant, calling him mad, and drove him away with insult.
verses.10: So he called out to his Lord, I am truly overwhelmed — help me.
verses.11: So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring rain —
verses.12: And caused the earth to burst forth with springs, until the waters met exactly as had already been decreed.
verses.13: And We carried him upon a vessel built of planks and nails —
verses.14: Sailing safely under Our watchful care, a reward for the one who had been so cruelly denied.
verses.15: And We left that vessel as a lasting sign — so is there anyone who will take heed?
verses.16: And how severe was My punishment, and how real was My warning.
verses.17: We have truly made this Quran easy to remember — so is there anyone who will take heed?
verses.18: 'Aad also denied — and how severe was My punishment, and how real was My warning.
verses.19: Indeed, We sent upon them a violent, howling wind, on a day of unrelenting misfortune —
verses.20: Tearing people away as though they were uprooted palm trunks.
verses.21: And how severe was My punishment, and how real was My warning.
verses.22: We have truly made this Quran easy to remember — so is there anyone who will take heed?
verses.23: Thamud denied the warning as well —
verses.24: Saying, are we to follow a single human being from among ourselves? That would surely be error and madness —
verses.25: Has the message truly been sent down to him alone, out of all of us? No — he is simply an arrogant liar.
verses.26: They will find out tomorrow exactly who the arrogant liar truly was.
verses.27: Indeed, We sent the she-camel as a test for them, so watch them closely, and be patient.
verses.28: And inform them that the water must be shared between them, each with an appointed turn to drink.
verses.29: But they called upon their companion, and he dared to strike her down.
verses.30: And how severe was My punishment, and how real was My warning.
verses.31: Indeed, We sent upon them a single, devastating blast, and they became like the dry, brittle debris left over from a pen of animals.
verses.32: We have truly made this Quran easy to remember — so is there anyone who will take heed?
verses.33: The people of Lot likewise denied the warning.
verses.34: So We sent upon them a storm of stones, except the family of Lot, whom We rescued before dawn —
verses.35: As a favor directly from Us; this is how We reward whoever is truly grateful.
verses.36: He had already warned them of Our coming assault, but they argued fiercely against the warning.
verses.37: They even demanded his guests from him, so We blinded their eyes, saying, taste now My punishment and My warning.
verses.38: And by morning, a lasting punishment had already overtaken them.
verses.39: So taste My punishment and My warning.
verses.40: We have truly made this Quran easy to remember — so is there anyone who will take heed?
verses.41: And warning came also to the people of Pharaoh —
verses.42: They denied every single one of Our signs, so We seized them with the seizure of One both Mighty and Perfectly Able.
verses.43: Are your own disbelievers somehow better than all of these earlier ones, or do you hold some special immunity written in the scriptures?
verses.44: Or do they say, we are a united force, strong enough to support one another? A confidence about to be tested.
verses.45: That gathered force will soon be defeated, and they will turn and flee.
verses.46: But the true appointment for them is the Hour, and the Hour will be even more devastating, and far more bitter.
verses.47: Indeed, the guilty are lost in error and delusion.
verses.48: On the Day they are dragged into the Fire upon their faces, it will be said, taste now the touch of Saqar.
verses.49: Indeed, We created all things according to a precise decree.
verses.50: And Our command is executed only once, swift as the blink of an eye.
verses.51: And We have already destroyed peoples just like you before — so is there anyone who will take heed?
verses.52: And everything they ever did is recorded in written accounts —
verses.53: Every small deed and every great one, all of it inscribed.
verses.54: Indeed, the righteous will dwell among gardens and rivers —
verses.55: In a seat of true honor, in the closest presence of a Sovereign both Perfect and Supremely Able — the surah closes here, its long history of warnings finally giving way to this quiet, dignified reward.

verses.1: The Most Merciful — the surah opens not with a command or a warning, but with a name, setting the entire tone for everything that follows.
verses.2: Taught the Quran — the very first gift named, revelation itself given before anything else.
verses.3: Created man —
verses.4: And taught him clear, eloquent speech — the ability to express thought, given as its own distinct blessing, separate even from creation itself.
verses.5: The sun and the moon move according to precise calculation —
verses.6: And the stars and the trees bow down in submission — the entire cosmos, animate and inanimate alike, already living in the very posture humans are invited to adopt.
verses.7: And the sky He raised, and set the balance —
verses.8: So that you would not transgress within that balance —
verses.9: So establish weight with justice, and do not skew the balance — cosmic order translated directly into a human responsibility toward fairness.
verses.10: And the earth He laid out for all His creatures —
verses.11: Within it, fruit, and palm trees with sheathed clusters —
verses.12: And grain within husks, and fragrant plants — abundance described in specific, sensory detail, things you could touch and smell.
verses.13: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny? The first appearance of a question that will echo again and again throughout this entire surah, refusing to let any single blessing pass unnoticed.
verses.14: He created man from clay, like that of pottery —
verses.15: And created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire — two very different beginnings, both shaped deliberately.
verses.16: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.17: Lord of the two sunrises, and Lord of the two sunsets — the shifting points on the horizon across the seasons, each one still entirely within His control.
verses.18: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.19: He released two seas, meeting side by side —
verses.20: Yet between them stands a barrier neither one crosses — fresh and salt water flowing close together, never truly merging.
verses.21: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.22: From both of them emerge pearl and coral —
verses.23: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.24: And His are the ships, sailing tall upon the sea like mountains —
verses.25: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.26: Everyone upon the earth will eventually perish —
verses.27: And what remains is the Face of your Lord, full of majesty and honor — the one constant left standing after everything else has faded.
verses.28: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.29: Everyone in the heavens and the earth turns to Him in need; every single day He is engaged in some new matter — never idle, never distant.
verses.30: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.31: We will attend to you, O humanity and jinn together — a direct address, almost intimate in tone, to both realms of accountable creation.
verses.32: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.33: O company of jinn and humankind, if you are able to pass beyond the boundaries of the heavens and the earth, then do so; but you will never pass except by authority granted to you — a boundary no power, human or otherwise, can cross uninvited.
verses.34: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.35: A flame of fire and smoke will be sent upon you, and you will be unable to defend yourselves —
verses.36: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.37: And when the sky splits apart, becoming rose-colored, like molten oil —
verses.38: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.39: On that Day, no human or jinn will need to be asked about their sin — already known, already recorded.
verses.40: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.41: The guilty will be recognized by their very features, and seized by their foreheads and their feet —
verses.42: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.43: This is the Hell that the guilty used to deny —
verses.44: They will move back and forth between it and scalding water, heated beyond endurance.
verses.45: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.46: But for the one who feared standing before his Lord, there are two gardens waiting —
verses.47: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.48: Filled with spreading, leafy branches —
verses.49: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.50: Within both, two flowing springs —
verses.51: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.52: Within both, every kind of fruit, paired —
verses.53: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.54: Reclining upon beds lined with silk brocade, the fruit of both gardens hanging low, within easy reach —
verses.55: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.56: Within them, companions who lower their gaze, untouched before by any man or jinn —
verses.57: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.58: As radiant as rubies and coral —
verses.59: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.60: Is the reward for good anything other than good? A simple, unanswerable truth placed right at the heart of the surah.
verses.61: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.62: And beyond these two gardens, two more gardens still —
verses.63: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.64: Deep green in color, lush beyond ordinary description —
verses.65: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.66: Within both, two springs, gushing forth continuously —
verses.67: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.68: Within both, fruit, and palm trees, and pomegranates —
verses.69: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.70: Within them, companions of beautiful character and beautiful form —
verses.71: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.72: Fair ones, kept close within their pavilions —
verses.73: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.74: Untouched before by any man or jinn —
verses.75: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
verses.76: Reclining upon green cushions, and exquisite, finely woven carpets —
verses.77: So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny? The refrain closing here for the final time, having echoed thirty-one times throughout this entire surah.
verses.78: Blessed is the name of your Lord, full of majesty and honor — the surah ends exactly where it began, returning to that same name of mercy that opened the very first verse.

verses.1: When the Occurrence occurs —
verses.2: There will be no denying it when it comes —
verses.3: It will bring some low, and raise others high — a single event, yet with two entirely opposite effects, depending on who is experiencing it.
verses.4: When the earth is shaken with violent convulsion —
verses.5: And the mountains are crushed and crumbled completely —
verses.6: Becoming scattered dust drifting in the air —
verses.7: And you will be divided into three distinct groups —
verses.8: The companions of the right — and what a fate awaits the companions of the right —
verses.9: And the companions of the left — and what a fate awaits the companions of the left —
verses.10: And the foremost, truly the foremost —
verses.11: Those are the ones brought closest to Allah —
verses.12: In the Gardens of Pleasure —
verses.13: A great many from the earlier peoples —
verses.14: And only a few from the later ones — this foremost rank drawn generously from history, though narrower among those who come after.
verses.15: Upon thrones finely woven —
verses.16: Reclining there, facing one another — companionship built into the very seating.
verses.17: Eternal youths will circulate among them —
verses.18: Carrying vessels, pitchers, and a cup from a flowing spring —
verses.19: Causing no headache, and no loss of clarity — pleasure here without any of its usual costs.
verses.20: And fruit, whatever they choose —
verses.21: And the meat of birds, whatever they desire.
verses.22: And there will be companions with beautiful, wide eyes —
verses.23: Like pearls carefully guarded and preserved —
verses.24: A reward given directly for everything they used to do.
verses.25: They will hear no idle talk there, and no sinful speech —
verses.26: Only the words, peace, peace — an entire atmosphere purified even of small unkindness.
verses.27: And the companions of the right — and what a fate awaits the companions of the right —
verses.28: Among lote trees stripped of their thorns —
verses.29: And banana trees layered heavily with fruit —
verses.30: And shade extended far and wide —
verses.31: And water poured out continuously —
verses.32: And fruit in great abundance —
verses.33: Never out of season, and never withheld —
verses.34: And beds raised high.
verses.35: Indeed, We have formed their companions through a special creation —
verses.36: And made them eternally youthful —
verses.37: Loving and devoted, all of matching age —
verses.38: For the companions of the right —
verses.39: A great many from the earlier peoples —
verses.40: And a great many from the later peoples too — unlike the foremost rank before them, this group draws generously from every era.
verses.41: And the companions of the left — and what a fate awaits the companions of the left —
verses.42: In scorching wind and scalding water —
verses.43: And the shade of thick, black smoke —
verses.44: Neither cool, nor of any comfort at all.
verses.45: Indeed, they had lived before this in excessive luxury —
verses.46: And persisted stubbornly in great sin —
verses.47: And used to say, once we die and become dust and bones, will we truly be raised again?
verses.48: And our forefathers too? Doubt voiced openly, treated as too absurd to take seriously.
verses.49: Say: indeed, the earlier and the later peoples alike —
verses.50: Will all be gathered together for the appointment of a known Day.
verses.51: Then indeed you, the deviators, the deniers —
verses.52: Will surely eat from the tree of Zaqqum —
verses.53: Filling your stomachs with it —
verses.54: And drinking on top of it scalding water —
verses.55: Drinking as desperately as thirsty camels drink.
verses.56: That is their welcome on the Day of Recompense.
verses.57: We created you — so why do you still not believe?
verses.58: Have you considered what you emit —
verses.59: Is it you who creates it, or are We the Creator? A question aimed at the most private, humble beginning of every human life.
verses.60: We have decreed death among you, and nothing can prevent Us —
verses.61: From changing your forms and bringing you back in a shape you do not yet know.
verses.62: And you already know the first creation — so will you not take heed?
verses.63: Have you considered what you plant in the soil —
verses.64: Is it you who makes it grow, or are We the grower?
verses.65: If We willed, We could reduce it to dry, useless debris, leaving you in astonishment —
verses.66: Saying, we are now in debt —
verses.67: Or rather, we have simply been deprived — dependence on a harvest suddenly revealed for what it always was.
verses.68: Have you considered the water you drink —
verses.69: Is it you who brought it down from the clouds, or are We the One who sends it down?
verses.70: If We willed, We could make it bitter — so why are you not grateful?
verses.71: Have you considered the fire that you kindle —
verses.72: Is it you who produced the tree that fuels it, or are We the producer?
verses.73: We made it a reminder, and a provision for travelers —
verses.74: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.
verses.75: So I swear by the positions of the setting stars —
verses.76: And indeed, that is truly a great oath, if only you understood.
verses.77: Indeed, it is a noble Quran —
verses.78: Preserved within a well-guarded Record —
verses.79: Touched only by those who are purified.
verses.80: A revelation from the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.81: Is it this message, then, that you treat with such indifference?
verses.82: And do you turn your gratitude for your provision into denial instead?
verses.83: So why is it, when the soul reaches the throat at the moment of death —
verses.84: And you are right there, watching —
verses.85: And We are nearer to that person than you are, though you cannot see it —
verses.86: Why is it, if you truly believe you are not accountable —
verses.87: That you cannot simply bring the soul back, if you are telling the truth? A challenge that exposes, quietly and completely, the limits of every claim to independence.
verses.88: And if the one who died was among those brought near to Allah —
verses.89: Then for him is rest, and abundance, and a garden of pleasure.
verses.90: And if he was among the companions of the right —
verses.91: It will be said, peace be upon you, for you are among the companions of the right.
verses.92: But if he was among the deniers who strayed —
verses.93: Then his welcome is scalding water —
verses.94: And burning in the blazing Fire.
verses.95: Indeed, this is the absolute, undeniable truth.
verses.96: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great — the surah closes exactly as its earlier movement did, returning attention one final time to the greatness that stands behind every single verse just spoken.

verses.1: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.2: To Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; He gives life and causes death, and He is capable of all things.
verses.3: He is the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden; and He has full knowledge of all things — a description that stretches across every possible boundary of time and visibility.
verses.4: It is He who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself above the Throne. He knows what enters the earth and what emerges from it, what descends from the sky and what ascends to it; and He is with you wherever you are. Allah sees clearly everything you do.
verses.5: To Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah all matters return.
verses.6: He causes the night to merge into the day, and the day to merge into the night; and He knows fully what lies within every chest.
verses.7: Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and spend from that which He has made you trustees over; for those among you who believe and spend, there is a great reward.
verses.8: And what excuse do you have for not believing in Allah, when the Messenger invites you to believe in your Lord, and He has already taken your covenant, if you are truly believers?
verses.9: It is He who sends down upon His servant clear, illuminating verses, so that He might bring you out of darkness and into light; and indeed, Allah is Kind and Merciful toward you.
verses.10: And why would you not spend in the cause of Allah, when to Allah belongs the entire inheritance of the heavens and the earth? Those who spent and fought before the great victory are not equal to those who did so afterward; the earlier ones hold a higher rank, though Allah has promised good to both. And Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.11: Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan, so that He may multiply it for him, and grant him a generous reward?
verses.12: On the Day you see the believing men and believing women, their light moving ahead of them and to their right, it will be said, good news for you today — gardens beneath which rivers flow, where you will remain forever; that is the great attainment.
verses.13: On that same Day, the hypocrite men and women will say to the believers, wait for us, so we might borrow some of your light; but it will be said, go back, and search for your own light. Then a wall will rise between them, with a gate — mercy on the inner side, and torment on the outer.
verses.14: They will call out, were we not with you? The believers will answer, yes, but you allowed yourselves to be tempted, hesitated, and doubted, and false hopes deceived you until Allah's command finally arrived, and the Deceiver deceived you concerning Allah —
verses.15: So today, no ransom will be accepted from you, nor from the disbelievers; the Fire is your only refuge — it truly suits you, and what a wretched destination it is.
verses.16: Has the time not yet come for the believers, that their hearts should soften at the remembrance of Allah and the truth He has revealed? And let them not become like those given scripture before, whose hearts hardened after too long a time had passed, leaving many of them defiantly disobedient.
verses.17: Know that Allah revives the earth after it has lain lifeless; We have made Our signs clear to you, so that you might understand.
verses.18: Indeed, charitable men and charitable women, who lend Allah a good loan, will have it multiplied for them, and theirs is a generous reward.
verses.19: And those who believe in Allah and His messengers — they are the truthful ones, and the witnesses, in the sight of their Lord; for them is their reward and their light. But those who disbelieved and denied Our verses — they are the companions of the blazing Fire.
verses.20: Know that the life of this world is merely play, amusement, adornment, boasting among yourselves, and competition in wealth and children — like rain that produces vegetation admired by farmers, then it withers, turns yellow, and crumbles into debris; while in the Hereafter is severe punishment, and forgiveness from Allah, and His approval. And what is the life of this world, except the enjoyment of illusion?
verses.21: Race toward forgiveness from your Lord, and a garden as wide as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who believed in Allah and His messengers; that is the favor of Allah, granted to whom He wills, and Allah is the possessor of great favor.
verses.22: No calamity strikes the earth or yourselves except that it was already recorded before We brought it into being; indeed, that is easy for Allah —
verses.23: So that you do not despair over what you have lost, nor grow overly proud over what you have been given; and Allah does not love the self-satisfied boaster —
verses.24: Those who are stingy and encourage others toward stinginess; and whoever turns away, then indeed, Allah is Free of need, Praiseworthy.
verses.25: We have sent Our messengers with clear evidence, and sent down with them the Scripture and the balance, so that people might uphold justice; and We sent down iron, containing great strength and benefit for people, so that Allah might make known who truly supports Him and His messengers, even unseen; indeed, Allah is Powerful and Exalted in Might.
verses.26: We had already sent Noah and Abraham, and placed prophethood and scripture among their descendants; some among them were guided, but many were defiantly disobedient.
verses.27: Then We sent Our messengers, one after another, following in their footsteps, and followed them with Jesus, son of Mary, giving him the Gospel; and We placed compassion and mercy in the hearts of those who followed him. As for monasticism, they invented it themselves — We had not prescribed it for them, except that they sought Allah's approval through it, though they did not observe it as they should have. So We gave the believers among them their reward, but many of them remained defiantly disobedient.
verses.28: O you who believe, hold awe of Allah and believe in His Messenger; He will grant you a double share of His mercy, provide you with a light to walk by, and forgive you; and Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.29: This is so that the People of the Scripture may know they have no power over Allah's favor at all, and that all favor rests entirely in Allah's hand, given to whomever He wills; and Allah is the possessor of great favor — the surah closes by tracing every act of charity, every ounce of iron's strength, and every scripture ever sent, back to this single, generous source.

verses.1: Allah has truly heard the words of the woman who pleaded with you concerning her husband, directing her complaint to Allah; and Allah hears the exchange between you both — indeed, Allah is Hearing and Seeing, attentive even to a single private conversation that felt, to her, unheard by anyone else.
verses.2: Those among you who liken their wives to their mothers in order to separate from them — their wives do not truly become their mothers; only those who gave birth to them hold that title. What they say is objectionable and false; yet Allah is Pardoning and Forgiving.
verses.3: Those who make such a statement and then wish to take it back must free a slave before they touch one another again; this is what you are instructed to do, and Allah is fully aware of what you do.
verses.4: And whoever cannot find a slave to free must fast two consecutive months before touching one another; and whoever is unable to fast must feed sixty needy people. This is so that you may hold complete faith in Allah and His Messenger; these are the limits set by Allah, and for the disbelievers is a painful punishment.
verses.5: Indeed, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger are brought low, just as those before them were brought low; We have already sent down clear verses, and for the disbelievers is a humiliating punishment —
verses.6: On the Day Allah will raise them all and inform them of everything they did; Allah has kept a full record, though they themselves have forgotten it, and Allah witnesses all things.
verses.7: Have you not considered that Allah knows everything in the heavens and on the earth? No private conversation between three occurs without Him being the fourth, nor between five without Him being the sixth, nor fewer or more without Him being present with them, wherever they are; then He will inform them of what they did, on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, Allah has knowledge of all things — no whisper, however private, ever truly escapes His awareness.
verses.8: Have you not seen those who were forbidden from secret conversation, yet returned to what they were forbidden, whispering about sin, hostility, and disobedience toward the Messenger? And when they come to you, they greet you in a way Allah never greets you, saying among themselves, why does Allah not punish us for what we say? Hell is sufficient for them; they will burn in it, and how wretched a destination.
verses.9: O you who believe, when you converse privately, do not speak of sin, hostility, or disobedience to the Messenger, but speak of righteousness and piety instead; and hold awe of Allah, to whom you will all be gathered.
verses.10: Private, harmful conversation comes only from Satan, meant to grieve the believers, though it can never harm them at all except by Allah's permission; so let the believers place their trust firmly in Allah.
verses.11: O you who believe, when you are told to make room in gatherings, then make room, and Allah will make room for you; and when you are told to rise, then rise, and Allah will raise those among you who believe, and those given knowledge, in status; and Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.12: O you who believe, when you wish to speak privately with the Messenger, offer some charity beforehand; that is better and purer for you. But if you find nothing to give, then indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.13: Were you afraid to offer charity before your private consultations? Since you did not, and Allah has forgiven you, then at least establish prayer, give charity, and obey Allah and His Messenger; and Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.14: Have you not seen those who take as allies a people with whom Allah is angry? They belong to neither side truly, and they swear to falsehood while knowing it is false.
verses.15: Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment; truly, what they have been doing is evil.
verses.16: They have used their oaths as a shield, turning others away from the path of Allah; for them is a humiliating punishment.
verses.17: Neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against Allah; they are the companions of the Fire, remaining there forever.
verses.18: On the Day Allah raises them all, they will swear to Him just as they once swore to you, thinking it will help them; but truly, they are the liars.
verses.19: Satan has overpowered them, causing them to forget the remembrance of Allah; they are the party of Satan, and truly, the party of Satan are the ultimate losers.
verses.20: Indeed, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger will be among the most disgraced.
verses.21: Allah has decreed: I will surely prevail, I and My messengers; indeed, Allah is Powerful and Exalted in Might.
verses.22: You will not find a people who truly believe in Allah and the Last Day showing affection to those who oppose Allah and His Messenger, even if they were their own fathers, sons, brothers, or kindred. For these, Allah has written faith into their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Himself; He will admit them into gardens beneath which rivers flow, remaining there forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him — these are the party of Allah, and truly, the party of Allah are the successful ones.

verses.1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.2: It is He who drove out the disbelievers among the People of the Scripture from their homes at the very first expulsion. You never imagined they would leave, and they were certain their fortresses would shield them from Allah; but Allah's decree reached them from a direction they never expected, casting terror into their hearts, so that they destroyed their own houses with their own hands, alongside the hands of the believers. So take heed, you who possess insight.
verses.3: And had Allah not decreed exile for them, He would have punished them severely in this world; and in the Hereafter awaits the punishment of the Fire.
verses.4: That was because they opposed Allah and His Messenger; and whoever opposes Allah — indeed, Allah is severe in punishment.
verses.5: Whatever palm trees you cut down, or left standing, was done by Allah's permission, so that He might disgrace those who persisted in defiance.
verses.6: And whatever Allah restored to His Messenger from them, you did not need to spur any horse or camel for it; rather, Allah grants His messengers authority over whomever He wills, and Allah is capable of all things.
verses.7: And whatever Allah restored to His Messenger from the people of these towns belongs to Allah, and to the Messenger, and to close relatives, orphans, the needy, and travelers — so that wealth does not simply circulate among the rich alone. Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; whatever he forbids you, refrain from it; and hold awe of Allah, for Allah is severe in punishment.
verses.8: This is for the poor emigrants, driven from their homes and their possessions, seeking Allah's favor and approval, and supporting Allah and His Messenger — these are the truthful ones.
verses.9: And also for those already settled in the city, who had embraced faith before them; they love those who emigrate to them, feel no envy in their hearts for what the emigrants were given, and prefer them over themselves, even amid their own hardship. Whoever is shielded from the greed of his own soul — those are the ones who truly succeed.
verses.10: And for those who came after them, saying, our Lord, forgive us, and our brothers who preceded us in faith, and leave no resentment in our hearts toward those who believed; our Lord, You are truly Kind and Merciful — three generations of believers, each honored in turn, each praying for the ones before them.
verses.11: Have you not seen the hypocrites, who say to their disbelieving companions among the People of the Scripture, if you are expelled, we will surely leave with you, and we will never obey anyone against you; and if you are fought, we will surely help you? Yet Allah bears witness that they are liars.
verses.12: If they are expelled, the hypocrites will not leave with them; and if they are fought, they will not help them; and even if they tried to help, they would surely turn and flee, leaving them utterly unsupported.
verses.13: You believers strike more fear into their hearts than Allah does; that is because they are a people who simply do not understand.
verses.14: They will not fight you together except from within fortified towns or from behind walls; their hostility among themselves is severe. You would think them united, but their hearts are deeply divided; that is because they are a people without reason.
verses.15: Their example is like those shortly before them, who tasted the bitter consequence of their conduct, and a painful punishment still awaits them.
verses.16: Like Satan, who tells a person, disbelieve — and once he disbelieves, Satan says, I am done with you; truly, I fear Allah, Lord of the worlds — abandonment offered as the final reward for following such counsel.
verses.17: So the outcome for both of them is the Fire, remaining there forever; and that is the just reward of the wrongdoers.
verses.18: O you who believe, hold awe of Allah, and let every soul consider carefully what it has prepared for tomorrow; hold awe of Allah, for Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.19: And do not be like those who forgot Allah, so He caused them to forget themselves; those are the truly defiant ones.
verses.20: The companions of the Fire and the companions of Paradise are never equal; the companions of Paradise are the true achievers.
verses.21: Had We sent this Quran down upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled, breaking apart out of awe of Allah; such examples We give to people, so that they might reflect.
verses.22: He is Allah, besides whom there is no god, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed; He is the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
verses.23: He is Allah, besides whom there is no god, the Sovereign, the Pure, the Source of Peace, the Granter of Security, the Overseer, the Exalted in Might, the Compeller, the Supreme; exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.
verses.24: He is Allah, the Creator, the Originator, the Fashioner; to Him belong the most beautiful names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise — the surah closes with this cascade of names, each one adding a fresh dimension to who He truly is.

verses.1: O you who believe, do not take My enemies and your enemies as close allies, offering them affection, when they have rejected the very truth that came to you, and even drove out the Prophet and yourselves simply for believing in Allah, your Lord. If you have truly come out striving in My cause, seeking My approval, do not secretly befriend them; I know fully what you conceal and what you reveal, and whoever does this has strayed from the right way.
verses.2: If they were to gain the upper hand over you, they would treat you as enemies, reaching out against you with harmful hands and tongues, wishing above all that you would abandon your faith.
verses.3: Neither your relatives nor your children will benefit you at all on the Day of Resurrection; He will judge between you, and Allah sees clearly everything you do.
verses.4: There is indeed an excellent example for you in Abraham and those with him, when they said to their people, we are truly free of you and of whatever you worship besides Allah; we reject you, and enmity and hatred have appeared between us until you believe in Allah alone — except for Abraham's words to his father, I will surely ask forgiveness for you, though I hold no power to help you against Allah at all. They said, our Lord, upon You we rely, and to You we turn, and to You is our final return —
verses.5: Our Lord, do not make us a trial for the disbelievers, and forgive us, our Lord; indeed, You are the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.6: There truly is an excellent example in them, for anyone whose hope rests in Allah and the Last Day; and whoever turns away, then indeed Allah is Free of need, Praiseworthy.
verses.7: Perhaps Allah will place affection between you and those you now consider enemies; Allah is capable of all things, and Allah is Forgiving and Merciful — hostility itself framed here as something that can dissolve, given time and divine will.
verses.8: Allah does not forbid you from being kind and just toward those who have not fought you over religion, nor driven you from your homes; indeed, Allah loves those who act justly.
verses.9: Allah only forbids you from allying closely with those who fought you over religion, drove you from your homes, and helped in your expulsion; whoever allies with them is truly among the wrongdoers.
verses.10: O you who believe, when believing women come to you as emigrants, examine their sincerity — Allah knows best their true faith. If you find them to be genuine believers, do not return them to the disbelievers; marriage is no longer lawful between them. Return what was spent on them, and there is no blame in marrying them once their due compensation is given. And do not hold on to marriage with disbelieving women; ask back what was spent, and let them do the same. This is the judgment of Allah between you, and He is Knowing and Wise.
verses.11: And if any of your wives leave you for the disbelievers, and you later gain something in return, give those whose wives left the equivalent of what they had spent; and hold awe of Allah, in whom you believe.
verses.12: O Prophet, when believing women come pledging that they will not associate anything with Allah, nor steal, nor commit unlawful relations, nor kill their children, nor bring forth slander invented between their hands and feet, nor disobey you in what is right — accept their pledge, and seek forgiveness for them; indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
verses.13: O you who believe, do not ally yourselves with a people with whom Allah has become angry; they have lost all hope in the Hereafter, just as the disbelievers have lost hope regarding those already in their graves — the surah closes on this stark image of despair, contrasted against the hope offered throughout to every sincere believer.

verses.1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.2: O you who believe, why do you say what you do not do? A direct, uncomfortable question aimed squarely at the gap between words and action.
verses.3: It is deeply hateful in the sight of Allah that you say what you do not do — sincerity demanded here, not mere declaration.
verses.4: Indeed, Allah loves those who fight in His cause in ranks, standing together as though they were a single, solid structure — unity so tight it becomes indistinguishable from strength itself.
verses.5: And remember when Moses said to his people, O my people, why do you harm me, when you already know that I am truly the messenger of Allah sent to you? And when they deviated, Allah let their hearts deviate further still; and Allah does not guide a people who persist in defiance.
verses.6: And remember when Jesus, son of Mary, said, O children of Israel, I am truly the messenger of Allah sent to you, confirming what came before me in the Torah, and bringing good news of a messenger to come after me whose name is Ahmad — yet when he came to them with clear evidence, they said, this is plainly magic.
verses.7: And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah while being invited toward Islam? And Allah does not guide a wrongdoing people.
verses.8: They wish to extinguish the light of Allah with their own mouths, but Allah will perfect His light, no matter how much the disbelievers dislike it — an image almost absurd in its imbalance, human breath attempting to blow out something no wind could ever touch.
verses.9: It is He who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth, to make it prevail over every other way of life, no matter how much those who associate partners with Allah dislike it.
verses.10: O you who believe, shall I guide you to a transaction that will save you from a painful punishment?
verses.11: That you believe in Allah and His Messenger, and strive in the cause of Allah with your wealth and your very lives — that is best for you, if only you knew it.
verses.12: He will forgive you your sins, and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow, and into pleasant dwellings within gardens of eternal residence; that is the great attainment.
verses.13: And another favor you will love — help from Allah, and a near, coming victory; so give good news to the believers.
verses.14: O you who believe, be supporters of Allah, just as Jesus, son of Mary, said to the disciples, who are my supporters in the cause of Allah? The disciples answered, we are Allah's supporters. And a group of the Children of Israel believed, while another group disbelieved, so We supported the believers against their enemy, and they became triumphant — the surah closes on this note of resolve, calling this community to the same steadfast loyalty once shown by the disciples before them.

verses.1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah, the Sovereign, the Pure, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.2: It is He who sent among the unlettered people a Messenger from among themselves, reciting to them His verses, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom — even though before this they had been clearly astray.
verses.3: And also to others who have not yet joined them — this message reaching far beyond its first audience, extended generously to every generation still to come. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
verses.4: That is the favor of Allah, which He grants to whom He wills; and Allah is the possessor of immense favor.
verses.5: The example of those entrusted with the Torah, who then failed to uphold it, is like that of a donkey carrying volumes of books — burdened with knowledge it can never truly grasp. How wretched is the example of a people who deny the signs of Allah; and Allah does not guide a wrongdoing people.
verses.6: Say: O you who are Jews, if you claim to be the close allies of Allah, exclusive of all other people, then wish for death, if you are being truthful —
verses.7: But they will never wish for it, because of what their own hands have already sent forth; and Allah knows well who the wrongdoers are.
verses.8: Say: indeed, the death you flee from will surely meet you regardless; then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, and He will inform you of everything you used to do.
verses.9: O you who believe, when the call to prayer is made on the day of Jumu'ah, hasten toward the remembrance of Allah and leave off your trading; that is better for you, if only you knew.
verses.10: And when the prayer has ended, disperse throughout the land and seek from the bounty of Allah, and remember Allah often, so that you may succeed.
verses.11: But when they saw some trade or entertainment, they rushed toward it and left you standing there. Say: what is with Allah is far better than entertainment or trade, and Allah is the best of providers — the surah closes with this quiet, pointed correction, redirecting attention back to what truly matters most.

verses.1: When the hypocrites come to you, they say, we testify that you are truly the Messenger of Allah; and Allah knows that you are indeed His Messenger, but Allah also testifies that the hypocrites are liars — their words technically correct, yet spoken without any real belief behind them.
verses.2: They have used their oaths as a shield, turning others away from the path of Allah; truly, what they have been doing is evil.
verses.3: That is because they believed, and then disbelieved, so their hearts were sealed shut, leaving them unable to truly understand.
verses.4: And when you see them, their appearance impresses you, and when they speak, you find yourself listening; yet they are like propped-up pieces of wood — hollow inside despite the polished exterior. They imagine every shout is aimed at them; they are the true enemy, so be wary of them. May Allah destroy them — how deceived they truly are.
verses.5: And when it is said to them, come, so that the Messenger of Allah may ask forgiveness for you, they turn their heads away, and you see them retreating in arrogance.
verses.6: It makes no difference whether you ask forgiveness for them or not; Allah will never forgive them. Indeed, Allah does not guide a people who persist in defiance.
verses.7: They are the ones who say, do not spend anything on those who follow the Messenger of Allah, so that they will eventually scatter and leave. Yet to Allah belong the treasures of the heavens and the earth — but the hypocrites simply do not understand.
verses.8: They say, if we return to the city, the more powerful among us will surely drive out the weaker. Yet honor truly belongs to Allah, and to His Messenger, and to the believers — but the hypocrites do not know this.
verses.9: O you who believe, do not let your wealth or your children distract you from remembering Allah; and whoever allows that — those are the ones who have truly lost.
verses.10: And spend from what We have provided you, before death approaches one of you and he cries out, my Lord, if only You would delay me a little longer, so I could give charity and become one of the righteous —
verses.11: But Allah will never delay a soul once its appointed time has arrived; and Allah is fully aware of everything you do — the surah closes on this sobering certainty, a plea for extra time that always arrives too late.

verses.1: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah; to Him belongs all dominion, and to Him belongs all praise, and He is capable of all things.
verses.2: It is He who created you, and among you is the disbeliever, and among you is the believer; and Allah sees clearly everything you do — the same act of creation producing both outcomes, the difference resting entirely in the choices made afterward.
verses.3: He created the heavens and earth in truth, and shaped you, giving your forms the finest proportion; and to Him is your final return.
verses.4: He knows what lies within the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal; and Allah knows fully what is within every chest.
verses.5: Has the news not reached you of those who disbelieved before? They tasted the bitter consequence of their conduct, and a painful punishment still awaits them.
verses.6: That was because their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they said, shall mere human beings guide us? So they disbelieved and turned away, and Allah had no need of them; Allah is Free of need, and Praiseworthy.
verses.7: Those who disbelieve claim they will never be raised again. Say: yes, by my Lord, you will surely be raised, then you will surely be informed of everything you did; and that is easy for Allah.
verses.8: So believe in Allah, and His Messenger, and the light We have sent down; and Allah is fully aware of everything you do.
verses.9: On the Day He gathers you for the Day of Gathering — that is the Day of Mutual Loss and Gain. And whoever believes in Allah and acts righteously, He will erase his misdeeds and admit him into gardens beneath which rivers flow, to remain there forever; that is the great attainment.
verses.10: But those who disbelieved and denied Our verses — they are the companions of the Fire, remaining there eternally; and how wretched a destination.
verses.11: No calamity strikes except by Allah's permission; and whoever believes in Allah, He guides his heart. And Allah has full knowledge of all things.
verses.12: So obey Allah, and obey the Messenger; but if you turn away, then upon Our Messenger rests only the duty of clear delivery.
verses.13: Allah — there is no god except Him; and upon Allah, let the believers place their trust.
verses.14: O you who believe, indeed among your spouses and your children are some who are enemies to you, so be cautious of them; but if you pardon, overlook, and forgive, then indeed Allah is Forgiving and Merciful — a striking, honest acknowledgment that even the closest relationships can pull a person away from what matters most.
verses.15: Your wealth and your children are simply a trial, and with Allah is a far greater reward.
verses.16: So hold awe of Allah as much as you are able, and listen, and obey, and spend generously; it is better for your own souls. And whoever is shielded from the greed of his own soul — those are the ones who truly succeed.
verses.17: If you lend Allah a good loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you; and Allah is Most Appreciative, Most Forbearing —
verses.18: Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Wise — the surah closes on this final, complete description, tying together everything already said about His knowledge and His generosity.

verses.1: O Prophet, when you divorce women, do so at the start of their waiting period, and count that period carefully, and hold awe of Allah, your Lord; do not remove them from their homes, nor should they leave, unless they commit a clear wrong. These are the limits set by Allah, and whoever crosses them has truly wronged himself; you never know — perhaps Allah will bring about a change in circumstances afterward.
verses.2: And when they approach the end of their term, either keep them honorably or part with them honorably, and call two just witnesses from among yourselves, and establish that testimony for the sake of Allah. This is instructed to whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day; and whoever holds awe of Allah, He will make a way out for him —
verses.3: And will provide for him from directions he never expected. And whoever places his trust in Allah, He is enough for him; indeed, Allah accomplishes what He intends. Allah has already set a measure for everything.
verses.4: As for women who no longer expect menstruation, if there is uncertainty, their waiting period is three months, and the same applies to those who have not yet menstruated; and for those who are pregnant, their term ends when they give birth. And whoever holds awe of Allah, He will make his affairs easy for him.
verses.5: That is the command of Allah, sent down to you; and whoever holds awe of Allah, He will erase his misdeeds and greatly increase his reward.
verses.6: House them wherever you yourselves live, according to your means, and do not harm them in order to make things difficult for them. If they are pregnant, spend on them until they give birth; and if they nurse the child for you, give them their due payment, and consult together in fairness; but if you reach an impasse, then let another woman nurse the child instead.
verses.7: Let a person of means spend according to his means, and let one whose provision is limited spend from what Allah has given him; Allah does not burden any soul beyond what He has given it. Allah will bring ease after hardship.
verses.8: And how many a town defied the command of its Lord and His messengers, so We called it to a severe account and punished it with a terrible punishment —
verses.9: It tasted the full weight of its own conduct, and the outcome of its affair was nothing but loss.
verses.10: Allah has prepared for such people a severe punishment; so hold awe of Allah, you who possess understanding and have believed. Allah has sent down to you a reminder —
verses.11: A Messenger, reciting to you the clear verses of Allah, so that he may bring those who believe and do righteous deeds out of darkness and into light. And whoever believes in Allah and acts righteously, He will admit him into gardens beneath which rivers flow, to remain there forever; Allah will have granted him the finest provision.
verses.12: It is Allah who created seven heavens, and of the earth, their equal; His command descends among them, so that you may know Allah is capable of all things, and that Allah encompasses everything in knowledge — the surah closes by widening its lens from personal matters of marriage and family all the way out to the scale of the entire universe, both governed by the same careful hand.

verses.1: O Prophet, why do you forbid yourself something Allah has made lawful for you, seeking only to please your wives? And Allah is Forgiving and Merciful — a gentle correction, addressed even to a prophet, showing that good intentions still need to align with what is actually required.
verses.2: Allah has already prescribed for you the way to release yourselves from such oaths; and Allah is your protector, the Knowing, the Wise — release offered here as mercy, not as failure.
verses.3: And remember when the Prophet confided something to one of his wives, and when she shared it further, and Allah made this known to him, he revealed part of it and withheld part; and when he confronted her, she asked, who told you this? He said, the Knowing, the Well-Acquainted told me — nothing said in confidence escapes the awareness of the One who knows all things.
verses.4: If you two turn to Allah in repentance, your hearts have indeed inclined that way; but if you support one another against him, then indeed Allah is his protector, and Gabriel, and the righteous among the believers, and the angels besides, all standing behind him as support.
verses.5: Perhaps his Lord, were he to divorce you all, would grant him wives even better than you — devoted, believing, obedient, repentant, worshipful, and reflective, both previously married and untouched — a reminder that no one is indispensable to the mission itself.
verses.6: O you who believe, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, watched over by angels stern and severe, who never disobey what Allah commands them, and who carry out exactly what they are ordered — a call to guard not just oneself but everyone under one's care.
verses.7: O you who disbelieved, make no excuses on that Day; you will only be repaid for exactly what you used to do — accountability arriving finally without room for negotiation.
verses.8: O you who believe, turn to Allah in sincere repentance; perhaps your Lord will erase your misdeeds and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow, on a Day when Allah will not disgrace the Prophet or those who believed alongside him. Their light will move ahead of them and beside them, and they will say, our Lord, perfect our light for us and forgive us; indeed, You are capable of all things — a scene of light literally guiding believers forward, their own prayer woven into that very light.
verses.9: O Prophet, strive earnestly against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be firm with them; their refuge is Hell, and how wretched a destination it is.
verses.10: Allah presents an example of those who disbelieved: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot — married to two of Our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them, and neither prophet's righteousness could shield them at all from Allah; it was said to them, enter the Fire along with everyone else who enters — proof that closeness to righteousness offers no protection without genuine belief of one's own.
verses.11: And Allah presents an example of those who believed: the wife of Pharaoh, who said, my Lord, build for me a home near You in Paradise, and save me from Pharaoh and his deeds, and save me from this wrongdoing people — belief flourishing even inside the household of the cruelest tyrant.
verses.12: And Mary, daughter of Imran, who guarded her chastity completely, so We breathed into her from Our Spirit, and she believed in the words of her Lord and His scriptures, and was among the truly devoted — the surah closing on two women, one married to power, one raised in purity, both united simply by sincere faith.

verses.1: Blessed is He in whose hand rests all dominion — every kingdom, every authority ever claimed by a human ruler is borrowed, ultimately held by One whose power over all things needs no permission from anyone.
verses.2: He who created death and life, to test which of you does best in deed — existence itself framed here as a proving ground, not a random accident, with mercy and might held together in the same hand.
verses.3: Who created seven heavens, layer upon layer — look again at the sky; you will find no flaw, no crack, no inconsistency in its design.
verses.4: Look once more, then again — your gaze will return to you defeated, exhausted, unable to find the failure it was searching for.
verses.5: And We have adorned the nearest sky with stars, some of them hurled at the devils who try to eavesdrop, a punishment of blazing fire prepared for them —
verses.6: And for those who reject their Lord is the punishment of Hell — a wretched destination for a wasted opportunity.
verses.7: When they are thrown into it, they will hear its terrifying inhale as it boils and rages —
verses.8: It nearly bursts apart from fury. Every time a group is thrown in, its keepers ask them, did no warner ever come to you?
verses.9: They will answer, yes, a warner came, but we denied him and said, Allah has sent down nothing; you are simply mistaken — arrogance recalled here in its own words, exposed at the worst possible moment.
verses.10: And they will say, if only we had truly listened, or truly reasoned, we would not be here among the companions of the Blaze — regret arriving far too late to matter.
verses.11: So they will confess their sin openly — and it is only isolation now, for those who once had every chance.
verses.12: But indeed, those who hold awe of their Lord even when unseen will have forgiveness, and a great reward — faith proven not in public, but in private moments no one else witnesses.
verses.13: Whisper your words quietly, or speak them aloud — it makes no difference; He knows fully what lies within every chest.
verses.14: Should He who created not know? He is the Subtle, the perfectly Aware, missing nothing however hidden it seems.
verses.15: It is He who made the earth manageable for you — so walk across its expanses and eat from what He provides, for to Him is the return of every soul.
verses.16: Do you feel entirely safe that the One in the heaven could not cause the earth to swallow you whole, shaking suddenly beneath your feet?
verses.17: Or do you feel safe that He could not send a storm of stones down upon you? Then you would truly understand the weight of the warning you once dismissed.
verses.18: Those before them denied as well — and how severe was the consequence that followed their denial.
verses.19: Do they not see the birds above them, wings spread, then folding in? Nothing holds them aloft except the Most Merciful — He alone sees every detail of their flight.
verses.20: Or who is it that could serve as an army to defend you, other than the Most Merciful? The disbelievers exist only in delusion, trusting in strength that was never truly theirs.
verses.21: Or who could provide for you if He withheld His provision entirely? Yet they persist in arrogance, turning away regardless.
verses.22: Is the one who stumbles forward, face down, better guided — or the one who walks upright on a straight path? The contrast could not be clearer, and yet many still choose to stumble.
verses.23: Say: it is He who produced you, and gave you hearing, sight, and hearts — yet how little gratitude you show for it.
verses.24: Say: it is He who scattered you across the earth, and to Him you will all be gathered once more.
verses.25: And they ask, when will this promise come, if you are telling the truth?
verses.26: Say: that knowledge belongs to Allah alone — I am only sent to warn you plainly.
verses.27: But when they finally see it approaching, the faces of the disbelievers will fall, and it will be said, this is exactly what you used to demand.
