verses.28: Say: consider this — whether Allah causes my death, and those with me, or shows us mercy, who could ever protect the disbelievers from a painful punishment?
verses.29: Say: He is the Most Merciful; we have believed in Him, and placed our trust entirely in Him — soon you will know who has truly been in error.
verses.30: Say: consider this too — if your water were to sink completely into the earth, who could bring you flowing water again? The surah closes on this humbling question, tracing every comfort back to a source none of us actually control.

verses.1: Nun. By the pen, and by everything it writes — an oath sworn on the simplest tool of knowledge, ink flowing across a page, carrying truth forward long after any single voice falls silent.
verses.2: By the grace of your Lord, you are not a madman — a direct, personal reassurance, answering an accusation thrown carelessly at someone whose character told an entirely different story.
verses.3: And indeed, for you is a reward that will never be cut off — patience under insult repaid with something lasting far beyond the insult itself.
verses.4: And indeed, you are of an exalted moral character — not just correct belief, but a character so refined it becomes its own evidence.
verses.5: So you will see, and they will see —
verses.6: Which of you is truly afflicted with madness — the accusation, thrown so confidently, will eventually reveal exactly whose judgment was actually clouded.
verses.7: Indeed, your Lord knows best who has strayed from His path, and He knows best who is rightly guided — the true measure resting with Him, not with loud accusation.
verses.8: So do not give in to the deniers —
verses.9: They wish you would compromise, so that they too might soften toward you — a tempting offer, mutual ease traded for truth quietly diluted.
verses.10: And do not obey any worthless, habitual oath-swearer —
verses.11: A scorner, circulating malicious gossip from place to place —
verses.12: A blocker of good, one who oversteps every boundary, sinful through and through —
verses.13: Harsh, coarse, and beyond that, of illegitimate standing — a portrait built verse by verse, each trait stacking onto the last.
verses.14: All because he possesses wealth and children — status mistaken for permission to behave however he pleases.
verses.15: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, tales of the ancients — dismissal offered instead of honest reflection.
verses.16: We will brand him clearly upon the snout — a mark of disgrace, permanent and unmistakable, matching the arrogance that earned it.
verses.17: Indeed, We tested them just as We tested the owners of the garden — when they swore to harvest its fruit early in the morning, before anyone else could benefit —
verses.18: Without ever saying, if Allah wills — confidence in their own plan, with no room left for humility or dependence.
verses.19: So a devastating blight came upon that garden from your Lord, while they lay sleeping —
verses.20: And by morning it looked as though it had been stripped completely bare —
verses.21: And they called out to one another at dawn —
verses.22: Saying, go early to your harvest, if you truly mean to cut the fruit —
verses.23: So they set out, whispering quietly among themselves —
verses.24: Making sure no poor person would enter it today — the very selfishness that had motivated their secret plan spoken openly among themselves.
verses.25: And they went early, determined, believing themselves fully capable of pulling it off —
verses.26: But when they saw it, they said, surely we have lost our way —
verses.27: No — rather, we have been utterly deprived — the shock of loss slowly turning into recognition.
verses.28: The most reasonable among them said, did I not tell you, why do you not glorify your Lord? A single voice of restraint, ignored earlier, proven right too late.
verses.29: They said, glory be to our Lord — indeed, we were truly wrongdoers.
verses.30: Then they turned toward one another, blaming each other for what had happened —
verses.31: They said, woe to us — we were indeed transgressors —
verses.32: Perhaps our Lord will grant us something better than this in its place; truly, we turn to Him with hope — even in ruin, a door toward repentance remains open.
verses.33: Such is the punishment of this world — but the punishment of the Hereafter is far greater, if only they understood.
verses.34: Indeed, for the righteous, with their Lord, are Gardens of Bliss —
verses.35: Then will We treat those who submit to Us the same as the guilty? The unfairness of that idea is dismissed outright.
verses.36: What is wrong with you? How do you judge such a thing?
verses.37: Or do you have a scripture from which you are studying —
verses.38: That tells you whatever you choose is yours by right? A pointed challenge to any assumption of automatic reward without accountability.
verses.39: Or do you hold binding oaths from Us, reaching until the Day of Resurrection, guaranteeing you whatever you decide? No such guarantee was ever given.
verses.40: Ask them which of them can vouch for such a claim —
verses.41: Or do they have partners to support it? Then let them bring their partners forward, if they are telling the truth.
verses.42: On the Day the shin will be uncovered, and they are called to prostrate, but they will be unable —
verses.43: Their eyes lowered, disgrace covering them completely, for they were once invited to prostrate while perfectly capable, and refused.
verses.44: So leave to Me whoever denies this message — We will draw them gradually toward their fate, from directions they will never anticipate.
verses.45: And I will grant them time — but My plan is firm and unshaken.
verses.46: Or are you asking them for payment, leaving them burdened with debt? No such demand was ever made of them.
verses.47: Or do they hold knowledge of the unseen, writing it down for themselves? No such access was ever theirs either.
verses.48: So be patient for the judgment of your Lord, and do not be like the companion of the fish, who cried out while overwhelmed with distress — a reminder drawn from another prophet's trial.
verses.49: Had a mercy from his Lord not reached him, he would have been left stranded on the bare shore, blamed and exposed.
verses.50: But his Lord chose him, and placed him among the righteous — restoration following hardship, exactly as promised elsewhere in this surah.
verses.51: And indeed, the disbelievers nearly unsettle you with their piercing looks whenever they hear this message, saying, surely he is possessed —
verses.52: But it is nothing less than a reminder to all the worlds — the surah closes by returning firmly to what this message has always been: guidance offered freely, whatever hostility meets it.

verses.1: The Inevitable Reality — a name given to something so certain it cannot be argued away, only faced.
verses.2: What is the Inevitable Reality? The question posed immediately, inviting closer attention.
verses.3: And what can make you know what the Inevitable Reality truly is? A second layer of mystery added before any answer arrives.
verses.4: Thamud and 'Aad denied the Striking Calamity — two great, ancient peoples, both convinced this warning would never reach them.
verses.5: As for Thamud, they were destroyed by an overpowering blast —
verses.6: And as for 'Aad, they were destroyed by a violent, howling wind —
verses.7: Which Allah unleashed upon them for seven nights and eight days without pause, until you would have seen them lying there like hollow, uprooted palm trunks —
verses.8: Do you see any trace of them remaining now? Entire civilizations, reduced to nothing worth pointing to.
verses.9: And Pharaoh came, and those before him, and the overturned cities steeped in sin —
verses.10: They disobeyed the messenger sent to them, so He seized them with an overwhelming grip.
verses.11: Indeed, when the floodwaters rose beyond all bounds, We carried your ancestors upon the sailing ship —
verses.12: That We might make it a lasting reminder, retained by any ear willing to truly listen.
verses.13: Then when the Horn is blown with a single blast —
verses.14: And the earth and mountains are lifted and crushed together in one stroke —
verses.15: On that Day, the Occurrence will finally occur —
verses.16: And the sky will split apart, fragile on that Day as it has never been before.
verses.17: The angels will stand at its every edge, and the Throne of your Lord will be carried above them all, that Day, by eight —
verses.18: On that Day, you will be laid open completely; nothing hidden within you will remain concealed.
verses.19: As for the one given his record in his right hand, he will say, here, read my record —
verses.20: I was always certain I would meet this reckoning — confidence rewarded, expectation matched by outcome.
verses.21: So he will be granted a pleasant life —
verses.22: In a garden raised high —
verses.23: Its fruit hanging low, within easy reach —
verses.24: Eat and drink with true satisfaction, for what you sent ahead in days gone by — reward given as direct response to effort once made.
verses.25: But as for the one given his record in his left hand, he will say, if only I had never been given this record —
verses.26: And had never known what my account contained —
verses.27: If only this had simply been the end of everything —
verses.28: My wealth was of no use to me —
verses.29: My authority has vanished completely — everything once relied upon proven worthless in this moment.
verses.30: Seize him, and bind him —
verses.31: Then drive him into the Blazing Fire —
verses.32: Then wrap him in a chain seventy cubits long — punishment described in stark, deliberate detail.
verses.33: Indeed, he never truly believed in Allah, the Most Great —
verses.34: Nor did he ever encourage the feeding of the poor — belief and compassion, absent together.
verses.35: So there is no devoted friend for him here today —
verses.36: Nor any food except the discharge of wounds —
verses.37: Which none will eat except those who sinned deliberately.
verses.38: So I swear by everything you can see —
verses.39: And everything you cannot see —
verses.40: Indeed, this is the word carried by a noble messenger —
verses.41: And it is not the word of a poet; how little you truly believe —
verses.42: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer; how little you truly reflect —
verses.43: It is a revelation from the Lord of all the worlds.
verses.44: And had he invented any false statement and attributed it to Us —
verses.45: We would have seized him firmly by the right hand —
verses.46: Then severed his very lifeline — a swift, decisive response to any counterfeit revelation.
verses.47: And not one of you could have held Us back from him.
verses.48: And indeed, this Quran is a reminder for the righteous —
verses.49: And indeed, We know well that among you are those who deny it —
verses.50: And indeed, it will become a source of deep regret for the disbelievers —
verses.51: And indeed, it is truth of absolute certainty —
verses.52: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great — the surah closes exactly as it began, returning attention to the greatness that gives this entire warning its weight.

verses.1: A questioner asked for a punishment bound to arrive —
verses.2: For the disbelievers; nothing exists that could hold it back once it comes —
verses.3: From Allah, master of the ways of ascent — a title given here to the One whose reach extends through every level of creation.
verses.4: The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him during a Day whose length equals fifty thousand years — a scale of time entirely beyond ordinary human experience.
verses.5: So be patient, with a patience that carries real grace — endurance offered as the fitting response to a wait this immense.
verses.6: Indeed, they see it as distant —
verses.7: But We see it as near — perspective itself becomes the difference between denial and readiness.
verses.8: On the Day the sky will look like molten, murky oil —
verses.9: And the mountains will become like loose tufts of wool —
verses.10: And no close friend will ask anything of another friend — every alliance once relied upon, suddenly meaningless.
verses.11: They will be shown to one another. The guilty will wish he could ransom himself from that Day's punishment even with his own children —
verses.12: And his wife, and his brother —
verses.13: And his closest relatives who once sheltered him —
verses.14: And everyone on earth entirely, if it could somehow save him — desperation stretching to include every relationship he ever had.
verses.15: No — indeed, it is the Flame —
verses.16: Stripping away everything on the surface, layer by layer —
verses.17: Calling out specifically to whoever turned their back and walked away from obedience —
verses.18: And gathered wealth, hoarding it tightly rather than sharing it.
verses.19: Indeed, mankind was created prone to anxious impatience —
verses.20: When hardship touches him, he grows restless and afraid —
verses.21: And when good fortune touches him, he clings to it, unwilling to share —
verses.22: Except those who are devoted to their prayer —
verses.23: Consistent and unwavering in it —
verses.24: And within whose wealth there is a known, recognized right set aside —
verses.25: For the one who asks, and the one too dignified to ask at all — generosity extended even to those who never voice their need.
verses.26: And those who believe wholeheartedly in the Day of Recompense —
verses.27: And those who live in genuine fear of their Lord's punishment —
verses.28: For indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not something anyone can feel entirely secure from.
verses.29: And those who guard their private lives with care —
verses.30: Except within the bounds of marriage, where no blame applies —
verses.31: But whoever seeks beyond that has crossed into transgression.
verses.32: And those who remain attentive to their trusts and their promises —
verses.33: And those who stand honest and upright in their testimony —
verses.34: And those who carefully maintain their prayer — a full portrait now complete, built verse by verse, of the character that earns real security.
verses.35: They will be in gardens, honored fully — the reward matching, point for point, the qualities just described.
verses.36: So what is wrong with the disbelievers, rushing toward you in such haste —
verses.37: Gathering to your right and left in scattered groups? Curiosity mixed with mockery, drawing crowds without genuine reflection.
verses.38: Does each one of them expect to be admitted into a garden of bliss? An expectation held with no real basis behind it.
verses.39: No — indeed, We created them from what they already know well — their own humble origin, easily forgotten in the midst of such confidence.
verses.40: So I swear by the Lord of every rising and every setting that indeed We are fully able —
verses.41: To replace them with people better than them; and nothing could ever outmatch Our ability to do so.
verses.42: So leave them to their idle talk and amusement, until they meet the Day they were promised —
verses.43: The Day they emerge from their graves in a rush, as though racing toward some idol they once served —
verses.44: Their eyes lowered, humiliation covering them completely. That is the very Day they were promised, and it arrives exactly as described, whether they were ready or not.

verses.1: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, saying, warn your people before a painful punishment reaches them — a mission given not out of cruelty but urgency, time still remaining to change course.
verses.2: He said, O my people, I am truly a clear warner to you —
verses.3: Worship Allah, hold Him in awe, and obey me — a simple, threefold invitation, nothing more complicated demanded of them.
verses.4: He will forgive you your sins and grant you time until an appointed term; and truly, when Allah's appointed time arrives, it cannot be delayed, if only you understood.
verses.5: He said, my Lord, I called my people night and day —
verses.6: But my calling only increased their flight from me — a lifetime of effort met with growing resistance rather than growing acceptance.
verses.7: And every time I called them so that You might forgive them, they stuffed their fingers in their ears, covered themselves with their garments, and persisted, arrogant in their arrogance — refusal not passive but deliberately, physically enacted.
verses.8: Then I called them openly, in public —
verses.9: Then I spoke to them aloud, and also confided with them privately — every possible method tried, public and private both, leaving no approach untested.
verses.10: And I said, seek forgiveness from your Lord; indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver —
verses.11: He will send down abundant rain upon you from the sky —
verses.12: And increase you in wealth and children, and grant you gardens, and grant you rivers — repentance framed not as punishment avoided but as blessing actively offered.
verses.13: What is wrong with you, that you do not attribute to Allah the majesty He is due —
verses.14: When He has created you in stages, each one deliberate and unhurried?
verses.15: Do you not consider how Allah created seven heavens, layer upon layer —
verses.16: And placed the moon within them as a reflected light, and the sun as a blazing lamp?
verses.17: And Allah caused you to grow from the earth in gradual, careful growth —
verses.18: Then He will return you to it, and bring you forth once more, another extraction entirely.
verses.19: And Allah made the earth for you a vast expanse —
verses.20: That you might travel upon its wide-open roads — every argument Noah could offer, drawn straight from creation itself, laid out patiently before his people.
verses.21: Noah said, my Lord, indeed they have disobeyed me, and followed one whose wealth and children brought him nothing but greater loss —
verses.22: And they plotted an immense plot against the truth —
verses.23: And said, do not abandon your gods; do not abandon Wadd, or Suwa, or Yaghuth, or Ya'uq, or Nasr — the very names of their idols invoked here, a stubborn loyalty defended by name.
verses.24: And they have already misled many; so, my Lord, increase the wrongdoers only in ruin — a plea finally born from exhausted patience.
verses.25: Because of their sins, they were drowned, and driven into the Fire, finding no helper for themselves besides Allah —
verses.26: And Noah said, my Lord, do not leave a single disbeliever dwelling upon the earth —
verses.27: For if You leave them, they will mislead Your servants, and beget nothing but wicked, persistent disbelievers —
verses.28: My Lord, forgive me, and my parents, and whoever enters my home in faith, and every believing man and believing woman; and grant the wrongdoers nothing but destruction — the surah closes on Noah's prayer, personal even in its final plea, care extended to his household even after generations of rejection from everyone else.

verses.1: Say, it has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened, and said, indeed, we have heard a truly amazing recitation — an unseen audience, discovering this message for the first time, reacting with genuine astonishment.
verses.2: It guides toward what is right, and we have believed in it; we will never again associate anyone with our Lord —
verses.3: And exalted is the majesty of our Lord; He has never taken a wife, nor a son —
verses.4: And the foolish ones among us have been saying outrageous things about Allah — even among the jinn, foolishness and wisdom exist side by side.
verses.5: And we had assumed that neither humans nor jinn would ever dare speak falsely about Allah —
verses.6: And there were men among mankind who sought protection from men among the jinn, but this only deepened their own burden — reliance placed in the wrong direction, making things worse rather than better.
verses.7: And they assumed, just as you assumed, that Allah would never send any messenger at all —
verses.8: And we tried to reach the heavens, but found them filled with powerful guards and burning flames —
verses.9: We used to sit there in certain positions to listen in, but now, whoever tries to listen finds a burning flame lying in wait for him — access once possible, now firmly closed off.
verses.10: And we do not know whether harm is intended for those on earth, or whether their Lord intends good guidance for them —
verses.11: And among us are the righteous, and among us are others who are not; we exist along divided paths, just as humans do.
verses.12: And we have come to realize with certainty that we can never overpower Allah upon this earth, nor can we escape Him by fleeing —
verses.13: And when we heard the guidance, we believed in it; and whoever believes in his Lord need not fear loss or injustice —
verses.14: And among us are those who submit fully, and among us are those who deviate; and whoever submits has truly sought out the right path —
verses.15: But as for those who deviate, they will become fuel for Hell — the jinn's own testimony closing here with this stark division.
verses.16: And had they remained steadfast upon the right way, We would have given them abundant, generous provision —
verses.17: So that We might test them through it; and whoever turns away from remembering his Lord, He will lead into an increasingly difficult punishment.
verses.18: And truly, the places of worship belong to Allah alone, so do not call upon anyone else alongside Him —
verses.19: And when the Servant of Allah stood up in prayer, they nearly pressed upon him in overwhelming crowds — devotion so striking it drew unusual attention.
verses.20: Say, I only call upon my Lord, and I associate no one with Him —
verses.21: Say, indeed, I hold no power to bring you harm or right guidance on my own —
verses.22: Say, indeed, no one could ever protect me from Allah, nor would I ever find refuge in anyone other than Him —
verses.23: But only a message from Allah, and His revelations; and whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger will have the fire of Hell, abiding there forever.
verses.24: Until, when they finally see what they were promised, they will know exactly who is weaker in supporters, and fewer in number.
verses.25: Say, I do not know whether what you are promised is near, or whether my Lord will grant it a longer time —
verses.26: Knower of the unseen, He does not disclose His knowledge of the unseen to just anyone —
verses.27: Except to a messenger He has approved, and even then He places watchers before him and behind him —
verses.28: So that He may know they have truly conveyed the messages of their Lord; He encompasses everything with them, and has counted all things precisely.

verses.1: O you who is wrapped in a cloak — an address so personal, so intimate, it pictures the exact posture of the person being spoken to, wrapped up for warmth or comfort.
verses.2: Rise for prayer during the night, except for a small portion of it —
verses.3: Half of it, or subtract a little from that —
verses.4: Or add to it, and recite the Quran slowly, with careful measure — flexibility offered even within a serious command, room made for what is realistically sustainable.
verses.5: Indeed, We will soon place upon you a weighty word — revelation described here almost physically, something with real, felt substance.
verses.6: Indeed, the hours of the night bring the heart and voice more fully into agreement, and are more suited for careful, deliberate words —
verses.7: Indeed, during the day you have long, demanding occupation — the night set apart specifically because daylight leaves no such stillness available.
verses.8: And remember the name of your Lord, and devote yourself to Him completely —
verses.9: Lord of the East and the West; there is no god but Him, so take Him alone as the One you rely on.
verses.10: And be patient with what they say, and withdraw from them with graceful, dignified distance — patience paired here with quiet dignity rather than confrontation.
verses.11: And leave to Me those who deny, those living comfortably; grant them just a little more time —
verses.12: Indeed, with Us are shackles, and a blazing Fire —
verses.13: And food that chokes going down, and a painful punishment —
verses.14: On the Day the earth and mountains will shake violently, and the mountains will become like heaped, pouring sand.
verses.15: Indeed, We have sent to you a Messenger as a witness over you, just as We sent a messenger to Pharaoh —
verses.16: But Pharaoh disobeyed that messenger, so We seized him with a devastating seizure.
verses.17: So how, then, will you protect yourselves, if you disbelieve, from a Day that turns even children's hair white?
verses.18: The sky itself will break apart because of it; His promise is always fulfilled, without fail.
verses.19: Indeed, this is a reminder; so whoever wills may take a path back to his Lord.
verses.20: Indeed, your Lord knows that you stand in prayer nearly two-thirds of the night, or half of it, or a third, as does a group of those with you. Allah measures the night and the day, and knows well that you cannot sustain such intensity forever, so He has turned to you with ease: recite what is manageable from the Quran. He knows some among you are ill, others traveling in search of His provision, others striving in His cause; so recite what is manageable, establish prayer, give in charity, and lend to Allah a generous loan. Whatever good you send ahead for yourselves, you will find it with Him, better and greater in reward. And seek His forgiveness; indeed, Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful.

verses.1: O you who covers yourself with a garment — the same intimate address as before, catching its listener in a private, unguarded moment.
verses.2: Arise, and warn —
verses.3: And magnify your Lord —
verses.4: And purify your clothing —
verses.5: And keep away from uncleanliness — instructions moving swiftly from the spiritual to the practical, faith meant to be lived out in visible, tangible ways.
verses.6: And do not give, expecting more in return —
verses.7: And for the sake of your Lord, be patient — generosity and patience both offered without any hidden transaction attached.
verses.8: And when the Horn is sounded —
verses.9: That will be, on that Day, a truly difficult Day —
verses.10: For the disbelievers, anything but easy.
verses.11: Leave to Me the one I created entirely alone —
verses.12: And gave him extensive wealth —
verses.13: And sons always present at his side —
verses.14: And smoothed everything before him, making his life effortlessly easy —
verses.15: Yet still, he desires that I give him even more.
verses.16: No — indeed, he has been persistently obstinate toward Our verses.
verses.17: I will burden him with a punishment that only grows more difficult.
verses.18: Indeed, he thought, and he calculated —
verses.19: So may he be ruined, for how he calculated —
verses.20: Then may he be ruined again, for how he calculated —
verses.21: Then he looked once more —
verses.22: Then he frowned, and scowled —
verses.23: Then he turned his back, and grew arrogant —
verses.24: And said, this is nothing but borrowed magic —
verses.25: This is nothing but the word of a mere human being — dismissal chosen deliberately, even after real, visible calculation about how convincing the message truly was.
verses.26: I will drive him into Saqar —
verses.27: And what can make you know what Saqar is?
verses.28: It spares nothing, and leaves nothing untouched —
verses.29: Scorching the skin completely.
verses.30: Over it stand nineteen — a specific, deliberate number, its precision offered as its own kind of proof.
verses.31: And We have made the keepers of the Fire none other than angels; and We made their number a trial for the disbelievers, so that those given the Scripture may become certain, and those who believe may grow further in faith, and neither group will doubt, while those with hypocrisy in their hearts and the disbelievers will ask, what could Allah possibly mean by this example? Thus does Allah leave astray whom He wills, and guide whom He wills; none knows the soldiers of your Lord except Him. And it is nothing but a reminder to all of humanity.
verses.32: No — by the moon —
verses.33: And by the night as it departs —
verses.34: And by the morning as it brightens —
verses.35: Indeed, it is among the greatest of warnings —
verses.36: A warning to all humanity —
verses.37: For whoever among you wishes to move forward, or to stay behind — the choice, once again, genuinely left open.
verses.38: Every soul will be held responsible for what it has earned —
verses.39: Except the companions of the right —
verses.40: Who will be in gardens, asking one another questions —
verses.41: About the guilty ones —
verses.42: Asking them, what led you into Saqar?
verses.43: They will answer, we were not among those who prayed —
verses.44: Nor did we feed the poor —
verses.45: And we used to indulge in idle talk alongside those who indulged —
verses.46: And we used to deny the Day of Recompense —
verses.47: Until certainty finally reached us — a full confession, four failures named honestly, one after another.
verses.48: So the intercession of intercessors will be of no benefit to them —
verses.49: So what is wrong with them, that they turn away from this reminder —
verses.50: As though they were terrified wild donkeys —
verses.51: Fleeing from a lion — an almost startling image of panic and instinctive avoidance, applied to their reaction toward truth itself.
verses.52: Rather, every one of them wants to be handed pages unrolled directly from heaven, addressed personally to him.
verses.53: No — rather, they simply do not fear the Hereafter.
verses.54: No — indeed, it is truly a reminder.
verses.55: So whoever wills may take heed of it.
verses.56: And they will not take heed unless Allah wills it; He alone is worthy of being feared, and He alone is worthy to grant forgiveness — the surah closes on this final balance, reverence and mercy held together in a single breath.

verses.1: I swear by the Day of Resurrection —
verses.2: And I swear by the self-reproaching soul — the very conscience that questions itself is invoked here as its own kind of evidence for what's coming.
verses.3: Does man think that We will never reassemble his bones?
verses.4: Yes indeed — We are able even to perfectly restore his fingertips, down to the smallest, most delicate detail.
verses.5: But man desires instead to keep sinning ahead of himself, unrestrained —
verses.6: He asks, mockingly, when is this Day of Resurrection?
verses.7: So when the sight is dazzled and overwhelmed —
verses.8: And the moon loses its light —
verses.9: And the sun and the moon are joined together —
verses.10: Man will cry out on that Day, where is there to run? Every mocking question from before answered now with sudden, desperate urgency.
verses.11: No — there is no refuge anywhere.
verses.12: To your Lord alone, that Day, is the final place of rest.
verses.13: Man will be told, that Day, everything he sent ahead and everything he left undone —
verses.14: In fact, man will be a witness against his very own self —
verses.15: Even if he offers every excuse he can think of.
verses.16: Do not move your tongue too hastily with it, trying to memorize it quickly — gentle guidance offered directly about how revelation should be received, patience rather than anxious rushing.
verses.17: Indeed, it is upon Us to gather it within you, and to make it recited —
verses.18: So when it is recited, follow along carefully with its recitation —
verses.19: Then it is also upon Us to make its meaning clear.
verses.20: No — rather, you love what is immediate —
verses.21: And leave behind any thought of the Hereafter.
verses.22: Some faces, that Day, will be radiant —
verses.23: Gazing directly upon their Lord — a closeness beyond anything words could fully capture.
verses.24: And other faces, that Day, will be twisted with dread —
verses.25: Certain that something crushing is about to happen to them.
verses.26: No — when the soul finally reaches the collarbone —
verses.27: And it is asked, who can cure him now?
verses.28: And he becomes certain that this is truly the moment of separation —
verses.29: And leg is wound about leg in that final struggle —
verses.30: To your Lord, that Day, is where he is finally driven.
verses.31: For he had never believed, nor had he ever prayed —
verses.32: But instead denied, and turned away —
verses.33: Then went back to his own people, walking with arrogant pride.
verses.34: Woe to you, and woe again —
verses.35: Then woe to you once more, and woe again — the warning repeated with rising intensity, matching the gravity of what's being described.
verses.36: Does man think he will simply be left without purpose, unaccountable? A rhetorical question closing out the surah's central argument.
verses.37: Was he not once a drop of emitted semen?
verses.38: Then a clinging clot, which He created and shaped in perfect proportion —
verses.39: And made from it two kinds, male and female —
verses.40: Is that Creator not able to bring the dead back to life? The surah closes with this final, unanswerable question, tracing resurrection back to a beginning already witnessed by everyone alive.

verses.1: Has there not been, for man, a period of time when he was nothing worth even mentioning? A humbling reminder of a beginning too insignificant to register at all.
verses.2: Indeed, We created man from a mixed fluid, in order to test him; and We gave him hearing and sight —
verses.3: Indeed, We guided him along the way, whether he chooses gratitude or ingratitude — the choice left genuinely open, guidance given without forcing the outcome.
verses.4: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains, shackles, and a blazing Fire.
verses.5: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup mixed with camphor —
verses.6: A spring from which the servants of Allah will drink, making it flow abundantly wherever they wish — provision so generous it responds directly to their will.
verses.7: They are the ones who fulfill their vows, and fear a Day whose evil spreads far and wide —
verses.8: And they give food, despite their own love for it, to the needy, the orphan, and the captive —
verses.9: Saying, we feed you only for the sake of Allah; we want no reward from you, nor even a thank you —
verses.10: Indeed, we fear from our Lord a Day both harsh and severe — generosity here rooted entirely in fear of that Day, not in any expectation of recognition.
verses.11: So Allah will shield them from the evil of that Day, and grant them radiance and joy —
verses.12: And will reward them, for their patience, with a garden, and with silk garments.
verses.13: Reclining there upon adorned couches, they will feel neither burning sun nor bitter cold —
verses.14: Its shade will hang close above them, and its fruit will bend low, easy to reach without effort.
verses.15: Vessels of silver will be passed among them, and cups as clear as glass —
verses.16: Glasses made of silver, yet measured with perfect precision — beauty and exactness combined in every detail.
verses.17: And they will be given a drink mixed with ginger —
verses.18: From a spring there called Salsabil.
verses.19: Eternal youths will circulate among them; seeing them, you would think they were scattered pearls.
verses.20: And wherever you look there, you will see only pleasure, and a vast, magnificent dominion.
verses.21: Upon them will be garments of fine green silk and brocade, adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a pure, cleansing drink.
verses.22: It will be said, indeed, this is your reward, and your effort has been fully recognized and appreciated.
verses.23: Indeed, it is We who have sent down to you this Quran, gradually, piece by piece —
verses.24: So be patient for the judgment of your Lord, and do not obey any sinner or ungrateful disbeliever among them.
verses.25: And remember the name of your Lord morning and evening —
verses.26: And prostrate to Him during part of the night, and glorify Him through the long hours of it.
verses.27: Indeed, these people love what is immediate, and leave behind them a heavy Day they will one day face.
verses.28: We created them, and strengthened their very forms; and whenever We will, We can replace their likeness entirely.
verses.29: Indeed, this is a reminder; so whoever wills may take a path back to his Lord.
verses.30: And you do not will it unless Allah wills it too; indeed, Allah is ever Knowing, ever Wise.
verses.31: He admits whom He wills into His mercy; but for the wrongdoers, He has prepared a painful punishment — the surah closes with this same dual ending found throughout the Quran: mercy freely given, and justice reserved for those who refused it.

verses.1: By those sent forth, one after another —
verses.2: And by the winds that blow with violent force —
verses.3: And by those that scatter and spread wide —
verses.4: And by those that separate truth from falsehood decisively —
verses.5: And by those who deliver a message —
verses.6: As justification, or as warning — an unbroken chain of oaths, angels and winds alike, each performing their appointed task with precision.
verses.7: Indeed, what you are promised will surely come to pass.
verses.8: So when the stars are extinguished —
verses.9: And when the sky is torn open —
verses.10: And when the mountains are blown away like dust —
verses.11: And when the messengers are given their appointed time —
verses.12: For what Day has all of this been set aside?
verses.13: For the Day of Judgment.
verses.14: And what can make you know what the Day of Judgment truly is?
verses.15: Woe, that Day, to the deniers — the first appearance of a refrain that will return again and again throughout this surah, like a bell tolling after each new proof.
verses.16: Did We not destroy the earlier peoples?
verses.17: We will make the later ones follow right behind them —
verses.18: This is simply how We deal with the guilty.
verses.19: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.20: Did We not create you from a fluid held in little regard?
verses.21: And place it in a secure resting place —
verses.22: For a known, appointed span —
verses.23: So We determined it precisely; and how excellent a determiner We are.
verses.24: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.25: Have We not made the earth a gathering place —
verses.26: For both the living and the dead?
verses.27: And placed within it towering, firmly rooted mountains, and given you sweet water to drink?
verses.28: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.29: It will be said, proceed now toward what you used to deny —
verses.30: Proceed toward a shadow with three columns —
verses.31: Offering no real coolness, and no shelter at all from the flame.
verses.32: Indeed, it throws sparks as massive as fortresses —
verses.33: As though they were dark, yellowish camels moving in the distance.
verses.34: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.35: This is a Day when they will not speak —
verses.36: Nor will they be given permission to offer any excuse.
verses.37: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.38: This is the Day of Judgment; We will have gathered you together, along with all the earlier peoples.
verses.39: So if you have some scheme in mind, then try scheming against Me.
verses.40: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.41: Indeed, the righteous will be among cool shades and flowing springs —
verses.42: And fruits of whatever they desire —
verses.43: Eat and drink in full satisfaction, for everything you used to do.
verses.44: Indeed, this is how We reward those who do good.
verses.45: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.46: Eat and enjoy yourselves a little while longer; indeed, you are guilty — a brief, almost bitter permission given to those who chose denial.
verses.47: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.48: And when it is said to them, bow down, they do not bow.
verses.49: Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
verses.50: So in what message after this one will they possibly believe? The surah closes on this final, unanswerable question, the refrain finally falling silent after having echoed ten times throughout.

verses.1: About what are they questioning one another? The surah opens mid-conversation, as though stepping into an argument already underway among the people.
verses.2: About the great news — something significant enough to divide opinion, discussed and debated everywhere.
verses.3: That over which they remain in disagreement — no consensus reached, some believing, others dismissing it entirely.
verses.4: No — they are going to know —
verses.5: Then again, no — they are going to know — the warning repeated twice, insisting that whatever disagreement exists now will be settled with certainty later, whether they are ready or not.
verses.6: Have We not made the earth a resting place — flat, stable, livable, exactly suited for those who walk upon it?
verses.7: And the mountains as anchoring stakes — holding the earth steady, quietly performing a function most people never think to notice.
verses.8: And We created you in pairs — companionship built into human design from the very beginning.
verses.9: And made your sleep a source of rest — a nightly gift, restoring what the day had worn down.
verses.10: And made the night as a covering — darkness offered like a blanket, concealing and calming.
verses.11: And made the day for seeking a livelihood — light given specifically for work, movement, and provision.
verses.12: And built above you seven strong heavens —
verses.13: And placed within them a burning lamp — the sun, radiant and constant, lighting the entire system beneath it.
verses.14: And sent down from the rain-heavy clouds, pouring water abundantly —
verses.15: That We might bring forth by it grain and vegetation —
verses.16: And gardens thick with growth — an entire cycle of provision, traced carefully from sky to soil to sustenance.
verses.17: Indeed, the Day of Judgment is an appointed time — fixed, certain, not left to chance or speculation.
verses.18: The Day the Horn is blown, and you come forth in crowds —
verses.19: And the heaven is opened, becoming as though it were made of gateways —
verses.20: And the mountains, once so firmly anchored, are set in motion and become like a mirage — the very stability described earlier in the surah, entirely undone.
verses.21: Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait —
verses.22: For the transgressors, a place they will ultimately return to —
verses.23: Where they will remain for endless ages — no temporary sentence, but a stay stretching on without an end in sight.
verses.24: They will taste therein no coolness, and no relief in drink —
verses.25: Except scalding water and foul discharge —
verses.26: A fitting recompense, matched exactly to what their choices earned them.
verses.27: Indeed, they never expected any reckoning at all —
verses.28: And they denied Our signs, rejecting them completely and repeatedly —
verses.29: But We have recorded everything, every single thing, in writing — nothing dismissed by them was ever actually forgotten or lost.
verses.30: So taste the consequence — and We will only increase you in torment, nothing more, nothing less than what was earned.
verses.31: Indeed, for the righteous is a true triumph —
verses.32: Gardens and grapevines —
verses.33: And splendid companions of equal age —
verses.34: And a cup filled to the brim — abundance described here in vivid, generous detail, the opposite in every way of the scalding drink offered elsewhere.
verses.35: They will hear no idle talk there, and no falsehood —
verses.36: A reward from your Lord, a gift given in perfect measure — nothing excessive, nothing lacking, exactly proportioned to what was deserved.
verses.37: From the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, the Most Merciful — before whom none possess the authority to simply speak without permission.
verses.38: On the Day the Spirit and the angels stand in ranks, none will speak except the one granted permission by the Most Merciful, and he will say only what is true — order and restraint even in that vast gathering, nothing left to chaos.
verses.39: That is the True Day — so whoever wills, let him take a path back toward his Lord, while the chance still remains.
verses.40: Indeed, We have warned you of a punishment drawing near, on the Day when a man will see clearly everything his own hands have sent ahead, and the disbeliever will cry out, if only I were nothing but dust — the surah closes on this final, aching regret, wishing for non-existence itself rather than facing what has finally arrived.

verses.1: By those who extract with violent force —
verses.2: And by those who draw out with gentle ease — two contrasting ways souls are taken at the moment of death, some seized harshly, others released softly.
verses.3: And by those who glide swiftly, as if swimming through their task —
verses.4: And those who race ahead of one another, swift and purposeful —
verses.5: And those who arrange each matter according to command — angelic forces invoked here, each carrying out precise duties without deviation.
verses.6: On the Day the first blast shakes everything violently —
verses.7: Followed immediately by the second — one convulsion followed swiftly by another, no pause offered in between.
verses.8: Hearts, on that Day, will be pounding, trembling —
verses.9: Their eyes lowered in humility — every trace of former confidence or arrogance stripped away in an instant.
verses.10: They used to say, will we really be returned to our former state —
verses.11: Even after becoming decayed bones? Disbelief in resurrection voiced here plainly, treated as an absurdity by those who once denied it.
verses.12: They said, that then would be a losing return — mockery aimed at the very idea, dismissed as pointless before it was ever proven true.
verses.13: But indeed, it will be only a single shout —
verses.14: And suddenly, they will find themselves standing on the earth's surface — the resurrection they had mocked as impossible arriving with startling, immediate ease.
verses.15: Has the story of Moses reached you? A shift now toward a familiar, well-known account.
verses.16: When his Lord called out to him in the sacred valley of Tuwa —
verses.17: Go to Pharaoh — indeed, he has transgressed beyond all bounds —
verses.18: And say to him, would you be willing to purify yourself —
verses.19: And let me guide you to your Lord, so that you might come to hold Him in awe? An invitation extended even to a tyrant, mercy offered before judgment.
verses.20: So he showed him the greatest sign — undeniable proof placed directly before him.
verses.21: But he denied it, and refused to obey —
verses.22: Then he turned his back, working hard against the truth he had just witnessed —
verses.23: Then he gathered his people and called out to them —
verses.24: Declaring, I am your lord, most high — arrogance reaching its absolute peak, a man claiming divinity for himself.
verses.25: So Allah seized him with an exemplary punishment, for both this final claim and his earlier defiance —
verses.26: Indeed, in that there is a lesson for anyone who holds true awe of his Lord — the entire account offered not as distant history but as a living warning.
verses.27: Are you, in your creation, more difficult than the heaven He built? A question redirecting attention from Pharaoh's small arrogance to the vast scale of what surrounds every person daily.
verses.28: He raised its structure and gave it perfect proportion —
verses.29: And darkened its night, and brought forth its light —
verses.30: And after all this, He spread out the earth —
verses.31: Bringing forth from it water and pasture —
verses.32: And setting the mountains firmly in place —
verses.33: As provision for you, and for your livestock — an entire, deliberate design, laid out generously for use and enjoyment.
verses.34: But when the greatest overwhelming calamity arrives —
verses.35: The Day when man will remember exactly what he strove for — every effort, every priority, brought suddenly and clearly into full view.
verses.36: And Hellfire will be made visible for anyone who looks —
verses.37: So as for the one who transgressed —
verses.38: And preferred the life of this world above all else —
verses.39: Then indeed, Hellfire will be his refuge — the very thing he chased in place of anything higher becomes, in the end, the shelter he is left with.
verses.40: But as for the one who feared standing before his Lord —
verses.41: And restrained his soul from its unlawful desires —
verses.42: Then indeed, Paradise will be his refuge — the same structure of consequence, this time rewarding the restraint chosen instead.
verses.43: They ask you about the Hour — when will it come?
verses.44: But what do you have to do with mentioning its exact timing? Even the one delivering this message is reminded that this knowledge lies beyond him.
verses.45: To your Lord alone belongs its final knowledge —
verses.46: You are only sent to warn those who hold awe of it — and on the Day they finally see it, it will feel as though they had lingered in this world for only a single evening, or a single morning, so brief will the whole of this life then seem.

verses.1: He frowned and turned away — a small, human moment of impatience, honestly recorded rather than hidden or softened.
verses.2: Because a blind man had come to him, interrupting — the timing felt inconvenient, the interruption unwelcome in that moment.
verses.3: But what could make you realize that perhaps he might have been purified by that very conversation — the surah gently turns the moment around, revealing what might have been missed.
verses.4: Or that the reminder might have genuinely benefited him — the man who was turned away carried a sincerity easy to overlook at first glance.
verses.5: As for the one who considers himself free of any need —
verses.6: To him, full attention was given — status and self-assurance drew focus, even without any guarantee of real change resulting from it.
verses.7: Yet there is no blame on you if he does not become purified — outcomes were never the responsibility of the one delivering the message, only the honest delivery itself.
verses.8: But as for the one who came running, eager, genuinely striving —
verses.9: While holding real reverence in his heart —
verses.10: From him, attention drifted away — the very person most ready to receive guidance was the one least attended to in that moment.
verses.11: No — truly, these verses are a reminder —
verses.12: So let whoever wills take heed of it — the lesson offered freely, without favoritism toward status or background.
verses.13: Recorded upon honored pages —
verses.14: Elevated, and kept pure —
verses.15: Carried by the hands of noble messengers —
verses.16: Honorable and devoted to their duty — the revelation's true dignity described here, regardless of how any single human moment unfolded around it.
verses.17: Cursed is man — how ungrateful he can be, even after everything given to him.
verses.18: From what did He create him? A question meant to humble, tracing his origin back to something utterly modest.
verses.19: From a small drop, He created him, and determined his form —
verses.20: Then made the way easy for him — life itself smoothed and enabled from the very beginning.
verses.21: Then causes his death, and provides him a grave —
verses.22: Then, when He wills, He will raise him again to life — the full arc of existence, birth to death to resurrection, entirely within His hand.
verses.23: No — man has still not fulfilled what was commanded of him — a gentle but firm rebuke, obligations left unmet despite every provision given.
verses.24: So let mankind consider the food he eats —
verses.25: How We poured down water in abundance —
verses.26: Then split open the earth, breaking it apart for growth —
verses.27: And caused grain to grow within it —
verses.28: And grapevines and green plants —
verses.29: And olive trees and palm trees —
verses.30: And gardens dense with growth —
verses.31: And fruit and pasture — an entire, generous chain of provision, traced from rainfall down to the meal on someone's plate.
verses.32: As enjoyment for you, and for your livestock — nourishment given not only to people but extended even to the animals who depend on them.
verses.33: But when the Deafening Blast arrives —
verses.34: On the Day a man will flee from his own brother —
verses.35: And from his mother and his father —
verses.36: And from his wife and his children — every bond once considered unbreakable, suddenly abandoned in the panic of that moment.
verses.37: For every person, that Day, will have concerns entirely their own, enough to consume them completely — no attention left over for anyone else, however dearly loved.
verses.38: Some faces, that Day, will be bright —
verses.39: Laughing, filled with joy at good news received —
verses.40: And other faces, that same Day, will be covered in dust —
verses.41: Darkness settling over them completely —
verses.42: Those are the disbelievers, the wicked ones — the surah closes on this stark division, returning finally to the same theme of attention and neglect it opened with, now applied on the grandest possible scale.

verses.1: When the sun is wrapped up and its light extinguished —
verses.2: And when the stars fall, losing their light and scattering —
verses.3: And when the mountains are set in motion, uprooted entirely —
verses.4: And when pregnant camels, among the most prized possessions of their owners, are abandoned and forgotten — even what once mattered most, left behind without a second thought.
verses.5: And when wild beasts are gathered together — creatures that never mix, brought suddenly into the same terrified company.
verses.6: And when the seas are set ablaze, boiling over —
verses.7: And when souls are paired, matched finally with those who share their true nature —
verses.8: And when the girl buried alive is finally asked —
verses.9: For what crime she was killed — an ancient injustice, once silenced completely, finally given its voice and its reckoning.
verses.10: And when the pages of every deed are laid open for all to see —
verses.11: And when the sky is stripped away entirely —
verses.12: And when Hellfire is set blazing —
verses.13: And when Paradise is brought near — each of these images arriving in rapid succession, building an unrelenting picture of total upheaval.
verses.14: Then every soul will know exactly what it has brought with it — after this cascade of transformation, the focus narrows to a single, personal reckoning.
verses.15: So I swear by the retreating stars —
verses.16: Those that move through their courses and vanish from sight —
verses.17: And by the night, as it closes in around everything —
verses.18: And by the dawn, as it breathes its first light — four fresh oaths, drawn once more from the sky's daily rhythm of appearing and vanishing.
verses.19: Indeed, this is the word carried by a noble messenger — the revelation traced here directly to its trustworthy source.
verses.20: Possessed of strength, and secure in position with the Owner of the Throne —
verses.21: Obeyed there, among the highest ranks, and entirely trustworthy — every possible doubt about the messenger's reliability addressed directly.
verses.22: And your companion is not, in any way, someone overtaken by madness — a direct answer to an accusation once thrown at him.
verses.23: He has truly seen him, the one who brought this message, on the clear horizon — no vague claim, but a witnessed, specific encounter.
verses.24: And he does not withhold anything of what has been shown to him from the unseen — nothing hidden, nothing kept back from those he was sent to guide.
verses.25: And this is not the word of a devil, cast out and rejected — dismissing entirely the suggestion that this revelation could come from anything corrupted or condemned.
verses.26: So where, then, are you going? A direct question, almost bewildered, aimed at anyone still turning away despite everything just laid out.
verses.27: It is nothing less than a reminder to all the worlds —
verses.28: For whoever among you wills to walk a straight path — the invitation left open, genuinely, to anyone willing to take it.
verses.29: But you do not will it, except that Allah, Lord of the worlds, wills it too — the surah closes by placing even human choice within a larger, sustaining will, humility built into the very last word.

verses.1: When the sky is torn apart —
verses.2: And when the stars fall, scattering in every direction —
verses.3: And when the seas burst forth, breaking their boundaries —
verses.4: And when the graves are overturned, releasing everything held within them —
verses.5: Then every soul will finally know what it sent ahead, and what it left behind — a complete, undeniable accounting, nothing forgotten on either side of the ledger.
verses.6: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous — a direct, almost puzzled question, addressed to anyone who has taken that generosity for granted.
verses.7: Who created you, shaped you, and balanced you in perfect proportion —
verses.8: Assembling you in whatever form He willed — the human body itself, formed with deliberate, careful intention, nothing left to chance.
verses.9: No — rather, you deny the Recompense entirely — the deception named plainly here, not confusion but outright denial of the reckoning still to come.
verses.10: And indeed, appointed over you are watchers —
verses.11: Honored ones, recording everything faithfully —
verses.12: Who know exactly what you do — nothing done in secret is ever truly unseen, whatever privacy might feel like in the moment.
verses.13: Indeed, the righteous will be in pure pleasure —
verses.14: And indeed, the wicked will be in Hellfire — two fates, stated side by side with striking simplicity.
verses.15: They will burn there on the Day of Recompense —
verses.16: And they will never be absent from it, not even for a moment — no relief, no temporary escape, permanence built into the punishment itself.
verses.17: And what can make you know what the Day of Recompense is? A question posed once —
verses.18: Then again, what can make you know what the Day of Recompense is? Repeated a second time, deepening the sense that this Day exceeds anything easily imagined.
verses.19: It is the Day when no soul will have any power to help another soul at all, and the command, that Day, belongs entirely to Allah — the surah closes on this total, singular authority, every human connection and alliance rendered powerless before it.

verses.1: Woe to those who give less than what is due — the surah opens by naming a very specific, everyday dishonesty: the small, deliberate shortfall in trade that most people never notice.
verses.2: Who, when they receive a measure from others, take the full amount owed to them —
verses.3: But when they measure or weigh out something for others, they cause loss — the same scale used honestly in their own favor and dishonestly against everyone else.
verses.4: Do they not consider that they will be raised again —
verses.5: For a tremendous Day —
verses.6: The Day when all mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds — the small dishonesty of the marketplace, it turns out, is never truly small; it is weighed on that same Day.
verses.7: No — indeed, the record of the wicked is stored in Sijjeen —
verses.8: And what can make you know what Sijjeen is? A pause built deliberately into the structure, letting the weight of the unknown settle before revealing it.
verses.9: It is a register, clearly inscribed — every fraudulent transaction, every stolen fraction, recorded permanently and precisely.
verses.10: Woe, on that Day, to the deniers —
verses.11: Who deny the Day of Recompense — the very Day that will finally expose every shortfall they once caused.
verses.12: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor — denial here framed not as innocent doubt but as the deliberate posture of those already committed to wrongdoing.
verses.13: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, tales of ancient peoples — dismissing truth as nothing more than old stories, easier to mock than to face.
verses.14: No — rather, a stain has covered their hearts from everything they kept earning through their own choices — the heart described here almost physically, coated over by accumulated wrongdoing until nothing else can get through.
verses.15: No — indeed, on that Day, they will be completely veiled from their Lord — separation as punishment, distance where closeness was always intended.
verses.16: Then indeed, they will enter and burn in Hellfire —
verses.17: Then it will be said to them, this is exactly what you used to deny — the Fire itself becomes the final, undeniable answer to every dismissal once made.
verses.18: No — indeed, the record of the righteous is stored in Illiyyun —
verses.19: And what can make you know what Illiyyun is? The same structure of anticipation used before, now applied to the opposite fate.
verses.20: It is a register, clearly inscribed —
verses.21: Witnessed by those brought near to Allah — an honored record, observed by an honored audience, standing in deliberate contrast to Sijjeen.
verses.22: Indeed, the righteous will be in pure pleasure —
verses.23: Upon adorned couches, gazing outward, taking it all in —
verses.24: You will recognize on their faces the radiance of pure joy — happiness that shows itself outwardly, unmistakable to anyone who looks at them.
verses.25: They will be given to drink a pure, sealed wine —
verses.26: Whose seal is musk — for this, let all who strive, strive — a reward held up here as the very thing worth competing for, unlike the petty dishonest gains described at the surah's opening.
verses.27: Its mixture drawn from Tasneem —
verses.28: A spring from which those brought near to Allah drink — the closest ones given access to the purest source, honor made tangible.
verses.29: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at the believers —
verses.30: And whenever they passed by them, they would exchange mocking glances —
verses.31: And when they returned to their own people, they would go back joking about it —
verses.32: And whenever they saw the believers, they would say, these people are truly lost — mockery repeated and rehearsed, a habit rather than a single lapse.
verses.33: But they were never appointed as guardians over them — their scorn had no real authority behind it, only presumption.
verses.34: So today, it is the believers who will laugh at the disbelievers —
verses.35: Upon adorned couches, watching everything unfold — the exact scene once directed at the believers, reversed completely.
verses.36: Have the disbelievers not been repaid, in full, for everything they used to do? The surah closes with this quiet, settled justice, mockery answered finally with reversal rather than revenge.

verses.1: When the sky splits apart — a structure that has held firm since the beginning of everything, finally giving way.
verses.2: And listens and responds to its Lord, exactly as it must — obedience total and immediate, without hesitation or resistance.
verses.3: And when the earth is stretched out flat —
verses.4: And throws out everything it has held within it, emptied completely — every grave, every buried secret, released at once.
verses.5: And listens and responds to its Lord, exactly as it must — the same total obedience shown by the sky, mirrored now in the earth beneath every foot that has ever walked it.
verses.6: O mankind, you are laboring toward your Lord with real, exhausting effort, and you will surely meet Him — this life itself described as a journey, every choice and action part of that ongoing labor.
verses.7: As for the one given his record in his right hand —
verses.8: He will be given an easy reckoning —
verses.9: And return to his people in joy — a homecoming, celebrated rather than feared.
verses.10: But as for the one given his record behind his back —
verses.11: He will cry out, longing for destruction, for an ending rather than what actually awaits him —
verses.12: And he will burn in a raging Blaze — the outcome as severe as his cry for escape suggests.
verses.13: For he had once lived among his people, comfortable and untroubled —
verses.14: Certain, in his heart, that he would never have to return to account for any of it — that certainty proven false in the worst possible way.
verses.15: But truly — his Lord was always watching him, the entire time, without exception.
verses.16: So I swear by the glow that lingers after sunset —
verses.17: And by the night, and everything it gathers into darkness —
verses.18: And by the moon, when it becomes full — three more oaths, each drawn from cycles of change repeating night after night.
verses.19: You will surely pass through stage after stage — human life itself described as a series of transitions, never remaining fixed in one state.
verses.20: So what is wrong with them, that they still do not believe —
verses.21: And when the Quran is recited to them, they do not bow down in submission? Given every sign, every warning, every pattern of change and return, this refusal seems all the more striking.
verses.22: Rather, those who disbelieve persist in denial —
verses.23: And Allah knows fully everything they keep hidden within themselves — no denial, however firmly held outwardly, is unseen from within.
verses.24: So give them news of a painful punishment —
verses.25: Except those who believe and do what is righteous — for them is a reward that never runs out, the surah closing with this narrow but real exception carved out of an otherwise severe warning.

verses.1: By the sky with its great constellations — an oath sworn on the night sky's grandest, most enduring formations, fixed points of reference for every generation that has ever looked upward.
verses.2: And by the Promised Day — a day already fixed in advance, awaited with certainty rather than mere possibility.
verses.3: And by the witness and what is witnessed — testimony and event bound together, nothing occurring without something, or someone, observing it fully.
verses.4: Cursed were the companions of the trench — a real atrocity now enters the narration, a group who dug a pit specifically to carry out cruelty.
verses.5: Filled with fire, fed constantly with fuel — the trench itself made into an instrument of torment, deliberately sustained rather than left to burn out.
verses.6: As they sat beside it, watching —
verses.7: Witnessing themselves exactly what they were doing to the believers — no distance, no denial possible; they watched their own cruelty unfold in real time.
verses.8: And they resented them for nothing more than their belief in Allah, the Exalted, the Praiseworthy — the entire persecution reduced to its true, absurd root: faith alone was the offense.
verses.9: To whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth — and Allah is witness over all things — the very One these victims believed in held complete authority the entire time, even as their persecutors believed themselves powerful.
verses.10: Indeed, those who tormented believing men and women, and never repented, will face the punishment of Hell, and the punishment of the Burning Fire — the cruelty inflicted in the trench answered in kind, but far beyond human scale.
verses.11: But those who believed and did what was righteous will have gardens beneath which rivers flow — that is the greatest triumph, the exact reversal of what their persecutors intended for them.
verses.12: Indeed, the seizing grip of your Lord is severe — a warning left standing plainly, without softening, for anyone who might consider similar cruelty.
verses.13: Indeed, it is He who originates creation, and repeats it — the same power that first brought everything into being is equally capable of bringing it back.
verses.14: And He is the Forgiving, the Loving —
verses.15: Owner of the Throne, the Glorious —
verses.16: Doer of whatever He intends — total, unmatched authority, carried out exactly as He wills, without obstruction from anyone.
verses.17: Has the story reached you — of the soldiers?
verses.18: Of Pharaoh, and of Thamud — two more examples of great power that once believed itself untouchable, invoked here as familiar precedent.
verses.19: Yet those who disbelieve remain in persistent denial — even with this history laid out plainly before them, denial continues, chosen again and again.
verses.20: While Allah surrounds them completely, from every side — their denial changes nothing about the reach of His knowledge and power.
verses.21: But this is a glorious Quran —
verses.22: Inscribed upon a preserved tablet — the surah closes on this note of permanence, a message safeguarded completely, immune to the same erasure and defeat that befell every tyrant named before it.

verses.1: By the sky and the night comer — an oath sworn on something arriving specifically at night, a visitor that only appears once darkness has fallen.
verses.2: And what can make you know what the night comer is? A pause built into the very structure of the verse, inviting curiosity before the answer arrives.
verses.3: It is the piercing star — light cutting through the darkness with such intensity it seems to pierce straight through it, small and distant yet impossible to ignore.
verses.4: There is no soul without a protector watching over it — every single person, unaware or not, has been assigned this quiet, constant guardianship.
verses.5: So let man reflect on what he was created from — a simple invitation toward humility, tracing the body back to its unremarkable, humble origin.
verses.6: He was created from a fluid, expelled forth —
verses.7: Emerging from between the backbone and the ribs — the biological beginning described here with striking, almost clinical precision.
verses.8: Indeed, He who first created is fully able to bring him back to life again — the same power responsible for that humble beginning is more than sufficient for resurrection.
verses.9: On the Day when every hidden secret is brought out and tested —
verses.10: He will have no strength of his own, and no helper to call on — everything relied on in this life, absent at the exact moment it is needed most.
verses.11: By the sky, which sends down rain again and again —
verses.12: And by the earth, which splits open to let new growth through — two more oaths, both drawn from cycles of return and renewal, quietly echoing the resurrection just described.
verses.13: Indeed, this Quran is a decisive statement —
verses.14: And it is no mere amusement — not entertainment, not idle poetry, but something meant to be taken with complete seriousness.
verses.15: Indeed, they are scheming a plan against it —
verses.16: But I too am planning a plan — a striking, almost startling response, meeting human scheming with a far greater, unmatched design.
verses.17: So give the disbelievers a little more time — let them be, for now — the surah closes with patience rather than immediate confrontation, trusting the outcome to a plan already set.

verses.1: Exalt the name of your Lord, the Most High — the surah opens with a command to elevate, to hold nothing above Him, setting the tone for everything about to be described.
verses.2: Who created, and shaped everything in perfect proportion — nothing left careless or accidental, every detail measured with intention.
verses.3: And who determined the destiny of all things, and guided them toward it — creation was never left to wander without direction; a path was set for everything from the start.
verses.4: And who brings forth the green pasture — life pushing up from bare earth, another quiet, recurring proof of His care.
verses.5: Then turns it into dry, dark stubble — the same growth that once flourished eventually fades, a small picture of how everything in this world rises and falls in its season.
verses.6: We will make you recite, and you will not forget — a reassurance given directly, that what is entrusted to memory here will not slip away.
verses.7: Except what Allah wills — even memory itself remains under His control, not the reciter's own. He knows what is spoken openly and what stays hidden.
verses.8: And We will ease you toward what is easiest — every step of this path made smoother by the same hand that shaped the pasture and guided its growth.
verses.9: So remind, wherever the reminder will actually help — wisdom is built into the instruction itself, delivering truth where it can genuinely take root.
verses.10: The one who has awe of his Lord will take the reminder to heart — a heart already softened by reverence is the one most ready to receive it.
verses.11: But the wretched one will turn away from it entirely —
verses.12: The one who will enter the greatest Fire —
verses.13: Neither dying there to escape it, nor truly living — suspended in a state worse than either, denied even the mercy of an ending.
verses.14: He has truly succeeded who purifies himself —
verses.15: And remembers the name of his Lord, and prays — success defined here simply, through remembrance and worship, not through wealth or status.
verses.16: But instead, you prefer the life of this world —
verses.17: Even though the Hereafter is better, and it lasts — the comparison could not be plainer, and yet the pull of the immediate so often wins anyway.
verses.18: Indeed, this truth is found in the earlier scriptures too —
verses.19: The scriptures given to Abraham and to Moses — the surah closes by tying this message to a long chain of revelation, a truth repeated across generations, never new, only renewed.

verses.1: Has news reached you of the Overwhelming Event — a moment so total in its reach that no ordinary word could name it, only this: the thing that covers and engulfs everything at once.
verses.2: Some faces, on that Day, will be downcast — humiliation written plainly across them, all pretense finally stripped away.
verses.3: Laboring, utterly exhausted — worn down not by honest work but by the weight of a life spent chasing the wrong things, now catching up all at once.
verses.4: They will burn in a Fire raging with intensity — the destination matching the exhaustion, no relief waiting on the other side of their striving.
verses.5: Given to drink from a boiling spring — even thirst, the most basic relief imaginable, turned here into its own torment.
verses.6: They will have no food but a bitter, thorny plant — nourishment in name only, offering nothing the body actually needs.
verses.7: Neither nourishing nor satisfying any hunger — a cruel irony, food that exists only to disappoint, forever.
verses.8: Other faces, that same Day, will be glowing with joy — the exact opposite fate, radiant rather than downcast.
verses.9: Pleased with everything their effort had earned them — satisfaction that comes from knowing the striving was never wasted.
verses.10: In a garden raised up high — elevated both literally and in honor, set apart from everything below.
verses.11: Where no idle or hurtful speech will ever be heard — an environment purified even of small unkindness, peace complete down to the last conversation.
verses.12: Within it, a flowing spring — water in constant, generous motion, the very opposite of the boiling spring described earlier.
verses.13: Within it, couches raised high — comfort built into the architecture itself, honor made physical.
verses.14: And cups already set in place — nothing to wait for, nothing to fetch, every desire anticipated before it is even felt.
verses.15: And cushions arranged in rows — the comfort described here is not scarce but abundant, prepared generously for everyone who earned it.
verses.16: And carpets spread out wide — even the ground itself dressed in comfort, nothing left plain or unadorned.
verses.17: So do they not look closely at the camels — how they were created? A sudden shift now, inviting the listener to notice the ordinary miracles surrounding them every day.
verses.18: And at the sky — how it was raised so high above them, without visible support, holding its place effortlessly?
verses.19: And at the mountains — how they were set firmly in place, immovable, anchoring the earth beneath them?
verses.20: And at the earth — how it was spread out flat and wide, made livable and walkable for every creature upon it?
verses.21: So remind them — for you are only sent to remind, nothing more demanded of the messenger than to deliver the message honestly.
verses.22: You are not placed over them as one who controls or compels — the responsibility of belief itself rests with each listener, not with the one delivering the reminder.
verses.23: But as for whoever turns away and rejects the truth entirely —
verses.24: Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment — the consequence reserved specifically for deliberate, persistent rejection, not for honest doubt or searching.
verses.25: Indeed, to Us is their return — no escape route exists from that final destination, however far someone might run in this life.
verses.26: Then indeed, upon Us rests their reckoning — the surah closes with this quiet certainty, that every account will eventually be settled, in full, before the One who alone holds it.

verses.1: By the dawn — an oath sworn on the first fragile light breaking each new day, a recurring, quiet miracle easy to sleep through and easy to take for granted.
verses.2: And by ten nights — a specific, sacred stretch of time, set apart and honored, its significance understood by those familiar with its blessings.
verses.3: And by the even and the odd — numbers themselves invoked here, the balance and pattern built into creation's very structure, down to something as abstract as counting.
verses.4: And by the night as it slips away — the moment darkness departs, quietly, without ceremony, making room again for light.
verses.5: Is there not, in all of this, an oath substantial enough for anyone with real perception? A direct question, almost impatient, aimed at anyone still failing to notice the weight of what has just been sworn upon.
verses.6: Have you not considered how your Lord dealt with the people of 'Aad — a real civilization now enters the narration, once mighty, now remembered chiefly through what became of them.
verses.7: The city of Iram, known for its towering pillars — architecture so remarkable it became legendary, a monument to human ambition and skill.
verses.8: The likes of which had never been built anywhere else in the land — unmatched, unprecedented, a civilization that believed its greatness placed it beyond consequence.
verses.9: And the people of Thamud, who carved their homes directly into the rock of the valley — another advanced people, shaping stone itself into shelter, equally convinced of their own permanence.
verses.10: And Pharaoh, master of the stakes — a ruler remembered for cruelty carried out with elaborate, deliberate methods of punishment and control.
verses.11: All of whom transgressed throughout their lands — three separate powers, three separate eras, united by the same underlying pattern of oppression.
verses.12: And spread corruption everywhere within them — their power was never contained to themselves; it poisoned everything and everyone around them.
verses.13: So your Lord poured upon them a scourge of punishment — decisive, overwhelming, arriving exactly when their arrogance had reached its peak.
verses.14: Indeed, your Lord is ever watching — the lesson closes with this quiet certainty, that nothing these powerful civilizations did had ever gone unseen.
